- `comment_id` added as an explicit required field in the report (currently implied but not in the prose list)
- A formal JSON schema defined in Skill A SKILL.md under a new "Subagent report schema" section, with the exact shape: `{comment_id, fix_outcome, fix_summary, fix_commit_sha, fix_gate_variant, verification_evidence}` and per-field type + conditional-presence rules
- `audit-subagent-report.sh` validates the incoming JSON report against this schema BEFORE running the SHA/commit-count/diff-hunk guards (schema violations exit 2; audit violations exit 1)
- The per-comment subagent prompt template (in Skill A Phase 4) is updated to instruct the subagent to emit its report as JSON matching the schema as its final output

Close `agents-config-3qz` on completion, referencing this bead.

Also update `docs/specs/2026-04-26-pr-review-skill-redesign.md` if it is the canonical schema source referenced by `validate-inventory.sh` — either sync the new subagent-report schema section or add a comment noting SKILL.md is now the authoritative source.

### R4. Model tier split

- **Skill A frontmatter**: `model: sonnet[1m]`, `effort: medium` (down from `opus[1m]`/`high`).
- **Per-comment subagent dispatch** (Skill A Phase 4): the `Agent` tool call MUST specify both:
  - `subagent_type: "bead-implementor"` — provides the required tool set (Read, Edit, Write, Grep, Glob, Bash) and the agent's own model definition
  - `model: "opus"` — explicit override guaranteeing Opus regardless of orchestrator tier or future bead-implementor definition changes
  - NOTE: `effort` is NOT a valid Agent tool parameter; the subagent's reasoning budget is set by bead-implementor's frontmatter definition, not by the dispatch call
- New Red Flag in Skill A: "I'll let the per-comment fix subagent inherit the orchestrator's model — if the orchestrator is on sonnet and the Agent tool dispatch omits `model: 'opus'`, the fix subagent silently runs on sonnet, which may regress classification quality and fix correctness."
- **Skill B frontmatter**: drop to `model: sonnet[1m]`, `effort: low`. Skill B's remaining in-model work after helper extraction is template substitution and bookkeeping. Note: Phase 1.5 interactive recovery requires structured multi-item dialog (batched prompt, ABANDON/DEFER/FIX-NOW routing, DEFER tracking-link validation) — this is more than pure bookkeeping, but the prompt is highly constrained with a fixed choice set and pinned validation rules, which is within sonnet's capability at `effort: low`.

### R5. Helper-script convention conformance

For all NEW helpers (existing helpers stay as-is — migration to named flags is agents-config-2gzy's scope):

- `set -euo pipefail` at top.
- **Named flags only** (no positional args). See Design Notes on coordination with 2gzy.
- Print usage on no-args / `-h` / `--help`.
- Atomic writes via `mktemp` + `mv` for any file mutation.
- Exit codes: `0` success; `1` operational failure; `2` contract/schema violation. Additional codes documented in each script's header.
- **Header block** at the top of each script documenting: purpose, inputs (named flags + their types), outputs (stdout format, exit codes), and for GraphQL helpers — the query projection and what fields are consumed downstream. This replaces the contract documentation that was previously inline in SKILL.md prose.
- Co-located in `<skill-dir>/` alongside existing scripts.
- Smoke test per script using a co-located `*_test.sh` shell harness (no external framework required — plain bash assert functions). Each test file exercises at least one happy path + one failure path per script.

## Out of scope

- **Parallel per-comment subagent dispatch.** v1's serial-only constraint stays; file-overlap prediction is a separate optimization deferred to a follow-up bead.
- **Standalone `reply-and-resolve-pr-threads` mode** (no `--from-inventory`/`--resume`). Already deferred in v1; this bead does not unblock it.
- **Live token measurement** (before/after on a real PR). Acceptance is structural-proxy only. Post-merge measurement is tracked as a follow-up: at implementation time, file a new bead ("Measure token-delta from PR skill optimization on 2-3 representative PR runs") as a `discovered-from` sibling of the implementation bead Y — target Y (the bead created by finalize), NOT the spec bead being closed by finalize.
- **Migrating existing helpers to named flags** (write-inventory.sh, validate-inventory.sh, poll-copilot-review.sh, etc.). Covered by agents-config-2gzy. This bead only ensures NEW helpers conform; the inconsistency between old-positional and new-named-flag helpers is acknowledged and tracked via 2gzy.
- **Backfilling smoke tests for existing helpers** (poll-copilot-review.sh, etc.). Acknowledged as an asymmetry; tracked separately.
- **Other formula-shared helper extractions** (parent-chain claim/close walks, merge-gate creator, label-collision detector). Covered by agents-config-fl80 / agents-config-acmh.12.
- **Cross-PR inventory housekeeping** beyond the existing 30-day pruning in `write-inventory.sh`.

## Acceptance criteria

1. **No inline jq pipeline or GraphQL query body in phase-prose sections of either SKILL.md.** One reference-invocation block per distinct helper script is allowed in the "Helper-script invocation patterns" section (for operator comprehension — each named helper may have one copy-pasteable reference block; multiple helpers = multiple blocks, one per helper). Verified by grep on phase-prose sections: `grep -E "jq -n|jq -nc|jq --null-input|gh api graphql" <phase-prose-only>` returns zero matches outside of allowed reference sections.
2. **The 5 duplicate inventory-builder blocks** in Skill A (Phases 5a, 5b, 5c, 7, 8) are each replaced by a single-line invocation of `build-inventory-body.sh`. The jq pipeline appears exactly once, in `build-inventory-body.sh` itself.
3. **Skill A frontmatter** is `model: sonnet[1m]`, `effort: medium`.
4. **Skill A's Phase 4 prose** documents the per-comment subagent dispatch with `subagent_type: "bead-implementor"` and `model: "opus"` in the Agent tool args, and includes a Red Flag: "I'll let the fix subagent inherit the orchestrator's model" → why it's wrong.
5. **Skill B has working helper scripts** for all 5 R2 targets; SKILL.md prose references each by stable command-line invocation; no inline implementations remain in phase-prose sections.
6. **Subagent fix_outcome JSON schema** is documented in Skill A under a "Subagent report schema" section listing all fields with types and conditional-presence rules. `audit-subagent-report.sh` validates incoming reports against the schema (exit 2 on schema violation). Bead `agents-config-3qz` is closed: `bd close agents-config-3qz --reason "Absorbed: contract documented in wait-for-pr-comments/SKILL.md; validated by audit-subagent-report.sh"`.
7. **Every new helper** (a) begins with `set -euo pipefail`, (b) accepts named flags only (no positional args), (c) has a header block documenting inputs/outputs/exit-codes and query projection for GraphQL helpers, (d) has a co-located `*_test.sh` smoke test exercising happy path + at least one failure path.
8. **Skill B frontmatter** is `model: sonnet[1m]`, `effort: low`.
9. **Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass** (existing tests + new smoke tests for all extracted helpers).

## Design notes

- **Why split model tiers, not drop both globally?** Classification in Skill A Phase 3 (FIX/SKIP/ESCALATE) is genuine semantic judgment, but the prompt is tight and structured — sonnet handles it adequately. The code-fix work (read code, propose change, verify) is the high-judgment task and must stay at opus. The orchestrator never fixes code; it dispatches. Dispatching with `subagent_type: "bead-implementor"` and `model: "opus"` ensures the fix work runs at the right tier regardless of what the orchestrator is doing.
- **Why `effort` is NOT in the Agent tool dispatch.** The Agent tool's `effort` parameter does not exist in v1 of the harness. `effort` is a frontmatter field for agent/skill definitions and is NOT propagated via the Agent tool call. bead-implementor's own frontmatter defines its reasoning budget; specifying `effort` at dispatch would be silently ignored.
- **Named-flags inconsistency with existing helpers.** The existing 6 helpers in Skill A all use positional arguments (matching write-inventory.sh, validate-inventory.sh, poll-copilot-review.sh patterns). New helpers will use named flags (per agents-config-2gzy preference). Until 2gzy lands and migrates the old helpers, the skill directory will contain a positional-args cohort and a named-flags cohort. This inconsistency is accepted as a known cost; 2gzy's scope should be updated to include aligning the new helpers once they land.
- **Why absorb 3qz here?** `audit-subagent-report.sh` is unimplementable without a formal subagent report contract — the audit IS schema validation + regex on contract-shaped output. Splitting the work leaves a stub helper waiting on a separate bead. Absorbing keeps the change atomic.
- **Comprehension preservation.** The existing SKILL.md inline jq/GraphQL blocks serve as contract documentation. Removing them from phase prose risks leaving operators unable to understand what a helper does without reading the script. Every new helper MUST have a header block documenting its contract (input, output, query projection for GraphQL). A single representative invocation-pattern example block may be retained in each skill's "Helper-script invocation patterns" section as operator orientation; phase prose uses command invocations only.
- **Test asymmetry acknowledged.** The existing 6 helpers in Skill A have zero tests. New helpers will have smoke tests (`*_test.sh` co-located). This raises the bar for new helpers without backfilling for old ones — the asymmetry is explicit and accepted; backfilling is a separate concern.
- **Structural-proxy success bar.** Live token-before/after measurement is not required for this bead's closure. Structural ACs (no inline jq/GraphQL in phase prose, frontmatter tier change, duplication elimination) are the observable proxies. A follow-up bead for empirical measurement will be filed at finalize step.
- **Helper location**: skill-internal (`<skill-dir>/`), matching existing precedent. No cross-skill shared library beyond `lib.sh` (already in Skill A; Skill B may grow its own `lib.sh` if needed during implementation).
- **Backward compat**: SKILL.md prose retains its phase structure and Red-Flag tables. The change is "inline block → command invocation"; phase semantics are preserved. All extraction lands in one PR for atomicity.

## Open questions

None blocking. (The test framework was pinned to a co-located `*_test.sh` shell harness during spec revision; no external bats dependency required.)

## Original problem framing (preserved from create-bead)

`wait-for-pr-comments` and `reply-and-resolve-pr-threads` have been consuming a significant % of token allowance recently. Goal: extract polling loops and decision logic into efficient helper scripts so model effort goes to decisions, not GitHub I/O loops. Current state: agents reconstruct polling logic at runtime instead of invoking stable commands. Precedent: `wait-for-pr-comments` internals already use `write-inventory.sh` and `validate-inventory.sh` pattern.

Route Z forwarding label points to abn9.3 so any future lookup on abn9.2 routes correctly through the chain.

title: [Impl] Update ralf-* skills and formulas to consume worker-report-v1 and dispatch new workers
description:
## Summary

Wire the new worker-agent architecture (7bk.19.1 / 19.2 / 19.3) into the two orchestration skills (`ralf-implement`, `ralf-review`). Update `ralf-implement` to consume `worker-report-v1` via `implement-bead`'s per-dispatch primitive; add a foreign-eyes option to `ralf-review`; rename prompt-template files using a `subagent-*` / `foreign-cli-*` scheme; wire concrete template-path references into both SKILL.md files; close two remaining formula-file gaps not addressed by PR #66 (header pointer comment + `subagent_type` plumbing in green-loop dispatch prose).

## Background / Motivation

7bk.19.1 defined the `worker-report-v1` contract. 7bk.19.2 created three focused worker agents (`tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser`). 7bk.19.3 rewrote `implement-bead` as a metadata-driven dispatcher with the per-dispatch primitive at `implement-bead/SKILL.md` §4 and the upstream-report retrieval at §6. PR #66 (commit `11c5bd1`, 2026-05-12 — "feat(agents): migrate bead-implementor to five role-named workers (acmh.13)") then migrated both formula files (`implement-feature.formula.toml`, `fix-bug.formula.toml`) to stage-declarative dispatch language and deleted `bead-implementor.md`.

This bead closes the remaining 19.4 work that PR #66 did **not** address:

1. The `ralf-implement` SKILL.md itself still has no `worker-report-v1` wiring, no explicit convergence predicate, and no reference to the per-dispatch primitive.
2. `ralf-review` has no foreign-eyes option (S2, from superseded `agents-config-abn9.9`).
3. Prompt-template files in both `ralf-*` folders are unreferenced by their SKILL.md (S1, from superseded `abn9.9`).
4. Two narrow formula-file gaps remain post-PR-#66: (a) no `ralf:required` header pointer comment in either formula file; (b) the green-loop dispatch prose does not pass `subagent_type: tdd-green-team` to the new ralf-implement contract (introduced in this bead), which is now a fail-fast input under R1.11.

## Requirements

### R1 — `ralf-implement/SKILL.md` rewrite

R1.1. Consume `worker-report-v1` evidence blocks and derive gate roll-up (`pass` / `fail` / `partial` / `n/a`) per `worker-report-v1.md` §1.1. Workers do NOT emit `gate_status`; the orchestrator derives it.

R1.2. Dispatch inner doer via Agent tool with caller-provided `subagent_type`. ralf-implement is intended for work whose success state is `gate=pass` (machine gates apply) OR `gate=n/a` (no machine gates apply, e.g. docs/config repos); it is NOT intended to orchestrate red-tests (success = intentionally-failing tests, gate-shape = `fail`) or diagnose (single-shot, no iteration).
- The canonical recommendation in the interactive prompt is `tdd-green-team` (the only named worker whose success state matches the convergence predicate at R1.6).
- `general-purpose` is the fallback when no project-specific worker fits. `general-purpose` is a Claude Code builtin `subagent_type`, NOT an agent file in the repo.
- Other workers (`tdd-red-team`, `bug-diagnoser`) are dispatched directly via the per-dispatch primitive from formula steps (not via ralf-implement) because their gate shape does not match the convergence predicate; do NOT offer them in the interactive prompt.
- If an interactive caller declines both options, ralf-implement aborts with a clear diagnostic naming `subagent_type` as the missing input. No silent fallback.

R1.3. Reference the per-dispatch primitive at `implement-bead/SKILL.md` §4 via informal section reference (`see implement-bead/SKILL.md §4 — Per-dispatch primitive`). Do NOT inline the procedure.

R1.4. Synthesize `status: failed` on crash/malformed worker output per `worker-report-v1.md` §4: empty `evidence`, `escalations[].reason` of `"Worker crashed"` or `"Worker emitted malformed report"`, `discovered_work: []`, `commits: []`.

R1.4.1. **Synthesize on schema violation in evidence sub-blocks** (cycle-4 F-malformed-evidence resolution). If the worker-report parses as YAML and carries `status: complete` but any present `evidence` sub-block violates the `worker-report-v1.md` §1.1 schema (e.g., `evidence.tests` is missing required `command` / `exit_code` / `skipped`; or a field is the wrong type — string where int expected, etc.), treat as malformed and synthesize `status: failed` with `escalations[].reason: "Worker emitted malformed report"` wrapping the schema violation in `escalations[].detail`. Without this, R1.6's status-first gate has a tunnel: a `status: complete` report with corrupt sub-fields would compute a misleading derived gate and route to a convergence-candidate path.

R1.5. Stamp `worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]` label per dispatch ONLY when the dispatched doer is a named worker covered by `implement-bead/SKILL.md` "Audit-label scope" (`tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser`). For `general-purpose` or any other non-worker doer, do NOT stamp `worker-audit-*`.

ralf-implement OWNS this conditional in policy. **In practice it is documentation-only until upstream `agents-config-go1w` lands** (cycle-4 F-unenforceable-conditional resolution): §4 step 5 currently stamps `worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]` unconditionally and §4 accepts no "skip step 5" directive. Stamping a label after §4 returns cannot retract a label already applied. Therefore, in 19.7's deliverable, `general-purpose` dispatches via §4 WILL receive a `worker-audit-general-purpose-iter<N>` label — accepting this is the explicit cost of NOT modifying §4 in this PR (per D3).

The 19.7 deliverable: `ralf-implement/SKILL.md` documents the policy ("non-named-worker doers SHOULD NOT carry `worker-audit-*`"), names `go1w` as the upstream tracking item, and notes that current behavior over-stamps in the `general-purpose` case until `go1w` lands. No active filtering or post-hoc label removal in ralf-implement — that would be an integration tangent for this bead.

R1.6. Explicit convergence predicate. The predicate branches on TWO signals from the worker-report: (a) `status` (verbatim from the report or synthesized per `worker-report-v1.md` §4), and (b) derived gate roll-up per `worker-report-v1.md` §1.1.

- **`status = failed` (real or synthesized crash/malformed report)**: NOT a convergence candidate, regardless of derived gate. Iterate until cap. At cap: `FAIL`.
- **`status = complete` AND `gate = pass`**: convergence candidate; converge if fresh-eyes finds no blocking/critical. At cap with no blocking/critical: `PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS`.
- **`status = complete` AND `gate = n/a`** (no machine gates apply — docs/config repos; not a crash report): convergence candidate; fresh-eyes alone decides convergence. Converge when fresh-eyes finds no blocking/critical. At cap with no blocking/critical: `PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS`.
- **`status = complete` AND `gate = fail` or `gate = partial`**: NOT a convergence candidate; iterate until cap. At cap: `FAIL`.
- **Otherwise at cap** (blocking/critical present after fresh-eyes, regardless of status/gate): `FAIL`.

The status check is the first gate. Without it, a synthesized crash report (which has `evidence: {}` → derived gate `n/a` per `worker-report-v1.md` §4) would silently pass through as a convergence candidate.

R1.7. Convergence-candidate definition: an iteration where `status = complete` AND derived gate roll-up ∈ {`pass`, `n/a`}. Any `status: failed` iteration is excluded from convergence-candidate-hood by definition.

R1.8. Per-cycle loop:
- Default cycle cap remains `RALF_IMPLEMENT_DEFAULT_CYCLES=3`. The cycle range is `1..20` per the existing skill contract.
- Cycle sequence: cycle 1 = foreign-eyes via Codex (`gpt-5.5`); cycle 2 = foreign-eyes via Gemini; cycle 3+ = pure-Claude fresh-eyes (matches existing skill semantics — extended cycles 4–20 are pure-Claude). NOTE: this sequence matches the current `ralf-implement/SKILL.md`; R1.8 is documentation alignment for this bullet, not a behavior change.
- **Degradation cascade** (cycle-4 F-cascade-asymmetry resolution): ralf-implement adopts the same cascade as ralf-review (see R2.2.1). On Codex degradation at any cycle: dispatch Gemini as the fallback for that cycle index (not pure-Claude). On Gemini degradation when Gemini was the fallback OR the planned cycle: fall back to pure-Claude. Cycle counts as consumed regardless. Evidence-store canonicalization (per R2.2.1 / D26) applies symmetrically: both attempt artifacts persist under `.ralf/<session-id>/`; cycle status reports both attempts (e.g. `cycle-N=Codex:<STATUS>,Gemini:COMPLETED`); fallback output is canonical input. The existing `ralf-implement/SKILL.md` "degrade cleanly to pure fresh-eyes" line is superseded by this cascade.
- `quality-reviewer` + `simplify` run ONLY on convergence candidates (per R1.7). They do NOT run on `status: failed` iterations or on `gate ∈ {fail, partial}` iterations.
- `simplify` is advisory-only. Trigger criterion (binary, measurable): after `simplify` lands its edits, re-run the project test command from the worker-report's `evidence.tests.command`. If any previously-passing test now fails, restart the loop via a new worker dispatch — `simplify`'s output is rejected. If tests still pass, accept `simplify` and converge. This eliminates judgment calls about what counts as "test-affecting." **N/A case** (cycle-4 F-simplify-na resolution): if `evidence.tests.command` is empty or absent (gate=n/a docs/config repos, or a worker that did not run tests), there is no test command to re-run — accept `simplify`'s output without a re-run and converge. The regression check is N/A; fresh-eyes review remains the convergence signal in that branch.
- `verify-checklist` is removed as a separate per-iteration step. Its inputs are split: tests/build/lint/typecheck evidence is now derived from each worker's `worker-report-v1` `evidence` blocks (the gate roll-up at R1.1); the quality-reviewer + simplify portion runs on convergence candidates (preceding bullet). The historical "run verify-checklist every iteration" behavior is intentionally retired.

R1.9. Iteration-routing section names each prompt template by concrete relative path. The paths are **documentation references** for human readers navigating the skill directory; they are NOT resolved at runtime by the dispatched Claude subagent (which receives a prompt string, not a `cwd` rooted at the skill directory).

At dispatch time, ralf-implement (the markdown skill interpreted by the orchestrator LLM) performs these **operational steps** (cycle-4 F-inline-paste-procedure resolution):

1. **`Read`** the relevant template file by its relative path inside the installed skill directory (the orchestrator resolves the path against the loaded skill's location).
2. **Substitute** every placeholder in the template body (e.g., `[PASTE ORIGINAL DOD]`, `[PASTE ORIGINAL SPEC]`, `[PASTE CONTEXT]`) with the corresponding caller-supplied input string. Placeholders are uppercase-bracket tokens by convention.
3. **Dispatch** via `Agent({subagent_type: <doer>, prompt: <substituted-body>})`. Templates are read fresh on each dispatch — do NOT assume cached content.

This matches the existing convention in `implementer-prompt.md` (which embeds `[PASTE ORIGINAL DOD]` placeholders the orchestrator fills before dispatch).

Templates:
- `./subagent-implementer.md` — initial-cycle dispatch (Claude subagent body)
- `./subagent-fresh-eyes.md` — pure-Claude fresh-eyes cycles (Claude subagent body)
- `./subagent-foreign-cycle.md` — foreign-augmented cycles (Claude subagent body, which then invokes the foreign CLI)
- `./foreign-cli-instructions.md` — instruction file piped to Codex / Gemini via stdin (NOT inline-pasted into an Agent-tool prompt; the dispatched Claude foreign-cycle subagent — itself the product of inline-pasting `subagent-foreign-cycle.md` — reads `foreign-cli-instructions.md` from its repo-relative path inside the worktree and pipes the body to `codex-companion.mjs task` / `gemini` on stdin).

R1.10. `ralf:required` label semantics documented in `ralf-implement/SKILL.md` as the canonical home (the label is its trigger signal).

R1.11. Non-interactive context detection is **caller-supplied**, not shell-evaluated. ralf-implement is a markdown skill interpreted by an LLM, not a shell script — it cannot reliably evaluate `[ -t 0 ]` from inside the model. The caller indicates non-interactive context by EITHER:
- passing an explicit argument (e.g. `non_interactive: true`) in the skill invocation context, OR
- setting `RALF_NONINTERACTIVE=1` in the harness environment (a value the model can read via a single `bash` call if needed).

**Argument syntax canonicalization** (cycle-4 F-syntax resolution): all arguments passed to ralf-implement in invocation prose use `<key>: <value>` form (colon-space-value, YAML-style). Examples: `subagent_type: tdd-green-team`, `non_interactive: true`, `max_cycles: 5`. The orchestrator LLM is the parser; consistent shape across all callers prevents drift. Form variants (`key=value`, `--key value`, `key:value` without space) are NOT canonical and SHOULD NOT be used in caller invocation prose.

In non-interactive context with `subagent_type` missing, ralf-implement fails fast with a diagnostic naming `subagent_type` as the missing input and explaining that orchestration callers (e.g. `implement-bead`, formula green-loop steps) must pass it explicitly. No prompt is offered.

Orchestration callers (`implement-bead`, `run-queue`, formula green-loop steps) MUST pass `subagent_type` explicitly; they are the canonical non-interactive consumers. R4.2 makes this concrete for the two formula files.

### R2 — `ralf-review/SKILL.md` rewrite

R2.1. Document `worker-report-v1` as N/A with rationale: review subagents are out-of-band per `implement-bead/SKILL.md` audit-label scope; their output is review findings, not gate evidence.

R2.2. Add foreign-eyes option (Q2 resolution: always-on, compressed):
- Default cycle cap stays at `RALF_REVIEW_DEFAULT_CYCLES=2`. Cycle range `1..20`.
- Default sequence (`cycles=2`): cycle 1 = foreign-eyes via Codex (`gpt-5.5`), cycle 2 = pure-Claude fresh-eyes.
- Extended sequence (`ralf:cycles=3`): cycle 1 = Codex, cycle 2 = Gemini, cycle 3 = pure-Claude.
- Cycles 4+ (when `ralf:cycles >= 4`): pure-Claude (matches ralf-implement's open-ended pure-Claude tail).
- Degradation status codes mirror `ralf-implement`: `UNAVAILABLE`, `TIMED_OUT`, `QUOTA_EXCEEDED`, `AUTH_FAILED`, `NO_OUTPUT`, `UNUSABLE_OUTPUT`.

R2.2.1. Degradation cascade (F17 resolution + F2 evidence-store canonicalization): preserve a foreign reviewer in the run whenever possible.

- On Codex degradation at cycle 1 with any of the six status codes: dispatch Gemini as the cycle-1 fallback (not pure-Claude). Cycle 2 runs as planned (pure-Claude at `cycles=2` default; Gemini at `cycles=3`).
- On Gemini degradation when Gemini was the fallback OR the planned cycle: dispatch pure-Claude for that cycle index.
- The cycle counts as consumed against the cap regardless of which model ran the fallback.
- Recording a degradation status without running the appropriate cascade is an error.

Evidence-store canonicalization (F2 resolution): when the cascade runs, both the failed-foreign-attempt artifact AND the successful-fallback artifact MUST be preserved under `.ralf/<session-id>/` (per the existing `foreign-eyes-prompt.md` naming convention `{agent_lower}-review-{timestamp}.md`). The cycle's foreign-eyes status reports BOTH attempts in the form `cycle-N=Codex:<STATUS_CODE>,Gemini:COMPLETED` (or similar). The fallback's output is the **canonical findings input** for that cycle index; the failed attempt's artifact is forensic only. The status string format MUST be machine-parseable so the orchestrator's structured report can render it without ambiguity.

R2.3. Create new template files paralleling `ralf-implement`:
- `subagent-foreign-cycle.md` — Claude reviewer subagent prompt for a foreign-augmented review cycle; structurally mirrors `ralf-implement/subagent-foreign-cycle.md` (the POST-rename file from R3.1) but adapted to review semantics (no "fix the implementation" steps; output is findings + scoring, not code changes).
- `foreign-cli-instructions.md` — template piped to Codex / Gemini for the review request; structurally mirrors `ralf-implement/foreign-cli-instructions.md` (the POST-rename file) but asks the foreign model to review the TARGET ARTIFACT (spec / plan / design doc / code change), not implementation state against a DoD.

"Paralleling" means **structural mirror** (content shape, section order, placeholder conventions), not literal source-derivation. Ordering: R3.1 renames complete first, then R2.3 creates `ralf-review/` files by structurally mirroring the post-rename `ralf-implement/` files. The implementer copies the shape, not the body.

R2.4. Iteration-routing section names each prompt template by concrete relative path (same documentation-reference / inline-paste convention as R1.9):
- `./subagent-fresh-eyes.md`
- `./subagent-foreign-cycle.md`
- `./foreign-cli-instructions.md`

### R3 — Template-file renames (Scheme A)

R3.1. `ralf-implement/`: rename `implementer-prompt.md` → `subagent-implementer.md`; `fresh-eyes-prompt.md` → `subagent-fresh-eyes.md`; `foreign-eyes-prompt.md` → `subagent-foreign-cycle.md`; `foreign-agent-prompt.md` → `foreign-cli-instructions.md`. The renames use `git mv` (or equivalent); old filenames MUST NOT exist on disk after the rename.

R3.2. `ralf-review/`: rename existing `fresh-eyes-prompt.md` → `subagent-fresh-eyes.md` (same disk-delete invariant). R2.3 then creates the two new files (`subagent-foreign-cycle.md`, `foreign-cli-instructions.md`). Implementation order: R3.1 → R3.2 → R2.3 (so R2.3 mirrors the post-rename ralf-implement files).

R3.3. All SKILL.md path references updated to new names. **In addition**, all intra-template references must be updated: the existing `foreign-eyes-prompt.md` body references `./foreign-agent-prompt.md`; after rename, `subagent-foreign-cycle.md` must reference `./foreign-cli-instructions.md`. No active template, skill file, or formula file may reference any of the four old filenames after the rename.

### R4 — Formula-file gaps not addressed by PR #66

R4.1. Add a `ralf:required` header pointer comment near the top of both `implement-feature.formula.toml` and `fix-bug.formula.toml`:
```
# ralf:required label semantics: see src/user/.agents/skills/ralf-implement/SKILL.md
```

R4.2. Update the green-loop step's `ralf:required` dispatch prose in BOTH `implement-feature.formula.toml` and `fix-bug.formula.toml` to pass `subagent_type: tdd-green-team` explicitly to ralf-implement. The two files have **different current prose** (fix-bug also propagates `the root-cause note`), so the prescriptive replacement is split per-file (cycle-4 F-critical-fix-bug-prose resolution).

**For `implement-feature.formula.toml`** (current prose, verbatim):
> "If label `ralf:required` is present: invoke `ralf-implement` with the bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, and optional max cycle count from `ralf:cycles=N`"

becomes:

> "If label `ralf:required` is present: invoke `ralf-implement` with the bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, `subagent_type: tdd-green-team`, and optional max cycle count from `ralf:cycles=N`"

**For `fix-bug.formula.toml`** (current prose, verbatim):
> "If label `ralf:required` is present: invoke `ralf-implement` with the root-cause note, bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, and optional max cycle count from `ralf:cycles=N`"

becomes:

> "If label `ralf:required` is present: invoke `ralf-implement` with the root-cause note, bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, `subagent_type: tdd-green-team`, and optional max cycle count from `ralf:cycles=N`"

**The root-cause note argument MUST be preserved** in the fix-bug form — it is the §6 propagation chain from the upstream diagnose step's worker-report. Dropping it would break fix-bug's downstream test-writing dispatch.

Rationale: R1.11 makes `subagent_type` a fail-fast input in non-interactive context. Formula green-loop steps run non-interactively. Without this update, every autonomous formula-driven ralf-implement run trips the new fail-fast contract.

The previously-listed (and later-dropped) `R4.2: root_cause_note propagation` is NOT re-added — that case is handled by `implement-bead/SKILL.md` §6's upstream-report retrieval primitive shipped in 19.3. The `reproduction_steps` schema-vs-§6.3 mismatch remains filed as upstream discovered work (out of 19.4 scope).

## Design Notes

**D1 — subagent_type: required input with interactive fallback (interactive contexts only).**
ralf-implement requires `subagent_type` from the orchestration caller. In interactive contexts, a missing `subagent_type` triggers an interactive prompt offering `tdd-green-team` (canonical) and `general-purpose` (fallback). User declining both → abort with diagnostic. In non-interactive contexts (caller-supplied flag or `RALF_NONINTERACTIVE=1` env), missing input fails fast with the same diagnostic. Rationale: keeps the contract clean (no hardcoded defaults) while preserving UX for human callers and refusing to hang in autonomous run-queue / implement-bead / formula orchestration.

**D2 — ralf-review: minimal update extended by S2 expansion.**
Originally D2 specified ralf-review as a minimal worker-report-v1-N/A documentation note only. Scope expansion (2026-05-12) added the foreign-eyes option (S2). The N/A documentation stands; the foreign-eyes work is additive.

**D3 — per-dispatch primitive: anchor reference, not duplication.**
ralf-implement references `implement-bead/SKILL.md`'s per-dispatch primitive via an informal section reference (`see implement-bead/SKILL.md §4 — Per-dispatch primitive`) rather than inlining the procedure; `implement-bead/SKILL.md` is NOT modified in this PR. Rationale: single canonical home; avoids drift between the two files.

**D4 — ralf:required documentation home.**
Canonical doc in `ralf-implement/SKILL.md` (label is its trigger signal). Short pointer comment in each formula file header (R4.1).

**D5 — bead-implementor.md deletion (SUPERSEDED — landed in PR #66).**
PR #66 (2026-05-12, commit `11c5bd1`) delivered the deletion ahead of this bead. The invariant grep AC remains in this bead for regression protection.

**D6 — Three follow-up beads filed.**
7bk.21: inline-execution directive for `implement-bead`. 7bk.22: explicit `agent:*` / `skill:*` dispatch label overrides. 7bk.23: `stage:<role>` label stamping for robust upstream step lookup. All three depend on 7bk.19.4 and are P4/backlog, unbrainstormed.

**D7 — Convergence predicate: status + gate + fresh-eyes combined.**
Converge when: `status = complete` AND derived gate ∈ {`pass`, `n/a`} AND fresh-eyes finds no blocking/critical. At cycle cap with `status = complete` + gate ∈ {`pass`, `n/a`} + no blocking/critical → `PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS`; blocking/critical or gate ∈ {`fail`, `partial`} or `status = failed` → `FAIL`. Scope: this predicate applies to ralf-implement orchestration of gate=pass-shaped OR gate=n/a-shaped workers (canonically `tdd-green-team` in code repos; `general-purpose` in docs/config repos). It does NOT apply to red-tests (gate-shape `fail` is success) or diagnose (single-shot, no iteration); those stages dispatch directly via the per-dispatch primitive from formula steps.

**D8 — Per-cycle loop: quality-reviewer + simplify on convergence candidate only.**
Convergence candidate = iteration where `status = complete` AND derived gate ∈ {`pass`, `n/a`} (per R1.7). quality-reviewer + simplify run on convergence candidates only, not on `status: failed` iterations or on `gate ∈ {fail, partial}` iterations. The legacy "run verify-checklist every iteration" step is removed: per-iteration test/build/lint/typecheck evidence is now derived from each worker's `worker-report-v1` evidence blocks (R1.1 gate roll-up), so a separate verify-checklist iteration is redundant.

**D9 — Formula step language: stage-declarative (SUPERSEDED — landed in PR #66).**
PR #66 (2026-05-12) rewrote both formula step descriptions to stage-declarative language and removed MAX_ITERATIONS literals and loop-prose. No further formula step-description rewrite needed in this bead beyond R4.1 (header pointer) and R4.2 (green-loop `subagent_type` plumbing).

**D10 — Convergence candidate definition (extended for n/a, gated by status).**
Cycle-2 review fix (F12): convergence candidate includes iterations where derived gate is `pass` OR `n/a`. Cycle-3 review fix (F3): adds the `status = complete` gate so synthesized crash reports (which have `evidence: {}` → derived gate `n/a` per `worker-report-v1.md` §4) are NOT mis-classified as convergence candidates. The `n/a` branch covers docs/config repos and any work where no machine-gate evidence applies AND the worker completed successfully — fresh-eyes alone is the convergence signal in that case. quality-reviewer + simplify still run on each convergence candidate.

**D11 — simplify is advisory; correctness changes restart the loop.**
Cycle-3 review fix (F8): `simplify`'s trigger criterion is now measurable. After `simplify` lands its edits, re-run the project test command from the worker-report's `evidence.tests.command`. Tests still pass → accept `simplify` and converge. Any previously-passing test now fails → reject `simplify`'s output and restart the loop with a new worker dispatch. This replaces the older "test-affecting production changes" phrasing, which required judgment about what counted.

**D12 — general-purpose fallback is a Claude Code builtin subagent_type.**
`subagent_type: 'general-purpose'` is a Claude Code builtin, not an agent file in the repo. Clarified in spec to avoid confusion with agent files.

**D13 — per-dispatch primitive reference is informal (not a formal markdown anchor).**
`implement-bead/SKILL.md` has no formal anchors and is not modified in this PR. `ralf-implement` uses an informal section reference (`see implement-bead/SKILL.md §4 — Per-dispatch primitive`).

**D14 — ralf-review foreign-eyes policy: always-on, compressed (Q2 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 brainstorm. ralf-review defaults to foreign-eyes-on (mirroring ralf-implement's default-on posture) but at the existing cycle budget of 2. Default sequence: Codex / pure-Claude. `ralf:cycles=3` extends to Codex / Gemini / pure-Claude. Cycles 4+ (at `ralf:cycles>=4`) are pure-Claude. Considered: always-on at cycles=3 default (rejected: too expensive for routine reviews); caller opt-in (rejected: asymmetric default behavior with ralf-implement).

**D15 — Template-filename naming scheme: subagent-* / foreign-cli-* (Scheme A).**
Added 2026-05-12 brainstorm. Old filenames confused audience: "foreign-eyes" sounded like the foreign model's prompt (it is actually the Claude subagent's mission), "foreign-agent" was ambiguous. New scheme: `subagent-*` = prompt body inline-pasted into a Claude subagent's Agent-tool dispatch; `foreign-cli-*` = template body piped on stdin to Codex/Gemini. Audience explicit at filename level.

**D16 — PR #66 supersedence: scope reduction.**
Added 2026-05-12 brainstorm. PR #66 (commit `11c5bd1`, 2026-05-12) landed the bulk of the original 19.4 formula-side scope ahead of this bead. Remaining 19.4 work this bead closes: (a) `ralf-implement` SKILL.md rewrite (R1), (b) `ralf-review` SKILL.md rewrite + S2 foreign-eyes (R2), (c) prompt-template renames + path references (R3 / S1), (d) two narrow formula gaps (R4.1: `ralf:required` header pointer; R4.2: green-loop `subagent_type` plumbing).

**D17 — ralf-implement gate-shape scope.**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-1 review (F2), extended cycle-2 review (F12), extended cycle-3 review (F3 — status check added). ralf-implement orchestrates work whose success state is `status: complete` AND gate ∈ {`pass`, `n/a`}. `status: failed` (real or synthesized crash) is never a convergence candidate. red-tests (success = gate=fail by design) and diagnose (single-shot, no iteration) dispatch directly via the per-dispatch primitive from formula steps. The interactive subagent_type prompt offers only `tdd-green-team` and `general-purpose` (R1.2).

**D18 — verify-checklist retirement scope.**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-1 review (F4). "verify-checklist subsumed by gate roll-up" specifically means: per-iteration test/build/lint/typecheck checks are now derived from worker-report-v1 evidence (no separate run); the quality-reviewer + simplify portion of verify-checklist runs on convergence candidates only (D8/D10). Historical per-iteration verify-checklist is intentionally retired, not silently dropped.

**D19 — Non-interactive context: caller-supplied flag, not shell test.**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-1 review (F6), corrected cycle-2 review (F14). The original D19 used `[ -t 0 ]` shell test; that does not work for an LLM-interpreted markdown skill. Replaced with caller-supplied indicator: explicit `non_interactive: true` argument OR `RALF_NONINTERACTIVE=1` env. Orchestration callers (`implement-bead`, `run-queue`, formula green-loop steps) MUST pass `subagent_type` explicitly and are the canonical non-interactive consumers.

**D20 — Docs-only repo validation: build/typecheck/tests AC replaced.**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-1 review (F7). This repo has no build / typecheck / test infrastructure (per project AGENTS.md). The historical "Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass." AC is replaced by docs-validation: filesystem-level rename checks (new files exist; old filenames absent), SKILL.md and intra-template path-reference greps, and the invariant grep at R5.1 / old-filename grep at R5.2.

**D21 — worker-audit label scope (R1.5 constraint).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-2 review (F19), refined cycle-3 review (F5). ralf-implement stamps `worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]` only when the dispatched doer is a named worker covered by `implement-bead/SKILL.md` "Audit-label scope" (`tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser`). For `general-purpose` or other non-worker doers, ralf-implement does NOT stamp `worker-audit-*`. The conditional lives in ralf-implement, not in §4 — an upstream §4 contradiction (step 5 stamps unconditionally) is filed as `agents-config-go1w`.

**D22 — Degradation cascade preserves foreign-eyes value (F17 resolution + F2 evidence-store canonicalization).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-2 review (F17), extended cycle-3 review (F2). On Codex degradation at any cycle, dispatch Gemini as the fallback for that cycle index (not pure-Claude). Only when Gemini also degrades — or Gemini was the planned cycle and degrades — fall back to pure-Claude. Cycle counts as consumed regardless. Both attempt artifacts persist under `.ralf/<session-id>/`; cycle status reports both attempts (`cycle-N=Codex:<STATUS>,Gemini:COMPLETED`); fallback's output is the canonical findings input.

**D23 — R4.2 (root_cause_note) dropped; R4.2 (subagent_type) re-added.**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-2 review (F16). The originally-scoped R4.2 ("propagate root_cause_note from diagnose to red-tests in fix-bug formula") is dropped: `implement-bead/SKILL.md` §6 already handles this via the upstream-report retrieval primitive shipped in 19.3. Cycle-3 review (F4) re-introduced an R4.2 slot for a DIFFERENT concern: formula green-loop dispatch prose now must pass `subagent_type: tdd-green-team` to ralf-implement, given R1.11's non-interactive fail-fast contract. The `reproduction_steps` schema-vs-§6.3 mismatch remains upstream discovered work.

**D24 — Template-path resolution mechanism (cycle-3 F1 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-3 review. The "concrete relative path" language in R1.9 / R2.4 / R3.3 is a documentation-reference convention for human readers; templates are NOT resolved at runtime by the dispatched Claude subagent. At dispatch time, ralf-implement (the markdown skill) **inline-pastes the relevant template body** into the Agent-tool prompt argument, matching the existing `[PASTE ORIGINAL DOD]` convention in `implementer-prompt.md`. The exception is `foreign-cli-instructions.md`, which is read by the foreign-cycle Claude subagent from its repo-relative path (inside the worktree) and piped to `codex-companion.mjs task` / `gemini` on stdin. Considered: a `${SKILL_DIR}` resolution convention with a defined lookup mechanism (rejected: requires harness-level support not present today; deferred until tracer-bullet work in 7bk.19.5).

**D25 — Formula green-loop subagent_type plumbing (cycle-3 F4 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-3 review. R1.11 makes `subagent_type` a fail-fast input in non-interactive context. Formula green-loop steps run non-interactively. Without R4.2's update to both formula files' dispatch prose, every autonomous formula-driven ralf-implement run would trip the fail-fast contract. The canonical green-loop worker is `tdd-green-team`, so the formula prose hardcodes that as the passed argument. Future stage/worker variants would need their own formula-step prose.

**D26 — Cascade evidence-store canonicalization (cycle-3 F2 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-3 review. When the degradation cascade runs (Codex degraded → Gemini fallback for the same cycle index), both attempt artifacts are preserved under `.ralf/<session-id>/{agent_lower}-review-{timestamp}.md`. The cycle's foreign-eyes status string reports both attempts (machine-parseable format), and the fallback's output is the canonical findings input for the cycle. The failed attempt's artifact is forensic only — it does not contribute to the convergence decision.

**D27 — F5 ralf-implement owns audit-label conditional (cycle-3 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-3 review. The "Audit-label scope" policy lives 50 lines below §4 in `implement-bead/SKILL.md`; §4 step 5 stamps unconditionally. R1.5 places the conditional in ralf-implement: when ralf-implement dispatches a non-named-worker doer (e.g. `general-purpose`), it either instructs §4 to skip step 5 or stamps the label itself. The upstream §4 contradiction is filed as `agents-config-go1w`. Once `go1w` lands, ralf-implement's conditional becomes documentation-only for the named-worker case (since §4 would itself condition).

**D28 — F7 R5.2 dated-design-doc exclusion (cycle-3 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-3 review. R5.2's grep matched 4 lines in `docs/specs/2026-04-19-ralf-it-form-reevaluation-design.md` (the predecessor design doc for the ralf-* skills) — that file is HISTORICAL and predates this rename. Excluding `docs/specs/` wholesale would mis-exclude active specs (e.g. `worker-report-v1.md`). The R5.2 exclusion is narrowed to the dated-design-doc pattern `^docs/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-*-design.md`. R5.1 is unchanged (it already passes against the current tree).

**D29 — F10 `<agent-name>` identifier normalization (cycle-3 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-3 review. R1.5, D21, and the AC bullet now uniformly use `<agent-name>` (matching `worker-report-v1.md` §3 and `implement-bead/SKILL.md` §4) rather than `<agent>`. Trivial but prevents copy-paste drift.

**D30 — CRITICAL R4.2 fix-bug prose mismatch (cycle-4 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-4 review. The cycle-3 R4.2 quoted the implement-feature prose verbatim and prescribed it for BOTH formula files. But fix-bug.formula.toml's prose differs — it propagates `the root-cause note` as a leading argument (per the §6 upstream-report retrieval chain shipped in 19.3). Applying R4.2 literally to fix-bug would silently delete the root-cause-note plumbing. R4.2 is now split into two per-file prescriptive replacements that preserve the root-cause-note argument in the fix-bug form.

**D31 — R1.5 audit-label conditional is documentation-only until `go1w` (cycle-4 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-4 review. The cycle-3 spec said ralf-implement "either invokes §4 with a directive to skip step 5 OR stamps the label itself after §4 returns" — but §4 has no skip directive and post-return stamping cannot retract an already-applied label. Both options are unimplementable today without modifying §4 (which D3 forbids in this PR). Resolution: in 19.7's deliverable, ralf-implement DOCUMENTS the policy and names `go1w` as the upstream tracking item. `general-purpose` dispatches via §4 WILL receive `worker-audit-general-purpose-iter<N>` until `go1w` lands — accepting this is the explicit cost of not modifying §4 in this PR.

**D32 — Argument syntax canonicalization (cycle-4 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-4 review. The cycle-3 spec used `subagent_type:tdd-green-team` (colon-no-space) in R4.2 but `non_interactive: true` (colon-space-value) in R1.11. The orchestrator LLM is the parser — consistent shape prevents drift. R1.11 now canonicalizes on `<key>: <value>` (colon-space-value, YAML-style) for all caller invocation prose, and R4.2's prescriptive replacements use the canonical form.

**D33 — Inline-paste mechanism operational procedure (cycle-4 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-4 review. The cycle-3 D24 described the inline-paste OUTCOME ("ralf-implement inline-pastes the template body") but not the PROCEDURE. R1.9 now spells out the three operational steps: (1) `Read` the template by relative path; (2) substitute uppercase-bracket placeholders with caller-supplied inputs; (3) dispatch via `Agent({subagent_type, prompt: <substituted-body>})`. Templates are read fresh on each dispatch.

**D34 — `simplify` trigger N/A case (cycle-4 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-4 review. The cycle-3 D11 made the simplify trigger criterion measurable (re-run test command after simplify's edits). But the gate=n/a branch — docs/config repos where `evidence.tests.command` is empty or absent — had no defined behavior. Resolution: when no test command exists, accept simplify's output without a re-run and converge. The regression check is N/A; fresh-eyes review remains the convergence signal.

**D35 — Malformed evidence sub-blocks (cycle-4 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-4 review. R1.6's status-first gate (cycle-3 fix for F3) is sound, but `worker-report-v1.md` §4's existing synthesis rules only fire on (i) crash, (ii) unparseable YAML, (iii) missing `status`, (iv) missing per-agent runtime-required field. A `status: complete` report with corrupt `evidence` sub-blocks (e.g., `evidence.tests` missing required `command`) parses cleanly and routes to R1.6's convergence path. New R1.4.1 synthesizes `status: failed` on §1.1 schema violations within `evidence` sub-blocks, closing the tunnel under the status-first gate.

**D36 — Cascade applied to ralf-implement (cycle-4 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-4 review. The cycle-3 R2.2.1 added the degradation cascade (Codex→Gemini→pure-Claude) to ralf-REVIEW only. ralf-IMPLEMENT retained the older "degrade cleanly to pure fresh-eyes" line. Asymmetry was unintentional — the same "preserve a foreign reviewer whenever possible" rationale applies to both skills. R1.8 now adopts the same cascade for ralf-implement; evidence-store canonicalization applies symmetrically. The existing ralf-implement/SKILL.md "degrade cleanly to pure fresh-eyes" line is superseded.

**D37 — Grep run-from-where preamble (cycle-4 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-4 review. R5.1 / R5.2 path-anchored exclusions (`^docs/plans/`, etc.) are relative to grep's start directory. R5 now states explicitly: run from the repository root. Worktrees still work (they are their own root) but the exclusion patterns assume top-level path structure.

## Open Questions

None. All design decisions resolved during brainstorming sessions (2026-05-08 spec review cycles 1-2; 2026-05-12 scope expansion + Q2 resolution + Scheme A naming + cycle-1 through cycle-4 adversarial reviews).
acceptance_criteria:
- ralf-implement/SKILL.md updated to consume worker-report-v1 (evidence blocks + derived gate roll-up per worker-report-v1.md §1.1); dispatches inner doer via Agent tool with required subagent_type; references implement-bead/SKILL.md §4 per-dispatch primitive via informal section reference (not inlined); synthesizes status:failed on crash or malformed worker output per worker-report-v1.md §4.
- ralf-implement/SKILL.md interactive subagent_type prompt offers tdd-green-team (canonical) and general-purpose (Claude Code builtin fallback) only; tdd-red-team and bug-diagnoser are NOT offered because their gate shapes (fail / single-shot) do not match the convergence predicate. User declining both prompt options causes ralf-implement to abort with a diagnostic. Non-interactive context (caller-supplied non_interactive:true argument OR RALF_NONINTERACTIVE=1 env) with missing subagent_type fails fast with the same diagnostic — no prompt.
- ralf-implement/SKILL.md documents the worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>] policy: the label SHOULD apply only to named workers covered by implement-bead/SKILL.md "Audit-label scope" (tdd-red-team, tdd-green-team, bug-diagnoser); general-purpose and other non-worker doers SHOULD NOT carry worker-audit-*. The policy is documentation-only in 19.7's deliverable — §4 currently stamps unconditionally and ralf-implement does not retract labels post-§4-return; this is filed as upstream tracking item agents-config-go1w. Until go1w lands, general-purpose dispatches via §4 WILL receive worker-audit-general-purpose-iter<N> labels (acknowledged in spec).
- ralf-implement/SKILL.md explicit convergence predicate enumerates BOTH status and derived-gate signals: status=failed (real or synthesized crash/malformed) → NOT a convergence candidate, regardless of derived gate; status=complete + gate=pass → convergence candidate if fresh-eyes finds no blocking/critical; status=complete + gate=n/a → convergence candidate; fresh-eyes alone decides convergence; status=complete + gate=fail|partial → not a candidate, iterate. PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS at cycle cap with status=complete AND gate∈{pass,n/a} AND no blocking/critical; FAIL otherwise. Convergence-candidate definition: iteration where status=complete AND gate ∈ {pass, n/a}. R1.4.1 extends the synthesis path: malformed evidence sub-blocks (e.g. evidence.tests missing required command/exit_code/skipped per worker-report-v1.md §1.1) cause synthesized status=failed with escalations[].reason "Worker emitted malformed report" — closes the tunnel under the status-first gate.
- ralf-implement/SKILL.md per-cycle loop: quality-reviewer + simplify run ONLY on convergence candidates (status=complete AND gate ∈ {pass, n/a}), not on status=failed iterations or on gate∈{fail,partial} iterations. simplify advisory-only with measurable trigger: after simplify lands edits, re-run the worker-report's evidence.tests.command — any previously-passing test now failing causes simplify's output to be rejected and the loop restarts via a new worker dispatch; tests still passing causes simplify's output to be accepted and the iteration converges. N/A case: if evidence.tests.command is empty or absent (gate=n/a docs/config repo), accept simplify's output without re-run and converge — fresh-eyes review is the convergence signal in that branch. verify-checklist removed as a per-iteration step (test/build/lint/typecheck evidence derived from worker-report-v1 evidence blocks; quality-reviewer + simplify on convergence candidate only); default cycle cap stays at RALF_IMPLEMENT_DEFAULT_CYCLES=3; cycle sequence: 1=Codex(gpt-5.5), 2=Gemini, 3+=pure-Claude (matches existing skill semantics for extended cycles). Degradation cascade applies symmetrically with ralf-review (R2.2.1): Codex degraded → Gemini fallback for that cycle index → pure-Claude only if Gemini also degrades; evidence-store canonicalization applies (both attempt artifacts persist; fallback output is canonical).
- ralf-implement/SKILL.md iteration-routing section references each prompt template by concrete relative path AS DOCUMENTATION REFERENCES. At dispatch time ralf-implement performs the operational procedure: (1) Read the named template file by its relative path inside the installed skill directory; (2) substitute uppercase-bracket placeholders (e.g. [PASTE ORIGINAL DOD], [PASTE ORIGINAL SPEC], [PASTE CONTEXT]) with caller-supplied input strings; (3) dispatch via Agent({subagent_type: <doer>, prompt: <substituted-body>}). Templates are read fresh on each dispatch — no caching. Templates: ./subagent-implementer.md (initial-cycle dispatch body), ./subagent-fresh-eyes.md (pure-Claude cycles body), ./subagent-foreign-cycle.md (foreign-augmented cycles body), ./foreign-cli-instructions.md (instruction file read by the foreign-cycle Claude subagent from its repo-relative path inside the worktree and piped to codex-companion.mjs task / gemini on stdin — NOT inline-pasted into an Agent-tool prompt).
- ralf-implement/SKILL.md documents ralf:required label semantics as the canonical home.
- ralf-review/SKILL.md documents worker-report-v1 as N/A with rationale (review subagents are out-of-band per implement-bead audit-label scope; output is review findings, not gate evidence).
- ralf-review/SKILL.md adds foreign-eyes option (Q2 resolution: always-on, compressed): default cycles=2 with sequence cycle 1=Codex(gpt-5.5), cycle 2=pure-Claude; ralf:cycles=3 extends to Codex/Gemini/pure-Claude; cycles 4+ are pure-Claude; degradation status codes mirror ralf-implement (UNAVAILABLE / TIMED_OUT / QUOTA_EXCEEDED / AUTH_FAILED / NO_OUTPUT / UNUSABLE_OUTPUT).
- ralf-review/SKILL.md degradation cascade preserves foreign reviewer: on Codex degradation at any cycle, dispatch Gemini as fallback for that cycle index; only when Gemini also degrades (or is the planned cycle and degrades) fall back to pure-Claude. Cycle counts as consumed regardless. Recording a degradation status without running the cascade is an error. Evidence-store canonicalization: both attempt artifacts persist under .ralf/<session-id>/{agent_lower}-review-{timestamp}.md; cycle foreign-eyes status reports both attempts (machine-parseable format, e.g. cycle-N=Codex:<STATUS_CODE>,Gemini:COMPLETED); the fallback's output is the canonical findings input for that cycle.
- ralf-review/SKILL.md iteration-routing section references each prompt template by concrete relative path AS DOCUMENTATION REFERENCES (same inline-paste mechanism as ralf-implement): ./subagent-fresh-eyes.md, ./subagent-foreign-cycle.md, ./foreign-cli-instructions.md.
- ralf-review/subagent-foreign-cycle.md created paralleling (structural mirror, not source-derivation) ralf-implement/subagent-foreign-cycle.md (the POST-rename file from R3.1); adapted to review semantics — no fix-the-implementation steps; output is findings + scoring, not code changes.
- ralf-review/foreign-cli-instructions.md created paralleling (structural mirror, not source-derivation) ralf-implement/foreign-cli-instructions.md (the POST-rename file from R3.1); foreign model is asked to review the target artifact, not implementation state against a DoD.
- Implementation ordering: R3.1 renames complete first (ralf-implement/), then R3.2 rename (ralf-review/), then R2.3 creates new ralf-review/ files by structurally mirroring the post-rename ralf-implement/ files.
- ralf-implement/ prompt-template renames per Scheme A: implementer-prompt.md → subagent-implementer.md; fresh-eyes-prompt.md → subagent-fresh-eyes.md; foreign-eyes-prompt.md → subagent-foreign-cycle.md; foreign-agent-prompt.md → foreign-cli-instructions.md. Old filenames do NOT exist on disk after the rename (git mv or equivalent; not copy).
- ralf-review/ prompt-template rename per Scheme A: existing fresh-eyes-prompt.md → subagent-fresh-eyes.md. Old filename does NOT exist on disk after the rename.
- After rename, no active template, skill file, or formula file references the four old template filenames (implementer-prompt.md, fresh-eyes-prompt.md, foreign-eyes-prompt.md, foreign-agent-prompt.md). Intra-template references are updated (e.g. subagent-foreign-cycle.md's body references ./foreign-cli-instructions.md, not ./foreign-agent-prompt.md).
- implement-feature.formula.toml and fix-bug.formula.toml header pointer comment added: `# ralf:required label semantics: see src/user/.agents/skills/ralf-implement/SKILL.md`.
- Both formula files' green-loop dispatch prose updated PER-FILE to include `subagent_type: tdd-green-team` in the ralf-implement invocation argument list (canonical YAML-style `<key>: <value>` syntax per R1.11). implement-feature.formula.toml: "invoke ralf-implement with the bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, and optional max cycle count from ralf:cycles=N" becomes "invoke ralf-implement with the bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, `subagent_type: tdd-green-team`, and optional max cycle count from ralf:cycles=N". fix-bug.formula.toml: "invoke ralf-implement with the root-cause note, bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, and optional max cycle count from ralf:cycles=N" becomes "invoke ralf-implement with the root-cause note, bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, `subagent_type: tdd-green-team`, and optional max cycle count from ralf:cycles=N". The root-cause-note argument MUST be preserved in the fix-bug form (it is the §6 propagation chain from the upstream diagnose worker-report).
- bead-implementor.md no longer exists at src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md (regression check — delivered by PR #66).
- Repo-wide bead-implementor invariant grep returns zero matches (exact grep command in R5.1; verified against current tree as of 2026-05-12 before any 19.7 implementation work).
- Old-filename-reference grep returns zero matches with exclusion-list extended for the dated-design-doc pattern `^docs/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-*-design.md` (exact grep command in R5.2). Rationale: docs/specs/2026-04-19-ralf-it-form-reevaluation-design.md is HISTORICAL and predates the rename; active specs in docs/specs/ (e.g. worker-report-v1.md) are NOT excluded.
- Docs-only repo validation: end-of-work checks are filesystem rename invariants (new template files exist at Scheme A paths; old filenames absent on disk), SKILL.md and intra-template path-reference greps for new names, and the R5.1 / R5.2 invariant greps returning zero matches. This repo has no build/typecheck/test runner.
