notes:
# Spec: Remove content-destruction traps in bead formulas and skills

## Summary

Two sequential `bd update --notes=` calls in the `fix-bug` formula silently
destroy each other. Expand the scope of this bead to eliminate all content-
destruction traps across the project's beads formulas and related skills,
not just fix-bug.

## Background / motivation

Discovered while working on agents-config-smg (which was scoped to
implement-feature only).

`bd update --notes` REPLACES the bead's notes field. `bd update` has
supported `--append-notes` natively all along (appends with newline
separator). The fix-bug formula's two diagnose steps both use `--notes`,
so the second step silently destroys the first step's content.

Scott chose expanded scope (option B, session 11a30b53): audit all formulas
and skills for content-destruction traps and remove them.

### Audit results (live sites only — file-history matches ignored)

HARD TRAPS (mechanical destruction — sequential overwrites in a formula):
- `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml` line 54
  (reproduce step) and line 78 (root-cause step)

RISKY PATTERNS (single overwrite of pre-existing content; relies on agent
discipline to preserve context):
- `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` lines
  152-153 (write-spec step): `--acceptance=` and `--notes=` REPLACE any
  pre-existing content authored by create-bead or user investigation
- `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md` line 99:
  `--notes=` on capture clobbers if create-bead is run twice

Good existing guards (keep as-is):
- `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml` line
  63: already warns "DO NOT call bd update --acceptance or --notes"

### Investigation options originally listed in the bead (superseded)

The original bead notes listed four fix approaches (a)-(d). All four are
workarounds for a semantic bd already supports natively via
`--append-notes`. Use the native flag wherever sequential appends are
needed.

## Requirements

### R1. fix-bug formula — replace both `--notes` calls with `--append-notes`

In `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml`:
- Line 54 (reproduce step):
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --notes "Repro: <steps>\nActual: ...\nExpected: ..."`
  →
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "Repro: <steps>\nActual: ...\nExpected: ..."`
- Line 78 (root-cause step):
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --notes "Root cause: <explanation>"`
  →
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "Root cause: <explanation>"`

### R2. brainstorm-bead formula — add "preserve prior context" guard to write-spec only

In `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/brainstorm-bead.formula.toml`,
add a preamble to the `write-spec` step's `description = """..."""`
triple-quoted string, positioned IMMEDIATELY ABOVE the existing
`bd update {{bead-id}} --acceptance=...` line (currently line 152).

The guard text MUST include the exact sentinel string "REPLACES the entire
notes field" so it is greppable:

  Before writing: `--notes=` REPLACES the entire notes field, and
  `--acceptance=` REPLACES the entire acceptance-criteria field. If the
  bead currently contains prior investigation context (run `bd show <id>`
  once more if in doubt — prior context typically comes from `create-bead`
  or user-added notes before brainstorming), either (a) incorporate it
  into the spec you write below, or (b) move it to a comment via
  `bd comments add` before writing.

The `revise` step (currently lines 209-210) does NOT need this guard —
by the time revise runs, the write-spec step has already replaced any
pre-existing notes with the spec document, so there is no "prior
investigation context" left to protect. Revise legitimately overwrites
the freshly-written spec with the revised spec.

Do NOT change the overwrite mechanic of either step — the write-spec
step is the canonical spec author and replacement is intentional. Only
the guard preamble is added.

### R3. create-bead skill — switch capture to `--append-notes`, add rationale for --acceptance asymmetry

In `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md` line 99:
- `bd update <id> --notes="<any additional context>"`
  →
- `bd update <id> --append-notes="<any additional context>"`

Line 98's `--acceptance=` stays as-is, but add a single inline comment
line immediately above or adjacent to line 98 explaining why the
asymmetry exists, e.g.:
- `# Note: bd has no --append-acceptance; a repeat create will replace AC intentionally.`

This makes the asymmetry self-documenting for future readers who
otherwise might "fix" it inconsistently.

## Out of scope

- Changing `brainstorm-bead` write-spec or revise to use `--append-notes`.
  The write-spec step IS the canonical spec author; replacement is
  intentional. Only the guard preamble is added (R2).
- Adding append-parity flags (`--append-acceptance`, `--append-design`,
  `--append-description`) to `bd` itself — that is upstream beads-plugin
  work, not ours. File as discovered work.
- Changing `implement-feature.formula.toml` — its line 63 is a warning,
  not a trap. Leave it alone.
- Migrating brainstorm-bead's spec from the `notes` field to the
  `description` field (to match `rules/beads.md` which says "specs may be
  written directly into the bead description"). This inconsistency is
  real but orthogonal to the trap fix. File as discovered work.
- Auditing file-history / paste-cache / transcripts — those are frozen
  snapshots, not live instructions.
- Automated test coverage for formula/skill content. Verification is
  manual + grep-based (see verification block in AC).

## Acceptance criteria

- fix-bug formula's reproduce + root-cause steps use `--append-notes` (not `--notes`). Grep check: `grep -cE 'bd update .* --notes "' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml` returns 0.
- brainstorm-bead formula's write-spec step carries the greppable guard sentinel. Grep check: `grep -c "REPLACES the entire notes field" src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` returns >=1. The sentinel MUST appear inside the write-spec step's `description = """..."""` block, positioned above the `bd update {{bead-id}} --acceptance=...` line.
- brainstorm-bead's revise step is NOT modified (no guard added there; revise legitimately overwrites).
- create-bead SKILL.md uses `--append-notes` for notes capture on what was line 99 (or wherever it migrates). Grep check: `grep -cE 'bd update <id> --notes=' src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md` returns 0.
- create-bead SKILL.md contains a one-line inline comment near the `--acceptance=` invocation explaining the absence of `--append-acceptance`. Grep check: `grep -c "append-acceptance" src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md` returns >=1.
- Manual verification — reproducible command block:

  ```bash
  # 1. Create throwaway bead
  THROW=$(bd create --title="throwaway: hft repro verification" --type=bug --priority=4 --json | jq -r '.id')

  # 2. Simulate reproduce step
  bd update "$THROW" --append-notes "Repro: SENTINEL-REPRO"

  # 3. Simulate root-cause step
  bd update "$THROW" --append-notes "Root cause: SENTINEL-ROOT-CAUSE"

  # 4. Confirm BOTH sentinels survive
  HITS=$(bd show "$THROW" --json | jq -r '.[0].notes' | grep -cE "SENTINEL-REPRO|SENTINEL-ROOT-CAUSE")
  [ "$HITS" = "2" ] && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"

  # 5. Cleanup
  bd delete "$THROW" --reason "throwaway after hft verification"
  ```

  Expected: "PASS" printed.

- No other `bd update --notes=` / `--acceptance=` / `--design=` overwrite patterns remain live in `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/` or `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/`. Grep check: `grep -rnE 'bd update [^|]*--(notes|acceptance|design)=' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/ src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/` returns only the brainstorm-bead write-spec/revise sites and create-bead SKILL.md's `--acceptance=` line (the intentional ones), PLUS a false-positive in `implement-feature.formula.toml:63` which is warning text ("DO NOT call bd update {{bead-id}} --acceptance or --notes"), not an invocation.
- Discovered work filed (see list below).
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass. (N/A — this project has no build/typecheck/test harness for TOML/MD content; verification is manual + grep-based per above.)

## Design notes

- `bd update --append-notes` already exists and adds a newline separator —
  no shell gymnastics needed.
- `--acceptance`, `--description`, `--design` have NO `--append-*` parity
  today. Any fix for those would require upstream `bd` changes or shell
  read-modify-write wrappers. Out of scope.
- The brainstorm-bead `write-spec` guard is advisory (agent-discipline
  based), not mechanical. A mechanical fix would require restructuring
  the formula's spec-authoring step — out of scope.
- The brainstorm-bead `revise` step intentionally receives NO guard: by
  the time revise runs, write-spec has already replaced any pre-existing
  notes. The revise overwrite is by design and protects nothing beyond
  the freshly-written spec.
- `implement-feature.formula.toml:63` contains the strings `--acceptance`
  and `--notes` inside a WARNING sentence. Any future automated audit
  should match only `^\s*bd update` invocations, not the bare flag
  substrings, to avoid false-positives here. Non-blocking for this bead.

## Open questions

None remaining. Scope and approach confirmed by Scott in session 11a30b53
(2026-04-20).

## Implementation notes (CRITICAL — for the implementer)

⚠️ **META-BOOTSTRAP: DO NOT pour the `fix-bug` formula on this bead.**

This bead fixes a bug in the `fix-bug` formula itself. The broken behavior
is exactly the two `bd update --notes=` calls in reproduce + root-cause.
Pouring `fix-bug` on `agents-config-hft` would run those two steps against
this very bead and DESTROY the spec you are reading right now.

Use `start-bead` Route B (trivial / inline) with explicit subagent
dispatch for the completion gate (code-reviewer → code-simplifier →
verify-checklist) instead of pouring a formula. See the
`meta-bead-formula-bootstrapping` memory for precedent (originally
discovered on `agents-config-smg`).

`run-queue` should skip `agents-config-hft` and let a human drive
implementation via the inline route. If `run-queue` cannot be told to
skip, the operator should claim the bead manually and implement via
Route B before `run-queue` gets to it.

## Discovered work to file after implementation

1. Upstream `bd` should support `--append-acceptance`, `--append-design`,
   `--append-description` to give full append-parity with `--append-notes`.
   Non-blocking; nice-to-have.
2. `brainstorm-bead` formula writes the spec to the bead's `notes` field,
   but `rules/beads.md` says "specs may be written directly into the bead
   description". Resolve by either (a) migrating brainstorm-bead's
   write-spec step to use `--description` (via `--body-file`) and updating
   the finalize/downstream steps, or (b) updating `rules/beads.md` to
   reflect the actual `notes`-field convention.
Implementation progress (session 66272d4a, 2026-05-06):
- R2 applied: brainstorm-bead.formula.toml write-spec guard preamble added with sentinel 'REPLACES the entire notes field' (line 153, above --acceptance= at line 162)
- R3 applied: create-bead/SKILL.md line 101 switched to --append-notes; comment 'bd has no --append-acceptance' added at line 100
- AC grep checks: AC1=0, AC2=1, AC3 revise step untouched (lines 213-214), AC4=0, AC5=1, AC7 only intentional sites + create-bead --acceptance= remain
- Manual repro test (throwaway bead): PASS — both SENTINEL-REPRO and SENTINEL-ROOT-CAUSE survive sequential --append-notes
- Discovered work filed: agents-config-f298 (upstream --append-* parity), agents-config-2yyb (notes-vs-description inconsistency); both linked discovered-from agents-config-hft
- Branch: fix/agents-config-hft-notes-overwrite (worktree: .claude/worktrees/fix-hft-notes-overwrite)
- Quality gate in flight: quality-reviewer agent dispatched

title: implement-feature and fix-bug formulas contain redundant brainstorm step; should be gated on brainstormed label or replaced
acceptance_criteria: AC1: implement-feature.formula.toml has no [[steps]] block with id='brainstorm'. AC2: implement-feature.formula.toml has [[steps]] block with id='confirm' as first step, no needs field. AC3: worktree step needs=['confirm']. AC4: formula description (line 25) and Phase 1 header reflect 'confirm' (no 'brainstorm' in headers/comments). AC5: Step text forbids invoking superpowers:brainstorming. AC6: Step text describes happy-path: read bead, restate plan in 1-3 sentences, proceed. AC7: Step text describes autonomous-mode missing-label path: bd human {{bead-id}}, no prompt, leave step open / abort molecule. AC8: Step text describes interactive-mode missing-label path: warn, ask user (a) abort or (b) proceed; honor choice. AC9: Step text instructs agent to detect autonomous vs interactive mode from context. AC10: fix-bug.formula.toml has no semantic changes (incidental whitespace permitted only if introduced by tooling). AC11: implement-bead/SKILL.md label preflight unchanged AND no SKILL.md prose references removed brainstorm step. AC12: confirm step description includes exact abort-mechanism guidance (leave step open, print operator follow-up, do not bd mol burn from within step).
notes:
# Spec — replace redundant `brainstorm` step in `implement-feature.formula.toml` (rev 2)

## Summary
Replace the `brainstorm` step (lines 43-66) in `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml` with a new `confirm` step that validates the bead is brainstormed, restates the plan, and never invokes `superpowers:brainstorming`. The `confirm` step has explicit branches for autonomous-mode (run-queue) vs interactive-mode (start-bead / direct human pour). No changes to `fix-bug.formula.toml`.

## Background / motivation
The pipeline is `brainstorm-bead` → produces AC + labels (`brainstormed`, `implementation-ready`) → `run-queue` pours `implement-feature` → FIRST step of the poured molecule is ANOTHER `superpowers:brainstorming` invocation. This is redundant with brainstorm-bead's work and violates the `rules/beads.md` Skill Partnership rule ("the bead description IS the plan"). It can also overwrite AC that brainstorm-bead already captured.

Evidence:
- `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml` step `brainstorm` (line 44) invokes `superpowers:brainstorming` unconditionally.
- Runtime confirmation: `mol-uaq.children` included `"Brainstorm: requirements for Split ralf-it into ralf-review + ralf-implement…"` despite `lu3.4` carrying `brainstormed` + `implementation-ready` labels.

## Scope correction (from original bead)
Original bead alleged `fix-bug.formula.toml` had the same flaw. Assessment showed `fix-bug`'s first steps are `reproduce` + `root-cause` invoking `superpowers:systematic-debugging` / `superpowers:root-cause-tracing` — bug-specific diagnosis, NOT generic brainstorming. They are not redundant in the same way. `fix-bug.formula.toml` is **out of scope** for this bead. (A SEPARATE concern in fix-bug — the `--notes` overwrite between reproduce and root-cause steps — is captured in `agents-config-hft`.)

## Requirements

### R1. Step replacement
- Remove the `brainstorm` step from `implement-feature.formula.toml`.
- Add a `confirm` step as the first step (no `needs` field).
- Update the `worktree` step's `needs = ["brainstorm"]` to `needs = ["confirm"]`.
- Update the formula's header comment (lines 11-17 phase list) and the `description` field (line 25) to reflect `confirm` instead of `brainstorm`. Update the `# Phase 1: Intent` banner to match the step's new role (e.g. "Phase 1: Intent — confirm bead is brainstormed and restate plan").

### R2. `confirm` step contract — happy path
When `bd label list {{bead-id}}` shows BOTH `brainstormed` AND `implementation-ready`:
1. `bd show {{bead-id}}` — read description and AC.
2. Restate the implementation plan in 1-3 sentences.
3. Mark the step done. The next step (worktree) runs.
4. Never invoke `superpowers:brainstorming`.

### R3. `confirm` step contract — missing-label path (TWO mechanisms)
When EITHER label is missing, the response depends on execution mode:

- **Autonomous mode** (run-queue, or any context with no human at the keyboard):
  - Escalate the bead: `bd human {{bead-id}}` with a clear note explaining the missing labels and recommending `start-bead` / `brainstorm-bead`.
  - Do NOT prompt — there is no operator to answer.
  - Abort the molecule: leave the `confirm` step open / not closed, so the molecule does not advance.
  
- **Interactive mode** (start-bead routing, or a human running `bd mol pour` directly):
  - Print a clear stdout/chat warning describing the missing labels.
  - Ask the user via natural conversation:
    - (a) Abort and route the bead through `start-bead` / brainstorm-bead first, OR
    - (b) Proceed with implementation using whatever is in the bead description.
  - On (a): mark step still-open / abort molecule.
  - On (b): proceed to worktree.

Abort mechanism (both paths): leave the `confirm` step `open` (do not close it). Print a one-line follow-up: `Molecule paused at confirm. Run brainstorm-bead on this bead and re-attempt, or 'bd mol burn <mol-id>' to discard.` Do NOT call `bd mol burn` from within the step — leave the disposition to the operator (or to run-queue policy).

Mode detection: the agent driving the molecule infers mode from its own context (was it dispatched by `run-queue`? Is it in interactive conversation with the user?). The formula step text instructs the agent to make this judgment explicitly. No formula-level interactive primitive is required — the formula step is text guidance for the agent.

### R4. No state mutation before confirm proceeds
`confirm` MUST run before any state mutation in the molecule. Specifically, the parent-chain claim walk (currently in `worktree` step around lines 87-94) is downstream of `confirm`, so this is satisfied by step ordering — no additional code needed, but the AC verifies it.

### R5. Belt-and-suspenders
The `implement-bead` skill's existing preflight label-check (`SKILL.md` L19-46) is **kept unchanged**. It guards the `implement-bead` invocation path. The new formula `confirm` step guards a DIFFERENT, narrower leak path: a human (or script) running `bd mol pour implement-feature --var bead-id=<id>` DIRECTLY, bypassing `implement-bead`. Both gates retained because they cover different paths.

## Out of scope
- `fix-bug.formula.toml` — no changes.
- Making `brainstorm-bead` type-aware for bug beads (potential follow-up).
- The `--notes` overwrite concern in fix-bug — tracked separately as `agents-config-hft`.
- Any formula-level interactive prompt primitive — explicitly chose to use agent-driven mode detection instead.

## Acceptance Criteria

**Structural (file-level):**
- AC1: `implement-feature.formula.toml` no longer contains a `[[steps]]` block with `id = "brainstorm"`.
- AC2: `implement-feature.formula.toml` contains a `[[steps]]` block with `id = "confirm"` as the first `[[steps]]` block; this block has no `needs` field.
- AC3: The `[[steps]]` block with `id = "worktree"` in `implement-feature.formula.toml` has `needs = ["confirm"]`.
- AC4: The formula's `description` field (line 25) and Phase 1 header comment are updated to reflect `confirm` (no remaining mention of "brainstorm" as the first step in headers / comments).

**Behavioral (runtime contract — described in the `confirm` step text):**
- AC5: Step text explicitly forbids invoking `superpowers:brainstorming`.
- AC6: Step text describes the happy-path (both labels present): read bead, restate plan in 1-3 sentences, proceed.
- AC7: Step text describes autonomous-mode missing-label path: call `bd human {{bead-id}}`, do not prompt, leave step open / abort molecule.
- AC8: Step text describes interactive-mode missing-label path: print warning, ask user (a) abort or (b) proceed; honor the choice.
- AC9: Step text instructs the agent to detect autonomous vs interactive mode from its own context (run-queue dispatch ⇒ autonomous; user-present ⇒ interactive).

**Negative space:**
- AC10: `fix-bug.formula.toml` has no semantic changes (incidental whitespace permitted only if introduced by tooling).
- AC11: `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md` label preflight (lines 19-46 area) is functionally unchanged AND no SKILL.md prose elsewhere references the removed `brainstorm` step (cross-check during implementation).
- AC12: `confirm` step description includes the exact abort-mechanism guidance (leave step `open`; print operator follow-up; do not call `bd mol burn` from within the step).

## Design notes
- **Two-path missing-label handling** addresses C1 from the adversarial review: a single "interactively ask" mechanism would deadlock run-queue. Splitting by mode keeps autonomous flow safe (escalate via `bd human`) while preserving the interactive escape hatch the user requested.
- **Behavioral AC alongside structural AC** addresses C2: structural AC alone would let a future implementer pass the spec by inserting the right strings into the description without honoring the contract at runtime. Step text describing each branch is the closest the formula gets to a runtime check (the agent driving the step is the executor).
- **Belt-and-suspenders justified by leak path** addresses N3: the skill preflight covers `implement-bead` invocations. The formula confirm covers direct `bd mol pour` invocations (operator console use, scripts, imports). Two distinct entry points, two gates.
- **Docs drift covered by AC4** addresses N2.
- **Mode detection via agent judgment** is the simplest design — no new formula primitive. Risk: an agent misreading the mode. Mitigation: the step text gives explicit cues ("dispatched by run-queue ⇒ autonomous").
- **start-bead Route B / no conflict** (M4): start-bead routes a NOT-ready bead to brainstorm-bead BEFORE implement-feature is ever poured. The new confirm step lives INSIDE implement-feature, which runs only AFTER routing. No overlap.

## Open questions
- C1 (mechanism for missing-label response) — **resolved** by R3 / two-path handling.
- C2 (behavioral vs grep-bait AC) — **resolved** by adding behavioral AC5–AC9.
- n3 (abort mechanism) — **resolved** by R3 abort-mechanism paragraph and AC12.
- None remaining.

---

## Original bead context (preserved)
Context: the bead pipeline is brainstorm-bead → produces AC + labels (brainstormed, implementation-ready) → run-queue pours implement-feature → FIRST step of the poured molecule is ANOTHER brainstorm. The second brainstorm is redundant with brainstorm-bead's work and violates the beads.md Skill Partnership rule ('the bead description IS the plan').

Evidence:
- src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml Phase 1 Intent step 'brainstorm' (lines 43-66 approx) unconditionally invokes superpowers:brainstorming.
- Confirmed at runtime: mol-uaq.children includes 'Brainstorm: requirements for Split ralf-it into ralf-review + ralf-implement…' despite lu3.4 carrying the brainstormed + implementation-ready labels.
- fix-bug.formula.toml was originally claimed to have the same flaw — reassessment showed this is false; fix-bug has bug-specific diagnosis steps, not redundant generic brainstorming. (Separate fix-bug concern about --notes overwriting tracked in agents-config-hft.)

Impact: double-brainstorming wastes cycles, can produce conflicting AC (the second brainstorm may overwrite the first), and undermines the whole brainstorm-bead → run-queue pipeline.
close_reason: Replaced redundant brainstorm step with confirm step in implement-feature.formula.toml. PR #16 merged (squash). Two-path handling (autonomous bd human + abort vs interactive warn+ask). Three Copilot mechanical fixes applied inline (string wrapping).

title: bd mol pour does not set parent on created molecule to target bead
notes:
Context: after run-queue poured implement-feature on lu3.4, the resulting molecule mol-uaq has parent=None, not lu3.4. Same issue on mol-gs8 (prior pour for Divergence 4 bead). No structural link exists from a molecule back to the bead it was poured for.

Repro:
  bd mol pour implement-feature --var bead-id=<bead-id> --var feature='<title>'
  bd show <new-mol-id> --json | python3 -c 'import sys,json; r=json.load(sys.stdin); r=r[0] if isinstance(r,list) else r; print(r.get("parent"))'
  # → None

Expected: new molecule's parent field points at the target bead, OR some other structural edge (dependency type, metadata field) encodes the 'molecule-for-bead' relationship so that 'find molecules for bead X' is answerable via bd list.

Impact: the 'does a molecule already exist for this bead?' check in start-bead Step 2 and run-queue orchestration is STRUCTURALLY impossible. The check always returns empty even when a live molecule exists. Combined with the text-output bug (filed separately), run-queue will re-pour molecules instead of resuming existing ones.

Possible fixes: (a) set parent = <bead-id> when pouring with --var bead-id=<id>; (b) add a 'molecule-for-bead' metadata/edge; (c) encode as a dependency.
close_reason: Worked around consumer-side in PR #15: stamp for-bead-<id> label on molecules at pour/wisp; probe with bd list --label ... --type molecule --json. Upstream beads bugs remain.

title: bd list text output ignores --parent and --type filters (JSON correct)
notes:
Context: discovered while run-queue checked for existing molecules on lu3.4.

Repro (run from this project):
  bd list --parent agents-config-lu3.4                       # returns lu3.4 ITSELF in text
  bd list --parent agents-config-lu3.4 --type mol            # returns lu3.4 (type=task) in text — both filters ignored
  bd list --parent agents-config-lu3.4 --json                # returns []
  bd list --parent agents-config-lu3.4 --type mol --json     # returns []

Actual: text output ignores --parent and --type filters; displays the referenced bead (or possibly the session's last-touched issue).
Expected: text output applies filters identically to JSON output.

Impact: any agent/skill/script relying on the text output is misled. In particular:
- start-bead Step 2 ('Check for existing molecule') uses 'bd list --parent <bead-id> --type mol 2>/dev/null' in text mode.
- run-queue uses the same pattern.
Both therefore ALWAYS find the bead itself in text and conclude 'a molecule exists' OR ignore that result — either way the check is unreliable.

Workaround until fixed: consumers must use --json and parse accordingly.
close_reason: Worked around consumer-side in PR #15: stamp for-bead-<id> label on molecules at pour/wisp; probe with bd list --label ... --type molecule --json. Upstream beads bugs remain.

title: Divergence 1: wisp formulas not materializing step beads (start-bead + formulas)
description:
# Root cause

`wisp.go:249-251` in the upstream `bd` binary forces root-only materialization unless the formula declares `pour = true`:

```go
if !rootOnly && subgraph != nil && !subgraph.Pour {
    rootOnly = true
}
```

The `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` has `phase = "vapor"` but no `pour = true`. When wisped, only a root issue is created — no child step beads are materialized. `bd mol current` returns "0/0 steps", the agent has no step IDs to operate on, and the CLI's follow-up output (`bd close <root>.<step>`) misleadingly suggests steps exist.

The `pour` field is misleadingly named: it means "materialize children," not "use pour command." Formulas need `pour = true` for `bd mol wisp` to produce actionable step beads. `bd mol pour` materializes children regardless.

Secondary cause: `start-bead` Route C says "wisp the formula, then execute as MAIN AGENT" with no execution loop. Even with a materialized molecule, the agent has no contract for how to drive it.

# Edits required

## Formulas (src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/)

Add `pour = true` to all four formulas:

- `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` (vapor — only ever wisped, bug fires)
- `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml` (vapor — only ever wisped, bug fires)
- `implement-feature.formula.toml` (liquid — defensive)
- `fix-bug.formula.toml` (liquid — defensive)

Update `# Usage:` comments in `brainstorm-bead` and `merge-and-cleanup` to add next-step navigation:

    # bd mol current <wisp-id>   # shows steps with status

## Skill (src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md)

Replace Route C action block with an explicit execution loop:

    Action: wisp the brainstorm-bead formula:
      bd mol wisp create brainstorm-bead --var bead-id=<id>

    Then drive the molecule as the MAIN AGENT (brainstorming requires
    interactive conversation — do NOT dispatch a subagent):

      1. bd mol current <wisp-id>           # shows all steps with markers
      2. For the first [ready] step:
         - bd update <step-id> --claim       # lock as in_progress
         - bd show <step-id>                  # read step instructions
         - Execute the step (interactive for discuss)
         - bd close <step-id> --continue      # closes + auto-claims next
      3. Repeat until all steps are [done]

    Use step IDs from `bd mol current` output — do NOT synthesize
    `<root>.<step>` patterns. Step IDs are independent hash-based.

Add a failure-mode block under Route C:

    ### Failure mode: 0/0 steps after wisp creation

    If `bd mol current <wisp-id>` shows "0/0 steps complete" with no
    step list, the formula is missing `pour = true`.
      bd mol burn <wisp-id>
    Tell the user it is a formula bug. Do NOT fall back to invoking
    the brainstorming skill inline.

    For other mid-run abandonment (partial progress made), prefer
      bd mol squash <wisp-id> --summary "Aborted: <reason>"
    to preserve history for debugging.

Add two Red Flags rows:

    | "The molecule looks empty, I'll just do the work inline" | STOP. 0/0 = formula bug. Burn + report. |
    | "I'll make up step IDs — they look like <root>.<step>"  | No. Use IDs from bd mol current output. |

# Design decisions

- **Defensive `pour = true` on all four formulas** (not just vapor ones): zero downside, protects against future wisp misuse of liquid formulas.
- **`bd close --continue` in the loop**: cleanest auto-advance; saves one `bd mol current` query per step.
- **Burn vs squash recovery**: burn only for the 0/0 formula-bug case (nothing to preserve); squash for any partial-progress abandonment.
- **No new "execute-molecule" skill**: 5-line loop fits inline in Route C. `implement-bead` has a different pattern (dispatches subagents between steps) so cannot reuse the same verbatim loop.
- **Keep `bd mol wisp create`** as the command in Route C text: `create` is a valid subcommand (back-compat per `wisp.go:813`). Changing to bare `bd mol wisp` is churn with no behavior delta.

# Acceptance criteria

- All four formulas declare `pour = true`
- `brainstorm-bead` and `merge-and-cleanup` formula header comments include `bd mol current <wisp-id>` guidance
- `start-bead` Route C has explicit execution loop with `bd update --claim` / `bd show` / execute / `bd close --continue`
- `start-bead` has a failure-mode block for the 0/0-steps case with burn-vs-squash guidance
- `start-bead` Red Flags table includes the two new entries
- Manual verification: wisp `brainstorm-bead` against a test bead, confirm child step beads are materialized, confirm `bd mol current` surfaces step IDs
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

# Notes

- Discovered during audit of discord-agent-scaffold session `64012e00`
- Related upstream `bd` CLI bug (misleading `bd close <root>.<step>` output at `wisp.go:303` when wisp is root-only) — tracked separately in the beads project issue tracker
- Expert consultation confirmed root cause via `wisp.go:249-251`, `pour.go:216`, and `mol_current.go:191-238` source evidence
close_reason: pour=true on all 4 formulas + start-bead Route C execution loop + failure mode + Red Flags

title: Harden bead-driven workflow boundaries (audit fixes + follow-on strategy)
description:
# Problem statement

AI agents working bead-tracked tasks silently bypass the formula/molecule protocol because of three compounding issues identified in the audit of `discord-agent-scaffold` session `64012e00`:

1. **Broken formula mechanism** — wisps were root-only by default; step beads never materialized; agents saw "0/0 steps" and abandoned the molecule.
2. **Skill-paradigm conflict** — beads (lifecycle) and superpowers (methodology) overlap on planning/execution. External superpowers skills pulled agents off the bead path via their auto-match triggers.
3. **Asymmetric authorization language** — rules emphasize "merge requires authorization" without parallel "PR creation is AUTOMATIC" language; agents over-generalize caution and stop mid-workflow.

Ralf-it also over-specifies outer-workflow concerns (worktree, delivery) that conflict with bead formulas, which emerged as a separate architectural question worth re-evaluating.

Strategy questions: after overlay rules ship, do residual risks warrant further closers? Is overlay-only sufficient or is a superpowers fork eventually needed?

# Context

- **Audit target**: discord-agent-scaffold session `64012e00-6f05-409f-95b2-243ae02e1f64`
- **Analysis session**: `c06c7b55` (April 2026)
- **Expert confirmation**: beads expert traced root causes to `wisp.go:249-251`, `pour.go:216`, `mol_current.go:191-238`
- **Upstream dep**: no changes needed in upstream `bd` binary for Phase 1; CLI output bug at `wisp.go:303` filed separately in the beads project issue tracker

# Initiative phases

## Phase 1 — Fix audit findings (P0/P1)

- **`lu3.1`** (P0) — Divergence 1: wisp formulas not materializing step beads. Add `pour = true` to four formulas; add execution loop to `start-bead` Route C; add failure-mode block for 0/0 state.
- **`lu3.2`** (P1) — Divergence 2: beads ↔ superpowers partnership contract. Rule section listing inner tools vs off-limits skills; formula step citations of methodology skills; red flags in start-bead and implement-bead. Scope also includes `implement-feature` delivery step citation (moved from lu3.3).
- **`lu3.3`** (P1) — Divergence 3: clarify delivery authorization boundaries. Action categorization (AUTOMATIC vs AUTHORIZATION-REQUIRED); "ready when you are" red flag; sharpen completion-gate HARD STOP; new beads overlay file for bead-aware delivery guidance.
- **`lu3.4`** (P1) — Re-evaluate ralf-it form (skill vs command vs agent). Design bead — needs brainstorming to decide form. Emerged from Divergence 3 analysis as a cleaner fix than patching ralf-it's over-specified workflow declarations in place.

## Phase 1.5 — Evaluate risk-closers (P3)

- **`717`** (P3) — After Phase 1 ships and is observed for drift rate, brainstorm and decide whether to implement: hook-level enforcement, user-direction guardrail, session-audit cadence, broader failure-mode block. Each option gets a `bd decision` record. Blocks Phase 2.

## Phase 2 — Fork superpowers if needed (P3)

- **`cx6`** (P3) — If overlay rules + risk-closers still leave drift, fork superpowers into a curated bead-aware plugin. Eliminates the three competing skills entirely; keeps the methodology keepers. Candidate names: `superbeadpowers`, `superbeads`, `beadpowers`. Blocked by `717` — cannot proceed until risk-closer evaluation completes.

# Scope

**In scope:**
- Formula TOML and skill text changes in `src/plugins/beads/`
- Core rule updates in `src/user/.claude/rules/`
- New beads overlay files in `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/` (appends via install mechanism)
- Ralf-it form re-evaluation and follow-on restructuring
- Strategic evaluation of risk-closers and fork option

**Out of scope:**
- Upstream `bd` CLI bugs (tracked in the beads project issue tracker)
- Non-bead workflow changes (superpowers usage outside bead context)
- Changes to the external superpowers plugin itself (unless Phase 2 fork is authorized)

# Acceptance criteria

The epic is complete when ALL of the following hold:

1. **Phase 1 closed**: `lu3.1`, `lu3.2`, `lu3.3`, `lu3.4` are all closed with their own acceptance criteria met.
2. **Observation**: the partnership contract has been observed in at least 2 subsequent session audits; drift rate documented.
3. **Phase 1.5 resolved**: `717` is closed — each risk-closer option has a `bd decision` (accepted/deferred/rejected with rationale). Accepted options are implemented as sub-beads.
4. **Phase 2 resolved**: `cx6` is either closed as "implemented" OR closed as "not needed — overlay + risk-closers sufficient" with rationale.
5. **End-to-end behavior**: an agent running `start-bead` on a fresh feature bead can execute the full `brainstorm → implement → deliver` pipeline without silently falling back to direct skill invocations or manual user handoffs.
6. **Quality gate**: Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

# Notes

- Phase numbering mirrors the time horizon: Phase 1 is immediate, Phase 1.5 is post-observation, Phase 2 is conditional escalation.
- The epic description was rewritten in session `c06c7b55` to cover all three phases under a single umbrella (was previously scoped to audit fixes only).
close_reason: Phase 1 children all closed; Phase 1.5/2 tracked separately under 717 and cx6

title: install.sh --dry-run fails: resolve-human-bead name collision between base and plugin
description: scripts/install.sh --dry-run exits 1 with 'Fatal collision: directory resolve-human-bead exists in both base and plugin content (or two plugins). Skill and agent directories must use unique names.' Confirmed pre-existing on main (not introduced by agents-config-acmh.14 PR). Both main tree and acmh.14 worktree show identical failure. title: prgroom — implementation (umbrella epic for all [Impl] beads)
description:
# prgroom — implementation umbrella

Sibling epic to agents-config-fca6 (brainstorming umbrella). This epic groups all [Impl] beads produced from prgroom brainstorm closures, so the implementation queue is visible at a glance regardless of which design section's brainstorm spawned each impl.

## Structure

- Source-of-truth design doc: `docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md`
- Brainstorming umbrella: agents-config-fca6 (sibling)
- This epic: the destination for every [Impl] bead spawned by closures under fca6

## Initial children (at creation)

- [Impl] prgroom CLI foundation — scaffold + core infrastructure (produced from the foundation brainstorm closure)
- agents-config-fca6.6 — Pre-implementation Go toolchain assessment (chore; moved from fca6 since it is an impl prerequisite, not a brainstorm artifact)

## Future children

Each remaining sub-design (fca6.1–fca6.5) will, when brainstormed and finalized, spawn its own [Impl] bead which lands here.
acceptance_criteria: All [Impl] beads for prgroom complete. Build passes. Tests pass.

title: prgroom — foundation brainstorm closure (Sections 1, 2, 5, 8-skeleton)
description:
# prgroom — foundation brainstorm closure

Memorializes the brainstorming work performed in a prior session for the prgroom CLI foundation (Sections 1, 2, 5, and 8-skeleton of the design document). This bead represents the brainstorm-closure artifact; the implementation work is tracked by the [Impl] bead under the prgroom-IMPL epic.

## Source of truth

- Design document: `docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md`
- Review: 4 rounds of crit, 41 comments addressed
- Source bead: agents-config-fca6 (epic, brainstorming umbrella)

## Sections covered by this closure (drafted/locked)

- Section 1 — Architecture overview
- Section 2 — WorkTracker interface + state schema
- Section 5 — Agent dispatch internals (Cluster + Fix contracts; EscalationSink; config; templates; logging)
- Section 8 — PR memory management (skeleton; sub-design carved out at agents-config-fca6.5, hooks in Contract B)
- Cross-cutting: precondition gating (3-tier model)

## Locked decisions (MVP)

- Language: Go (single static binary, github.com/cli/go-gh/v2, sub-100ms cold start)
- CLI framework: cobra
- Repo placement: same agents-config repo, new cmd/prgroom/ + internal/ tree, Go module rooted at repo root
- Agent boundary: CLI shells out to agent-CLIs synchronously; fresh agent context per shell-out
- Command shape: subcommand verbs (poll, cluster, fix, push, rereview, reply, resolve, resolve-escalated, wait, status, run, sweep)
- MVP scope: PR-grooming only (excludes create-PR, merge, cleanup, bead-lifecycle helpers)
- Migration path: incremental — Phase 1 absorbs wait-for-pr-comments; Phase 2 absorbs reply-and-resolve-pr-threads
- State persistence: WorkTracker interface; MVP default file adapter; memory adapter for tests; bd adapter deferred to v2
- Agent contracts: Contract A = cluster (local-first via ollama+Gemma 4 → claude haiku → codex-mini), Contract B = fix (sonnet[1m] orchestrator that decides disposition AND implements)
- Escalation routing: EscalationSink interface (stderr default; bd + file adapters available)
- Per-contract config: primary + fallback chains in TOML; token-usage logged to JSONL
- Test discipline: interfaces-first; fit-tests per module; coverage gate 80% line / 70% branch on changed code

## Not in this closure

Sections 3, 4, 6, 7, and full Section 8 remain still-sketched. Each is a separate brainstorm-candidate sub-design bead under fca6:
- fca6.1 — Section 3 (Lifecycle state machine)
- fca6.2 — Section 4 (Quiescence model)
- fca6.3 — Section 6 (Migration plan)
- fca6.4 — Section 7 (Build, distribution, test discipline)
- fca6.5 — Section 8 (PR memory management, full)

## Acceptance

This closure bead is closed once its [Impl] bead exists under prgroom-IMPL with the correct formula labels (`produced-from-agents-config-<this-id>`, `implementation-ready`, `implementation-readied-session-<sid>`).
acceptance_criteria: Closure bead created and closed; [Impl] bead exists with produced-from-* label linking back; brainstorm-formula contract restored.
close_reason: Brainstorm closure complete. Spawned [Impl] bead agents-config-abn9.8.1 under the prgroom-IMPL epic, with produced-from-agents-config-fca6.8 + implementation-ready + implementation-readied-session-a82c6b20. Brainstorm work memorialized; design doc at docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md remains authoritative.

title: prgroom — PR-grooming CLI (replace wait-for-pr-comments + reply-and-resolve-pr-threads)
description:
# prgroom — PR-grooming CLI

Replace the `wait-for-pr-comments` and `reply-and-resolve-pr-threads` skills with a Go CLI (`prgroom`) that takes over phase orchestration, reducing agentic-token cost by an order of magnitude.

## Source of truth

Design document: `docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md` (reviewed via 4 rounds of crit; 41 comments addressed).

## Locked decisions (MVP)

- **Language:** Go (single static binary, `github.com/cli/go-gh/v2`, sub-100ms cold start)
- **CLI framework:** `cobra`
- **Repo placement:** same `agents-config` repo, new `cmd/prgroom/` + `internal/` tree, Go module rooted at repo root
- **Agent boundary:** CLI shells out to agent-CLIs (`claude -p` / `codex exec`) synchronously; fresh agent context per shell-out
- **Command shape:** subcommand verbs (poll, cluster, fix, push, rereview, reply, resolve, resolve-escalated, wait, status, run, sweep)
- **MVP scope:** PR-grooming only (excludes create-PR, merge, cleanup, bead-lifecycle helpers)
- **Migration path:** incremental — Phase 1 absorbs `wait-for-pr-comments`; Phase 2 absorbs `reply-and-resolve-pr-threads`
- **State persistence:** `WorkTracker` interface; MVP default `file` adapter (`$XDG_STATE_HOME/prgroom/<owner>-<repo>-<n>.json`); `memory` adapter for tests; `bd` adapter deferred to v2
- **Agent contracts (Section 5):** Contract A = `cluster` (cheap grouping; local-first via ollama+Gemma 4 → claude haiku → codex-mini), Contract B = `fix` (sonnet[1m] orchestrator that decides disposition AND implements)
- **Escalation routing:** `EscalationSink` interface (stderr default; bd + file adapters available)
- **Per-contract config:** primary + fallback chains in TOML; token-usage logged to JSONL for baseline (no analysis yet)
- **Test discipline:** interfaces-first; fit-tests per module; coverage gate 80% line / 70% branch on changed code

## Sections drafted vs sketched

**Drafted (in design doc):**
- Section 1 — Architecture overview
- Section 2 — `WorkTracker` interface + state schema
- Section 5 — Agent dispatch internals (Cluster + Fix contracts; EscalationSink; config; templates; logging)
- Section 8 — PR memory management (skeleton; sub-design carved out, hooks in Contract B)
- Cross-cutting: precondition gating (3-tier model)

**Still sketched (need brainstorm before implementation):**
- Section 3 — Lifecycle state machine (phase transitions, run verb's loop, error-code registry, failure handling)
- Section 4 — Quiescence model (probability function, thresholds, wait verb semantics, human-review signal, hard cap, auto-merge eligibility check)
- Section 6 — Migration plan (Phase 1 + Phase 2 detail, rollback strategy, coexistence)
- Section 7 — Build, distribution, test discipline (build pipeline, install path, versioning; test pyramid partially fleshed)

Each still-sketched section is a child sub-design bead.

## Supersedes

- `agents-config-d73c` — original *Optimize wait-for-pr-comments and reply-and-resolve-pr-threads skills* task

## Downstream / affected

- `agents-config-gmxo` (Redesign merge-gate bead) may depend on this MVP to define its boundary cleanly; user noted this dep direction in brainstorm
- `agents-config-td39` (Risk-tiered auto-merge policy) is further downstream and depends on gmxo

## Out of scope (deferred to v2+)

- Create-PR, merge, worktree cleanup (stay in `finishing-a-development-branch` / `merge-and-cleanup`)
- bd adapter for `WorkTracker`
- Parallel fix subagents
- `detect-pr-push.sh` PostToolUse hook rework
- Brainstorm/implement-bead formula changes
- Executable-bead primitive
- gmxo's structural changes
- Auto-detection of in-flight PR migration (none; in-flight PRs finish on the legacy skill)
- Cross-PR memory layer
- Token-usage analysis/aggregation (we log; we don't analyze yet)
acceptance_criteria: Sub-design beads for Sections 3, 4, 6, 7, 8 all closed. Toolchain assessment chore closed. CLI foundation (scaffold + tracker + preconditions + Cluster + Fix contracts) implemented and tested. wait-for-pr-comments skill thinned to a CLI invocation. reply-and-resolve-pr-threads skill thinned to a CLI invocation. d73c closed as superseded. Build passes. Tests pass. Coverage gate (80% line / 70% branch) holds on changed code.

title: Memory/context compaction governance for overnight runs
description: Vision-Gap-3 from the 2026-05-09 roadmap. Beads persist work; formulas persist process. But agent memory (MEMORY.md, mempalace) is not governed during overnight runs — what gets written, what gets pruned, what survives a compaction event. This silently undermines commitment 5's 'work survives overnight runs' promise. This bead must pass through the M2 brainstorm-readiness gate before implementation begins (self-referential: the M2 gate informs how this work gets specced).
acceptance_criteria: Memory governance rules documented for overnight runs (write-policy, prune-policy, compaction-survival contract). A synthetic compaction event mid-run is recoverable: bead state, memory artifacts, and molecule progress can all be reconstructed post-compaction. Verified by running an overnight smoke that triggers a synthetic compaction.

title: [Impl] Audit skills, agents, commands, rules for best practices & coherence
description:
## Spec

Canonical spec: docs/specs/2026-05-09-acmh-comprehensive-audit-design.md (commit d9fc28d)

## Summary

Comprehensive multi-dimensional audit of all agent-facing content (skills, agents,
commands, rules, formulas, instruction templates, helper scripts/code) for
quality, coherence, signal-to-noise, right-tool-for-the-job placement, and
alignment with the project's 85/5/10 vision. Produces a tiered remediation
plan: Tier 1 mechanical fixes applied inline in this PR; Tier 2 design-required
work deferred to per-category follow-up beads; Tier 3 bd-sequence concerns
flagged and routed to existing/new follow-up beads.

## Architecture (3 phases)

- Phase 1 — Parallel category audits: 7 Opus 1M xhigh effort subagents
  (one per category: skills, agents, commands, rules, formulas, instruction
  templates, helper scripts/code) → docs/audits/phase1/<category>.md
- Phase 2 — Cross-cutting adversarial review: 6 Codex GPT-5.5 subagents,
  one per cross-cutting use case → docs/audits/phase2/<use-case-slug>.md
- Phase 3 — Aggregation: 1 Sonnet 1M subagent reconciles by category, by
  file, decision log, REMEDIATION_PLAN.md. USER REVIEW GATE before Tier 1
  application.

## Key Design Decisions

- Vision/Mission injected at dispatch from live AGENTS.md (no static drift).
- Every finding carries a mandatory Vision-advancement field explaining how
  the recommendation advances the 85/5/10 ratio (regression of cited issue
  is implicit).
- Right-tool-for-the-job inventory: skill ↔ rule ↔ command ↔ agent ↔ shell
  script/helper code.
- Pre-research primer set committed to docs/primers/ (FORMULAS, SKILLS,
  AGENTS, COMMANDS, RULES) so subagents share a knowledge baseline.
- xacz absorbed; bf6 and 2gzy linked but separate.
- wmjy chosen direction (script-extraction) covers dep-migration; remaining
  Tier 3 (children-check, label-copy-filter) goes to a new follow-up bead.

## Out of Scope

- Implementation of Tier 2 design changes (deferred to per-category beads).
- Long-bead spec externalization (agents-config-bf6).
- Helper script extraction itself (agents-config-2gzy / wmjy receive
  findings as input only).
- Refactoring or restructuring the install pipeline.

## Acceptance Criteria

See bead acceptance field. Full numbered list in spec §12.

## Execution Sequence

12 steps; spec §13. USER REVIEW GATE between Phase 3 aggregation and Tier 1
application is mandatory.

## Resource Budget (estimate)

- Phase 1: 7 × Opus 1M xhigh — heavy
- Phase 2: 6 × Codex GPT-5.5 — moderate
- Phase 3: 1 × Sonnet 1M — moderate
- Tier 1 inline fixes + completion gate + delivery — standard

See spec §15 for tradeoffs.

## Open Questions

None blocking. Optional refinements may surface during Phase 1 dispatch
prompt finalization (e.g. exact category slugs); these are in-flight
implementation details, not architectural.
acceptance_criteria:
1. All 7 Phase 1 category audit files exist at docs/audits/phase1/<category>.md
2. All 6 Phase 2 use-case adversarial files exist at docs/audits/phase2/<use-case-slug>.md
3. Phase 3 outputs complete: by-category/<category>.md per category, by-file/<file-slug>.md per source file with ≥1 finding, decisions.md, REMEDIATION_PLAN.md
4. Every finding has all schema fields populated, including non-empty Vision-advancement
5. User has reviewed REMEDIATION_PLAN.md and approved the Tier 1 fix list
6. All approved Tier 1 fixes applied to source files
7. scripts/install.sh --dry-run passes after Tier 1 fixes
8. One follow-up bead filed per category with ≥1 Tier 2 finding
9. Tier 3 bd-sequences follow-up bead filed (children-check + label-copy-filter)
10. Comments posted on agents-config-2gzy (script-interface findings) and agents-config-wmjy (dep-migration extraction confirmation)
11. AGENTS.md updated if audit surfaces findings against it
12. Completion gate passed: quality-reviewer → simplify → verify-checklist (all pass with evidence)
13. PR created via finishing-a-development-branch skill; summary links to REMEDIATION_PLAN.md
notes: Step 6 USER REVIEW GATE reached at 2026-05-10T07:39:25Z. REMEDIATION_PLAN.md ready at docs/audits/phase3/REMEDIATION_PLAN.md (worktree HEAD f623236). AUDIT_INPUT_SHA=af9c1bfc342bf7578ad491cc63dc95b07618c851. Authorize Tier 1 by adding 'APPROVED af9c1bfc342bf7578ad491cc63dc95b07618c851' as the first non-blank line of REMEDIATION_PLAN.md, OR posting a bd comment matching that pattern. Verifiable check #4 (tier-C share <= 30%) FAILED at 46.1% — flagged for user judgment, not auto-demoted.
close_reason: Audit complete and shipped via PR #61 (merge commit 9ed3a0b). 32 Tier-1 fixes applied across 7 categories. 12 Tier-2 follow-up beads filed (agents-config-acmh.1 through acmh.12) with discovered-from links. Adjacency notifications posted on agents-config-2gzy and agents-config-wmjy. All 4 Copilot review comments addressed and resolved.

title: Reconcile persona 'Doubt yourself, ask when unsure' vs orchestration 'Decide, don't defer'
description:
## Problem

The persona file (`AGENT-PERSONA.md.template`) instructs agents: *"Doubt yourself until you've checked facts. Ask when unsure."* The orchestration rules (`INSTRUCTIONS.md.template` > `<orchestration>`) instruct: *"Decide, don't defer."*

Root cause: the persona stanza conflates two distinct concepts — (a) epistemic humility (verify before acting, compatible with orchestration) and (b) escalation default (ask vs. decide, conflicts with orchestration). Only the last line conflicts.

Secondary finding: the same over-broad escalation default appears in `agents/tech-lead.md` (duplicates a matrix) and `skills/ralf-implement/implementer-prompt.md:34` ("If ANYTHING is unclear, ask").

## Solution

### Edit 1a — Insert `<decision-matrix>` block into `INSTRUCTIONS.md.template`

Use the **exact tag name `<decision-matrix>`** (do not rename or alias). Place between `</constraints>` and `<workflow>` using this diff (the blank line on line 27 becomes the insertion anchor):

```diff
 </constraints>
 
+<decision-matrix>
+<!-- Canonical escalation hierarchy. Reference by concept, not by file path —
+     this content is flattened into all assembled instruction files at install
+     time via DYNAMIC-INCLUDE. Do not add per-tool copies. -->
+
+When facing a question or unknown, classify the situation, then act:
+
+| Situation                                                              | Action   | Quadrant                       |
+|------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------|--------------------------------|
+| Verifiable fact                                                        | Verify   | verify-facts                   |
+| In-scope decision; OR architectural but clear best option exists       | Decide   | decide-in-scope                |
+| Architectural w/ genuinely balanced trade-offs, or option-space needs human context | Escalate | escalate-architectural |
+| Conflicting directions L0–L3 precedence cannot resolve                 | Escalate | escalate-conflicting           |
+
+- **verify-facts** — the answer exists in the codebase, docs, git history, or
+  prior conversation. Read/grep/test. Do NOT ask.
+- **decide-in-scope** — implementation detail, naming, local refactor,
+  reasonable trade-off within stated requirements, OR an architectural question
+  where you can identify a clearly better option by standard engineering
+  judgment. Choose it. Do NOT ask for validation.
+- **escalate-architectural** — architectural or requirements implications where
+  a clearly better option CANNOT be identified: trade-offs are genuinely
+  balanced with no engineering-judgment winner, or the options themselves
+  depend on human context unavailable to the agent. If you can rank options,
+  you are in decide-in-scope — do NOT escalate for approval.
+- **escalate-conflicting** — instructions, constraints, or stakeholders pull
+  in incompatible directions that L0–L3 law precedence cannot resolve. Escalate,
+  surfacing the conflict with your read of each side.
+
+**Risk-asymmetric defaults are NOT escalations.** Specific rules that say
+"when in doubt, do the safe thing" (merge authorization, `git restore`
+coordination, merge-guard's `pass 0`) override the matrix on their narrow
+domain and are not affected by this change.
+</decision-matrix>
+
 <workflow>
```

**Cross-tool propagation is automatic.** The matrix reaches `.codex/AGENTS.md.template`, `.gemini/GEMINI.md.template`, and `.opencode/AGENTS.md.template` via their `<!-- DYNAMIC-INCLUDE: src/user/.agents/INSTRUCTIONS.md.template -->` markers (verified: `.opencode/AGENTS.md.template` line 5 carries this marker). Do NOT add per-tool edits.

### Edit 1b — Collapse `<orchestration>` "Decide, don't defer" bullet in `INSTRUCTIONS.md.template`

The exact current single-line content to replace (verify verbatim match before applying):

```diff
-- **Decide, don't defer**: Use your best judgment on implementation details and non-architectural questions. Only escalate to the user when: (a) the answer has significant architectural or requirements implications not already specified, or (b) there are conflicting directions that require human resolution. Decide everything else yourself. Do not ask questions you can answer from the codebase, existing instructions, or standard engineering judgment.
+- **Decide, don't defer**: apply the canonical decision matrix.
+  Quadrants `verify-facts` and `decide-in-scope` resolve without the user.
```

**Other `<orchestration>` bullets** (Subagents, Self-improvement, Verify before done, Tracer bullets, Isolate work, Elegance check) are untouched — they encode other behaviors, not escalation defaults.

### Edit 2 — `AGENT-PERSONA.md.template` surgical line replacement

```diff
 **Self-awareness**:

 - You assume too much
 - Doubt yourself until you've checked facts
-- Ask when unsure
+- Apply the canonical decision matrix to every ask-vs-decide moment
```

**Intentional preservation note:** Lines "You assume too much" and "Doubt yourself until you've checked facts" are deliberately kept. They encode epistemic humility — a reminder to verify facts before acting (aligns with the `verify-facts` quadrant). They are NOT escalation directives and do not conflict with orchestration. Only "Ask when unsure" is a de-facto escalation default that conflicts; only it is removed.

### Edit 3 — `agents/tech-lead.md` replace escalation criteria section

Replace the entire "Decision Criteria for Human Escalation" section (lines 78–86, heading through last bullet). Current six bullets and disposition:

```
- Subagent explicitly requests human input             ← KEEP (orchestrator-specific)
- Multiple agents report conflicting recommendations   ← KEEP (orchestrator-specific)
- Critical architectural decisions (long-term)         ← DROP — covered by escalate-architectural
- Resource constraints or technical limitations        ← DROP — covered by decide-in-scope or escalate-architectural
- Ambiguous requirements needing clarification         ← DROP — covered by escalate-architectural
- Team appears stuck after multiple attempts           ← KEEP (orchestrator-specific)
```

Replacement text:

```markdown
### Decision Criteria for Human Escalation

Apply the canonical decision matrix. Role-specific additions for the
tech-lead orchestrator:

- A dispatched subagent explicitly requests human input
- Multiple subagents report conflicting recommendations
- Team appears stuck after multiple attempts (loop detection)

The architectural-decisions, resource-constraints, and
ambiguous-requirements bullets dropped here are already covered by the
`escalate-architectural` quadrant of the canonical decision matrix.
```

### Edit 4 — `skills/ralf-implement/implementer-prompt.md` line 34

```diff
-If ANYTHING is unclear, ask before proceeding. Don't guess.
+If unclear, apply the canonical decision matrix. Don't guess.
```

## Scope

**In scope (4 files, 5 edits):**
- `src/user/.agents/INSTRUCTIONS.md.template` — two edits: (1a) insert `<decision-matrix>` block, (1b) collapse "Decide, don't defer" bullet
- `src/user/.agents/AGENT-PERSONA.md.template` — replace line 18 (Edit 2)
- `src/user/.agents/agents/tech-lead.md` — replace lines 78–86 (Edit 3)
- `src/user/.agents/skills/ralf-implement/implementer-prompt.md` — replace line 34 (Edit 4)

**Out of scope:**
- FIX/SKIP/ESCALATE comment classifications in `wait-for-pr-comments` and `reply-and-resolve-pr-threads` — different concept (PR triage, not agent escalation)
- `bugfix/SKILL.md:117` "escalate to complementary skill" — skill-to-skill handoff, not user escalation
- Risk-asymmetric defaults already in place (merge auth, `git restore`, merge-guard) — explicitly preserved by matrix caveat
- Per-tool subtrees (`.codex`, `.gemini`, `.opencode`) — propagation is automatic via DYNAMIC-INCLUDE; no direct edits needed

**Reference style constraint:** All new references to the matrix in changed files must use conceptual phrasing ("canonical decision matrix" and/or quadrant names: `verify-facts`, `decide-in-scope`, `escalate-architectural`, `escalate-conflicting`). Do NOT use file-path citations. Pre-existing path references unrelated to escalation (e.g., `.claude/agents/*` in tech-lead.md line 41) may remain unchanged.

## Smoke test (manual)

After all edits, read the four changed files and answer each question, citing the quadrant name (or "role-specific addition" for Q3):

| # | Question | Expected answer |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 | "Should I ask the user before deciding a variable naming convention?" | No → `decide-in-scope` |
| Q2 | "Two equally valid retry strategies exist for this component — one adds complexity, the other a latency penalty. No clear winner." | Ask with options + recommendation → `escalate-architectural` (balanced trade-offs, no engineering-judgment winner) |
| Q3 | "I am tech-lead and a dispatched subagent says it needs human input." | Escalate → tech-lead role-specific addition (not a generic matrix quadrant) |
| Q4 | "A completion-gate rule and a persona instruction pull in incompatible directions." | Ask → `escalate-conflicting` |
| Q5 | "I know the retry behavior should be exponential backoff — it's standard for this class of service." | Decide → `decide-in-scope` (can identify clearly better option) |

A fresh agent must cite the correct quadrant name (or "role-specific") for each. "I'll just ask to be safe" on Q1, Q2, or Q5 is a failing answer.
acceptance_criteria:
1. <decision-matrix> block (exact tag name) inserted between </constraints> and <workflow> in INSTRUCTIONS.md.template with all four named quadrants and risk-asymmetric-defaults caveat.
2. AGENT-PERSONA.md.template line 18 replaced; 'Ask when unsure' removed; lines 16-17 preserved with intentional note documented.
3. INSTRUCTIONS.md.template <orchestration> 'Decide, don't defer' bullet collapsed to two-line matrix reference (single-line old string replaced verbatim).
4. agents/tech-lead.md Decision Criteria section: three orchestrator-specific bullets retained; three generic bullets dropped with rationale.
5. skills/ralf-implement/implementer-prompt.md line 34 replaced with matrix reference.
6. All new matrix references use conceptual phrasing (quadrant names or 'canonical decision matrix') — no file-path citations.
7. Cross-tool propagation requires no per-tool edits (verified: DYNAMIC-INCLUDE markers present in all four per-tool templates).
8. Smoke test passes: fresh agent cites correct quadrant for all five questions (Q1 decide-in-scope, Q2 escalate-architectural, Q3 tech-lead role-specific, Q4 escalate-conflicting, Q5 decide-in-scope).

title: Add brainstorm-readiness gate (verify-brainstorm skill) to enforce spec quality before implementation
description:
**Goal**: The missing twin of `verify-checklist`. The completion gate audits implementations; this gate audits *brainstorm artifacts* before they earn the `implementation-ready` label, supporting the project's 85/5/10 human-time vision (see AGENTS.md > Vision).

**Problem**: Today, the bounce-back from agent to human ("no, not ready — your spec is missing X") relies on the brainstorming skill being well-followed, with no mechanical checklist that the AI must satisfy before stamping `implementation-ready`. Since brainstorming is the largest time slice in the target ratio, this gate is the highest-leverage point in the entire system.

**Approach sketch**: New skill (e.g., `verify-brainstorm`) under src/user/.agents/skills/, paralleling the `verify-checklist` pattern. Wired into start-bead / brainstorm-bead routing so that `implementation-ready` is gated on the audit passing.

**Out of scope**: rewriting the brainstorming skill itself; building any UI; replacing the start-bead flow.
acceptance_criteria:
- New skill under src/user/.agents/skills/ (name TBD: verify-brainstorm or similar)
- Skill audits a bead spec for at minimum: testable acceptance criteria; identified risks; explicit out-of-scope; pinned data shapes/contracts; ambiguity tags
- Skill blocks (or refuses to stamp) the implementation-ready label until criteria met
- Skill documents what to do when criteria cannot be met (escalate vs defer)
- Wired into the existing brainstorm/start-bead flow
- AGENTS.md and README.md reference the gate as part of the 85/5/10 enabler set

title: Extract bd orchestration sequences into idempotent shell helpers
description:
The brainstorm-bead finalize step embeds complex bd CLI sequences directly in LLM-executed formula prose. This creates two classes of bugs: (1) the LLM can issue side-effectful calls (bd create, dep migration loops) multiple times via parallel tool batching, producing duplicate beads; (2) the same boilerplate sequences (claim walk, close walk) are copy-pasted across 3-5 formula files with no single source of truth.

Extract the deterministic bd orchestration sequences into focused shell helper scripts that (a) encode idempotency internally, (b) present a single named command to the LLM (one invocation = one side effect), and (c) live in .beads/scripts/ so install.sh deploys them with chmod +x.

## Scope — Tier 1 (fixes the duplicate-bead root cause)
- bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh: creates the implementation bead during brainstorm finalize; built-in intra-step orphan guard prevents parallel-call duplicates
- Update brainstorm-bead.formula.toml finalize Step 4 to call this script

## Scope — Tier 2 (DRY violation fix)
- bd-claim-walk.sh: I1 claim walk (bead + ancestor epics → in_progress); used in brainstorm-bead, implement-feature, fix-bug
- bd-close-walk.sh: I2 close walk (close bead + cascade-close empty ancestors); used in brainstorm-bead, merge-and-cleanup
- Update all formula files that contain the inline equivalent sequences

## Out of scope
- Tier 3 scripts (children-check, dep-migration, label-copy-filter) — tracked separately
- docs-only formula (no claim/close walk patterns present)
acceptance_criteria:
bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh: --help prints purpose + contract; idempotency guard prevents duplicate bead creation when called twice with same args; formula Step 4 updated to single script invocation.
bd-claim-walk.sh: --help prints purpose + contract; marks bead + all ancestor epics in_progress; brainstorm-bead Phase 0 and implement-feature and fix-bug preflight Step 7 updated to single script invocation.
bd-close-walk.sh: --help prints purpose + contract; closes target bead with --reason then cascades up closing empty ancestors; brainstorm-bead Step 8 and merge-and-cleanup Step 6 updated to single script invocation.
All scripts live in src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/ (flat; install.sh deploys via existing glob).
No existing formula behaviour is changed — only the mechanism (inline bash vs named script).
close_reason: Merged PR #54 — bd orchestration shell helpers (bd-claim-walk, bd-close-walk, bd-finalize-create-impl-bead)
