title: Update ralf-* skills and formulas to consume worker-report-v1 and dispatch new workers
description:
## Purpose

Update both formula files (implement-feature, fix-bug) AND the orchestration skills (ralf-implement, ralf-review) to consume the new worker-agent architecture established in 7bk.19.1 / 7bk.19.2 / 7bk.19.3. Without this wiring, the new workers and the rewritten implement-bead orchestrator have no callers and no inner-doer integration with the convergence-loop skills.

## Architecture (mirrors 7bk.19.3)

Per the refined architecture established in 7bk.19.3, the layers are:

- **implement-bead skill** = metadata-driven dispatcher. Reads step-bead labels + description to decide orchestration skill (if any) and execution agent (if any). Documents the per-dispatch primitive as a reusable imperative block.
- **ralf-implement / ralf-review** = orchestration skills. Own the convergence loop, iteration counter, and foreign-eyes / adversarial review. Dispatch the primary doer (an execution agent) via implement-bead's per-dispatch primitive.
- **Execution skills** (e.g. `superpowers:test-driven-development`) = methodology guides loaded into the doer's context.
- **Execution agents** (tdd-red-team, tdd-green-team, bug-diagnoser) = pure task functions emitting worker-report-v1 YAML.

Loop logic does NOT live in formula step prose. Formula steps signal loop intent via metadata labels (`ralf:required`, `ralf:cycles=N`). The dispatcher reads those labels and invokes the matching orchestration skill, which owns iteration. This avoids duplicating loop boilerplate across stages and keeps methodology in skills (where it can evolve) rather than in formula files.

The 7bk.19.1 path convention (`<repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/<agent-name>[-iter<N>].yaml`) and audit-label convention (`worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]` on the step-bead) are load-bearing throughout.

## Scope

### `src/user/.agents/skills/ralf-implement/SKILL.md` — update

- Consume **worker-report-v1**: the inner doer's output is the YAML report at the deterministic path, not free-form "report what you did" prose. Use `worker-report-v1.md` §1.1 evidence blocks + derived gate roll-up as the primary convergence signal (alongside the score from any foreign-eyes review).
- Dispatch the inner doer via implement-bead's **per-dispatch primitive** (Agent tool with `subagent_type: <agent-name>`, absolute target report path passed in task-spec, synthesize `status: failed` per `worker-report-v1.md` §4 on crash/malformed, stamp `worker-audit-<agent>[-iter<N>]` audit label on step-bead).
- Use the audit label set as the per-iteration forensic marker (in addition to the existing `RALF-IT-ITER:<n>/<MAX>` step-bead notes marker; the two are complementary, not redundant).
- Parameterize the doer agent identity: ralf-implement does NOT hardcode tdd-green-team. The orchestration caller (implement-bead) passes the doer's `subagent_type` as input. This keeps ralf-implement reusable across stages and modes.
- When `subagent_type` is missing from the invocation, prompt the user interactively for a doer selection; provide context-aware recommendations (e.g. `tdd-green-team` for implement tasks, `tdd-red-team` for test-writing, `bug-diagnoser` for diagnosis); offer a general-purpose fallback (`subagent_type: 'general-purpose'` — a Claude Code builtin) when no confident recommendation can be made.
- **Convergence predicate**: converge when derived gate roll-up is `pass` AND the fresh-eyes pass finds no blocking or critical findings. Continue until cycle cap otherwise. At cycle cap: `gate=pass` + no blocking/critical residuals → `PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS`; blocking/critical residuals, `gate=fail`, or `gate=partial` → `FAIL`.
- **Per-cycle loop (updated structure)**: (1) dispatch worker via per-dispatch primitive, derive gate roll-up from evidence blocks; (2) an iteration is a **convergence candidate** when derived gate roll-up is `pass`; if it is, run quality-reviewer + simplify once on changed code before the fresh-eyes pass — simplify is advisory on clarity/duplication only and is not expected to change test-affecting behavior; if quality-reviewer findings require production-code correctness changes (not just clarity), the iteration is NOT a convergence candidate — continue with a new worker dispatch; (3) fresh-eyes pass (cycle 1: Codex, cycle 2: Gemini, cycle 3+: pure-Claude). The legacy "run project quality checks" step (old step 2) is subsumed by gate derivation from worker-report-v1 evidence and removed from the per-cycle body. verify-checklist is similarly replaced by gate roll-up; it is not dispatched per cycle.

### `src/user/.agents/skills/ralf-review/SKILL.md` — update

- If ralf-review dispatches any worker agents (review subagents, etc.), update those dispatches to use the per-dispatch primitive and consume worker-report-v1 where applicable.
- If ralf-review only orchestrates code-review subagents (no worker-report-v1 production), document that worker-report-v1 consumption is N/A and keep the update minimal.
- Implementer's call on minimal-vs-full update based on ralf-review's actual responsibility scope.

### `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml` — update

Per-stage changes:

- **`red-tests` step**: replace step description with stage-declarative language (e.g. `[red-tests stage] implement-bead dispatches tdd-red-team per the stage→agent map (implement-feature mode, multi-AC)`). Do NOT use the self-referential phrase "invoke implement-bead skill." Single-shot by default. Remove all loop-ownership prose.
- **`green-loop` step**: replace step description with stage-declarative language (e.g. `[green-loop stage] implement-bead routes to ralf-implement when ralf:required is present on the source bead, or dispatches tdd-green-team directly; ralf-implement owns the convergence loop`). Remove all loop-ownership prose.
- **Other stages** (`preflight`, `quality-sweep`, `verify-ac`, `create-pr`, `review-cycle`, `merge-or-handoff`): unchanged in scope; existing dispatch shape preserved.

### `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml` — update

Per-stage changes:

- **`diagnose` step**: replace step description with stage-declarative language (e.g. `[diagnose stage] implement-bead dispatches bug-diagnoser; worker emits root_cause_note into its worker-report-v1 file at the deterministic path`). Single-shot. Remove all loop-ownership prose.
- **`red-tests` step (regression test)**: replace step description with stage-declarative language. Dispatcher pulls `root_cause_note` + `reproduction_steps` from the prior diagnose step's worker-report file (path resolved from diagnose step-bead id) and includes them in the task-spec built for tdd-red-team. Single-shot. Remove all loop-ownership prose.
- **`green-loop` step (fix)**: replace step description with stage-declarative language. Presence of `ralf:required` causes dispatcher to invoke `ralf-implement` with `root_cause_note` from diagnose's report; absence causes single direct dispatch + gate verification. Remove all loop-ownership prose.
- **Other stages**: unchanged.

### Mandatory removals (both formula files)

- All references to `bead-implementor` in step descriptions.
- All per-step loop-ownership prose (including but not limited to `MAX_ITERATIONS=<N>` literals, explicit convergence predicates, and max-iterations-exhausted escalation blocks). Loop ownership belongs in orchestration skills, not formula step prose.
- Any stale guidance referring to old bead-implementor stage parameters.

### Delete `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md`

The agent is fully replaced by tdd-red-team, tdd-green-team, and bug-diagnoser. This PR removes its last callers from both formula files. Delete the file in this same PR — leaving dead code between 19.4 and a cleanup bead serves no purpose. Run the repo-wide invariant grep (see AC) after all edits to confirm zero references remain before committing.

Note: `scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh` still references bead-implementor and is explicitly excluded from the invariant grep (its update is 7bk.19.5 scope).

### Documentation

`ralf:required` label semantics MUST be documented somewhere it can be found by formula authors and by orchestration-skill maintainers — either in a header comment of each formula file, or in `ralf-implement`'s SKILL.md, or both. Recommendation: document in `ralf-implement`'s SKILL.md as the canonical home (label is a signal to it), and add a short pointer comment in each formula file.

## Dependencies

This child requires 7bk.19.1 (path convention + report contract), 7bk.19.2 (workers), and 7bk.19.3 (rewritten implement-bead with per-dispatch primitive). Explicit dep edges to all three.

## Out of Scope

- Architecture spec catalog updates in `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` (deferred to 7bk.19.5).
- Smoke / tracer-bullet for the new dispatch flow end-to-end, including verify-artifacts.sh update (deferred to 7bk.19.5).
- `inline-execution` directive for implement-bead (tracked as 7bk.21).
- Explicit `agent:*` / `skill:*` dispatch label overrides (tracked as 7bk.22).
- `stage:<role>` label stamping for robust upstream step lookup (tracked as 7bk.23).

## Acceptance Criteria

- `ralf-implement` SKILL.md updated to: consume worker-report-v1 (evidence blocks + derived gate roll-up); dispatch the inner doer via implement-bead's per-dispatch primitive (Agent tool, absolute report path, synthesis on crash, audit-label stamping, gate roll-up derivation); parameterize the doer's `subagent_type` as required input; when `subagent_type` is missing, prompt the user interactively with context-aware recommendations and a general-purpose fallback; explicit convergence predicate combining gate roll-up and fresh-eyes severity; updated per-cycle loop (quality-reviewer+simplify on convergence candidate only, not per-iteration; verify-checklist replaced by gate roll-up).
- `ralf-review` SKILL.md updated for worker-report-v1 consumption and per-dispatch-primitive usage in any worker dispatches it owns (or documented as N/A with rationale if it does not).
- `implement-feature.formula.toml`: `red-tests` and `green-loop` step descriptions use stage-declarative language (not self-referential "invoke implement-bead skill" phrasing). No per-step loop-ownership prose.
- `fix-bug.formula.toml`: `diagnose`, `red-tests`, and `green-loop` step descriptions use stage-declarative language. `red-tests` and `green-loop` pass `root_cause_note` (and `reproduction_steps` for `red-tests`) sourced from the prior diagnose step's worker-report file. No loop-prose mandates.
- No string `bead-implementor` remains in either formula file.
- No per-step loop-ownership prose remains in formula step descriptions (including MAX_ITERATIONS literals, explicit convergence predicates, or max-iter-exhausted escalation blocks).
- `ralf:required` label semantics documented in `ralf-implement` SKILL.md (canonical home) with optional pointer comments in formula files.
- `bead-implementor.md` no longer exists at `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md`.
- Repo-wide invariant grep `grep -rn 'bead-implementor' --include='*.md' --include='*.toml' --include='*.sh' . | grep -v '.claude/worktrees/' | grep -v '.git/' | grep -v '.beads/issues.jsonl' | grep -v 'scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh'` returns zero matches.
- Existing formula tests (if any) still pass.
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
acceptance_criteria:
ralf-implement SKILL.md updated: consumes worker-report-v1 (evidence blocks + derived gate roll-up); dispatches inner doer via Agent tool with required subagent_type; when subagent_type missing, prompts user interactively with context-aware recommendations and general-purpose fallback (subagent_type:'general-purpose', a Claude Code builtin); dispatches via implement-bead per-dispatch primitive (informal section reference, not inline); synthesizes status:failed on crash/malformed; stamps worker-audit-<agent>[-iter<N>] label; explicit convergence predicate (converge when gate=pass AND no blocking/critical fresh-eyes findings; PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS at cycle cap with only minor/major; FAIL on blocking/critical or gate=fail/partial); convergence candidate defined as iteration where gate=pass; per-cycle loop updated (quality-reviewer+simplify on convergence candidate only; simplify advisory-only — correctness changes restart loop with new worker dispatch; verify-checklist replaced by gate roll-up derivation)
ralf-review SKILL.md updated: worker-report-v1 N/A documented with rationale (review subagents are out-of-band per implement-bead §audit-label-scope); minimal update only
implement-feature.formula.toml: red-tests and green-loop step descriptions use stage-declarative language (not self-referential "invoke implement-bead skill" phrasing); no per-step loop-ownership prose
fix-bug.formula.toml: diagnose, red-tests, and green-loop step descriptions use stage-declarative language; red-tests and green-loop pass root_cause_note (and reproduction_steps for red-tests) from prior diagnose step worker-report file; no loop-prose mandates
No string bead-implementor remains in either formula file
No per-step loop-ownership prose remains in formula step descriptions (including MAX_ITERATIONS literals, explicit convergence predicates, or max-iter-exhausted escalation blocks)

Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
notes:
## Summary

Wire the new worker-agent architecture (7bk.19.1/19.2/19.3) into the two orchestration skills and both formula files. Update ralf-implement to dispatch worker agents via the per-dispatch primitive and consume worker-report-v1; update ralf-review minimally; simplify formula step dispatch language by removing loop-ownership prose; delete the now-dead bead-implementor.md agent in the same PR.

## Background / Motivation

7bk.19.1 defined the worker-report-v1 contract. 7bk.19.2 created three focused worker agents (tdd-red-team, tdd-green-team, bug-diagnoser). 7bk.19.3 rewrote implement-bead as a metadata-driven dispatcher with the per-dispatch primitive. Without 7bk.19.4, the new agents exist but no caller dispatches them — the two formula files still reference bead-implementor, and ralf-implement has no worker-report-v1 wiring.

## Requirements

1. ralf-implement dispatches the named worker agent via implement-bead's per-dispatch primitive (Agent tool, absolute report path, worker-report-v1 parse, crash synthesis, audit-label stamp).
2. ralf-implement's doer identity is parameterized (subagent_type is a required input from the caller); missing input triggers an interactive recommendation prompt rather than a hard fail.
3. ralf-implement has an explicit convergence predicate combining gate roll-up and fresh-eyes severity; the per-cycle loop is updated to drop quality checks (subsumed by gate derivation) and limit quality-reviewer+simplify to convergence candidates only.
4. Both formula files replace verbose loop-ownership prose with stage-declarative dispatch language (not self-referential "invoke implement-bead skill" phrasing).
5. fix-bug formula correctly propagates root_cause_note from diagnose step to red-tests and green-loop.
6. bead-implementor.md deleted; invariant grep (with verify-artifacts.sh exclusion) confirms zero references.

## Out of Scope

- Architecture spec catalog updates in docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md (7bk.19.5)
- Smoke / tracer-bullet for end-to-end dispatch flow; verify-artifacts.sh update (7bk.19.5)
- inline-execution directive for implement-bead (7bk.21)
- Explicit agent:* / skill:* dispatch label overrides (7bk.22)
- stage:<role> label stamping for upstream lookup (7bk.23)

## Design Notes

**D1 — subagent_type: required input with interactive fallback (not hard-fail)**
ralf-implement requires subagent_type from the orchestration caller. When invoked manually (not via implement-bead), a missing subagent_type triggers an interactive prompt rather than an unconditional error. The skill offers context-aware recommendations (tdd-green-team for implement tasks, tdd-red-team for test-writing, bug-diagnoser for diagnosis) with a general-purpose fallback when no confident match exists. Rationale: keeps the contract clean (no hardcoded defaults) while preserving UX for human callers.

**D2 — ralf-review: minimal update only**
ralf-review dispatches review subagents (fresh-eyes, adversarial), not worker-report-v1-emitting workers. The per-dispatch primitive and worker-report-v1 consumption are N/A for its responsibility scope. Update is a documentation note only. Implementer's call confirmed during brainstorming.

**D3 — per-dispatch primitive: anchor reference, not duplication**
ralf-implement references implement-bead/SKILL.md's per-dispatch primitive via an informal section reference (e.g. `see implement-bead/SKILL.md §4 — Per-dispatch primitive`) rather than inlining the procedure; implement-bead/SKILL.md is not modified in this PR. Rationale: single canonical home; avoids drift between the two files.

**D4 — ralf:required documentation home**
Canonical doc in ralf-implement/SKILL.md (label is its trigger signal). Short pointer comment in each formula file header.

**D5 — bead-implementor.md deletion moved into 19.4**
Originally deferred to 7bk.19.5. Moved into 19.4 because this PR removes the file's last callers (both formula files). Leaving dead code between PRs is unnecessary. Invariant grep (with verify-artifacts.sh exclusion) added as an AC verification step. Note: verify-artifacts.sh still references bead-implementor and is intentionally excluded — its update remains in 7bk.19.5.

**D6 — Three follow-up beads filed**
7bk.21: inline-execution directive for implement-bead
7bk.22: explicit agent:* / skill:* dispatch label overrides
7bk.23: stage:<role> label stamping for robust upstream step lookup
All three depend on 7bk.19.4 and are P4/backlog, unbrainstormed.

**D7 — Convergence predicate: gate roll-up + fresh-eyes combined**
Added after adversarial spec review cycle 1 (C2). Converge when: derived gate=pass AND fresh-eyes finds no blocking/critical. At cycle cap: gate=pass + no blocking/critical → PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS; blocking/critical or gate=fail/partial → FAIL.

**D8 — Per-cycle loop: quality-reviewer+simplify on convergence candidate only**
Added after adversarial spec review cycle 1 (M1). The legacy "run quality checks" step is subsumed by gate derivation from worker-report-v1 evidence and removed from the per-cycle body. verify-checklist is replaced by gate roll-up. quality-reviewer+simplify run once when the iteration is a convergence candidate (not per-iteration), then fresh-eyes pass follows.

**D9 — Formula step language: stage-declarative, not self-referential**
Added after adversarial spec review cycle 1 (m2). Formula step descriptions should use stage-declarative language (e.g. "[red-tests stage] implement-bead dispatches tdd-red-team...") rather than the self-referential phrase "invoke implement-bead skill," which reads as circular in a step that implement-bead is already executing.

**D10 — Convergence candidate defined as gate=pass**
Added after adversarial spec review cycle 2 (N1). An iteration is a convergence candidate when derived gate roll-up is `pass`. quality-reviewer+simplify run only in that case. This avoids running the deeper quality check on every failing iteration.

**D11 — simplify is advisory; correctness changes restart the loop**
Added after adversarial spec review cycle 2 (N2). simplify is expected to make clarity/duplication improvements only, not test-affecting production changes. If quality-reviewer findings require correctness changes, the iteration is not a convergence candidate — dispatch a new worker rather than patching inline (avoids stale gate evidence).

**D12 — general-purpose fallback is a Claude Code builtin subagent_type**
Added after adversarial spec review cycle 2 (N4). `subagent_type: 'general-purpose'` is a Claude Code builtin, not an agent file in the repo. Clarified in spec to avoid confusion with agent files.

**D13 — per-dispatch primitive reference is informal (not a formal markdown anchor)**
Added after adversarial spec review cycle 2 (N3). implement-bead/SKILL.md has no formal anchors and is not modified in this PR. ralf-implement uses an informal section reference (`see implement-bead/SKILL.md §4 — Per-dispatch primitive`).

## Open Questions

None. All design decisions resolved during brainstorming session and adversarial spec review cycles 1–2 (2026-05-08).

title: Rewrite implement-bead orchestrator: dispatch new agents, parse worker-report-v1, stamp audit label
description:
## Summary

Rewrite the `implement-bead` skill (`src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md`) and slash command (`src/plugins/beads/.claude/commands/implement-bead.md`) to be a **metadata-driven dispatcher** that routes step-bead execution to the appropriate orchestration skill (when signaled by labels) or runs a direct dispatch otherwise. Document the per-dispatch primitive — build task-spec, dispatch worker via Agent tool, parse `worker-report-v1`, synthesize on crash/malformed, stamp audit label, derive gate roll-up — as a reusable in-session procedure. This closes the orchestrator-side gap left by `7bk.19.1` (the report contract) and `7bk.19.2` (the worker agents).

## Background and Motivation

Per parent epic `7bk.19`, the previous `bead-implementor` was an all-purpose worker with four structural defects. `7bk.19.1` fixed Defect C (no structured report contract — now `worker-report-v1`). `7bk.19.2` fixed Defect A (workers no longer touch bead state — three pure-task agents now exist: `tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser`). `7bk.19.3` fixes Defect B (orchestrator concerns mixed into the worker, and stage-branching mega-skill) by replacing the implement-bead body with a thin metadata-driven dispatcher.

Without this rewrite, the new workers and the `worker-report-v1` contract have no orchestrator-side caller.

## Architecture

The post-19.3 layering (mirrored in `7bk.19.4`):

| Layer | Identity | Owns |
|---|---|---|
| Entry | `claude -p /implement-bead <bead-id>` (per-stage Claude session, spawned by shell driver) | Lifecycle: read step-bead, dispatch into orchestration, exit |
| Context-assembler / dispatcher | implement-bead skill | Read step-bead description + labels. Resolve execution context. Decide dispatch shape from metadata. Document the per-dispatch primitive. Hand off to orchestration skill or run direct dispatch. |
| Orchestration skill | `ralf-implement`, `ralf-review` (updated in `7bk.19.4`) | Convergence loop, iteration counter, foreign-eyes / adversarial review. Dispatches the doer via the per-dispatch primitive. Runs as **in-session skill code**, NOT as a subagent. |
| Execution skill | `superpowers:test-driven-development`, etc. | Methodology guide loaded into the doer's context. |
| Execution agent (subagent) | `tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser` | Pure task function. Emits `worker-report-v1` YAML at the absolute path. |

**Loop logic does NOT live in implement-bead's body.** It lives in orchestration skills (ralf-implement, ralf-review) when signaled by `ralf:required`. implement-bead is per-stage at the entry but its body is small because it delegates: it reads the step-bead, resolves context, decides shape from metadata, and either calls an orchestration skill or runs a single direct dispatch.

The 7bk.19.1 path convention (`<repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/<agent-name>[-iter<N>].yaml`) and audit-label convention (`worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]`) are load-bearing throughout.

### Invocation contexts (CRITICAL)

The skill body executes in a SINGLE Claude session — the one spawned by the shell driver via `claude -p /implement-bead <bead-id>`. Within that session:

- The **per-dispatch primitive** is a documented in-session procedure, not a separately-invoked tool. It is executed by whatever code path needs it (the skill's main flow on the direct path, OR the in-session orchestration skill on the orchestration path).
- The **Agent tool dispatch** in step 4 of the per-dispatch primitive — `Agent({ subagent_type: <doer>, prompt: <task-spec> })` — must be invoked from the top-level orchestrator session. Subagents cannot spawn subagents (Claude Code runtime constraint). Therefore:
  - When implement-bead's main flow runs the per-dispatch primitive directly, it dispatches the worker via Agent tool. ✓
  - When implement-bead invokes `ralf-implement` for the orchestration path, `ralf-implement` runs as **in-context skill code in the same top-level session** — NOT as a subagent. It executes the per-dispatch primitive, which dispatches the worker via Agent tool from the top-level session. ✓
  - implement-bead does NOT recursively invoke itself via `claude -p` to run the primitive. The primitive is invoked in-session as a procedure call.

This invocation model is the only valid interpretation given the runtime constraint, and it must be stated explicitly so the implementor does not introduce a `claude -p` recursion loop or attempt to dispatch ralf-implement as a subagent.

## Scope

### `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md` — full rewrite

Imperative-only body covering, in order:

1. **Read step-bead and labels.** From `bd show <step-bead-id>` and `bd label list <step-bead-id>`. The slash-command-driving context (the surrounding Claude session) has already located the step-bead from the source bead-id; the skill operates on the step-bead.

2. **Resolve execution context.**
   - **Worktree path**: decode the molecule's `worktree-path-*` label using the existing bijection (`__ → /` then `_u → _`); verify with `git -C <path> rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree`.
   - **Mode**: canonical lookup is the source bead's `formula-<name>` label, per `bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §3 (preflight) and §4.2 (policy-knob labels). Preflight is what stamps formula selection on the source bead from `formula-<name>`, falling back to per-bead-type defaults (feature/task/chore → `implement-feature`, bug → `fix-bug`, epic → flag-human). implement-bead reads the same authority: `bd label list <source-bead-id> | awk '/^formula-/{sub(/^formula-/,""); print; exit}'`. The source-bead-id is derived from the molecule's `for-bead-<source-bead-id>` linkage label (per the molecule→bead linkage convention in `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md`). Molecule id is reachable via the step-bead's `parent` field. On absent/ambiguous source-bead label, fall back to molecule title: `bd show <mol-id> --json | jq -r '.[0].title'`. Allowed mode values: `implement-feature` | `fix-bug`. Document the chosen lookup procedure inline in SKILL.md.
   - **Target report path template**: `<repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/<agent-name>[-iter<N>].yaml`. Resolve `<repo-root>` via `dirname $(git rev-parse --path-format=absolute --git-common-dir)` per `worker-report-v1.md` §2.

3. **Decide dispatch shape from metadata.**
   - **`ralf:required` label present on the source bead** → invoke the orchestration skill matching the stage role (`ralf-implement` for green-loop / fix; `ralf-review` for review-cycle-style stages). Pass: spec, Definition of Done, quality commands, optional `ralf:cycles=N` max-cycles override, the doer's `subagent_type`, the worktree path, the target report path template, and a reference to the per-dispatch primitive procedure documented in this skill. The orchestration skill is responsible for substituting `<agent-name>` and `-iter<N>` into the template per cycle when invoking the per-dispatch primitive in §4 (this is the contract handoff that `7bk.19.4` will consume). The orchestration skill runs in-session (see "Invocation contexts" above).
   - **No `ralf:required`** → load the appropriate execution skill for the stage (e.g. `superpowers:test-driven-development`) and run the per-dispatch primitive once with the doer's `subagent_type`.

4. **Per-dispatch primitive** (the reusable in-session procedure). Given inputs `(stage, mode, iteration?, execution_context, doer_subagent_type)`:
   1. Compute report path by substituting `<step-bead-id>`, `<agent-name>`, and (if `iteration` supplied) the `-iter<N>` suffix into the path template.
   2. Build the worker task-spec containing: worktree path, mode-specific inputs (per the per-agent specs in `worker-report-v1`), absolute target report path, project test-runner command, iteration counter (if applicable). For `(red-tests, fix-bug)` and `(green-loop, fix-bug)` the task-spec includes `root_cause_note` (and `reproduction_steps` for red-tests) — see "Upstream report retrieval" below.
   3. Dispatch via `Agent({ subagent_type: <doer>, prompt: <task-spec> })`.
   4. After worker exits:
      - **Non-zero exit OR no file at target path** → synthesize `status: failed` report at the target path per `worker-report-v1.md` §4 (`evidence: {}`, `escalations[].reason: "Worker crashed"`, `escalations[].detail`: exit code + stderr tail, `discovered_work: []`, `commits: []`).
      - **File present but unparseable / missing required field** → synthesize `status: failed` wrapping the parse error + raw bytes. Required-field minimum is per `worker-report-v1.md` §4.1 — at minimum `status`. **Per-agent specs may add runtime-required fields**: specifically, `bug-diagnoser-report-v1` requires non-empty `root_cause_note`; missing/empty triggers synthesis.
      - **Otherwise** → read and parse the YAML.
   5. Stamp `worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]` label on the step-bead (forensic, append-only, namespace-prefix-stable).
   6. Derive gate roll-up from evidence blocks per `worker-report-v1.md` §1.1 (`pass` / `fail` / `partial` / `n/a`). Workers do NOT emit `gate_status`; the orchestrator derives it.
   7. Return `{ report, gate, audit_label }` to the caller.

5. **Stage→agent map** used by the per-dispatch primitive's task-spec construction and `subagent_type` selection:
   - `(red-tests, implement-feature)` → `tdd-red-team` (multi-AC mode; AC bullets in task-spec)
   - `(red-tests, fix-bug)` → `tdd-red-team` (single-regression mode; `root_cause_note` + `reproduction_steps` from the prior diagnose step's report file)
   - `(green-loop, implement-feature)` → `tdd-green-team` (no `root_cause_note` in task-spec)
   - `(green-loop, fix-bug)` → `tdd-green-team` (with `root_cause_note` from diagnose's report)
   - `(diagnose, fix-bug)` → `bug-diagnoser`

6. **Upstream report retrieval.** For dispatches that consume prior step output (specifically `(red-tests, fix-bug)` and `(green-loop, fix-bug)` consuming the diagnose step's `root_cause_note`):
   1. Locate the upstream step-bead-id within the same molecule. Canonical lookup: `bd list --parent <mol-id> --all --type task --json | jq -r '.[] | select(.title | startswith("Diagnose:")) | .id'`. Step-beads inside a molecule are connected to the molecule root by the `parent` edge (NOT `dependents`); `--all` is required because the diagnose step will already be `closed` when downstream stages read its report. Implementer may use a more robust lookup if available (e.g. a stable `stage:diagnose` label, if such convention is added later).
   2. Construct the upstream report path: `<repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/<upstream-step-bead-id>/<upstream-agent-name>.yaml` (no iter suffix; diagnose is direct dispatch).
   3. Read and parse the YAML; extract `root_cause_note` (and `reproduction_steps` if applicable).
   4. Include extracted fields in the downstream worker's task-spec.
   5. **Failure mode**: if the upstream report is missing or unparseable, the orchestrator stamps `human` label on the step-bead and appends a missing-context detail; do NOT proceed with an empty `root_cause_note`. The dependency is hard.

7. **File `discovered_work` as new beads** using the I3 sibling test (per `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md`).
   - File **before** applying status outcomes (step 8), so any escalations can reference newly-filed beads.
   - File for any non-synthetic report — including reports where `status` is `complete`, `needs_human`, or `failed` (workers may legitimately surface discovered work even on `needs_human` exits).
   - Synthetic reports always have `discovered_work: []` per `worker-report-v1.md` §4 — moot but explicit.
   - Default placement: orphan + `discovered-from` edge to the source bead. Workers do NOT decide placement — the orchestrator does (sibling-test gate is the orchestrator's call).

8. **Apply status outcomes** to the step-bead.
   - On the **direct dispatch path** (no `ralf:required`): the orchestrator processed exactly one report. Apply outcomes:
     - `complete` + `gate: pass` → close step-bead with summary, exit.
     - `complete` + `gate: fail | partial` → stamp `human` label, append gate-fail evidence to step-bead notes, close step-bead with summary, exit.
     - `needs_human` → stamp `human` label, append escalations, close step-bead with summary, exit.
     - `failed` (real or synthesized) → stamp `human` label, append failure detail, close step-bead with summary, exit.
   - On the **orchestration (RALF) path**: ralf-implement (or ralf-review) returns an aggregate verdict (final report, final derived gate, foreign-eyes status) when convergence is reached or max-cycles is exhausted. Apply the same outcome logic above to the step-bead using the aggregate verdict, then close the step-bead and exit. ralf-implement is responsible for stamping per-iteration `worker-audit-*` labels during the loop; implement-bead is responsible for closing the step-bead at the end.

### Audit-label scope

Audit labels (`worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]`) apply ONLY to dispatches of the three named worker agents (`tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser`). Review / fresh-eyes / adversarial subagents dispatched by orchestration skills are out-of-band — they do NOT receive audit labels under the `worker-audit-` namespace. Their audit trail (if any) is the orchestration skill's responsibility, not implement-bead's.

### Strip from current SKILL.md

- All mode-detection by title-sniffing of step-bead description.
- Driver-coupling narrative.
- "When to Use" framing paragraphs.
- Hand-off-rules narrative paragraphs.
- "Report back what you did" subagent-dispatch boilerplate (the new contract is `worker-report-v1` YAML at an absolute path).
- All references to `bead-implementor` (its dispatch is replaced by per-dispatch primitive routing to the new workers).

### Length budgets

- SKILL.md: advisory ≤120 lines, hard ceiling 150. Implementor may lean on `worker-report-v1.md` §-anchored references rather than restating spec content inline to fit the budget.
- Slash command: advisory ≤30 lines.

### Structural anti-drift criteria (the real gate)

These are prose-discipline checks; verified by code review (see `[h]`-tagged AC), not mechanical witness:

- No mode-detection by title-sniffing or prose-parsing of step-bead description.
- No driver-coupling narrative.
- No narrative paragraphs (imperative steps and bullets only).
- No RALF-loop ownership in the skill body (no iteration count / convergence predicate / max-iter check IN this skill — those live in orchestration skills).
- No stage-branching control flow at the skill-body level (no per-stage `if/elif` blocks). The only metadata-driven branch is `ralf:required` → orchestration skill vs direct dispatch.

### `src/plugins/beads/.claude/commands/implement-bead.md` — light edits

- Keep as thin wrapper.
- Update notes section to document: workers dispatched via Agent tool from the top-level session (not `claude -p` re-entry); orchestration loops owned by `ralf-*` skills (not by this skill); per-stage iteration tracking persisted via step-bead notes by orchestration skills.

## Conformance fixes (from prior brainstorm investigation)

These align 19.3 with the canonical contracts established in 19.1/19.2, fixing stale text in earlier drafts:

- Path convention: `<repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/...` (NOT `worker-reports/`).
- Audit label prefix: `worker-audit-` (NOT `worker-report-`).
- Workers dispatched via Agent tool with `subagent_type` (NOT `claude -p` re-entry).
- Synthesis on crash/malformed per `worker-report-v1.md` §4 — synthetic report contains only shared-core fields; per-agent extensions like `root_cause_note` are NOT fabricated.
- Gate roll-up DERIVED from evidence per `worker-report-v1.md` §1.1 — workers do NOT emit `gate_status` (it does not exist in the schema).

## Out of Scope

- Updates to `ralf-implement` and `ralf-review` skills — `7bk.19.4`.
- Updates to formula TOMLs (`implement-feature.formula.toml`, `fix-bug.formula.toml`) — `7bk.19.4`.
- Architecture spec catalog updates in `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` — `7bk.19.5`.
- Smoke / tracer-bullet for end-to-end dispatch flow — `7bk.19.5`.
- Deletion of `bead-implementor.md` — `7bk.19.5`.
- Future `inline-execution` directive (orchestrator does work without subagent delegation) — separate follow-up bead.
- Adding new explicit dispatch labels (`agent:*`, `skill:*`) — current `ralf:required` + `ralf:cycles=N` is sufficient for 19.3 scope.
- Stamping a `stage:<role>` label at pour time for more robust upstream lookup — out of scope; current title-prefix lookup is canonical for 19.3.
- Shell driver changes — driver invocation contract (per-stage `claude -p` with stable session-id and stage-specific cwd) is unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria

Mechanical (`[m]`) — verifiable by file-existence, grep, line-count, or specific string presence:

- [m] `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md` exists.
- [m] SKILL.md uses path convention `<repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/<agent-name>[-iter<N>].yaml`. No occurrences of `worker-reports/` remain.
- [m] SKILL.md uses audit-label prefix `worker-audit-`. No occurrences of `worker-report-` (as a label-name prefix) remain.
- [m] SKILL.md instructs dispatching workers via `Agent({ subagent_type: <agent-name>, prompt: ... })` syntax. No `claude -p` re-entry instructions for worker dispatch remain.
- [m] SKILL.md documents synthesis on crash and malformed report cases per `worker-report-v1.md` §4.
- [m] SKILL.md documents gate roll-up derivation from evidence blocks per `worker-report-v1.md` §1.1.
- [m] SKILL.md documents the per-agent runtime-required field check (specifically: `root_cause_note` non-empty for `bug-diagnoser` triggers synthesis when missing or empty).
- [m] SKILL.md documents the stage→agent mapping for all five (stage, mode) combinations: `(red-tests, implement-feature)`, `(red-tests, fix-bug)`, `(green-loop, implement-feature)`, `(green-loop, fix-bug)`, `(diagnose, fix-bug)`.
- [m] SKILL.md's metadata-driven dispatch branch checks for `ralf:required` label and routes to `ralf-implement` or `ralf-review`; otherwise direct dispatch.
- [m] SKILL.md documents I3 sibling-test placement for `discovered_work` with default = orphan + `discovered-from` edge to source bead.
- [m] SKILL.md documents discovered_work filing ordering: BEFORE applying status outcomes.
- [m] SKILL.md documents the audit-label scope (worker-only; review/fresh-eyes subagents are out-of-band).
- [m] SKILL.md documents the upstream report retrieval procedure for `(red-tests, fix-bug)` and `(green-loop, fix-bug)` (lookup, parse, propagate to task-spec; failure-mode = stamp human).
- [m] SKILL.md documents the invocation-contexts model (Agent dispatch from top-level session only; orchestration skills run as in-session code).
- [m] SKILL.md is ≤150 lines (hard ceiling).
- [m] `src/plugins/beads/.claude/commands/implement-bead.md` exists and is updated to reflect Agent-tool dispatch and orchestration-skill loop ownership; ≤30 lines advisory (≤40 hard ceiling).
- [m] No `bead-implementor` references remain in either the rewritten SKILL.md or the slash command.
- [m] Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

Human-review (`[h]`) — prose-discipline / structural review, not mechanically witnessable:

- [h] SKILL.md body is imperative-only (no narrative paragraphs).
- [h] SKILL.md does NOT contain stage-branching control flow at the skill-body level (no per-stage `if/elif` blocks beyond the single `ralf:required` metadata branch).
- [h] SKILL.md does NOT own iteration counting (no iteration increment, no max-iter check, no convergence predicate in the skill body).
- [h] Code review confirms imperative discipline and pass against the structural anti-drift criteria listed in §Scope.
acceptance_criteria: src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md fully rewritten as imperative-only metadata-driven dispatcher with no stage-branching, no RALF-loop ownership, and no driver-coupling narrative; documents the per-dispatch primitive as a reusable block (compute path, build task-spec, dispatch via Agent tool with subagent_type, synthesize on crash/malformed per worker-report-v1.md §4, stamp worker-audit-* label, derive gate roll-up per §1.1, return to caller); uses canonical .beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/<agent>[-iter<N>].yaml path convention; documents stage->agent map covering all five (stage,mode) combos; routes to ralf-implement/ralf-review when ralf:required label present, else direct dispatch; applies I3 sibling-test placement with discovered-from default; applies complete/needs_human/failed outcomes; ≤150 line hard ceiling. src/plugins/beads/.claude/commands/implement-bead.md updated to reflect Agent-tool dispatch and orchestration-skill loop ownership; ≤30 line advisory. No bead-implementor references remain. Build passes, typecheck passes, tests pass.

VERIFY-AC RESULT: verify-7bk.19.3.sh exit 0; 37/37 assertions PASS. SKILL.md=90 lines (≤150), slash-cmd=23 lines (≤40); bead-implementor refs=0. [h] ACs deferred to quality-reviewer in completion gate.
PR created: https://github.com/scotthamilton77/agents-config/pull/36. Verify-AC: 37/37 PASS. Quality-review: APPROVE-WITH-MINOR; M1/M2/N3 addressed. Simplify: no changes warranted.
ESCALATE: PR #36 comment 3193307037 — outcome rule complete+fail conflicts with red-tests semantics where fail-gate is expected.

Per docs/specs/tdd-red-report-v1.md §Stage-specific expectations: 'Expected derived gate roll-up: fail — specifically, evidence.tests.failing > 0. The orchestrator does not advance the pipeline on a pass derivation from a red-team dispatch; that outcome means the new tests didn't actually test anything new.'

The current SKILL.md §8 direct-dispatch outcome rule treats complete+fail uniformly as a human-escalation:
  - complete + gate fail or partial → stamp human, append gate-fail evidence, close

This is correct for green-loop (where gate=fail means tests genuinely failed), but INCORRECT for red-tests (where gate=fail is the success signal — the worker proved the new tests fail against current production code).

For tdd-green-report-v1 the expected gate roll-up is pass (per worker-report-v1.md §1.3 'red-team expects fail, green-team expects pass'). So the outcome interpretation depends on stage.

Resolution options (defer to user):

(a) Stage-aware outcome rules — special-case red-tests to invert the success criterion (complete+fail = success, complete+pass = needs_human). Violates [h] anti-drift principle (orchestrator generic over stages).

(b) Per-agent gate-roll-up redefinition — invert the derivation for tdd-red-team so its 'success-derived gate' = pass when evidence.tests.failing > 0. Requires worker-report-v1 spec change.

(c) Workers themselves emit a stage-aware status that already accounts for expected-gate inversion — e.g. tdd-red-team emits status=complete only when its tests fail as intended, and the orchestrator's 'complete + gate=fail' rule is left intact, BUT the orchestrator's interpretation of 'complete = success-conditioned-on-stage' must be made explicit in SKILL.md §8.

Defer to user / 7bk.19.5 (architecture spec wiring stage).
close_reason: PR #46 (94468c4) merged. Stage-blind §8 outcomes implemented per option (c); §9 override removed; three per-agent specs tightened; verify-implement-bead.sh AC18 rewritten. Quality gate 40/40 PASS. Copilot: LGTM.

title: Create three worker agents: tdd-red-team, tdd-green-team, bug-diagnoser
description:
## Purpose

Replace the single bead-aware mega-agent (`bead-implementor`) with three focused, bead-agnostic worker agents. Each is a pure task function: receives a task spec, does the work, writes a `worker-report-v1` YAML file, exits.

The four canonical report contracts (delivered in 7bk.19.1) are the single source of truth for each agent's output shape:

- `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md` — shared core
- `docs/specs/tdd-red-report-v1.md` — `tdd-red-team` extension
- `docs/specs/tdd-green-report-v1.md` — `tdd-green-team` extension
- `docs/specs/bug-diagnoser-report-v1.md` — `bug-diagnoser` extension (adds required `root_cause_note`)

Each agent body MUST cite its per-agent spec doc by **repo-root-relative path** (e.g., `docs/specs/tdd-red-report-v1.md`) so the worker has exactly one concrete schema to follow.

## Scope

Create three new files under `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/`:

### Common across all three

- **Frontmatter:**
  - `model: opus` — rationale: these agents perform open-ended reasoning under contract constraints (TDD-red authoring meaningful failing tests, bug-diagnoser doing root-cause traversal, tdd-green making minimum-viable production changes). Cost is acceptable because dispatches are infrequent (per epic 7bk.19, formula step owns the loop and bounds iterations). Re-tuning to a cheaper model can be filed as a separate optimization bead.
  - `effort: high` (per epic 7bk.19 Q3, flat across all three)
  - `tools: Read, Edit, Write, Grep, Glob, Bash` (least-privilege; mirrors current `bead-implementor`)
  - `skills:` — agent-specific (see per-agent sections)
  - `description:` — orchestrator-facing metadata. MAY reference the bead pipeline as the calling context (read by the dispatcher, not the worker), but MUST NOT contain imperatives directed at the worker itself.
- **Body discipline:**
  - Imperative role definition only — short bullet/heading sections, not paragraphs of narrative
  - Body length advisory: ≤ 120 lines (prose); ≤ 200 lines hard ceiling. The 19.3 orchestrator skill's ≤ 80 advisory is tighter; these agent files carry contract-clauses too, so a small headroom is allowed.
  - ZERO `bd <subcommand>` shell invocations anywhere in the body
  - Body MUST NOT instruct the worker to read, write, append-notes-to, or interpret bead state
  - Body MAY reference inputs the orchestrator provides (`worktree path`, `target report path`, `iteration counter`, `root_cause_note`, etc.); body MUST NOT reference `step-bead-id`, `source-bead-id`, or any bead identifier as a thing the worker should parse or use
  - Path examples in the body MUST use `<step-bead-id>` placeholder syntax (literal angle-bracketed token), NOT a real bead id — keeps the worker from inferring "I'm in the bead pipeline" from its system prompt
- **Output contract — file-based, sandbox-safe:**
  - Path convention (canonical, defined in `worker-report-v1.md` §2 and superseding the parent epic 7bk.19 Q1 text, which still says the older `worker-reports/` name): `<repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/<agent-name>[-iter<N>].yaml`
  - The orchestrator computes and passes the FULLY-RESOLVED absolute path to the worker. The worker does NOT construct the path itself.
  - The worker MUST use the `Write` tool with the absolute path. NOT Bash redirection (`echo > path`, `tee path`, here-docs). Body MUST include a one-sentence rationale explaining WHY (paths live outside the worktree; `claude -p` subagents can hit CWD/sandbox constraints that block Bash redirection there; the `Write` tool resolves through Claude Code's sandbox-aware filesystem layer). Without the rationale, an LLM-worker may rationalize around the rule under load. The 7bk.19.5 smoke includes a tracer-bullet that proves this contract end-to-end.
- **Schema discipline (shared invariants):**
  - Workers MUST NOT emit a `gate_status` field — it does not exist in `worker-report-v1`. The orchestrator derives `pass | fail | partial | n/a` from the evidence blocks (`worker-report-v1.md` §1.1).
  - Workers MUST NOT decide `discovered_work` placement (`parent_hint`, `relation`, etc.). Schema forbids those fields; the orchestrator applies the I3 sibling test.
  - `commits` are full 40-char SHAs only.
  - `iteration` is NOT a field of `worker-report-v1`. The orchestrator encodes iteration in the file path and audit label.

### tdd-red-team.md

- **Purpose:** Given a worktree and target failing-tests context, write failing tests that capture intended-but-unimplemented behavior.
- **Skills:** `superpowers:test-driven-development`, `superpowers:writing-unit-tests`, `superpowers:testing-anti-patterns`, `superpowers:using-git-worktrees`
- **Spec reference (cited in body):** `docs/specs/tdd-red-report-v1.md` (repo-root-relative)
- **Input contract — TWO MODES, documented as two distinct subsections in the body** (e.g., headed `### Input mode: implement-feature` and `### Input mode: fix-bug`), each enumerating the inputs the worker should expect:
  - **`implement-feature` mode (multi-AC):** worktree path; AC bullets; project test-runner command; absolute target report path
  - **`fix-bug` mode (single regression test):** worktree path; `root_cause_note` (from prior `bug-diagnoser` dispatch); `reproduction_steps` (optional); project test-runner command; absolute target report path
- **Stage-specific obligations (from `tdd-red-report-v1.md`):**
  - Commits MUST contain ONLY test-file additions/modifications. Production-code changes are a contract violation.
  - The agent MUST run the test command before committing and confirm tests FAIL (`evidence.tests.failing > 0`).
  - On unexpected `pass` derivation (new tests didn't actually test anything new), set `escalations[].reason = "red-tests-passed-unexpectedly"` and `status: needs_human`.
  - `evidence.tests.passing` and `evidence.tests.failing` are required whenever the test command runs.

### tdd-green-team.md

- **Purpose:** Given a worktree and a failing-test list, implement the minimum production code to make them pass without breaking existing tests.
- **Skills:** `superpowers:test-driven-development`, `superpowers:verification-before-completion`, `superpowers:testing-anti-patterns`, `superpowers:using-git-worktrees`
  - Rationale for `testing-anti-patterns`: green-team writes production code that satisfies tests; without this skill the agent may introduce test-only methods or other shortcuts that pass tests but degrade design.
- **Spec reference (cited in body):** `docs/specs/tdd-green-report-v1.md` (repo-root-relative)
- **Input contract:**
  - worktree path
  - failing test list (or pointer to the red-team commit)
  - iteration number (RALF-IT context for the agent's own awareness — NOT recorded in YAML body)
  - `root_cause_note`: **absent in `implement-feature` dispatches; REQUIRED in `fix-bug` dispatches** (per `tdd-green-report-v1.md` line 52)
  - project test-runner command
  - absolute target report path
- **Stage-specific obligations (from `tdd-green-report-v1.md`):**
  - The iteration counter is INPUT context only. Workers MUST NOT include `iteration` inside the YAML body — the field does not exist.
  - Run the FULL test command as part of verification before commit. The orchestrator does not re-run tests post-dispatch.
  - Expected derived gate roll-up: `pass` (every present evidence block has `exit_code == 0` AND `skipped == false`).
  - When the test runner exits non-zero before emitting parseable counts: set `passing: null, failing: null` and capture stderr in `escalations` with `reason: "tests-runner-unparseable"`.

### bug-diagnoser.md

- **Purpose:** Given a worktree and bug description, reproduce and root-cause the bug; emit a written root-cause analysis. NO production-code changes.
- **Skills:** `superpowers:systematic-debugging`, `superpowers:root-cause-tracing`, `superpowers:using-git-worktrees`
- **Spec reference (cited in body):** `docs/specs/bug-diagnoser-report-v1.md` (repo-root-relative)
- **Input contract:**
  - worktree path
  - bug description (passed by orchestrator from bead context)
  - reproduction steps (optional)
  - failure evidence: logs, failing-test names (passed by orchestrator)
  - absolute target report path
- **Stage-specific obligations (from `bug-diagnoser-report-v1.md`):**
  - `root_cause_note` is REQUIRED, non-empty free text. Empty/missing triggers a `status: failed` synthesis + escalation by the orchestrator (see `worker-report-v1.md` §4.1).
  - `commits` MUST be `[]`. Diagnoser does NOT commit. Non-empty `commits` is a contract violation.
  - `evidence` is typically `{}`. If tests were run to confirm the bug, the `tests` block reports the FAILING state (`failing > 0`) — informational, not a gate.
  - On inability to identify a root cause: set `status: needs_human` with an escalation describing what is unclear. Do NOT emit a vague `root_cause_note`.
  - `root_cause_note` content minimum: (a) what the underlying defect is, (b) why the surface symptom manifests from that defect, (c) a proposed fix direction.

## Constraints

- All three agents are pure task functions; agent BODIES MUST NOT run `bd` commands or operate on bead state.
- All three write reports to the absolute path provided by the orchestrator using the `Write` tool. NOT Bash redirection.
- Agent body language is imperative. Bullets/headings, not paragraphs.
- Each agent file cites its per-agent spec doc by repo-root-relative path as the YAML schema source of truth.
- Frontmatter `description:` is metadata for the orchestrator (dispatcher) and MAY reference the bead pipeline contextually. The body, which becomes the worker's system prompt at dispatch, MUST remain bead-agnostic.

## Out of Scope

- Deletion of `bead-implementor.md` (deferred to 7bk.19.5 cleanup).
- Updates to formula TOMLs to dispatch the new agents (deferred to 7bk.19.4).
- Rewrite of `implement-bead/SKILL.md` and `commands/implement-bead.md` (deferred to 7bk.19.3).
- Variable-effort policy (epic 7bk.19 Q3 fixed `effort: high` flat across all three).
- Smoke / tracer-bullet for the Write-tool sandbox contract (deferred to 7bk.19.5).
- Updating parent epic 7bk.19 Q1's stale `worker-reports/` text — deferred to 7bk.19.5 documentation cleanup; this bead notes the supersession but does not edit the epic.
- Scrubbing historical `bead-implementor` references in `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md` and `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` — those are legitimate background references and need exclusion in 19.5's invariant-grep.
- `color:` frontmatter — NOT required by AC, but SUGGESTED: pick a coherent palette for visual pairing in tooling that displays it (e.g., red-leaning, green-leaning, neutral for diagnoser). Implementer's call.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/tdd-red-team.md` exists.
- `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/tdd-green-team.md` exists.
- `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bug-diagnoser.md` exists.
- Each frontmatter has `model: opus`, `effort: high`, and `tools: Read, Edit, Write, Grep, Glob, Bash`.
- Each `skills:` frontmatter list matches its per-agent assignment in §Scope (including `superpowers:using-git-worktrees` on all three; `superpowers:testing-anti-patterns` on tdd-red-team and tdd-green-team).
- Each body contains zero `bd <subcommand>` shell invocations. Verified by review (the AC does not prescribe a brittle grep; the constraint is structural — no `bd` shell commands, no instructions to operate on bead state).
- Each body cites its per-agent spec doc by repo-root-relative path (`docs/specs/tdd-red-report-v1.md`, `docs/specs/tdd-green-report-v1.md`, or `docs/specs/bug-diagnoser-report-v1.md`).
- Each body explicitly instructs the worker to use the `Write` tool with the orchestrator-supplied absolute path AND explicitly forbids Bash redirection AND includes a one-sentence rationale (paths outside worktree; sandbox/CWD constraints).
- Body length: each agent file ≤ 200 lines total; advisory ≤ 120 lines for the body proper.
- Path examples in each body use `<step-bead-id>` placeholder syntax, NOT literal bead identifiers.
- `tdd-red-team` body documents BOTH input modes as two distinct headed subsections enumerating the inputs for each mode.
- `tdd-red-team` body explicitly forbids production-code commits (tests-only).
- `tdd-green-team` body documents that the iteration counter is input context only — NOT recorded in YAML body.
- `tdd-green-team` body documents that `root_cause_note` is absent for `implement-feature` dispatches and REQUIRED for `fix-bug` dispatches.
- `bug-diagnoser` body documents that `root_cause_note` is REQUIRED non-empty, `commits` MUST be `[]`, and `evidence` is typically `{}`.
- Frontmatter `description:` does NOT contain imperatives directed at the worker itself (description is for the dispatcher, not the worker).
- `bead-implementor.md` is NOT yet deleted (handled in 7bk.19.5).
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
acceptance_criteria: Three new agent files exist (tdd-red-team.md, tdd-green-team.md, bug-diagnoser.md) under src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/; each frontmatter is model:opus + effort:high + tools whitelist matching bead-implementor; skills lists match §Scope (including using-git-worktrees on all three, testing-anti-patterns on tdd-red and tdd-green); each body is bead-agnostic (no bd commands, no bead-state operations, ≤200 lines, ≤120 line body advisory), cites its per-agent spec doc by repo-root-relative path, uses Write tool (NOT Bash redirection) with one-sentence rationale; path examples use <step-bead-id> placeholder syntax not literal ids; tdd-red-team has two headed input-mode subsections + tests-only commits rule; tdd-green-team body documents iteration as input-only and root_cause_note as absent-for-implement-feature/required-for-fix-bug; bug-diagnoser documents root_cause_note required non-empty + commits MUST be empty + evidence typically {}; description frontmatter does NOT contain imperatives directed at worker; bead-implementor.md NOT yet deleted; build passes, typecheck passes, tests pass.
