title: [preflight] {{title-slug}}
description:
Validate the bead spec and create the worktree before any implementation work.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: high

## Inputs
Read the bead and its labels:
  bd show {{bead-id}}
  bd label list {{bead-id}}

## Actions

1. Verify `brainstormed` and `implementation-ready` labels are present.
   If either is absent: apply human-flag protocol and exit.

2. Note: formula selection and pour have already happened — this step was
   created by implement-bead pouring implement-feature. There is nothing
   to select or pour here. Proceed directly to project-config.toml validation.

3. Read project-config.toml and verify:
   - `[gates]` section present (or stubs acceptable).
   - Coverage applicability ([coverage].applicable): if explicitly set to `false`,
     coverage does NOT apply to this project (docs-only repos, config repos, etc.).
     Skip the report-location check entirely and proceed to step 4. Record this
     by running:
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "preflight: coverage opt-out (project-config has [coverage].applicable = false) — skipped report-location check"
   - Coverage report location ([coverage].report-location): only checked when
     `[coverage].applicable` is true OR absent (default). If absent OR empty
     string (i.e. `report-location = ""`), apply the human-flag protocol and
     stop this stage:
       bd label add {{bead-id}} human
       bd label add <step-bead-id> human
       bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "preflight: no coverage report location configured in project-config.toml"
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "preflight: no coverage report location configured — molecule parked"
     An empty string is treated the same as absent: explicit opt-out requires
     `[coverage].applicable = false` — a bare empty string is ambiguous and
     must be resolved by the human.
     Do NOT continue to worktree creation. Exit cleanly.
   - [static-analysis] section: if absent or empty, mark quality-sweep skippable.

4. Stamp the molecule→bead lookup label if not already stamped:
   bd label add <mol-id> for-bead-{{bead-id}}

5. Create the worktree:
   Branch name: feat/{{bead-id}}-<slug-from-title>
   Location: <repo-root>/.claude/worktrees/<branch-name>
   Command:
     git worktree add .claude/worktrees/<branch> -b <branch>
   CRITICAL: the formula is already poured (this step was created by the pour).
   If worktree creation fails: squash the molecule and flag-human.

6. Stamp the worktree-path label on the molecule:
   Encoding (apply IN ORDER):
     _ → _u
     / → __
   Then: bd label add <mol-id> worktree-path-<encoded>

7. Claim the bead and walk the parent chain:
   ~/.beads/scripts/bd-claim-walk.sh --bead-id {{bead-id}}

## Definition of Done
Poured molecule + worktree exists + worktree-path-* label stamped on molecule.
OR: human label + structured gap-note + (if pour completed) molecule squashed.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check: does an active molecule exist? Does the worktree path decode to a real
git worktree? If so, skip work already done.
notes: preflight: coverage opt-out (project-config has [coverage].applicable = false) — skipped report-location check
close_reason: Worktree created at .claude/worktrees/feat/agents-config-he9o-impl-dep-health-check-skill-fi; coverage opt-out confirmed; worker-audit initialized; bead claimed in_progress

title: [quality-sweep] {{title-slug}}
description:
Run the full project quality suite (not just changed files).
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skippable when preflight marked it so (no [static-analysis] in project-config.toml,
or empty gates). Also skips when review-level:none is set.

## Order (fixed)
1. Lint auto-fix: [lint-autofix].command from project-config.toml (run FIRST)
2. Build + typecheck + lint (from [gates])
3. Static analysis (from [static-analysis] — semgrep, depcheck, etc.)
4. Tests (from [gates].test)

ALWAYS end with build + tests after any code changes (guarantees verify-ac starts green).

## Coverage

This stage does not separately enforce a coverage threshold; coverage gating,
if any, lives in green-loop's Definition of Done and is conditional on
[coverage].applicable. See docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md §3
(quality-sweep).

## Auto-fix vs flag-human
Auto-fix unless issues betray a design flaw requiring fundamental changes.
Use judgment based on the size of the fix.

## Definition of Done
All gate commands exit zero (or stage skipped per preflight / review-level:none).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates state and runs only what is not already green.

title: implement-feature
description: Per-stage feature pipeline: preflight → red-tests → green-loop → quality-sweep → verify-ac → create-pr → review-cycle → merge-or-handoff

title: [green-loop] {{title-slug}}
description:
Implement the feature using label-driven skill dispatch.
Model: claude-opus-4-7  Effort: medium

## Inputs
- Bead spec from: bd show {{bead-id}}
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule

## Defense-in-depth (re-evaluate red-tests escalate triggers)

Before any implementation work, re-evaluate the red-tests escalate triggers
independently — same logic, same `acceptance_criteria` field counting:

  m_count=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json \
    | jq -r '.[0].acceptance_criteria // ""' \
    | grep -cvE '^([[:space:]]*$|\[h\][[:space:]])')
  # Section-body extraction: range pattern /^\[gates\]/,/^\[/ is a no-op
  # (the [gates] header satisfies BOTH boundaries on the same line).
  test_runner=$(awk '/^\[gates\]/{flag=1;next} /^\[/{flag=0} flag' project-config.toml \
    | awk -F'=' '/^test[[:space:]]*=/{gsub(/[[:space:]"\047]/,"",$2); print $2}')

If m_count == 0 OR test_runner is empty: this molecule should not have
advanced from red-tests. Determine the trigger note text:
  <trigger-note> = "Trigger A: no test runner" |
                    "Trigger B: no [m] AC lines" |
                    "Trigger A: no test runner AND Trigger B: no [m] AC lines"

Then halt with an explicit human-flag protocol (the literal prefix
`green-loop defense-in-depth:` is grep-able for forensic search,
mirroring the `red-tests escalate:` convention):

  bd label add {{bead-id}} human
  bd label add <step-bead-id> human
  bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "green-loop defense-in-depth: red-tests escalate trigger should have fired but molecule advanced. <trigger-note>. Likely manual molecule resume that bypassed reroute."
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "green-loop defense-in-depth: <trigger-note>. Halted before any RALF dispatch."
  bd update <step-bead-id> --status open
  exit 0

Do NOT proceed with implementation. This path is unreachable in normal
operation (the red-tests reroute burns the molecule before green-loop).
It catches manual molecule resumes that bypass red-tests, ensuring the
unsatisfiable-DoD contract never silently propagates downstream.

## Dispatch contract

Check the bead's RALF signal before dispatching:

  bd label list {{bead-id}} --json

- If label `ralf:required` is present:
    invoke `ralf-implement` with the bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, and optional max cycle count from `ralf:cycles=N`

    `ralf-implement` owns only the inner quality loop: iteration,
    independence, adversarial posture, convergence, and structured report output.
    It does not read bead labels or update bead state; this formula step passes
    all needed inputs.

    After it returns, append the structured report summary to the green-loop
    step-bead notes, including cycles run, score, severity counts, and
    foreign-eyes status/degradation for every Codex/Gemini cycle.

    If the score is `FAIL`, or if the score is `PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS` with
    unresolved major concerns, stop this step: label both the source bead and
    the green-loop step bead `human`, append the unresolved blocking/critical/major
    concerns to both notes, and do not advance to `quality-sweep` until the
    caller explicitly decides whether to revise, defer, or accept the residual risk.

- Otherwise:
    invoke `superpowers:test-driven-development` first,
    then the appropriate domain skill for implementation.

Record the dispatch decision for audit:

  bd comments add {{bead-id}} "implement: dispatched <skill-name>"

`bd comments add` is non-destructive; do NOT use `--notes` (replaces notes) — use `--append-notes` for step output. See beads rules §Notes vs Comments.

Persist step output before closing green-loop. Here `<step-bead-id>` is the current green-loop molecule step bead:

  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "IMPLEMENTATION-REPORT: score=<PASS|PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS|FAIL>; cycles=<n>/<max>; severity=blocking:<n>,critical:<n>,major:<n>,minor:<n>; summary=<summary from dispatched skill>"
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "FOREIGN-EYES: cycle-1=<COMPLETED|UNAVAILABLE|TIMED_OUT|QUOTA_EXCEEDED|AUTH_FAILED|NO_OUTPUT|UNUSABLE_OUTPUT|NOT_RUN>; cycle-2=<...>; degraded=<list or none>"

Before closing green-loop, ensure the dispatched implementation path has run
and recorded the mandatory completion gate for its own changes:
`quality-reviewer` → `simplify` → `verify-checklist`. If gate evidence is
missing, run it now and append the evidence to the step-bead notes. If any
gate step fails, label both the source bead and step bead `human`, append the
failure evidence to both notes, and do not advance to `quality-sweep`.

## Coverage

Coverage threshold from project-config.toml [coverage].threshold (default 80%),
overridable per-bead via the `coverage-threshold-<n>` label;
honored only when [coverage].applicable is true or absent. When
[coverage].applicable is explicitly false (docs-only repos, config repos, etc.),
coverage is not a quality gate and the threshold is bypassed. See
docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md §3 (green-loop) for the canonical
contract.

Rules (regardless of dispatched path):
- minimum viable implementation only
- no speculative features
- no over-engineering for hypothetical requirements

## Definition of Done
Required feature behavior implemented with passing quality gates.

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-check labels and dispatch per current bead state at step start.

title: [review-cycle] {{title-slug}}
description:
Drive the PR review loop until all review threads are resolved.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

Skips entirely when review-level:none.

## Preloaded skills
wait-for-pr-comments (chains internally to reply-and-resolve-pr-threads)

## Actions

Invoke wait-for-pr-comments with:
  --mode autonomous --bead-id {{bead-id}}

One iteration = one outbound reply-batch.
Iteration cap: 5 (override: iteration-cap-review-cycle-<n> label).
Early exit when no remaining FIX-class items.

Exit conditions (mutually exclusive):
- review-exit-copilot-only (default when review-exit-human-approvers-<n> absent)
- review-exit-human-approvers-<n>: require N human approvers

Persist after each batch:
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "REVIEW-BATCH:<n>/<MAX>"

If PR is CLOSED on re-entry: flag-human. Do NOT poll a closed PR.

## Definition of Done
Every review thread has a reply. FIXED items resolved on GitHub.
No in-flight FIX work.

title: [merge-or-handoff] {{title-slug}}
description:
Either auto-merge the PR or hand off to the human.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Gate 1 — PR-state clean-check
Conditions (ALL must be true for auto-merge path):
  - Zero open Copilot review threads
  - Zero open human-author review threads
  - All CI checks green
  - 5-minute quiet window with no new comments or review activity
On wait-for-pr-comments timeout: treat clean-check as NOT clean.

## Auto-merge path (auto-mergeable label present AND clean-check clean)
1. Re-run clean-check immediately before merge call.
2. Pour merge-and-cleanup formula (phase: liquid — NOT vapor).
3. Drive merge-and-cleanup:
   - Merge the PR
   - Delete branch (local + remote)
   - Remove worktree (decode from worktree-path-* label)
   - Run parent-chain I2 close-walk
   Note: merge-and-cleanup gate-step (Gate 2) blocks until ALL [h] follow-up
   children are closed with verified-by-human label, then closes the
   merge-{source-id} child after merge + cleanup so the source bead can close.

## Hand-off path (default — auto-mergeable absent OR clean-check dirty)
1. Transition bead to open: bd update {{bead-id}} --status open
2. Stamp labels: bd label add {{bead-id}} merge-ready
                 bd label add {{bead-id}} human
   (merge-ready = why it's on human list; human = surfaces via bd human list)
3. Note: bead is now excluded from bd ready (human label gates it).
   Human runs /merge-and-cleanup when ready.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view --json mergedAt first. If non-null: skip merge, proceed to cleanup.

title: [merge-gate] tier-2-commands-cleanups-f1-f2

title: [Impl] Tier 2: commands/ cleanups (F1 F2 F4 F5 F6 F7)
description:
## Summary

Six Tier-2 audit findings (F1/F2/F4/F5/F6/F7) from the Phase 3 commands/ audit. Two major commands embed full methodology inline instead of delegating to skills; a third hardcodes a plugin dependency and duplicates skill content inline. Fix: create two new skills, reshape all three commands to lean-delegation shape, add availability fallback and correct default path.

## Background / Motivation

Phase 3 audit (SHA af9c1bfc) found commands/ violating the lean-body target (<=80 lines) and the lean-delegation pattern. optimize-my-skill.md (233 lines) and optimize-my-agent.md (107 lines) carry full methodologies inline. refresh-agents-md.md hardcodes a plugin skill with no fallback and duplicates optimize-agents-md principles in two steps. Each creates a secondary maintenance surface that drifts from the canonical skill over time.

## Requirements

F1: Create src/user/.agents/skills/optimize-my-skill/SKILL.md — extract Phase 2-5 methodology and the 'What Makes Skills Effective' reference section. Required sections: Phase 1 discovery, Phase 2 quality criteria (frontmatter checks, folder structure, body content, anti-patterns), Phase 3 propose improvements, Phase 4 suggest testing, Phase 5 confirm/apply, 'What Makes Skills Effective' reference (file structure, frontmatter fields, body structure, key principles). Each Phase section >=10 lines of substantive content; 'What Makes Skills Effective' sub-sections each >=5 lines of substantive content. During extraction, normalize any '5,000 words' threshold to '500 lines' — absorbs Tier-1 F3.
Frontmatter: name=optimize-my-skill, model=sonnet, allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, AskUserQuestion (comma-separated, per optimize-agents-md convention). Description: SKILLS_PRIMER-compliant third-person, >=3 observable triggers, explicit negative trigger: 'Do NOT use for agent persona files or AGENTS.md configuration files'. No XML angle brackets in values; name does not contain 'claude' or 'anthropic'.

F2: Create src/user/.agents/skills/optimize-my-agent/SKILL.md — extract 5-phase optimization workflow, quality rubric, and failure checklist. Required sections: all 5 optimization phases, quality rubric with exactly the 6 AGENTS_PRIMER persona-file quality issues (titles verbatim with exact AGENTS_PRIMER casing and punctuation: 'Over-broad role'; 'No examples in description'; 'Wrong model tier'; 'Body mixes role with task'; 'Bead references in shared agents'; '`skills` lists unused skills'), 7-item failure checklist, phase 4 recommendation structure (the Missing/Weak Area / Current / Recommended Addition / Why template from the source command). Each section >=10 lines of substantive content. Scope: agent persona files (agents/*.md with frontmatter name/description/model/color/tools).
Frontmatter: name=optimize-my-agent, model=sonnet, allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, AskUserQuestion (comma-separated). Description: SKILLS_PRIMER-compliant third-person, >=3 observable triggers, explicit negative trigger: 'Do NOT use for SKILL.md files or AGENTS.md configuration files'. No XML angle brackets; name does not contain 'claude' or 'anthropic'.

F4: optimize-my-skill.md empty-ARGUMENTS default path. 'Has skills' predicate: directory exists AND contains at least one immediate subdirectory (depth 1) containing a SKILL.md file (case-sensitive exact; regular file or symlink to regular file). Fallback order: (1) ~/.claude/skills/ — if fails predicate, (2) .claude/skills/ — if fails predicate, (3) emit 'No skills found. Pass a path: /optimize-my-skill <path>'. Non-existent directory satisfies 'fails predicate'.

F5 (resolved via F2): Replace optimize-my-agent rubric with AGENTS_PRIMER persona-file quality issues verbatim. Delivered by F2 skill AC.

F6: refresh-agents-md.md Step 1 — add a-priori availability check: check whether the dispatching-parallel-agents skill directory exists by probing these paths in order (match any): ~/.claude/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md, ~/.claude/plugins/*/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md. If probe matches: run three discovery subagents simultaneously. If not: run sequentially — (1) Git Analyzer, (2) File Inventory, (3) Directory Discovery. This applies regardless of whether the obra/superpowers plugin is installed — it is NOT a cross-tool concern; the command file is Claude-specific.

F7: refresh-agents-md.md Steps 3b and 3d — remove inline principle enumeration.
- Step 3b: replace instruction block with a direct invocation reference to the optimize-agents-md skill, with explicit instruction to skip scope questions and infer context from the codebase. Must also forward focus_areas value and parent-file context gathered in Step 3a. Semantic requirement: no inline principle enumeration; skill referenced by name; scope-questions skip stated; context-handoff present.
- Step 3d: replace inline validation checklist with reference to the optimize-agents-md skill's Validation Checklist section. Semantic requirement: no inline checklist; section referenced by name.

After F1+F2 skills are created, reshape commands:
- optimize-my-skill.md: parse ARGUMENTS (target: directory or specific skill path), invoke optimize-my-skill skill, report results. Retain command-shape content only. No occurrences of '5,000 words'.
- optimize-my-agent.md: parse ARGUMENTS (target: agent persona file path), invoke optimize-my-agent skill, report results. Scope: agent persona files only.

## Out of Scope

- F3 (5,000-words threshold fix) — absorbed by F1 at extraction time; any open Tier-1 F3 bead can be closed as already-resolved after this PR lands
- F8 (optimize-my-agent.md heading mismatch) — Tier 1; the lean-delegation rewrite replaces the heading body anyway; F8 bead can be closed as already-resolved after this PR lands
- Refactoring optimize-agents-md SKILL.md — F7 references its existing Validation Checklist section as-is
- Migration of installed ~/.claude/commands/ versions — install.sh handles on next run
- Any commands not named in F1/F2/F4/F5/F6/F7

## Design Notes

- Single PR: all six findings are Tier 2, same audit, same category, thematically unified. Expected diff: ~600+ lines added (two new skills), ~400 lines removed (three commands reshaped).
- F5 scope decision: user confirmed optimize-my-agent targets agent persona files (not AGENTS.md config). New skill rubric uses AGENTS_PRIMER quality issues table titles verbatim including casing and backticks.
- F4 path preference: command installs user-wide (~/.claude/commands/) so ~/.claude/skills/ is canonical; .claude/skills/ is project-level fallback. User-level skills take precedence even when project-level skills exist; pass explicit path to target project skills.
- F4 predicate: case-sensitive 'SKILL.md'; regular file or symlink; depth-1 only.
- F6 probe: check ~/.claude/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md and ~/.claude/plugins/*/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md. Not a cross-tool concern — command is Claude-specific; probe accounts for varying superpowers install locations.
- F6 fallback: sequential order: Git Analyzer, File Inventory, Directory Discovery.
- F7 peer dependency: 'skip the 5 scope questions' relies on optimize-agents-md retaining its numbered five-question structure. Future edits to that skill must update refresh-agents-md.md in lockstep.
- F7 context handoff: Step 3b invocation must forward focus_areas and parent-file context; these context inputs are gathered by Step 3a and must not be silently dropped.
- F3 + F8 absorbed: F1 extraction normalizes '5,000 words' => '500 lines'; F2/F5 command rewrite replaces the heading. Both Tier-1 beads (if tracked) can be closed after this PR.
- allowed-tools: comma-separated format used throughout (matching optimize-agents-md peer); SKILLS_PRIMER documents space-separated but this codebase normalizes on comma form.

## Open Questions (post-ralf-review cycle cap, for implementer judgment)

- F2 phase 4 recommendation structure: 'phase 4 recommendation structure' means the Missing/Weak Area / Current / Recommended Addition / Why template from source command lines 36-46. AC references this template; implementer should preserve all four fields.
- F1 'What Makes Skills Effective' sub-sections: each sub-section should preserve the substantive content from the source command; headers-only is not sufficient.
- F4 edge case: empty-but-existing SKILL.md still satisfies the predicate (file presence, not content validity).
- F7 Step 3a/3b boundary: Step 3a (Gather Context) survives unchanged; only the principle-enumeration block in 3b is replaced; surrounding scaffolding that gathers context or applies focus_areas remains.
acceptance_criteria:
src/user/.agents/skills/optimize-my-skill/SKILL.md exists; ≤500 lines; frontmatter: name=optimize-my-skill, model=sonnet, allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, AskUserQuestion (comma-separated); SKILLS_PRIMER-compliant description ≥3 observable triggers, negative trigger contains 'Do NOT use for agent persona files or AGENTS.md configuration files', no XML angle brackets, name excludes 'claude'/'anthropic'; contains Phase 1 discovery, Phase 2 quality criteria (frontmatter checks, folder structure, body content, anti-patterns), Phase 3 propose improvements, Phase 4 suggest testing, Phase 5 confirm/apply, 'What Makes Skills Effective' reference (file structure, frontmatter fields, body structure, key principles); each Phase section ≥10 lines; each 'What Makes Skills Effective' sub-section ≥5 lines; no occurrences of '5,000 words'
src/user/.agents/skills/optimize-my-agent/SKILL.md exists; ≤500 lines; frontmatter: name=optimize-my-agent, model=sonnet, allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, AskUserQuestion (comma-separated); SKILLS_PRIMER-compliant description ≥3 observable triggers, negative trigger contains 'Do NOT use for SKILL.md files or AGENTS.md configuration files', no XML angle brackets; contains all 5 optimization phases, quality rubric with exactly 6 items matching AGENTS_PRIMER titles verbatim with exact casing (Over-broad role; No examples in description; Wrong model tier; Body mixes role with task; Bead references in shared agents; `skills` lists unused skills), 7-item failure checklist, phase 4 recommendation structure (Missing/Weak Area / Current / Recommended Addition / Why template); each section ≥10 lines; scoped to agent persona files
optimize-my-skill.md command ≤80 lines; invokes optimize-my-skill skill; no occurrences of '5,000 words'; empty ARGUMENTS: probe ~/.claude/skills/ depth-1 SKILL.md subdirs → probe .claude/skills/ same predicate → emit 'No skills found. Pass a path: /optimize-my-skill <path>' if both fail; non-existent directory satisfies fails-predicate
optimize-my-agent.md command ≤80 lines; invokes optimize-my-agent skill; scope: agent persona files only
refresh-agents-md.md Step 1: a-priori probe for dispatching-parallel-agents (matches ~/.claude/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md or ~/.claude/plugins/*/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md); if matches run simultaneously; if not run sequentially — Git Analyzer, File Inventory, Directory Discovery
refresh-agents-md.md Step 3b: no inline principle enumeration; optimize-agents-md skill referenced by name; skip-scope-questions instruction present; focus_areas and parent-file context forwarded (semantic check)
refresh-agents-md.md Step 3d: no inline validation checklist; optimize-agents-md skill Validation Checklist section referenced by name (semantic check)
scripts/install.sh --dry-run exits 0
PR description references F1/F2/F4/F5/F6/F7 and links to docs/audits/phase3/by-category/commands.md and docs/audits/phase3/REMEDIATION_PLAN.md
notes: verify-ac: AC1-7 PASS (all file/content witnesses green); AC8 (install.sh --dry-run) FAIL but pre-existing on main (identical failure on both main and branch — resolve-human-bead collision, filed as agents-config-fnh8); AC9 deferred to create-pr step (PR not yet created)

title: Synthesize skill-creator + writing-skills into native merged skill
description:
Merge the best-of-breed from two overlapping skill-creation skills into a single coherent native skill owned by this repo.

Source skills:
- skill-creator:skill-creator (claude-plugins-official) — full pipeline: draft, eval runs, quantitative benchmarks, description optimizer loop, packaging
- superpowers:writing-skills — TDD discipline (RED-GREEN-REFACTOR for skills), CSO, token efficiency, rationalization-resistance for discipline-enforcing skills

Key conflict to resolve: description content. writing-skills wins — 'NEVER summarize workflow in description' (empirically tested: workflow summary causes Claude to read description as shortcut and skip the skill body). skill-creator's 'be pushy' advice is valid but only for triggering conditions, not process. Synthesis rule: descriptions aggressive about WHEN to trigger, silent about HOW.

Additive from skill-creator (no conflict): eval/benchmark infrastructure, automated description optimizer loop (run_loop.py), workspace iteration directories, packaging (.skill files), environment variants (Claude Code / Claude.ai / Cowork / headless), audience-adaptive communication guidance.

Output: a new native skill (e.g. creating-skills) that is this repo's canonical skill creation methodology, informed by both sources, carrying neither verbatim.

title: Reconcile merge-or-handoff formulas with arch §5.6 hand-off escalation contract
description:
Arch `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §3 (line 299) and §5.6 (line 611) say the `merge-or-handoff` hand-off path creates a SEPARATE escalation bead with title `[Merge gate] <source-title>` and labels `human` + `merge-ready`, plus a `bd dep add <source-id> <handoff-escalation-id>` blocker on the source.

The actual formula implementations (`implement-feature.formula.toml` lines 670-673, `fix-bug.formula.toml` lines 669-672, `docs-only.formula.toml` lines 376-379) instead stamp `merge-ready` + `human` labels on the EXISTING source bead — no separate escalation bead, no title rename, no explicit dep blocker. The formulas' inline comment claims `human` label gates `bd ready`, but the HEP premise (`beads.md` §HEP) explicitly says `human` is a visibility tag, NOT a ready-gate; only open blocking deps gate readiness. Gating in practice still works because the `merge-{source-id}` close-gate child created at `brainstorm-bead.finalize` keeps the parent unready via parent-child semantics, but that mechanism is incidental not architectural.

Decisions needed:
1. Pick a canonical shape: fix the formulas to match spec (create separate `[Merge gate]`-titled escalation bead + dep edge), OR update spec §3/§5.6 to authorize the label-on-source shape the formulas currently produce.
2. Once the canonical shape is settled, simplify `resolve-human-bead` Probe 1 — today it accepts either shape because of this divergence. If the spec-conforming shape becomes mandatory, Probe 1 can drop the explanatory paragraph about the formula impl.
3. Audit `merge-and-cleanup` formula's closure path: spec §5.6 says `/merge-and-cleanup` closes the hand-off escalation bead and the `merge-{source-id}` close-gate child as final actions. Verify both actually happen, regardless of which canonical shape is picked.

Discovered during PR #67 (agents-config-7bk.13) when Codex review caught `resolve-human-bead` Probe 1's title-prefix discriminator never matching real hand-offs.
notes:
Cross-references:
- Arch authority lines: 299, 307-308, 312, 611-612
- Formula divergence: implement-feature.formula.toml:670-673, fix-bug.formula.toml:669-672, docs-only.formula.toml:376-379
- Discovered-from: agents-config-7bk.13 (PR #67)
- Skill-side mitigation already shipped: resolve-human-bead Probe 1 uses label-only discriminator with explanatory paragraph documenting the divergence

title: [merge-gate] sub-design-lifecycle-state-mac
description: Merge gate child for agents-config-fca6.10. Closed only by merge-and-cleanup after merge + branch/worktree cleanup.

title: [Impl] Sub-design: Lifecycle state machine (Section 3) — prgroom CLI
description:
# Section 3 spec summary

Full spec lives in the design doc: docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md, Section 3.

## Coverage delivered

- 3.1 Phase state graph (expanded from Section 2's sketch; every edge labeled with verb+condition trigger)
- 3.2 Phase × verb transition matrix (10 verbs × 6 phases; precondition-fail / no-op / state-change pinned per cell)
- 3.3 `run` aggregate verb algorithm (pseudocode for the long-running blocking loop; interactive variant)
- 3.4 Round-counter semantics (increment only on push that uploaded ≥1 commit; external-push attribution)
- 3.5 Hard-cap behavior (default MaxRounds=3, --max-rounds flag, PRGROOM_MAX_ROUNDS env, .prgroom.toml override)
- 3.6 Failure tier model (extends Section 1's 3-tier precondition gating into runtime: TRANSIENT / TERMINAL_USER / CONTRACT_AUDIT / STATE / LIFECYCLE_CAP; sysexits.h exit codes)
- 3.7 Error-code registry (PRECONDITION_*, RUNTIME_*, CONTRACT_*, STATE_*, LIFECYCLE_*; each with what/why/how)

## Key design decisions during brainstorming

1. `run --autonomous` is **long-running blocking** (locks PR for full lifecycle until terminal-for-CLI phase). Chosen over one-shot-per-cron-tick (Option B) and hybrid (Option C). Rationale: matches Section 1's wording; simpler caller-side semantics; partial-state interleaving is already prevented by per-verb atomic Write so the long lock-hold does not introduce new failure modes.
2. **Round increments on visible-to-reviewer push only** — zero-commit fix cycles count toward quiescence (§4) but not the hard cap. Manual external pushes observed by poll also increment Round to keep the counter honest under mixed-mode operation.
3. **End-of-cycle phase resolution is a priority-ordered cascade** (hard-cap > HumanReviewRequired > unresolved escalations > commits-pushed > quiescence > stay-awaiting-review) rather than an unordered set of rules, so the transition logic is deterministic.
4. **Failure tiers extend the Section 1 precondition pattern** rather than introduce a parallel taxonomy. TRANSIENT → exit non-zero, no phase change, scheduler retries. TERMINAL_USER → human-gated + Sink escalation. CONTRACT_AUDIT → per-item failed disposition, may promote phase.
5. **Error codes use `<CATEGORY>_<SPECIFIC>` structure** with stable identifiers; adding new codes is non-breaking, renaming is breaking.

## Terminal phases

- Graph-terminal: `merged` only
- Terminal-for-CLI (autonomous re-entry requires external signal): `quiesced`, `human-gated`, `merged`
- Re-entry paths: `resolve-escalated` (operator), `poll` observing external push or merge

## Open hand-offs to other sections

- §3.5 hard-cap interaction with §4 quiescence threshold trip — both can fire in the same end-of-cycle resolution; the cascade order in §3.2 disambiguates.
- §3.6 `LIFECYCLE_HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED` is set by an upstream signal whose mechanism is §4's responsibility.
- §3.3 `run` calls `wait` between cycles; `wait`'s timeout + break conditions are §4's responsibility.


## Cycle 1 revisions (applied 2026-05-12)

Cycle 1 was a parallel Codex experiment (V1 fresh-eyes-prompt as-is; V2 same + confidence-loop appendix), both at model gpt-5.5. Reports captured in .beads/worker-audit/agents-config-wisp-do2t/codex-v1.md and codex-v2.md. Consolidated 13 findings (2 BLOCKING / 4 CRITICAL / 6 MAJOR / 1 MINOR after dedupe). All addressed in the doc:

- **Lock-model fix (Findings 1, 2).** Section 2 transactional model rewritten to introduce public locking wrappers + lock-assuming `*Locked` internal lifecycle functions. `run` is the only verb that acquires the lock once and calls multiple `*Locked` internals in sequence; on terminal-for-CLI phase it returns and releases the lock so resolve-escalated can acquire.
- **Diagram fix (Finding 4).** Section 3.1 ASCII diagram redrawn — '≥1 commit pushed' edge now loops back to awaiting-review, not merged. Terminal/non-terminal edge labels disambiguated.
- **rereview integration (Finding 3).** Section 3.3 run algorithm now calls rereviewLocked after a successful push when required bot reviewers need fresh review.
- **Self-heal disambiguation (Finding 5).** Section 3.2 matrix preamble clarifies cells show terminal --no-prework behavior; default self-heal auto-runs missing deterministic prework.
- **Failure tiers wired into pseudocode (Finding 6).** Section 3.3 run pseudocode now has handle_verb_error() that maps each tier to action (continue, propagate, transition to human-gated, set LastError, file Sink event).
- **CONTRACT_FIX_AUDIT_FAILED resolution (Finding 5/V2).** Section 3.2 end-of-cycle priority 3 now explicitly routes any unresolved Disposition.Kind == failed (from contract OR runtime cause OR agent-reported) to human-gated.
- **HumanReviewRequired routing (Finding 12).** Section 2 + Section 3.2 priority 2 reconciled: HumanReviewRequired=true always routes to human-gated, never quiesced.
- **Round off-by-one fix (Finding 7).** Hard-cap check moved pre-push in run pseudocode (§3.3, §3.5). With MaxRounds=3 the visible push history is exactly initial (Round=1) → fix-push #1 (Round=2) → fix-push #2 (Round=3) → cap blocks fix-push #3.
- **CLI-vs-external SHA attribution (Finding 8).** Section 2 schema gains LastPushedHeadSHA field; Section 3.4 specifies the attribution rule (CLI push sets LastPushedHeadSHA in same write as Round++; external push leaves it untouched and pollLocked increments Round).
- **Commit-queue detection (Finding 9).** Section 3.4 specifies that queued commits are derived from git rev-list @{upstream}..HEAD (git is source of truth, no separate state field); crash-after-fix-before-push recovers idempotently via re-invocation.
- **EscalationSink always exists (Finding 11).** Section 3.2 priority 4 rewritten: any unresolved escalated disposition routes to human-gated and files one Sink event per cycle (deduped). The 'no Sink resolution path' branch and 'autonomous-with-no-autodefer' language are deleted as undefined.
- **AC verb count (Finding 10).** AC updated to '11 per-PR lifecycle verbs' with sweep called out as the cross-PR aggregator outside the matrix.
- **STATE_LOCK_STALE clarification (Finding 13).** §3.7 now distinguishes operational notice (NOTICE_LOCK_STALE_CLEANED, advisory only, exit 0, no phase change) from failure-tier STATE_CORRUPT (genuine state corruption, human-gated, exit 78).


## Cycle 2 revisions (applied 2026-05-12)

Cycle 2 was a Claude general-purpose fresh-eyes pass (.beads/worker-audit/agents-config-wisp-do2t/claude-cycle2.md). Found 0 BLOCKING / 6 CRITICAL / 13 MAJOR / 9 MINOR. Cap extended to 4 cycles by operator. Cycle 2 revisions applied; addressing all 6 criticals + 10 of 13 majors + 5 of 9 minors:

- **C1 — Merge edges (§3.1).** Added textual note: every non-terminal phase transitions to merged when poll observes PR-closed; diagram edges omitted for clarity but enumerated in §3.2 matrix.
- **C2 — wait/fixes-pending (§3.2, §3.7).** New code PRECONDITION_WAIT_NOT_APPLICABLE (exit 2 EX_USAGE) replaces the misleading PRECONDITION_NO_WORK label in the matrix cell.
- **C3 — failed disposition gating (§3.2, §3.6).** Unified to 'any item with Disposition.Kind == failed → human-gated, regardless of underlying cause'. §3.6 CONTRACT_AUDIT_FAILED row updated to match.
- **C4 — cap predicate (§3.6, §3.7).** LIFECYCLE_CAP and LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED entries now use the pre-push guard predicate (has_queued_fix_commits(state) AND Round >= MaxRounds), aligned with §3.3/§3.5.
- **C5 — external push + cap silent steal (§3.5).** Rewrote 'External pushes and the cap' bullet to explicitly accept the behavior with rationale; pushLocked emits a stderr warning when approaching MaxRounds.
- **C6 — Round init (§3.4).** Pinned zero-value Round=0; first successful pollLocked observing any HEAD (detected by LastPollSHA == '') sets Round=1 unconditionally; attribution rules apply only after Round>=1.
- **M7/M21/M23 — Escalation functions and dedup (§3.3).** Inline definitions for escalate() and escalate_if_needed(); per-item dedup via Disposition.EscalationFiled flag (already in §2 schema); handle_verb_error and resolver share the same flag.
- **M8 — Lock-hold prose (§3.3).** Tightened: lock held continuously through each cycle's full verb sequence; defer release fires only on runLocked return.

Public Run wrapper applies it to translate tier-tagged errors into sysexits codes (2/65/75/77/78). handle_verb_error returns Disposition enum (Continue/Propagate) instead of raw error.
- **C2 — idle/interactive silent no-op.** Pseudocode + waitLocked contract now emits stderr advisory ('prgroom: nothing to do — PR has no commits yet (phase=idle)') on idle/interactive return.
- **C3 — cap-trip + escalate_if_needed double-emit.** Restructured: ALL Sink emission flows through the single loop-top escalate_if_needed call. Cap-trip and end-of-cycle resolver only WRITE state, setting LifecycleEscalationFiled=false to invite one emit. Added LifecycleEscalationFiled bool to §2 schema.
- **C4 — state shadowing + re-reads.** Each *Locked internal now returns (*PRGroomingState, error). runLocked threads in-memory state through; no disk re-reads. Section 2 lock guarantees single-owner.
- **C5 — waitLocked contract.** Pinned contract surface in §3.3 (signature, behavior, error tiers, cancellation, lock semantics); §4 owns quiescence/timeout details but §3.3 specifies enough surface to be implementation-ready.
- **M7 — direct-invocation vs run-driven matrix.** Added preamble: §3.2 matrix describes public verb behavior on direct invocation; §3.3 describes run-driven flow (bypasses per-verb self-heal).
- **M8 — has_required_reviewers_to_refresh.** Added explicit predicate definitions in §3.4 (has_queued_fix_commits, has_required_reviewers_to_refresh, push_uploaded_commits_this_cycle, new_lifecycle_gate_this_cycle).
- **M9 — PRECONDITION_LOCK_HELD §3.6 footnote.** Added note explaining the precondition-named-but-transient-exit classification rationale (lock contention is short-lived; scheduler retry-on-cadence is the right recovery).
- **M10 — poll × quiesced external-push.** Updated poll row's quiesced and human-gated cells to handle external-push transitions (→ awaiting-review or fixes-pending respectively, with Round++).
- **M11 — *Locked idempotency contract.** Added explicit subsection in §3.3 formalizing the idempotency requirement per internal (cluster/fix/reply/resolve/push), required for safe re-entry per §3.2 priority 7.
- **M12 — First-poll on already-active PR.** Added §3.5 paragraph: cap counts only rounds observed by prgroom; historical out-of-band rounds don't consume budget.
- **M14 — Tier column in registry.** Added per-code tier assignment in §3.7 RUNTIME_* table; added tier-assignment-rule preamble for other tables. All codes now map to a tier.
- **M15 — RUNTIME_PUSH_REJECTED tier.** Pinned to RUNTIME_TERMINAL_USER (exit 77, human-gated). Retry without manual intervention is futile; scheduler shouldn't burn cycles.
- **M16 — Auto-bootstrap policy.** Pseudocode comment: 'Auto-bootstrap is performed regardless of --no-prework; absence of state is a discovery condition, not a precondition failure.'
- **M17 — Partial-write self-correction.** Added paragraph in §3.4: Round increments exactly once via the external-push attribution path on the next pollLocked when state-write fails mid-push.
- **M18 — Per-API-call retry budget.** §3.6 retry policy now explicit: per logical API call, not per verb. A single pollLocked may issue several gh calls; each gets its own 3-attempt budget independently.
- **M19 — STATE_SCHEMA_UNKNOWN exit code.** Now pinned to STATE_CORRUPT tier (exit 78 EX_CONFIG) via the registry tier assignment rules.
- **M21 — bd Sink notes idempotency.** Documented: bd-adapter MUST use label-only emit or content-hash dedup on notes; --append-notes is not idempotent.

Remaining (deferred to follow-up if Cycle 4 surfaces or budget exhausted): minor matrix readability (poll row density), .prgroom.toml cross-reference to §7, zero-commit draft PR semantics, PRECONDITION_NO_AUTH ↔ RUNTIME_GH_TERMINAL bidirectional cross-reference, mid-cycle resolve-escalated operator UX note.

title: Section 3 spec refinements: Cycle 6 follow-up majors + minors
description:
Cycle 6 of the ralf-review on `docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md` Section 3 (closed via parent bead agents-config-fca6.1) scored PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS. The 2 criticals and 2 majors of operational consequence (C1, C2, M1, M4) were patched inline before finalize. This bead tracks the remaining items the reviewer flagged — none are architectural; all are surgical doc/spec refinements that improve implementer clarity but do not block Go transliteration of Section 3.

Source review report: `.beads/worker-audit/agents-config-wisp-do2t/claude-cycle6.md`. Each item below cites that report's location and recommendation verbatim where possible.
acceptance_criteria: All 11 items below addressed (either patched in-spec or explicitly deferred with rationale). Spec re-review (lightweight, single-cycle) confirms no remaining ambiguity in the listed locations. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
notes:
## Items (3 majors + 8 minors)

### M2 — clear-on-success enumeration of realistic carry-over codes
- Location: §3.3 `runLocked` end-of-cycle resolver block (clear-on-success rule)
- Problem: Rule clears state.LastError whenever phase ∉ {human-gated} but only enumerates LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED as the realistic carry-over case
- Recommendation: Add one-line: realistic carry-overs are LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED (after --max-rounds raise) and LIFECYCLE_HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED (after upstream signal clear); other BlockingErrorCodes keep phase at human-gated and never reach the clear

### M3 — PRECONDITION_NO_AUTH tier classification ambiguity
- Location: §3.7 PRECONDITION_NO_* exception rule + PRECONDITION_NO_AUTH entry
- Problem: PRECONDITION_NO_* exception is stated by prefix but enumerated as NO_ITEMS/NO_CLUSTERS/NO_COMMITS/NO_UNREPLIED/NO_UNRESOLVED/NO_ESCALATIONS. Does PRECONDITION_NO_AUTH match by prefix (exit 0) or by enumeration (exit 2)?
- Recommendation: State explicitly: exception applies only to enumerated codes; NO_AUTH and NO_PR_DETECTED are PRECONDITION_USER_ERROR tier (exit 2). Or rename work-related codes to PRECONDITION_NOWORK_* prefix

### M5 — idle → fixes-pending transition missing from §3.1 phase graph
- Location: §3.1 graph vs §3.2 line 508 (poll-from-idle row)
- Problem: §3.2 allows idle → fixes-pending direct jump; §3.1 graph shows only idle → awaiting-review → fixes-pending
- Recommendation: Add idle → fixes-pending edge to §3.1 graph (preferred), OR remove the direct branch from §3.2 and force canonical idle → awaiting-review → fixes-pending

### m1 — rereview from awaiting-review zero-match behavior under-specified
- Location: §3.2 rereview-from-awaiting-review cell
- Problem: When no reviewers match {not_requested, timeout, declined}, no-op exit 0 or PRECONDITION_NO_*?
- Recommendation: Add one-line: 'no-op exit 0 if no reviewers match'

### m2 — wait direct-invocation return contract
- Location: §3.2 wait-from-awaiting-review cell
- Problem: Does wait return on any phase change or only on quiescence? Matrix says 'sleeps; poll-event may transition' — implies any transition
- Recommendation: Tighten matrix cell or explicitly defer to §4 waitLocked contract

### m3 — escalate_if_needed Sink failure handling unspecified
- Location: §3.3 escalate_if_needed pseudocode
- Problem: If EscalationSink.File errors (stderr write failure, bd-adapter API blip), behavior undefined
- Recommendation: State explicitly: 'Sink failures are swallowed (best-effort emit); dedup flag is NOT set on Sink error → next invocation retries'

### m4 — handle_verb_error default branch state mutation leak
- Location: §3.3 handle_verb_error default case
- Problem: Unknown-tier path returns Propagate without tracker.Write — verb-level state mutations leak (only escalate_if_needed writes on emit-relevant fields)
- Recommendation: Add tracker.Write(state) to default branch, OR document 'unknown tier is programmer error; crash-loud is intentional'

### m5 — waitLocked SIGINT/SIGTERM exit code 75 non-conventional
- Location: §3.3 waitLocked cancellation rule
- Problem: Exit 75 conflates user cancellation with transient-retryable error. Scheduler seeing 75 retries — but user's Ctrl-C means STOP. Risk of cancelled-work resurrection
- Recommendation: Add RUNTIME_CANCELLED tier with exit 128+signum (130 SIGINT / 143 SIGTERM), or document stderr-token disambiguation

### m6 — status verb lock starvation under long-running run --autonomous
- Location: §3.3 lock-hold duration + §2 transactional model
- Problem: While run --autonomous blocks in awaiting-review, prgroom status fails with PRECONDITION_LOCK_HELD (exit 75). UX regression vs current skills
- Recommendation: Carve out read-only path for status that doesn't acquire the lock (worst case: torn read — acceptable for diagnostics)

### m7 — sweep semantics with long-running blocking lock underspecified
- Location: §1 sweep verb definition (line 115)
- Problem: sweep iterates open PRs and invokes run for each. Combined with long-running blocking lock, single sweep could block indefinitely on one awaiting-review PR. Marked 'OPTIONAL MVP if cheap' but serialization is real
- Recommendation: Either explicitly defer sweep semantics to later section, or document serial PR processing, no tracker-wide lock, failures don't abort sweep

### m8 — fixLocked restart-safety guarantee for partial cluster processing
- Location: §3.7 RUNTIME_AGENT_TIMEOUT/RUNTIME_AGENT_UNAVAILABLE + §3.3 pseudocode
- Problem: Transient-tier agent failures abort cycle. Restart-safety of fixLocked on partial cluster processing is implicit (relies on per-item Disposition != nil skip)
- Recommendation: Add one-line explicit guarantee: 'fixLocked is restart-safe; items with Disposition == nil are reprocessed; items with Disposition != nil are skipped'

## Trace
- Discovered by: ralf-review Cycle 6 fresh-eyes pass against §3 (post-patch)
- Discovered from: agents-config-fca6.1 (parent sub-design bead)
- Full report: .beads/worker-audit/agents-config-wisp-do2t/claude-cycle6.md

title: [green-loop] {{title-slug}}
description:
Implement the feature using label-driven skill dispatch.
Model: claude-opus-4-7  Effort: medium

## Inputs
- Bead spec from: bd show {{bead-id}}
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule

## Defense-in-depth (re-evaluate red-tests escalate triggers)

Before any implementation work, re-evaluate the red-tests escalate triggers
independently — same logic, same `acceptance_criteria` field counting:

  m_count=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json \
    | jq -r '.[0].acceptance_criteria // ""' \
    | grep -cvE '^([[:space:]]*$|\[h\][[:space:]])')
  # Section-body extraction: range pattern /^\[gates\]/,/^\[/ is a no-op
  # (the [gates] header satisfies BOTH boundaries on the same line).
  test_runner=$(awk '/^\[gates\]/{flag=1;next} /^\[/{flag=0} flag' project-config.toml \
    | awk -F'=' '/^test[[:space:]]*=/{gsub(/[[:space:]"\047]/,"",$2); print $2}')

If m_count == 0 OR test_runner is empty: this molecule should not have
advanced from red-tests. Determine the trigger note text:
  <trigger-note> = "Trigger A: no test runner" |
                    "Trigger B: no [m] AC lines" |
                    "Trigger A: no test runner AND Trigger B: no [m] AC lines"

Then halt with an explicit human-flag protocol (the literal prefix
`green-loop defense-in-depth:` is grep-able for forensic search,
mirroring the `red-tests escalate:` convention):

  bd label add {{bead-id}} human
  bd label add <step-bead-id> human
  bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "green-loop defense-in-depth: red-tests escalate trigger should have fired but molecule advanced. <trigger-note>. Likely manual molecule resume that bypassed reroute."
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "green-loop defense-in-depth: <trigger-note>. Halted before any RALF dispatch."
  bd update <step-bead-id> --status open
  exit 0

Do NOT proceed with implementation. This path is unreachable in normal
operation (the red-tests reroute burns the molecule before green-loop).
It catches manual molecule resumes that bypass red-tests, ensuring the
unsatisfiable-DoD contract never silently propagates downstream.

## Dispatch contract

Check the bead's RALF signal before dispatching:

  bd label list {{bead-id}} --json

- If label `ralf:required` is present:
    invoke `ralf-implement` with the bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, and optional max cycle count from `ralf:cycles=N`

    `ralf-implement` owns only the inner quality loop: iteration,
    independence, adversarial posture, convergence, and structured report output.
    It does not read bead labels or update bead state; this formula step passes
    all needed inputs.

    After it returns, append the structured report summary to the green-loop
    step-bead notes, including cycles run, score, severity counts, and
    foreign-eyes status/degradation for every Codex/Gemini cycle.

    If the score is `FAIL`, or if the score is `PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS` with
    unresolved major concerns, stop this step: label both the source bead and
    the green-loop step bead `human`, append the unresolved blocking/critical/major
    concerns to both notes, and do not advance to `quality-sweep` until the
    caller explicitly decides whether to revise, defer, or accept the residual risk.

- Otherwise:
    invoke `superpowers:test-driven-development` first,
    then the appropriate domain skill for implementation.

Record the dispatch decision for audit:

  bd comments add {{bead-id}} "implement: dispatched <skill-name>"

`bd comments add` is non-destructive; do NOT use `--notes` (replaces notes) — use `--append-notes` for step output. See beads rules §Notes vs Comments.

Persist step output before closing green-loop. Here `<step-bead-id>` is the current green-loop molecule step bead:

  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "IMPLEMENTATION-REPORT: score=<PASS|PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS|FAIL>; cycles=<n>/<max>; severity=blocking:<n>,critical:<n>,major:<n>,minor:<n>; summary=<summary from dispatched skill>"
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "FOREIGN-EYES: cycle-1=<COMPLETED|UNAVAILABLE|TIMED_OUT|QUOTA_EXCEEDED|AUTH_FAILED|NO_OUTPUT|UNUSABLE_OUTPUT|NOT_RUN>; cycle-2=<...>; degraded=<list or none>"

Before closing green-loop, ensure the dispatched implementation path has run
and recorded the mandatory completion gate for its own changes:
`quality-reviewer` → `simplify` → `verify-checklist`. If gate evidence is
missing, run it now and append the evidence to the step-bead notes. If any
gate step fails, label both the source bead and step bead `human`, append the
failure evidence to both notes, and do not advance to `quality-sweep`.

## Coverage

Coverage threshold from project-config.toml [coverage].threshold (default 80%),
overridable per-bead via the `coverage-threshold-<n>` label;
honored only when [coverage].applicable is true or absent. When
[coverage].applicable is explicitly false (docs-only repos, config repos, etc.),
coverage is not a quality gate and the threshold is bypassed. See
docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md §3 (green-loop) for the canonical
contract.

Rules (regardless of dispatched path):
- minimum viable implementation only
- no speculative features
- no over-engineering for hypothetical requirements

## Definition of Done
Required feature behavior implemented with passing quality gates.

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-check labels and dispatch per current bead state at step start.
notes:
IMPLEMENTATION-REPORT: score=PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS; cycles=2/3; severity=blocking:0,critical:0,major:1,minor:~12; summary=resolve-human-bead skill + HEP rollout shipped. Cycle 1 worker green'd tests; quality-reviewer flagged 2 blocking (B1 broken jq probe shape, B2 start-bead Step 2 bare-stamps human). Cycle 2 worker fixed both blockers; quality-reviewer recheck verdict PASS. Tests: 16 files, 260 assertions, 0 failures, exit 0. title: [create-pr] {{title-slug}}
description:
Create the pull request and persist its URL to the molecule.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch

## Actions

1. Invoke superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch.

2. PR body MUST include:
   - Bead reference: {{bead-id}} and bead title
   - AC (full list with [m]/[h] tags)
   - implementation iteration summary (from green-loop step-bead notes)
   - verify-ac validation report
   - Bug beads filed during verify-ac (if any)
   - Human-required AC follow-up bead references (if any [h] lines)
   - Foreign-eyes status section (list which iterations had degraded foreign-eyes)

3. Stamp pr-url-* label on molecule:
   Encoding: same bijection as worktree-path (though URLs rarely need it).
   bd label add <mol-id> pr-url-<encoded-url>

## PR creation failure
Flag-human with worktree intact. Do NOT tear down the worktree.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view first. If a PR exists for the branch and is OPEN: skip creation,
persist URL idempotently.
If PR exists and is CLOSED: flag-human. Do NOT auto-reopen or create a new PR.

title: [review-cycle] {{title-slug}}
description:
Drive the PR review loop until all review threads are resolved.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

Skips entirely when review-level:none.

## Preloaded skills
wait-for-pr-comments (chains internally to reply-and-resolve-pr-threads)

## Actions

Invoke wait-for-pr-comments with:
  --mode autonomous --bead-id {{bead-id}}

One iteration = one outbound reply-batch.
Iteration cap: 5 (override: iteration-cap-review-cycle-<n> label).
Early exit when no remaining FIX-class items.

Exit conditions (mutually exclusive):
- review-exit-copilot-only (default when review-exit-human-approvers-<n> absent)
- review-exit-human-approvers-<n>: require N human approvers

Persist after each batch:
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "REVIEW-BATCH:<n>/<MAX>"

If PR is CLOSED on re-entry: flag-human. Do NOT poll a closed PR.

## Definition of Done
Every review thread has a reply. FIXED items resolved on GitHub.
No in-flight FIX work.

title: [merge-or-handoff] {{title-slug}}
description:
Either auto-merge the PR or hand off to the human.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Gate 1 — PR-state clean-check
Conditions (ALL must be true for auto-merge path):
  - Zero open Copilot review threads
  - Zero open human-author review threads
  - All CI checks green
  - 5-minute quiet window with no new comments or review activity
On wait-for-pr-comments timeout: treat clean-check as NOT clean.

## Auto-merge path (auto-mergeable label present AND clean-check clean)
1. Re-run clean-check immediately before merge call.
2. Pour merge-and-cleanup formula (phase: liquid — NOT vapor).
3. Drive merge-and-cleanup:
   - Merge the PR
   - Delete branch (local + remote)
   - Remove worktree (decode from worktree-path-* label)
   - Run parent-chain I2 close-walk
   Note: merge-and-cleanup gate-step (Gate 2) blocks until ALL [h] follow-up
   children are closed with verified-by-human label, then closes the
   merge-{source-id} child after merge + cleanup so the source bead can close.

## Hand-off path (default — auto-mergeable absent OR clean-check dirty)
1. Transition bead to open: bd update {{bead-id}} --status open
2. Stamp labels: bd label add {{bead-id}} merge-ready
                 bd label add {{bead-id}} human
   (merge-ready = why it's on human list; human = surfaces via bd human list)
3. Note: bead is now excluded from bd ready (human label gates it).
   Human runs /merge-and-cleanup when ready.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view --json mergedAt first. If non-null: skip merge, proceed to cleanup.

title: [red-tests] {{title-slug}}
description:
Write failing tests (TDD red phase) before any implementation code.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:test-driven-development, writing-unit-tests, testing-anti-patterns

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (read [m]-tagged lines)
- Worktree path: decode worktree-path-* label from molecule

## Decode worktree-path label before any work
  1. Decode: __ → /  then  _u → _
  2. Verify decoded path exists and is a git worktree:
       git -C <path> rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree
  3. Operate from that cwd.

## Actions

### Hard-escalate + auto-reroute (evaluate FIRST, before any reviewer dispatch)

This step has a canonical escalate predicate. Evaluate triggers in this fixed
order at the top of the step:

1. Trigger A — no test runner. If `[gates].test == ""` in project-config.toml,
   the project has no way to run tests:
     red-tests escalate: no test runner ([gates].test=='')

2. Trigger B — no [m] AC lines. If the bead's `acceptance_criteria`
   field (the canonical AC store per spec §4.1, NOT `description`) has
   zero `[m]`-classified lines, there is nothing for tests to assert.
   Apply the canonical line parser (`^\\[(m|h)\\]\\s(.*)$`; untagged lines
   default to `[m]` per §4.1's backwards-compat rule). Counting
   `description` would both false-positive on `[m]` tokens inside notes
   AND false-negative on canonical (non-bullet) AC formats. Pinned
   line-anchored grep -cvE counts every non-blank line that is NOT
   explicitly `[h]`-tagged (i.e. [m] lines plus untagged-defaults-to-[m]):
     m_count=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json \\
       | jq -r '.[0].acceptance_criteria // ""' \\
       | grep -cvE '^([[:space:]]*$|\\[h\\][[:space:]])')
   If m_count == 0:
     red-tests escalate: no [m] AC lines

3. Both triggers fire (no test runner AND no [m] AC lines):
     red-tests escalate: both (no test runner AND no [m] AC lines)

4. Otherwise, proceed with the normal red-tests behavior below.

Conversely: a bead with [m] ACs and a non-empty test runner MUST run
red-tests normally — the escalate triggers are the only way to skip.
There is no per-bead `red-tests:enable` opt-IN or `red-tests:skip`
opt-OUT label. AC tagging + project [gates].test are the only signals.

### Reroute protocol (when any escalate trigger fires)

The bead is misrouted: an `implement-feature` molecule is running on work
that legitimately has no code tests. Auto-reroute to the `docs-only` formula
by cloning the bead — no `human` label, no manual intervention.

1. Determine the trigger note text from the predicate above:
     <trigger-note> = "Trigger A: no test runner" |
                       "Trigger B: no [m] AC lines" |
                       "Trigger A: no test runner AND Trigger B: no [m] AC lines"

2. Read the original bead's parent (if any) to preserve epic membership,
   and its priority (so the merge-gate child created in step 4 inherits it):
       ORIG_PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
       ORIG_PRIORITY=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].priority // "P3"')

3. Create the new bead as a partial copy of the original:
   - Inherit: `title`, `description`, `acceptance_criteria` from {{bead-id}}.
     Compose the `--description` value as a two-LF-separated string:
       "⚠️ Rerouted from `{{bead-id}}` — formula corrected to `docs-only`."
       (blank line)
       <original description>
     Use `printf '%s\\n\\n%s' "<warning>" "<original>"` or the `$'...\\n\\n...'`
     form to ensure the LFs are real newlines, not literal `\\n` characters.
   - Do NOT inherit `notes` (the new bead starts clean; the original's
     notes contain stale design history).
   - Preserve epic linkage: if `ORIG_PARENT` is non-empty, pass
     `--parent "$ORIG_PARENT"` so the rerouted-clone remains under the
     same epic. The `discovered-from` dep edge below records the audit
     trail; the parent edge keeps epic-level reporting intact.
   - Stamp labels on the new bead: `brainstormed`, `formula-docs-only`,
     `implementation-ready`. `brainstormed` is honest here — the original
     bead was already brainstormed and the reroute clones its
     `acceptance_criteria` and `description` verbatim, so the spec carries
     over; only the formula tag changes. Stamping `brainstormed` also
     satisfies `docs-only`'s preflight (which strictly requires
     `brainstormed`) so the rerouted clone runs autonomously instead of
     flag-humaning on entry.
   - Do NOT stamp any `implementation-readied-session-*` label — the new
     bead is intended for a separate run-queue session, not for the
     session that just burned this molecule.
   - Add a discovered-from edge:
       bd dep add <new-bead-id> {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from

4. Create the merge-gate child for the new bead (mirrors brainstorm-bead.finalize §4.3 — without this, merge-and-cleanup gate 2 has nothing to enforce against, and the rerouted bead cannot complete its eventual PR cycle):
       bd create \
         --type task \
         --parent <new-bead-id> \
         --title "[Merge gate] <new-bead-title>" \
         --description "Closes when the merge-and-cleanup formula's merge step completes." \
         --priority "$ORIG_PRIORITY"
       # Capture the returned id and stamp the merge-gate label:
       bd label add <new-merge-gate-id> merge-gate

   `<new-bead-title>` is the title inherited from the original (already set
   on the new bead in step 3). Do NOT label `human` (system-managed gate
   child, not a human-attention item). Do NOT label `implementation-ready`
   (the shell driver ignores it).

5. Re-parent any open `[h]` follow-up children of the original bead to
   the new bead. Per spec §4.3, brainstorm-bead's finalize step files one
   `[h]` follow-up child for every `[h]` AC line. The new bead inherits
   `acceptance_criteria` (which still contains those `[h]` lines) but
   does NOT re-run brainstorm-bead's finalize. Re-parenting the children
   preserves the verify-ac contract on the new bead (verify-ac's "[h]
   bullet has matching follow-up" check still passes):
       for child in $(bd list --parent {{bead-id}} --label human --json \
         | jq -r '.[] | select(.status != "closed") | .id'); do
         bd update "$child" --parent <new-bead-id>
       done
   The original bead's merge-gate child is handled separately in step 8 —
   it is closed, not re-parented. The new bead's merge-gate child was
   already filed in step 4 above.

6. Stamp the original bead with the redirect marker:
       bd label add {{bead-id}} REROUTED-TO:<new-bead-id>

7. Append a note to the original bead:
       bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "Rerouted to <new-bead-id>. <trigger-note>. New bead created with formula-docs-only and implementation-ready. Run-queue will pick it up in a separate session."

8. Close any merge-gate child of the original bead (so the original can
   close cleanly). Iterate over all matches in case duplicates exist:
       bd list --parent {{bead-id}} --label merge-gate --json \
         | jq -r '.[] | select(.status != "closed") | .id' \
         | while read -r merge_child; do
             bd close "$merge_child" --reason "Parent bead rerouted."
           done

9. Close the original bead:
       bd close {{bead-id}} --reason "Rerouted to <new-bead-id> (<trigger-note>)."

10. Append a note to the current step-bead (forensic; persists in molecule
   audit even after burn). The literal prefix `red-tests escalate: ` is
   grep-able for verify-ac groundedness and for green-loop's
   defense-in-depth check:
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "red-tests escalate: <trigger-note>. Cloned to <new-bead-id> with formula-docs-only."

11. Burn the current molecule:
       bd mol burn <mol-id> --force

After burn, this stage and the entire molecule cease execution. Run-queue
in a separate session will pick up <new-bead-id> organically and pour the
`docs-only` formula on it. No human action required.

### Normal flow (when no escalate trigger fires)

1. Dispatch tdd-red-team (opus, high effort) in multi-AC mode to write tests:
   - Dispatch with: worktree path, AC bullets (all [m]-tagged lines),
     step-bead ID, project test-runner command, absolute report path,
     and the explicit input mode "multi-AC mode" (one or more failing
     tests per [m]-tagged AC bullet — see tdd-red-team.md for the
     multi-AC vs single-regression mode contract).
   - Tests must cover: happy path, edge cases, error paths.
   - Do NOT mock what has not been verified.
   - Do NOT write test-only methods in production code.
   - The worker emits a tdd-red-report-v1 YAML to the report path; the
     orchestrator parses it after exit.

2. Run the Claude reviewer (test-review skill) on the written tests.
   When review-level:none is set: skip test reviewers (tests still written).

3. Run Codex adversarial review of the tests (gpt-5.5 default).
   Model from: project-config.toml [foreign-cli].codex_red_tests_model
   When review-level:none: skip.
   Subject to foreign-eyes degradation tracking (FOREIGN-EYES-ITER-<n>).

4. Iteration cap: 2 (override: iteration-cap-red-tests-<n> label).
   At cap with reviewer disagreement: write disagreement note to step-bead notes.
   Escalate human ONLY if BOTH reviewers agree on a critical/major design flaw.

5. Commit failing tests to the feature branch.

## Definition of Done
Tests committed to feature branch; both reviewers approve "shippable" state
(or iteration cap reached with disagreement note recorded).
OR step rerouted per the canonical escalate predicate above: original bead
closed with `REROUTED-TO:<new-id>`, new bead created with
`formula-docs-only` + `implementation-ready`, current molecule burned, and
`red-tests escalate: ...` note appended to step-bead notes.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check filesystem (committed tests) and step-bead notes. Continue from prior state.
close_reason: Red phase complete: 16 AC buckets, 71 failing assertions, test-only commit d6568bf

title: [quality-sweep] {{title-slug}}
description:
Run the full project quality suite (not just changed files).
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skippable when preflight marked it so (no [static-analysis] in project-config.toml,
or empty gates). Also skips when review-level:none is set.

## Order (fixed)
1. Lint auto-fix: [lint-autofix].command from project-config.toml (run FIRST)
2. Build + typecheck + lint (from [gates])
3. Static analysis (from [static-analysis] — semgrep, depcheck, etc.)
4. Tests (from [gates].test)

ALWAYS end with build + tests after any code changes (guarantees verify-ac starts green).

## Coverage

This stage does not separately enforce a coverage threshold; coverage gating,
if any, lives in green-loop's Definition of Done and is conditional on
[coverage].applicable. See docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md §3
(quality-sweep).

## Auto-fix vs flag-human
Auto-fix unless issues betray a design flaw requiring fundamental changes.
Use judgment based on the size of the fix.

## Definition of Done
All gate commands exit zero (or stage skipped per preflight / review-level:none).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates state and runs only what is not already green.

title: [preflight] {{title-slug}}
description:
Validate the bead spec and create the worktree before any implementation work.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: high

## Inputs
Read the bead and its labels:
  bd show {{bead-id}}
  bd label list {{bead-id}}

## Actions

1. Verify `brainstormed` and `implementation-ready` labels are present.
   If either is absent: apply human-flag protocol and exit.

2. Note: formula selection and pour have already happened — this step was
   created by implement-bead pouring implement-feature. There is nothing
   to select or pour here. Proceed directly to project-config.toml validation.

3. Read project-config.toml and verify:
   - `[gates]` section present (or stubs acceptable).
   - Coverage applicability ([coverage].applicable): if explicitly set to `false`,
     coverage does NOT apply to this project (docs-only repos, config repos, etc.).
     Skip the report-location check entirely and proceed to step 4. Record this
     by running:
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "preflight: coverage opt-out (project-config has [coverage].applicable = false) — skipped report-location check"
   - Coverage report location ([coverage].report-location): only checked when
     `[coverage].applicable` is true OR absent (default). If absent OR empty
     string (i.e. `report-location = ""`), apply the human-flag protocol and
     stop this stage:
       bd label add {{bead-id}} human
       bd label add <step-bead-id> human
       bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "preflight: no coverage report location configured in project-config.toml"
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "preflight: no coverage report location configured — molecule parked"
     An empty string is treated the same as absent: explicit opt-out requires
     `[coverage].applicable = false` — a bare empty string is ambiguous and
     must be resolved by the human.
     Do NOT continue to worktree creation. Exit cleanly.
   - [static-analysis] section: if absent or empty, mark quality-sweep skippable.

4. Stamp the molecule→bead lookup label if not already stamped:
   bd label add <mol-id> for-bead-{{bead-id}}

5. Create the worktree:
   Branch name: feat/{{bead-id}}-<slug-from-title>
   Location: <repo-root>/.claude/worktrees/<branch-name>
   Command:
     git worktree add .claude/worktrees/<branch> -b <branch>
   CRITICAL: the formula is already poured (this step was created by the pour).
   If worktree creation fails: squash the molecule and flag-human.

6. Stamp the worktree-path label on the molecule:
   Encoding (apply IN ORDER):
     _ → _u
     / → __
   Then: bd label add <mol-id> worktree-path-<encoded>

7. Claim the bead and walk the parent chain:
   ~/.beads/scripts/bd-claim-walk.sh --bead-id {{bead-id}}

## Definition of Done
Poured molecule + worktree exists + worktree-path-* label stamped on molecule.
OR: human label + structured gap-note + (if pour completed) molecule squashed.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check: does an active molecule exist? Does the worktree path decode to a real
git worktree? If so, skip work already done.
notes: preflight: coverage opt-out (project-config has [coverage].applicable = false) — skipped report-location check. Static-analysis absent — quality-sweep skippable.

## Definition of Done
Every [m] AC bullet has explicit witness evidence.
Human-review bullets have follow-up beads filed (confirm presence).

Re-run re-evaluates and continues from prior pass.

title: Decision: run --autonomous is long-running blocking, not one-shot-per-cron-tick
notes:
During fca6.1 brainstorm, three options were considered for how run --autonomous handles the gap between push-fixes and next reviewer engagement: (A) long-running blocking loop, (B) one-shot per invocation with cron driving cadence, (C) hybrid (one cycle + short wait, then exit).

Section 1 wording ('iteration until quiescent or hard cap') implied A; Section 2's 'Recovery = re-invoke' principle and lock-per-PR semantics implied B/C. The tension was real and required a human decision.

User chose A. The CLI holds the PR lock for the entire run invocation, including internal wait() between cycles. Section 2's per-verb atomic state Write means partial-state interleaving is impossible even with a long lock-hold, so option A does not introduce new failure modes beyond what B/C would have. Concurrent invocations on the same PR exit immediately with PRECONDITION_LOCK_HELD.

Implication for §3.3 algorithm: the run loop calls wait() internally between cycles, where wait's break conditions are owned by §4 (quiescence model). Implication for crash recovery: unchanged from Section 2's transactional model — OS releases the file-adapter lock on process death; next invocation resumes from last successful Write.
close_reason: Resolved in spec/work for agents-config-fca6.1

title: run --autonomous is long-running blocking, not one-shot-per-cron-tick
[Impl] prgroom CLI foundation — scaffold + core infrastructure
description:
Implement the prgroom CLI's foundational scaffolding per the design doc at `docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md`.

## Brainstorm source

Produced from agents-config-fca6.8 (foundation brainstorm closure). See that bead and the design doc for locked decisions.

## In scope

title: Sub-design: PR memory management (Section 8) — prgroom CLI
description:

What this bead must produce:

title: Sub-design: Quiescence model (Section 4) — prgroom CLI
description:
Flesh out Section 4 (Quiescence model) of the prgroom design doc at `docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md`.

Coverage required:
- Probability function inputs: time-since-last-update, required-reviewer statuses (every `Required=true` reviewer must be in `review_found` or `declined` for quiescence), open-thread count, CI state, items-with-no-Disposition count
- Threshold parameters and how they're set (flags? config file? per-repo `.prgroom.toml`? heuristics bead?)
- `wait` verb semantics: how long to sleep, what events break the wait
- `human-review-required` upstream signal: how marked (brainstorm? hand-flagged label?), how the CLI reads it, override semantics
- Hard cap parallel to current `wait-for-pr-comments` round-3 cap
- Auto-merge eligibility check signals (handed off to future merge-gate / `td39`; outside MVP)

This section is core to the design's "approval as probability" framing.
acceptance_criteria: Section 4 of docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md fleshed; probability function specified concretely enough to be implementation-ready; thresholds and config decisions made.
