title: Split model tier: orchestrator at sonnet[1m]/medium, fix subagents at opus[1m]/high via explicit dispatch
notes: PR-review skills (wait-for-pr-comments, reply-and-resolve-pr-threads) currently run wholly at opus[1m]/high. Inspection shows ~70% of phase work is mechanical (jq/GraphQL/git/regex). The legitimate model judgment is comment classification (Skill A Phase 3) and code-fix authoring (per-comment subagents, Phase 4). Decision: drop orchestrators to sonnet[1m]/medium and explicitly dispatch fix subagents at opus[1m]/high in the Agent tool call. Subagent dispatch must NOT inherit the parent's model — silent inheritance regressions are the failure mode this guards against. Captured as an explicit Red Flag in Skill A and as AC #4 of agents-config-abn9.3.
close_reason: Resolved in spec for agents-config-abn9.3

title: Split model tier: orchestrator at sonnet[1m]/medium, fix subagents at opus[1m]/high via explicit dispatch
Codify periodic content audit as repeatable formula + skill (audit-codebase)
description:
Periodic content audits of the agents-config repo (exemplar: il69, PR #61) are fully ad hoc: no standard category checklist, no tier rubric, no remediation plan format, no gate convention. Each run re-derives all of these from scratch.

Build two artifacts to make the audit pipeline repeatable:

## Artifact 1: audit-codebase formula (pipeline backbone)
Location: src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/audit-codebase.formula.toml

Phases (in order, matching il69 exemplar):
1. audit — parallel subagent dispatch, one per content category; each produces a findings list
2. triage — aggregate findings; classify each by tier + vision tag; generate REMEDIATION_PLAN.md
3. gate — formula gate step; blocks until human annotates plan (opt-out gate model)
4. apply-t1 — single bead-implementor pass applying all Tier-1 (mechanical) fixes
5. file-t2-beads — create child beads for every Tier-2+ finding; link discovered-from to audit bead
6. delivery — standard create-pr + review-cycle steps

## Artifact 2: audit-codebase skill (methodology guide)
Location: src/user/.agents/skills/audit-codebase/SKILL.md

Must contain:
- Content category checklist (7 categories from il69: agents/, skills/, commands/, rules/, formulas/, scripts/, templates/)
- Tier classification rubric (see Notes)
- Vision rubric (see Notes)
- REMEDIATION_PLAN.md format/template
- Opt-out gate annotation convention (see Notes)
- T2+ bead-filing pattern (discovered-from dep, parent=audit-epic, strip inherited labels)
notes:
## Design decisions from il69 exemplar run (2026-05-10, PR #61)

### Tier classification rubric
- T1 = Mechanical / no judgment required — string fixes, dead-code removal, missing exit-code docs, portability fixes. Can batch into a single commit per category. Budget: respect vision C-share cap (≤30% of T1 items may be vision-C cleanup).
- T2 = Judgment call — behavioral changes, naming decisions, structural refactors, semantic contradictions. File as individual beads; do NOT apply in the T1 pass.
- T3 = Extraction / major refactor — helper-script extractions, skill splits, formula rewiring. File as beads; may need their own epics.

### Vision rubric (from REMEDIATION_PLAN.md gate)
- Vision A: directly advances 85/5/10 ratio (e.g., removes human-in-loop friction, adds spec-readiness gate)
- Vision B: indirectly supports the vision (e.g., clarity improvement enabling faster agent comprehension)
- Vision C: repo hygiene / cleanup (e.g., dead HTML comments, stale references) — cap at 30% of T1 items to prevent scope creep masquerading as vision work

### Opt-out gate convention (from il69 session — user-approved alternative to column-0 markers)
Default: every finding is APPROVED unless annotated otherwise.
Annotations applied inline in REMEDIATION_PLAN.md by human reviewer:
- [APPROVED] — explicit approval (optional; default is already approved)
- [DROP] — do not implement; finding is invalid or out of scope
- [DEMOTE] — move finding to lower tier (e.g., T1 → T2 or T2 → T3)
- [NOTE: ...] — contextual instruction for the implementor (e.g., 'keep existing naming paradigm')
- [DISCUSS: ...] — escalate; requires human decision before implementing
Gate releases when human posts 'approved' in chat OR when all findings carry inline annotation.

### REMEDIATION_PLAN.md format
Columns: | Finding ID | Category | Description | Tier | Vision | Status | Decision/Notes |
Finding IDs: category-prefix + sequential number (e.g., A1, SK3, CM2, R5, F4, SC2, T1)
Status values: APPROVED / DROP / DEMOTE / DISCUSS
Decision record: decisions.md file alongside the plan; each row D1..Dn capturing rationale

### T2+ bead-filing pattern
- Parent: the audit epic (NOT the audit bead itself — sibling pattern)
- dep type: discovered-from pointing to the audit bead
- Strip inherited labels post-create (bd create --parent copies parent lifecycle labels; audit and strip immediately)
- Group related T2 findings into single beads where they share a coherent fix story
- T3 extractions may warrant their own sub-epics

### Known pitfalls from il69
1. Main-branch merge mid-pipeline: if origin/main had fixes since AUDIT_INPUT_SHA, merge main into audit branch BEFORE applying T1 to avoid conflicting edits. Record superseded findings as D-series decisions.
2. TOML false positives: Python 3.9 toml package rejects triple-quoted strings; production bd formula show parser accepts them. Do not treat Python parse failure as a regression.
3. label inheritance: bd create --parent copies ALL parent labels including lifecycle markers. Always audit and strip after filing T2 beads.
4. Grep pattern precision: column-aligned TOML fields use two-space alignment (name  = ) not one-space (name = ). Single-space grep misses ~17 occurrences per run.

### Exemplar reference
- Audit bead: agents-config-il69 (closed, PR #61, squash commit 9ed3a0b)
- AUDIT_INPUT_SHA: af9c1bfc342bf7578ad491cc63dc95b07618c851
- Findings: 100 P1 + 48 P2; 31 T1 applied; 12 T2 beads filed (acmh.1–acmh.12)
- Audit artifacts committed at: docs/audits/phase3/REMEDIATION_PLAN.md, docs/audits/phase3/decisions.md

title: Spike: feedback loops at points of greatest realization + processing/improvement mechanism
description: Research spike per Scott's round-2 ask. Beyond agents-config-4htl (spec post-mortem), the roadmap currently has no built-in feedback hooks at strategic 'points of greatest realization' — moments in the bead workflow where lessons surface and could be captured. Examples: PR review (Copilot finds an anti-pattern → did our tests catch this?), HEP escalation (worker hit a decision point → was the spec under-specified?), overnight-run completion (what unexpected outcomes emerged?), bead closure (did the AC accurately predict success criteria?). Spike investigates: (1) what 'points of greatest realization' map to in the actual bead workflow; (2) what mechanisms could capture lessons at those points (hooks, audit beads, memory writes); (3) how to process and act on captures (likely overlaps with Vision-Gap-3 memory governance). Outcome: produce concrete adoption beads per identified hook, OR close as deferred if cost/benefit doesn't pencil.
acceptance_criteria: Spike outcome documented: (a) list of identified feedback points; (b) per-point capture mechanism proposal; (c) processing/improvement mechanism proposal; (d) per-point verdict — file concrete adoption bead, or close as deferred. Spike itself closes when all verdicts are applied.

title: Define PR classification policy for RALF-only vs human-required review
description: Vision-Gap-2 from the 2026-05-09 roadmap. RALF substitutes cross-model review for human review (commitment 3), but no open bead defines the decision rule for which PR classes get RALF-only vs human-required review. This is the prerequisite for agents-config-td39 (risk-tiered auto-merge): without a classification policy, the tiering has nothing to tier against. Output: a documented PR-classification policy keyed off blast radius, file types touched, and security surface — specific enough that a script can read a PR diff and emit a tier verdict.
acceptance_criteria: A docs artifact (in this repo) defines PR classification rules with concrete examples per tier. A script (or specification) exists that maps a PR diff to a tier verdict (RALF-only / RALF + human / human-required). agents-config-td39 references this policy as its blocker dep.

Reference: docs/plans/2026-05-09-roadmap.md Section 6.

PR description references F1/F2/F4/F5/F6/F7 and links to docs/audits/phase3/by-category/commands.md and docs/audits/phase3/REMEDIATION_PLAN.md
notes:
## Summary

Six Tier-2 audit findings (F1/F2/F4/F5/F6/F7) from the Phase 3 commands/ audit. Two major commands embed full methodology inline instead of delegating to skills; a third hardcodes a plugin dependency and duplicates skill content inline. Fix: create two new skills, reshape all three commands to lean-delegation shape, add availability fallback and correct default path.

## Background / Motivation

Phase 3 audit (SHA af9c1bfc) found commands/ violating the lean-body target (<=80 lines) and the lean-delegation pattern. optimize-my-skill.md (233 lines) and optimize-my-agent.md (107 lines) carry full methodologies inline. refresh-agents-md.md hardcodes a plugin skill with no fallback and duplicates optimize-agents-md principles in two steps. Each creates a secondary maintenance surface that drifts from the canonical skill over time.

## Requirements

F1: Create src/user/.agents/skills/optimize-my-skill/SKILL.md — extract Phase 2-5 methodology and the 'What Makes Skills Effective' reference section. Required sections: Phase 1 discovery, Phase 2 quality criteria (frontmatter checks, folder structure, body content, anti-patterns), Phase 3 propose improvements, Phase 4 suggest testing, Phase 5 confirm/apply, 'What Makes Skills Effective' reference (file structure, frontmatter fields, body structure, key principles). Each Phase section >=10 lines of substantive content; 'What Makes Skills Effective' sub-sections each >=5 lines of substantive content. During extraction, normalize any '5,000 words' threshold to '500 lines' — absorbs Tier-1 F3.
Frontmatter: name=optimize-my-skill, model=sonnet, allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, AskUserQuestion (comma-separated, per optimize-agents-md convention). Description: SKILLS_PRIMER-compliant third-person, >=3 observable triggers, explicit negative trigger: 'Do NOT use for agent persona files or AGENTS.md configuration files'. No XML angle brackets in values; name does not contain 'claude' or 'anthropic'.

F2: Create src/user/.agents/skills/optimize-my-agent/SKILL.md — extract 5-phase optimization workflow, quality rubric, and failure checklist. Required sections: all 5 optimization phases, quality rubric with exactly the 6 AGENTS_PRIMER persona-file quality issues (titles verbatim with exact AGENTS_PRIMER casing and punctuation: 'Over-broad role'; 'No examples in description'; 'Wrong model tier'; 'Body mixes role with task'; 'Bead references in shared agents'; '`skills` lists unused skills'), 7-item failure checklist, phase 4 recommendation structure (the Missing/Weak Area / Current / Recommended Addition / Why template from the source command). Each section >=10 lines of substantive content. Scope: agent persona files (agents/*.md with frontmatter name/description/model/color/tools).
Frontmatter: name=optimize-my-agent, model=sonnet, allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, AskUserQuestion (comma-separated). Description: SKILLS_PRIMER-compliant third-person, >=3 observable triggers, explicit negative trigger: 'Do NOT use for SKILL.md files or AGENTS.md configuration files'. No XML angle brackets; name does not contain 'claude' or 'anthropic'.

F4: optimize-my-skill.md empty-ARGUMENTS default path. 'Has skills' predicate: directory exists AND contains at least one immediate subdirectory (depth 1) containing a SKILL.md file (case-sensitive exact; regular file or symlink to regular file). Fallback order: (1) ~/.claude/skills/ — if fails predicate, (2) .claude/skills/ — if fails predicate, (3) emit 'No skills found. Pass a path: /optimize-my-skill <path>'. Non-existent directory satisfies 'fails predicate'.

F5 (resolved via F2): Replace optimize-my-agent rubric with AGENTS_PRIMER persona-file quality issues verbatim. Delivered by F2 skill AC.

F6: refresh-agents-md.md Step 1 — add a-priori availability check: check whether the dispatching-parallel-agents skill directory exists by probing these paths in order (match any): ~/.claude/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md, ~/.claude/plugins/*/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md. If probe matches: run three discovery subagents simultaneously. If not: run sequentially — (1) Git Analyzer, (2) File Inventory, (3) Directory Discovery. This applies regardless of whether the obra/superpowers plugin is installed — it is NOT a cross-tool concern; the command file is Claude-specific.

F7: refresh-agents-md.md Steps 3b and 3d — remove inline principle enumeration.
- Step 3b: replace instruction block with a direct invocation reference to the optimize-agents-md skill, with explicit instruction to skip scope questions and infer context from the codebase. Must also forward focus_areas value and parent-file context gathered in Step 3a. Semantic requirement: no inline principle enumeration; skill referenced by name; scope-questions skip stated; context-handoff present.
- Step 3d: replace inline validation checklist with reference to the optimize-agents-md skill's Validation Checklist section. Semantic requirement: no inline checklist; section referenced by name.

After F1+F2 skills are created, reshape commands:
- optimize-my-skill.md: parse ARGUMENTS (target: directory or specific skill path), invoke optimize-my-skill skill, report results. Retain command-shape content only. No occurrences of '5,000 words'.
- optimize-my-agent.md: parse ARGUMENTS (target: agent persona file path), invoke optimize-my-agent skill, report results. Scope: agent persona files only.

## Out of Scope

- F3 (5,000-words threshold fix) — absorbed by F1 at extraction time; any open Tier-1 F3 bead can be closed as already-resolved after this PR lands
- F8 (optimize-my-agent.md heading mismatch) — Tier 1; the lean-delegation rewrite replaces the heading body anyway; F8 bead can be closed as already-resolved after this PR lands
- Refactoring optimize-agents-md SKILL.md — F7 references its existing Validation Checklist section as-is
- Migration of installed ~/.claude/commands/ versions — install.sh handles on next run
- Any commands not named in F1/F2/F4/F5/F6/F7

## Design Notes

- Single PR: all six findings are Tier 2, same audit, same category, thematically unified. Expected diff: ~600+ lines added (two new skills), ~400 lines removed (three commands reshaped).
- F5 scope decision: user confirmed optimize-my-agent targets agent persona files (not AGENTS.md config). New skill rubric uses AGENTS_PRIMER quality issues table titles verbatim including casing and backticks.
- F4 path preference: command installs user-wide (~/.claude/commands/) so ~/.claude/skills/ is canonical; .claude/skills/ is project-level fallback. User-level skills take precedence even when project-level skills exist; pass explicit path to target project skills.
- F4 predicate: case-sensitive 'SKILL.md'; regular file or symlink; depth-1 only.
- F6 probe: check ~/.claude/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md and ~/.claude/plugins/*/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md. Not a cross-tool concern — command is Claude-specific; probe accounts for varying superpowers install locations.
- F6 fallback: sequential order: Git Analyzer, File Inventory, Directory Discovery.
- F7 peer dependency: 'skip the 5 scope questions' relies on optimize-agents-md retaining its numbered five-question structure. Future edits to that skill must update refresh-agents-md.md in lockstep.
- F7 context handoff: Step 3b invocation must forward focus_areas and parent-file context; these context inputs are gathered by Step 3a and must not be silently dropped.
- F3 + F8 absorbed: F1 extraction normalizes '5,000 words' => '500 lines'; F2/F5 command rewrite replaces the heading. Both Tier-1 beads (if tracked) can be closed after this PR.
- allowed-tools: comma-separated format used throughout (matching optimize-agents-md peer); SKILLS_PRIMER documents space-separated but this codebase normalizes on comma form.

## Open Questions (post-ralf-review cycle cap, for implementer judgment)

- F2 phase 4 recommendation structure: 'phase 4 recommendation structure' means the Missing/Weak Area / Current / Recommended Addition / Why template from source command lines 36-46. AC references this template; implementer should preserve all four fields.
- F1 'What Makes Skills Effective' sub-sections: each sub-section should preserve the substantive content from the source command; headers-only is not sufficient.
- F4 edge case: empty-but-existing SKILL.md still satisfies the predicate (file presence, not content validity).
- F7 Step 3a/3b boundary: Step 3a (Gather Context) survives unchanged; only the principle-enumeration block in 3b is replaced; surrounding scaffolding that gathers context or applies focus_areas remains.

The skill constructs and passes an explicit absolute report path — grep for 'worker-audit' or '.pr-comment-fixer-' in the updated SKILL.md returns at least one match confirming path-construction logic is present.

notes:
## Spec

### Summary

Complete the migration from the legacy `bead-implementor` mega-agent to a typed-report worker family (the existing trio plus two new specialists), and apply five smaller agent-quality fixes from the il69 audit (F6/F7/F8/F9, with F4 closing as superseded). Result: `bead-implementor` is deleted; every former caller dispatches a purpose-built worker that emits a typed `worker-report-v1` YAML; the architecture spec, smoke verifier, and all dispatch surfaces reflect the new family.

### Background / motivation

The agents-config-il69 Phase 3 audit identified six Tier 2 findings against `agents/`:

| ID | Severity | File | Issue |
|----|----------|------|-------|
| F4 | Med | `bead-implementor.md` | model tier (sonnet/medium) — deferred until F11 resolved |
| F6 | Low | `quality-reviewer.md` | `memory: project` with no eviction policy |
| F7 | High | `tech-lead.md` | missing "Do NOT dispatch when:" negative triggers |
| F8 | Med | `tech-lead.md` | hardcoded `.claude/agents/*` (Claude-only) in shared content |
| F9 | Med | `tech-lead.md` | code-free orchestrator but no `disallowedTools` enforcement |
| F11 | High | `bead-implementor.md` | bead-implementor vs trio: two living specs disagree |

Pre-brainstorm investigation (2026-05-12) established the F11 ground truth:

- bead-implementor dispatched by **4 active surfaces**: `implement-feature.formula.toml` (red-tests), `fix-bug.formula.toml` (red-tests + diagnose), `docs-only.formula.toml` (apply-edits), `wait-for-pr-comments` SKILL (per-comment fixer).
- The dedicated trio (`tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser`) dispatched by **exactly one surface**: `implement-bead`'s stage-map skill.
- agents-config-7bk.19.2 (closed, merged commit eac41ca) deliberately introduced the trio with the stated purpose: *"Replace the single bead-aware mega-agent (`bead-implementor`) with three focused, bead-agnostic worker agents."*
- Migration stalled: trio agents exist + dispatch skill points to them, but formulas, architecture spec, smoke verifier, and `wait-for-pr-comments` still name bead-implementor.

Design improvements the trio captures (lost if we keep bead-implementor):

- Pure task functions — no `bd` commands in agent bodies; orchestrator owns bead-state lifecycle
- Typed `worker-report-v1.yaml` output — machine-parsable evidence at every stage transition
- Bead-agnostic — usable in non-bead contexts in principle
- model: opus, effort: high — matches reasoning load of authoring meaningful tests / root-cause traversal

### Requirements

#### R1 — Migrate the formula dispatch surfaces (F11)

R1.1 — `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml` red-tests normal flow: replace "Dispatch bead-implementor (sonnet, medium effort) to write tests" with dispatch of `tdd-red-team` in **multi-AC mode**, passing AC bullets + worktree path + step-bead ID. Reports written to `<repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/tdd-red-team.yaml`.

R1.2 — `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml` red-tests normal flow: replace bead-implementor dispatch with `tdd-red-team` in **single-regression mode**, passing `root_cause_note` + `reproduction_steps` from the upstream `bug-diagnoser` report. Reports written to `<repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/tdd-red-team.yaml`.

R1.3 — `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml` diagnose stage: replace bead-implementor dispatch with `bug-diagnoser`. Reports written to `<repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/bug-diagnoser.yaml`.

R1.4 — `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/docs-only.formula.toml` apply-edits stage: replace bead-implementor dispatch with `docs-edits-team`. The apply-edits contract is a single-pass docs/spec/prose change — it doesn't fit the TDD red/green/diagnose taxonomy, so a dedicated specialist is cleaner than overloading tdd-green-team with a stage flag.

R1.5 — `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md`: remove ALL references to bead-implementor (§6 agent table, §3 red-tests prose, §3.5 diagnose prose, docs-only apply-edits subsection, RALF-IT note ~line 723, scope-item-7 cross-reference ~line 812, and any other occurrence). Add rows for `tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser`, `docs-edits-team`, `pr-comment-fixer-team` to the §6 agent table; **each new row must include a markdown link to its per-agent report spec doc** (e.g., docs-edits-team → `docs/specs/docs-edits-report-v1.md`). Update all referencing prose sections to name the appropriate worker per stage.

R1.6 — Delete `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md`. No deprecated stub; no transition window.

R1.6.x — Update `scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh`: remove the `bead-implementor agent exists` assertion block (currently ~lines 76-79). Replace with assertions for the five new worker agents: `tdd-red-team.md`, `tdd-green-team.md`, `bug-diagnoser.md`, `docs-edits-team.md`, `pr-comment-fixer-team.md` — all under `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/`.

R1.6.y — Update `tests/verify-implement-bead.sh`: remove any stale bead-implementor references in comments or assertion text. The AC17 comment block ("# AC 17 — No bead-implementor in either file") should be updated to reflect the current assertion semantics (asserting legacy-agent absence is still valid; update the comment text to remove the bead-implementor name and describe the assertion generically).

#### R2 — Introduce two new specialists

R2.1 — Author `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/docs-edits-team.md`:
- Frontmatter: `model: opus`, `effort: high`, `color: cyan`, `tools: Read, Edit, Write, Grep, Glob, Bash`
- Body: pure task function; zero `bd` subcommand invocations; cites `docs/specs/docs-edits-report-v1.md` by repo-root-relative path
- Contract: receives worktree path + bead description + acceptance_criteria + step-bead ID + **explicit absolute report path**; applies the documented changes; commits with a descriptive message referencing the source bead; writes `docs-edits-report-v1.yaml` to the absolute path the caller provides
- Hard rules in body: no new code requiring a test harness; no speculative refactoring; no test scaffolding (if tests needed, flag-human per existing protocol)

R2.2 — Author `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/pr-comment-fixer-team.md`:
- Frontmatter: `model: opus`, `effort: high`, `color: orange`, `tools: Read, Edit, Write, Grep, Glob, Bash`
- Body: pure task function; zero `bd` subcommand invocations; cites `docs/specs/pr-comment-fix-report-v1.md` by repo-root-relative path
- Contract: receives a single PR comment object (id, thread_id, body, code-location) + repo path + **explicit absolute report path**; either commits a fix, recognizes the concern as already-addressed, or escalates — writes `pr-comment-fix-report-v1.yaml` to the absolute path the caller provides
- **Contract-purity rule:** the agent contains NO logic that detects or branches on whether beads infrastructure is present. The caller is always responsible for providing the absolute report path; the agent writes there unconditionally.

R2.3 — Author `docs/specs/docs-edits-report-v1.md`:
- Opens with a relative link to `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md` as the shared base contract
- Describes only the extension delta (added fields):
  - `files_changed: [string]` (paths)
  - `commit_sha: string`
  - `summary: string` (1-line)
  - `skipped_items: [{path, reason}]` (when worker chose not to touch a section)
- Add `docs-edits-team` to the `### 1.3 Per-agent extensions` table in `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md` (columns: Agent | Schema | Adds).
- Also update the Quick Reference table near the top of `worker-report-v1.md` (the one listing per-agent report extension filenames) to include `docs-edits-report-v1.md`.

R2.4 — Author `docs/specs/pr-comment-fix-report-v1.md`:
- Opens with a relative link to `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md` as the shared base contract
- Describes only the extension delta (added fields):
  - `comment_id: string`
  - `comment_thread_id: string`
  - `classification: enum { FIX, SKIP, ESCALATE }`
  - `action: enum { COMMITTED_FIX, ALREADY_ADDRESSED, NO_ACTION }`
  - `fix_summary: string` (when action == COMMITTED_FIX)
  - `commit_sha: string` (when action == COMMITTED_FIX)
  - `already_addressed_by_sha: string` (when action == ALREADY_ADDRESSED)
  - `escalation_reason: string` (when classification == ESCALATE)
- Add `pr-comment-fixer-team` to the `### 1.3 Per-agent extensions` table in `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md`.
- Also update the Quick Reference table near the top of `worker-report-v1.md` to include `pr-comment-fix-report-v1.md`.

R2.5 — Update `src/user/.agents/skills/wait-for-pr-comments/SKILL.md`:
- Line ~207 dispatch: change `subagent_type: "bead-implementor"` → `subagent_type: "pr-comment-fixer-team"`
- Adjust dispatch task-spec to match R2.4 input contract
- The skill always computes and passes an explicit absolute report path to the agent — `<repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/pr-comment-fixer-team.yaml` in a beads workflow, or `<cwd>/.pr-comment-fixer-<comment-id>.yaml` otherwise. The agent never infers context — it writes to the caller-provided path, always.

#### R3 — tech-lead.md edits (F7, F8, F9)

R3.1 (F7) — Add a new H2 section `## Do NOT Dispatch When` to the **body** (not the frontmatter `description:` YAML field) of `src/user/.agents/agents/tech-lead.md`, covering at minimum:
- Work already routed via start-bead, implement-bead, or run-queue
- Tasks already decomposed by the caller (sub-task list handed to tech-lead)
- Single-worker tasks (no multi-component orchestration warranted)

Include at least one negative example (e.g., "User asks to fix a typo in CLAUDE.md → do NOT dispatch tech-lead; do the edit inline").

R3.2 (F8) — In the body, all three existing occurrences where `.claude/agents/*` is used as a primary source of callable agents must be replaced. Currently:
- ~line 43: "Scan `.claude/agents/*` to understand available team members"
- ~line 55: "Scan `.claude/agents/*` to inventory available expertise"
- ~line 121: "You must work only with available agents in `.claude/agents/*`"

All three must be rewritten to follow audit decision D19: "use the caller-provided roster of callable agents and tool limits; if the caller does not provide one, inspect the current tool's agent registry or documented agent directory as a fallback." `.claude/agents/*` may be cited at most once in the body, and only in a clause that explicitly labels it as "the Claude Code-specific fallback example."

R3.3 (F9) — Add `disallowedTools: Write, Edit` to the frontmatter of `src/user/.agents/agents/tech-lead.md`.

#### R4 — quality-reviewer.md Memory Protocol (F6)

R4.1 — Keep `memory: project` (per audit decision D20 — PARTIAL synthesis).

R4.2 — Add a `## Memory Protocol` section to `src/user/.agents/agents/quality-reviewer.md` body:

- **Categories persisted** (exhaustive):
  1. Recurring vulnerability patterns (observed across ≥ 2 reviews)
  2. Project-specific anti-patterns (conventions violated more than once)
  3. Prior false-positive corrections (findings the reviewer raised that human ratified as not-an-issue)

- **Horizon**: 30 review cycles per category. After 30 cycles without reference, entry is eligible for eviction.

- **Eviction rule**: LRU within each category; on overflow, drop least-recently-referenced first.

- **Hard cap**: 50 entries total across all categories.

- **Write trigger**: memory appended only when a finding's outcome receives **explicit human ratification** (PR merge, explicit user response). No auto-persist of every observation.

#### R5 — F4 resolves as superseded

bead-implementor.md is deleted under R1.6; its model/effort tuning is moot. No standalone code change needed. Recorded via architectural decision bead agents-config-yijq.

### Out of scope

- Migration of the `wait-for-pr-comments` skill itself from `src/user/.agents/skills/` to `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/` — tension noted in DW1 below; not in acmh.1 scope
- ralf-implement / ralf-review skill internals
- green-loop's label-driven skill dispatch (it dispatches *skills*, not agents)
- The `merge-and-cleanup` formula (no bead-implementor usage)
- New tech-lead capability beyond F7/F8/F9 edits
- Schema extensions to `worker-report-v1` beyond the two new per-agent extensions
- Re-evaluation of opus/high settings on the trio post-migration

### Design notes (key decisions made during brainstorming)

1. **Complete the migration vs hybrid/rollback/defer** — chosen: complete. Rationale: honors 7bk.19.2's stated intent; eliminates the two-living-specs surface; the trio's typed-report contract gives the pipeline a stronger audit trail than bead-implementor's note-appending model.

2. **All six findings in one bead** — chosen over split. F4 is moot once F11 lands; F6/F7/F8/F9 are independent small edits; coordinated PR cycle is acceptable.

3. **New `pr-comment-fixer-team` specialist vs reuse trio vs keep bead-implementor** — chosen: new specialist. Rationale: PR-comment fixing has its own task shape (classified comment → fix or already-addressed or escalate) that doesn't fit the TDD taxonomy.

4. **`docs-edits-team` specialist for docs-only apply-edits** — chosen on the same principle as #3: apply-edits is a single-pass non-TDD stage; overloading tdd-green-team with a "stage=apply-edits, skip test_results" flag would muddy that agent's contract. A dedicated specialist with its own report is symmetric with the pr-comment-fixer-team decision and keeps each worker's contract tight.

5. **bead-implementor deleted, not stubbed** — no transition window; worker agents have no external API.

6. **Concrete report-path contract (C2 resolution)** — orchestrator always computes and passes an explicit absolute report path; agents write to that path unconditionally. No in-agent branching on beads vs non-beads context. This is already the trio pattern; R2.5 makes it explicit for the new agents.

7. **`verify-artifacts.sh` must be updated (C1 resolution)** — the smoke verifier asserts agent file existence. Adding R1.6.x ensures the verifier reflects the new worker family; failing to do so would make the smoke gate contradict the repo state.

8. **F6 horizon numbers calibration** — 30 cycles ≈ a medium-sized multi-PR epic where the same quality-reviewer accumulates cross-PR pattern knowledge; 50-entry hard cap fits in under 2KB of context (100 chars avg × 50 entries). Both are v1 starting points; re-tuning is a separate optimization bead.

9. **wait-for-pr-comments cross-tool tension** — accepted as-is for this bead; the skill lives in shared content but dispatches a beads-plugin agent. This tension is tracked as DW1 for a follow-up bead.

### Discovered work (file as follow-up beads, not in acmh.1 scope)

DW1 — `wait-for-pr-comments` skill lives in `src/user/.agents/skills/` (shared) but post-migration dispatches `pr-comment-fixer-team` from `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/` (beads-plugin only). Resolutions: (a) move skill into beads plugin; (b) move agent into shared content; (c) design graceful degradation for non-beads contexts. R2.5 implements the minimal fix (absolute-path contract); DW1 is the long-term resolution.

DW2 — Post-migration, re-evaluate whether opus/high is still right for the trio's actual workload. Analogous to how F4 was originally framed for bead-implementor.

### Open questions

None blocking after ralf-spec-review cycle 2 revisions (see resolution summary below).

---

## Pre-brainstorm analysis (assess step, 2026-05-12) — preserved for audit trail

### F11 usage findings: bead-implementor IS still active

**Active dispatchers of bead-implementor (4 callers):**
1. `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml:293` — red-tests stage normal flow
2. `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml:327` — red-tests stage normal flow
3. `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/docs-only.formula.toml:131` — apply-edits stage (sole worker)
4. `src/user/.agents/skills/wait-for-pr-comments/SKILL.md:207` — per-comment fixer

**Where the dedicated trio is dispatched (zero formula callers):** Only in `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md` (stage-map: red-tests→tdd-red-team, green-loop→tdd-green-team, diagnose→bug-diagnoser).

**The actual conflict:** The formula TOML prose and the implement-bead skill's dispatch map disagree. This bead lands the alignment.

**Git history (decisive):** agents-config-7bk.19.2 (commit eac41ca) stated intent: "Replace the single bead-aware mega-agent (`bead-implementor`) with three focused, bead-agnostic worker agents." Migration stalled; acmh.1 is the resumption.

### ralf-spec-review cycle 1 findings addressed

**C1** — `scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh` bead-implementor assertion: addressed via R1.6.x.
**C2** — R2.5 fallback contract TBD: resolved by concrete absolute-path contract (design note #6); R2.5 and R2.2 updated.
**M1** — New report specs must reference worker-report-v1 and update rollup table: addressed in R2.3/R2.4.
**M2** — R3.1 body-vs-frontmatter ambiguity: resolved — H2 section in body per R3.1 text.
**M3** — R3.2 AC too semantic: resolved — split into two mechanical grep-checkable sub-ACs.
**M4** — F6 numbers lack calibration: addressed in design note #8.
**M5** — docs-edits-team "ratification hedge" removed: committed to the decision; design note #4 updated.
**m1** — Agent colors: docs-edits-team cyan, pr-comment-fixer-team orange, per R2.1/R2.2.
**m2** — Mode arguments in formula dispatch: added to requirements R1.1/R1.2 and AC.
**m3** — R1.5 scope too narrow for bead-pipeline-architecture.md: R1.5 now requires removal of ALL references in that file.
**m4** — "historical-state-only sections" vague: cross-cutting AC now enumerates explicit exemptions (docs/audits/, docs/plans/ only).

### ralf-spec-review cycle 2 findings addressed (applied post-cycle-cap)

**F1 (blocking)** — `tests/verify-implement-bead.sh` has stale bead-implementor comment: addressed via R1.6.y; cross-cutting AC scope extended to include tests/.
**F2 (critical)** — worker-report-v1.md rollup AC was imprecise: updated R2.3/R2.4 to name the exact `### 1.3 Per-agent extensions` table AND the Quick Reference table; rollup ACs updated to match.
**F3 (major)** — R2.5 behavioral AC not grep-verifiable: added AC asserting `worker-audit` or `.pr-comment-fixer-` appears in updated SKILL.md (confirms path-construction logic is present).
**F4 (major)** — tech-lead.md three `.claude/agents/*` occurrences; AC missed line 121: R3.2 now enumerates all three lines; R3.2b AC updated to catch all three (zero-match or one-example-labeled-only match).
**F5 (major)** — arch spec §6 table lacks report-spec links: R1.5 requirement updated; AC updated to check new rows include markdown links.
**F6 (minor)** — R4.2(e) write-trigger AC not grep-checkable: AC updated to require phrase 'human ratification' or 'explicit human ratification'.
**F7 (minor)** — tdd-red-team mode semantics unlinked: R1.1/R1.2 AC updated to include cross-reference to tdd-red-team.md.

comments.text: Brainstorm input from user (2026-05-12): Before resolving F11 (bead-implementor vs dedicated trio), do a usage analysis: (1) is bead-implementor still dispatched anywhere — formulas, skills, run-queue, manual? (2) If yes, in which context(s)? (3) Based on those contexts, does it need to be repurposed (e.g., as a fallback or specialized worker), deprecated, or removed entirely? This analysis gates F11 which in turn gates F4 (model tier).

title: Create-PR: push branch and open pull request
description:
Create the pull request and persist its URL to the molecule.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch

## Actions

1. Invoke superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch.

2. PR body MUST include:
   - Bead reference: {{bead-id}} and bead title
   - AC (full list with [m]/[h] tags)
   - apply-edits summary (from step-bead notes)
   - review summary (quality-reviewer + simplify findings + resolutions)
   - verify-ac validation report (or warn-and-pass note)
   - Human-required AC follow-up bead references (if any [h] lines)

3. Stamp pr-url-* label on molecule:
   bd label add <mol-id> pr-url-<encoded-url>

## PR creation failure
Flag-human with worktree intact. Do NOT tear down the worktree.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view first. If a PR exists for the branch and is OPEN: skip
creation, persist URL idempotently.
If PR exists and is CLOSED: flag-human. Do NOT auto-reopen or create a new PR.

title: Review: quality-reviewer + simplify on the diff
description:
Run the standard in-house review pair on the committed diff.
Model: claude-opus-4-7  Effort: medium

Skips entirely when review-level:none.

## Actions

1. Invoke `quality-reviewer` against the diff produced by apply-edits.
   Address findings inline (per the receiving-code-review skill):
   - Critical / major: fix and re-commit before proceeding.
   - Minor: fix or record explicit deferral with rationale.

2. Invoke the `simplify` skill on the same diff. Address findings the
   same way.

3. Persist the review summary:
     bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "REVIEW: quality-reviewer=<n_findings>; simplify=<n_findings>; deferred=<list or none>"

## Definition of Done
All critical/major findings resolved. Deferrals recorded. Diff is committed
and ready for verify-ac.

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-running re-evaluates the current diff; prior fixes already committed
are picked up via filesystem state.

title: docs-only
description: Per-stage docs-only pipeline: preflight → apply-edits → review → verify-ac → create-pr → review-cycle → merge-or-handoff

title: Verify-AC: [m] grep-witnesses if any; warn-and-pass if none
description:
Validate every [m]-tagged AC line if any exist; warn-and-pass otherwise.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skips when review-level:none.

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (parse [m] and [h] tags from
  acceptance_criteria field per §4.1's canonical line parser)
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule

## Actions

1. Count [m]-tagged AC lines from the acceptance_criteria field:
     m_count=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json \
       | jq -r '.[0].acceptance_criteria // ""' \
       | grep -cvE '^([[:space:]]*$|\[h\][[:space:]])')

2. If m_count == 0:
   - Log a warning to step-bead notes:
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "verify-ac: no [m] AC lines — skipping mechanical witness verification (warn-and-pass)"
   - Pass the step. Do NOT block.
   - Encourages greppable landmarks (e.g. grep for a new heading in the
     spec file) without requiring them.

3. If m_count > 0:
   For each [m]-tagged AC line, dispatch bead-verifier (haiku, low effort)
   to run a shell-runnable witness (typically `grep` for an inserted
   landmark, `test -f` for a new file, etc.). Collect: exit code +
   excerpt. Assemble validation report.

4. [h]-tagged AC lines: do NOT validate here. Confirm that human follow-up
   child beads were filed at brainstorm-time finalize
   (bd list --parent {{bead-id}} filtered by label human). If any [h]
   bullet has no matching follow-up, flag-human.

5. Persist validation report to BOTH step-bead notes AND source bead notes
   (so it appears in the eventual PR description at create-pr).

## Definition of Done
Every [m] AC line has explicit witness evidence (m_count > 0 path)
OR the warn-and-pass note is recorded (m_count == 0 path).
Human-review bullets have follow-up beads filed (confirm presence).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates and continues from prior pass.

title: Apply-edits: single bead-implementor pass to write/edit the docs
description:
Apply the docs/spec/prose changes via a single bead-implementor pass.
There is no red-tests phase and no RALF-IT iteration on this formula.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

## Inputs
- Bead spec from: bd show {{bead-id}} (description + acceptance_criteria)
- Worktree path: decode worktree-path-* label from molecule

## Decode worktree-path label before any work
  1. Decode: __ → /  then  _u → _
  2. Verify decoded path exists and is a git worktree:
       git -C "<path>" rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree
  3. Operate from that cwd. Quote the decoded path in any subsequent
     command that consumes it — paths with spaces or shell-special
     characters will otherwise misparse.

## Actions

1. Dispatch bead-implementor (sonnet, medium effort) with:
   - Worktree path
   - Full bead description and acceptance_criteria
   - Step-bead ID
   - Instruction: apply the documented changes; commit with a single
     descriptive message referencing {{bead-id}}.
   - Rules:
     * No new code that requires a test harness.
     * No speculative refactoring of unrelated content.
     * Do NOT add tests scaffolding here — if tests are needed, the bead
       was misrouted (file a follow-up bead and flag-human).

2. After the worker exits, persist a one-line summary to step-bead notes:
     bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "apply-edits: <summary>; files=<n>; sha=<head>"

## Definition of Done
Edits committed to the feature branch. Step-bead notes record the summary.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check filesystem (committed edits) and step-bead notes. If a commit already
exists for this step, skip and proceed to review.

title: Review-cycle: address PR review feedback
description:
Drive the PR review loop until all review threads are resolved.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

Skips entirely when review-level:none.

## Preloaded skills
wait-for-pr-comments (chains internally to reply-and-resolve-pr-threads)

## Actions

Invoke wait-for-pr-comments with:
  --mode autonomous --bead-id {{bead-id}}

One iteration = one outbound reply-batch.
Iteration cap: 5 (override: iteration-cap-review-cycle-<n> label).
Early exit when no remaining FIX-class items.

Exit conditions (mutually exclusive):
- review-exit-copilot-only (default when review-exit-human-approvers-<n> absent)
- review-exit-human-approvers-<n>: require N human approvers

Persist after each batch:
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "REVIEW-BATCH:<n>/<MAX>"

If PR is CLOSED on re-entry: flag-human. Do NOT poll a closed PR.

## Definition of Done
Every review thread has a reply. FIXED items resolved on GitHub.
No in-flight FIX work.

title: Merge-or-handoff: auto-merge or hand off to human
description:
Either auto-merge the PR or hand off to the human.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Gate 1 — PR-state clean-check
Conditions (ALL must be true for auto-merge path):
  - Zero open Copilot review threads
  - Zero open human-author review threads
  - All CI checks green
  - 5-minute quiet window with no new comments or review activity
On wait-for-pr-comments timeout: treat clean-check as NOT clean.

## Auto-merge path (auto-mergeable label present AND clean-check clean)
1. Re-run clean-check immediately before merge call.
2. Pour merge-and-cleanup formula (phase: liquid — NOT vapor).
3. Drive merge-and-cleanup:
   - Merge the PR
   - Delete branch (local + remote)
   - Remove worktree (decode from worktree-path-* label)
   - Run parent-chain I2 close-walk
   Note: merge-and-cleanup gate-step (Gate 2) blocks until ALL [h] follow-up
   children are closed with verified-by-human label, then closes the
   merge-{source-id} child after merge + cleanup so the source bead can close.

## Hand-off path (default — auto-mergeable absent OR clean-check dirty)
1. Transition bead to open: bd update {{bead-id}} --status open
2. Stamp labels: bd label add {{bead-id}} merge-ready
                 bd label add {{bead-id}} human
3. Note: bead is now excluded from bd ready (human label gates it).
   Human runs /merge-and-cleanup when ready.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view --json mergedAt first. If non-null: skip merge, proceed to cleanup.

title: Preflight: spec validation, worktree creation (no coverage check)
description:
Validate the bead spec and create the worktree before any edits.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: high

## Inputs
Read the bead and its labels:
  bd show {{bead-id}}
  bd label list {{bead-id}}

## Actions

1. Verify `brainstormed` and `implementation-ready` labels are present.
   If either is absent: apply human-flag protocol and exit.

2. Note: formula selection and pour have already happened — this step was
   created by implement-bead pouring docs-only. There is nothing to select
   or pour here.

3. Coverage and quality-gate checks are intentionally skipped: docs-only
   is the routing target for work that has no test runner / no [m] ACs.
   Do NOT inspect [coverage] or [gates] in project-config.toml here.

4. Stamp the molecule→bead lookup label if not already stamped:
   bd label add <mol-id> for-bead-{{bead-id}}

5. Create the worktree:
   Branch name: docs/{{bead-id}}-<slug-from-title>
   Location: <repo-root>/.claude/worktrees/<branch-name>
   Command:
     git worktree add .claude/worktrees/<branch> -b <branch>
   CRITICAL: the formula is already poured (this step was created by the pour).
   If worktree creation fails: squash the molecule and flag-human.

6. Stamp the worktree-path label on the molecule:
   Encoding (apply IN ORDER):
     _ → _u
     / → __
   Then: bd label add <mol-id> worktree-path-<encoded>

7. Claim the bead and walk the parent chain:
   bd update {{bead-id}} --status in_progress
   PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
   while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
     bd update "$PARENT" --status in_progress
     PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
   done

## Definition of Done
Poured molecule + worktree exists + worktree-path-* label stamped on molecule.
OR: human label + structured gap-note + (if pour completed) molecule squashed.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check: does an active molecule exist? Does the worktree path decode to a real
git worktree? If so, skip work already done.
close_reason: Preflight complete: labels validated, worktree created at .claude/worktrees/docs/agents-config-il69-audit-skills-agents-commands-rules, path label stamped, source bead claimed

title: Redesign merge-gate bead: sibling-with-dep model + async merge-batch queue
description:
## Summary

Redesign the merge-gate bead from a passive sentinel child (no deps, invisible to the dep graph) into a first-class sibling bead with a structural dependency on its source implementation bead. This enables async/batch merge processing decoupled from the implementation run-queue.

## Background / Context

### Current design (discovered in session 2026-05-10)

The merge-gate bead is created as a **child** of the implementation bead Y (in `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` finalize step 5b, and in `implement-feature.formula.toml` red-tests reroute step 4):

```bash
bd create --type task --parent "$Y_ID" \
  --title "merge-$Y_ID" \
  --description "Merge gate child for $Y_ID..." \
  --labels "merge-gate" \
  --no-inherit-labels
```

No `--deps` argument. The bead is excluded from `bd ready` only by the absence of `implementation-ready` label — not by any structural dep. The sequencing guarantee (merge must happen after implementation) is entirely **procedural** (enforced by the `merge-and-cleanup` formula's step ordering), not **structural** (not visible in the dep graph).

The `merge-and-cleanup` formula's `source-bead-gate` step finds the merge-gate by listing Y's children and checking each for the `merge-gate` label. The `cleanup` step closes it explicitly after branch/worktree cleanup, which allows Y to close via the I2 close-walk.

### Key structural problem

`bd dep list` reveals no relationship between the merge-gate and its source bead or molecule. If you query 'what is blocking the merge-gate?' — the answer is 'nothing.' The implicit contract is entirely in formula prose, not the graph. This is a discoverability deficit.

## Proposed Design

### Structural change

Make the merge-gate a **sibling** of the impl bead Y (both children of the same parent epic), with a `blocked-by` dep on Y:

- Title convention: `[Merge] <impl-bead-title>` — clearly attributable, grep-able
- Created as a sibling of Y under Y's parent epic (or as an orphan if Y has no parent)
- `blocked-by Y` dep — unblocks automatically when Y closes
- New label: `merge-ready` (distinct from `implementation-ready`) to enable a separate merge-batch queue

### Y lifecycle redefinition (critical prerequisite to making deps work)

`bd ready` unblocks a dep only when the blocking bead is **closed**. Currently Y closes only *after* merge + cleanup complete. This creates a circular dependency: the merge-gate is supposed to enable the merge, but deps wouldn't unblock until after the merge happened.

**Resolution**: Redefine Y's closure point. Y closes at `merge-or-handoff` time (when the PR is created, reviewed, and ready to merge) — not post-cleanup. 'Y closed' means 'implementation done, PR ready to merge,' not 'fully merged.' This is a more honest semantic.

Consequence: the I2 close-walk at Y-close time would walk up to Y's parent epic, but the epic stays open because the merge-gate sibling is still open. The epic only closes after the merge-gate closes (post merge+cleanup). This is correct behavior.

### Async merge-batch queue

With the merge-gate as a first-class sibling bead labeled `merge-ready`:

- Implementation queue: `bd ready --label implementation-ready`
- Merge queue: `bd ready --label merge-ready`

These can run in separate sessions, at separate cadences (e.g., implement during the day, merge in a nightly batch window). PRs accumulate as ready-to-merge beads and are drained asynchronously.

### Formula changes required

1. **`brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` finalize step 5b** — create merge-gate as sibling (Y's parent), not child of Y; add `blocked-by Y` dep; title `[Merge] <Y-title>`; label `merge-ready` instead of (or in addition to) `merge-gate`
2. **`implement-feature.formula.toml` red-tests reroute step 4** — same sibling creation pattern
3. **`docs-only.formula.toml` and `fix-bug.formula.toml`** — if they have merge-gate creation, same change
4. **`merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml` `source-bead-gate` step** — find merge-gate via dep edge backwards (or by label scan of Y's parent's children) rather than listing Y's children
5. **`merge-or-handoff` step** (all formulas) — close Y here (PR-ready closure) and transition merge-gate to unblocked/ready, rather than keeping Y open through cleanup
6. **`merge-and-cleanup` `cleanup` step** — close the merge-gate sibling and run I2 close-walk on it (not on Y, which is already closed)

### Open question: orphan impl beads (no parent epic)

If Y has no parent, the sibling would also be an orphan. This is structurally clean but means `bd list --parent <epic>` won't surface the merge-gate alongside its impl bead. May need a query convention or label to re-associate.

## Related beads

- **agents-config-td39** (Risk-tiered auto-merge policy for low-risk PR classes) — should depend on this work; the merge-gate as a first-class dispatchable unit is a prerequisite for tier-routing merges
- **agents-config-mptb** (Improve step-bead naming conventions across all formulas) — potential prerequisite; the `[Merge]` prefix convention may fall under its scope and should be consistent with whatever naming standard mptb establishes

## Acceptance Criteria

To be defined during brainstorming. Likely includes:
- All formulas create merge-gate as sibling-with-dep
- Y closes at merge-or-handoff (not post-cleanup)
- `bd ready --label merge-ready` returns only merge-gate beads whose impl bead is closed
- `bd dep list <merge-gate-id>` shows the blocked-by relationship
- merge-and-cleanup formula works correctly against the new structure
- Orphan impl bead edge case handled

title: Improve step-bead naming conventions across all formulas
acceptance_criteria:
[m] All five formula .toml files declare [vars.title-slug] with required=true and descriptive description.
[m] Every [[steps]] title in all five formulas follows the exact format [<step-id>] {{title-slug}}.
[m] The start-bead SKILL.md documents the slug generation algorithm (R6) and the actual bd mol wisp create brainstorm-bead code block in Route C includes --var title-slug=<slug>.
[m] The implement-bead SKILL.md documents the slug generation algorithm (R6) and every bd mol pour code block for the four formulas includes --var title-slug=<slug>.
[m] brainstorm-bead.formula.toml finalize-step shell script creates child beads with titles [merge-gate] <slug>, [verify] <slug> - <ac-line>, [escalate] <slug> (pre-Y escalation beads excluded).
[m] Finalize-step idempotency probes (step 5a merge-gate probe, step 5b human-verify probe) are updated to match new title patterns, with dual old/new pattern matching during a bounded transition window.
[m] No existing open step beads or child beads are renamed.
[m] Grep: grep -c 'title-slug' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/*.formula.toml returns >= 5.
[m] Grep: grep -cE '^\[vars\.title-slug\]' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/*.formula.toml returns >= 5.
[m] Grep: grep -cE 'title = "\[[a-z-]+\] \{\{title-slug\}\}"' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/*.formula.toml returns count equal to total [[steps]] across all five formulas.
[m] Grep: grep -c 'title-slug' src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md returns >= 1; same for implement-bead/SKILL.md.
notes:
## Summary

Establish a uniform, human-readable naming convention for all beads created by formula pour/wisp operations and by helper scripts within the `brainstorm-bead` formula. Every new formula step bead and `brainstorm-bead` finalize child bead will carry a title of the form `[<step-or-gate>] <title-slug>`, where `<title-slug>` is a kebab-cased derivative of the source bead or feature title.

## Background / motivation

Formula step beads currently use static, generic titles like "Preflight: spec validation, formula selection, worktree creation". When multiple molecules run the same formula in parallel, `bd mol current` and `bd list` produce indistinguishable walls of identical titles. The auto-generated numeric suffixes (`.1`, `.9`) added by `bd mol pour/wisp` are opaque and convey no semantic information. This makes it impossible to tell at a glance which molecule step belongs to which source bead, forcing users to click through every bead.

## Requirements

### R1. Uniform `title-slug` variable across all formulas

Add `[vars.title-slug]` to every `.formula.toml`:
- `brainstorm-bead`
- `implement-feature`
- `fix-bug`
- `docs-only`
- `merge-and-cleanup`

The variable is `required = true` and described as: "Kebab-cased slug derived from the source bead/feature title (≤30 chars). Used to make step-bead titles human-readable and source-identifiable."

### R2. Formula step titles use `[<step-id>] {{title-slug}}`

Update every `[[steps]] title` in all five formulas to the format:
```
[<step-id>] {{title-slug}}
```

where `<step-id>` is the step's `id` field (e.g., `preflight`, `red-tests`, `claim`, `verify`).

Examples:
- `[preflight] {{title-slug}}`
- `[red-tests] {{title-slug}}`
- `[claim] {{title-slug}}`
- `[merge] {{title-slug}}`

### R3. `start-bead` skill generates and passes `title-slug`

When the `start-bead` skill creates a `brainstorm-bead` wisp:
```bash
bd mol wisp create brainstorm-bead --var bead-id=<bead-id> --var title-slug=<slug>
```

The slug is generated from the source bead's title using the canonical algorithm (R6).

### R4. `implement-bead` skill generates and passes `title-slug`

When the `implement-bead` skill pours any formula (`implement-feature`, `fix-bug`, `docs-only`, `merge-and-cleanup`):
```bash
bd mol pour <formula> --var bead-id=<source-bead-id> --var title-slug=<slug> [other vars]
```

The slug is generated from the source bead's title using the canonical algorithm (R6).

### R5. Script-generated child beads in `brainstorm-bead` finalize follow the convention

Update the shell script in `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml`'s finalize step so that child beads created via `bd create` use:
- Merge-gate child (under Y): `[merge-gate] <title-slug>`
- Human-verify children (under Y): `[verify] <title-slug> - <ac-line>` (AC line retained for uniqueness)
- Escalation children (under Y): `[escalate] <title-slug>`

Pre-Y escalation beads (steps 1–2, created as orphan task beads linked to the source bead X via `blocked-by` dependency during idempotency probes) are **excluded** from this convention; they retain their diagnostic titles.

**Idempotency probe synchronization:** The finalize script uses exact-title probes to prevent duplicate child creation:
- Step 5a probes for `child_title = "merge-{{bead-id}}"` to identify legacy merge-gate children.
- Step 5b probes for `title == "[Human verify] $ac_line"` to avoid duplicate human-verify children.

These probes MUST be updated to match the new title patterns:
- Step 5a: probe for `child_title = "[merge-gate] $TITLE_SLUG"` (or a dual probe matching both old and new patterns during a transition window).
- Step 5b: probe for `title == "[verify] $TITLE_SLUG - $ac_line"`.

**Shell variable binding:** At the top of the finalize script, set:
```bash
TITLE_SLUG="{{title-slug}}"
```
`bd mol pour/wisp` substitutes `{{title-slug}}` with the literal slug value at molecule creation time, so the script sees `TITLE_SLUG="context-aware-session-renaming"` and can reference `$TITLE_SLUG` consistently in all `bd create` title strings and `jq --arg title` probes.

**Transition window for old-format children:** Step 5a's rename and step 5b's duplicate-avoidance probes MUST match BOTH old and new title patterns during a bounded transition window (until all beads created before this convention's deployment are closed, or 30 days from deployment, whichever is shorter). After the window, probes may match only the new patterns.

### R6. Canonical slug generation algorithm

The title-slug is generated by:
1. Lowercasing the source bead/feature title
2. Replacing spaces with hyphens
3. Stripping all characters except `a-z`, `0-9`, and hyphens
4. Collapsing consecutive hyphens to a single hyphen
5. Truncating to a maximum of 30 characters
6. Stripping leading and trailing hyphens
7. **Fallback:** If the result is empty (e.g., the title contained only non-ASCII characters or symbols), fall back to the first 30 characters of the bead-id.

Example: "Context-aware session renaming" → `context-aware-session-renaming`

### R7. No migration of existing beads

Existing open step beads and child beads are left as-is. The convention applies only to new `bd mol pour/wisp` operations and new `bd create` calls within formula scripts.

## Out of scope

- Renaming or migrating existing open molecule steps or child beads.
- Changing `bd` CLI behavior (e.g., auto-generating slugs at the tool level).
- Applying the convention to beads created outside of formulas (e.g., manual `bd create` by users).
- Modifying the numeric suffix behavior of `bd mol pour/wisp` itself — we work within the existing suffix mechanism by making the prefix human-readable.
- Script-generated beads created by formulas other than `brainstorm-bead` (e.g., `discovered_work` filings in `implement-feature` or `fix-bug`).

## Design notes

- **Why step-id and not step name?** The `id` field is stable, short, and already used for step referencing within formulas. Using it in the title provides immediate visual correlation with `bd mol current` output.
- **Why kebab-case slug instead of full title?** Titles can be 100+ characters. Truncating to 30 chars keeps `bd list` and `bd mol current` output readable while still conveying the source identity.
- **Why not include the bead ID?** The original proposal included `{source_bead_id}`, but the user explicitly chose a shorter slug-only format for readability. The `for-bead-<bead-id>` molecule linkage label already provides the machine-readable association.
- **Formula vars strategy:** We add `title-slug` as a new uniform variable alongside existing context-specific vars (`feature`, `bug`, `topic`, `pr`). Existing vars remain for other uses within step descriptions.
- **merge-and-cleanup:** This formula currently only has `pr` and `bead-id` vars. It gains `title-slug` so its steps (e.g., `[merge] {{title-slug}}`) are also source-identifiable.

## Acceptance criteria

1. [m] All five formula `.toml` files declare `[vars.title-slug]` with `required = true` and a descriptive `description`.
2. [m] Every `[[steps]] title` in all five formulas follows the exact format `[<step-id>] {{title-slug}}` (bracketed step id, single space, `{{title-slug}}`).
3. [m] The `start-bead` SKILL.md documents the slug generation algorithm (R6) and the actual `bd mol wisp create brainstorm-bead` code block in Route C includes `--var title-slug=<slug>`.
4. [m] The `implement-bead` SKILL.md documents the slug generation algorithm (R6) and every `bd mol pour` code block for the four formulas includes `--var title-slug=<slug>`.
5. [m] The `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` finalize-step shell script creates child beads with titles matching `[merge-gate] <slug>`, `[verify] <slug> - <ac-line>`, and `[escalate] <slug>` (pre-Y escalation beads excluded).
6. [m] The finalize-step idempotency probes (step 5a merge-gate probe, step 5b human-verify probe) are updated to match the new title patterns.
7. [m] No existing open step beads or child beads are renamed.
8. [m] Grep verification: `grep -c 'title-slug' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/*.formula.toml` returns ≥ 5.
9. [m] Grep verification: `grep -cE '^\[vars\.title-slug\]' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/*.formula.toml` returns ≥ 5.
10. [m] Grep verification: `grep -cE 'title = "\[[a-z-]+\] \{\{title-slug\}\}"' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/*.formula.toml` returns a count equal to the total number of `[[steps]]` across all five formulas.
11. [m] Grep verification: `grep -c 'title-slug' src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md` returns ≥ 1; same for `implement-bead/SKILL.md`.

## Open questions

1. **Reroute-created merge-gate children (M2 from RALF cycle 2):** `implement-feature` and `fix-bug` contain red-tests→docs-only reroute paths that may create merge-gate child beads. The title format for these reroute beads should be verified during implementation to ensure consistency with the `[merge-gate] <title-slug>` convention.
2. **Preflight branch naming unification (m2 from RALF cycle 2):** The `implement-feature`/`fix-bug`/`docs-only` preflight steps compute `<slug-from-title>` for git branch names (e.g., `feat/{{bead-id}}-<slug-from-title>`). Unifying this with `{{title-slug}}` is a future refinement not in this bead's scope.
