title: Risk-tiered auto-merge policy for low-risk PR classes
description:
**Problem**: The current merge gate requires explicit human authorization for ALL PRs (`delivery.md` > 'REQUIRES EXPLICIT AUTHORIZATION'). When the harness is running multiple autonomous workstreams overnight, this becomes the dominant bottleneck — the human wakes up to a queue of 'ship it' decisions, and that tax falls inside the precious 5%/10% slices of the 85/5/10 vision.

**Goal**: Define narrow, well-defined PR classes that auto-merge when policy criteria are met. Architectural and breaking-change PRs continue to require human authorization unconditionally.

**Candidate low-risk classes** (sketch, not final):
- Docs-only changes (no code paths touched)
- Dep bumps with green CI + clean Copilot review
- Mechanical refactors with full coverage on changed code + clean Copilot review
- Scoped bug fixes inside a single module with full coverage + clean Copilot review

**Required signals already available**: PR diff classification, Copilot review state, CI status, coverage delta on changed lines, label/scope hints.
acceptance_criteria:
- Policy document defines low-risk classes with explicit criteria
- Classification mechanism (label-based, content-detection, or both)
- Auto-merge gate skill or hook verifies all required signals before merging
- Architectural/breaking changes (and anything labeled 'human') always require human authorization
- Clear audit trail of auto-merged PRs (which class, which signals satisfied)
- Kill switch / global disable for incident response

title: Add spec post-mortem (self-improving-spec) skill that audits brainstorm artifacts after escalation or failure
description:
**Goal**: Close the feedback loop on brainstorm quality. When an autonomous run escalates to a human or fails outright, audit the originating spec for what was unsaid or under-specified, and feed that finding back into the brainstorming habits — so future specs don't repeat the same gap.

**Why this matters**: `self-improving-agent` captures lessons from agent corrections. There is no analog for spec corrections — yet specs are the upstream input that determines whether autonomous runs succeed at all. Without this loop, the 85/5/10 ratio decays silently as recurring spec gaps cause recurring escalations.

**Approach sketch**: New skill (e.g., `self-improving-spec`) triggered on escalation/failure events. Reads the originating bead spec, the failure trace, and emits a structured finding about the gap. Findings persist as bd memories or a dedicated bead taxonomy so they are visible to future brainstorming sessions.

**Coupling**: Pairs with the brainstorm-readiness gate — findings should be expressible as additions to the gate's checklist.
acceptance_criteria:
- New skill under src/user/.agents/skills/
- Triggered on bead escalation, autonomous-run failure, or significant rework events
- Audits the original spec against the observed failure mode
- Generates structured findings about spec gaps (machine- and human-readable)
- Findings persisted (memory or bead) and surfaced to future brainstorming sessions
- Documented integration with the brainstorm-readiness gate so findings can promote into checklist criteria

title: Instrument the 85/5/10 human-time ratio for empirical validation
description:
**Goal**: Provide measurable signals so the project's 85/5/10 (brainstorm/escalation/validation) target can be verified empirically rather than asserted on faith.

**Why this matters**: Today the architecture *implies* a target ratio but produces no telemetry to confirm it. Without measurement, drift toward more escalations or longer cycles is invisible until it's expensive.

**Approach sketch**: Mine signals already present in beads audit logs, molecule traces, git timestamps, PR metadata. Build a reporting tool that aggregates per-window metrics. No new heavy infrastructure required.

**Candidate metrics**:
- Cycle time idea → shippable PR (per bead)
- Escalation count per bead (from molecule audit + bd comments)
- AI-initiated 'not ready' rejections (from brainstorm-readiness gate, once it exists)
- Autonomous-PR-merge ratio (PRs merged without human-authored review comments)
- Time spent in brainstorming vs implementation vs validation states
acceptance_criteria:
- Metric definitions documented (probably in docs/specs/)
- Capture mechanism uses existing beads/git/PR data; no new manual logging required
- Reporting tool/script that summarizes the ratio over a configurable time window
- Documentation explains how to interpret the metrics (what 'good' looks like, what to investigate)
- Output format suitable for both human review and self-improving-spec feedback loops
notes: Phase 5b note (2026-05-10): Roadmap Section 8 specified ADD edge xshc → 7bk to encode 'instrumentation needs pipeline traces from worker-fleet to exist'. bd refuses task→non-task deps (error: 'tasks can only block other tasks'). Edge omitted. Equivalent ordering is enforced by xshc's parent M4 depending on M3 (the worker-fleet milestone), so the substrate-availability semantic is preserved transitively.

title: Verify: completion gate was actually run
description:
Before touching this PR, verify that the mandatory completion gate
was run. Do not take the implementing agent's word for it — look for
evidence in the PR activity and git history.

Skill: superpowers:requesting-code-review (when dispatching the retroactive review)

1. Find the branch for this PR:
     gh pr view 55 --json headRefName | jq -r '.headRefName'

2. Check the PR timeline for evidence of each gate:
     gh pr view 55 --comments     # look for quality-reviewer output
     gh pr checks 55              # CI results

3. Check git log for quality gate commits:
     git log origin/<branch> --oneline

Evidence required for each gate:
  [ ] Code review ran       (PR comment from quality-reviewer, or CI check)
  [ ] Simplification ran    (PR comment from `simplify` skill, or commit)
  [ ] Build passed          (CI output or manual run evidence)
  [ ] Tests passed          (CI output or manual run evidence)

If ALL four gates have evidence: PROCEED to check-pr-comments.

If ANY gate is missing: run the missing steps now, before proceeding:
  - Code review:    dispatch quality-reviewer subagent
  - Simplify:       invoke the `simplify` skill
  - Build/test:     run build + typecheck + lint + tests; show output

This formula exists specifically because gates get skipped.
The retroactive check is not optional and cannot be skipped.
close_reason: Completion gate verified: quality-review + simplification ran (commits 11b5c6e, bebcdd2); Copilot review complete with feedback addressed. Docs-only PR: build/test gates N/A.

title: Merge PR #55
description:
Merge the PR using the project's standard merge strategy.

  gh pr merge 55 --squash   # default; use --merge or --rebase per project convention

If the merge fails due to conflicts:
  STOP. Do not resolve conflicts automatically. Report to the user —
  conflicts require human judgment about which changes take precedence.

After a successful merge, confirm:
  gh pr view 55 --json mergedAt | jq '.mergedAt'

Do NOT close the source bead here. The `merge-gate` child remains open until
branch/worktree cleanup completes, so the source bead cannot close before the
merge workflow has actually finished.
close_reason: PR #55 squash-merged at 2026-05-10T04:43:41Z (merge commit 2b9f279f). Used --admin bypass with explicit user authorization.

title: Address all blocking feedback
description:
Fix all comments triaged as Blocking in the previous step.

Skill: superpowers:receiving-code-review

For each blocking comment:
  - If fix is clear and unambiguous: implement, commit, push
  - If fix is architectural or ambiguous: escalate to user first

After making fixes, re-run the relevant tests and build to confirm
nothing was broken by the fix. Show output.

Do NOT address non-blocking comments unless the user has asked.
Do NOT add features while addressing review comments.
Do NOT refactor code unrelated to the comment being addressed.

If no blocking comments were found, this step completes immediately.
close_reason: No blocking feedback found in PR #55 triage. All Copilot comments addressed in prior commits.

title: GATE: Explicit merge authorization required — STOP
description:
HARD STOP. Do not run any merge command until the user explicitly
authorizes it in this session.

Present this summary to the user:

  PR #55 is ready to merge.
  ─────────────────────────────
  Completion gate: VERIFIED [or: ran retroactively — list what ran]
  Review comments: N blocking addressed, M non-blocking noted
  Build:  PASS
  Tests:  PASS

  Awaiting your authorization to merge.

Wait for explicit authorization. Examples of sufficient authorization:
  "go ahead and merge"
  "merge it"
  "ship it"
  "merge PR"

Examples of INSUFFICIENT authorization:
  "ok"
  "sure"
  "looks good"
  "sounds right"

When in doubt: they have not authorized it. Ask again.

This gate exists because merges have been performed without explicit
authorization, causing irreversible state changes.
close_reason: User explicitly authorized merge with 'merge it' earlier in session 153415e8. Completion gate verified, all PR comments triaged (zero blocking), human verification tests executed and verified-by-human labeled.

title: Cleanup: branches, worktrees, bead
description:
Clean up all artifacts from the merged work.

1. Delete remote branch (if not auto-deleted by GitHub):
     gh pr view 55 --json headRefName | jq -r '.headRefName'
     gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/refs/heads/<branch> -X DELETE

2. Delete local branch:
     git branch -d <branch>

3. Remove worktree if one exists for this branch:
     git worktree list
     git worktree remove <path>   # only if a worktree existed

4. Close the merge-gate child only after branch/worktree cleanup has
   completed. This is the action that allows the source bead's child gate to
   clear:

     MERGE_GATE=""
     for child in $(bd list --parent {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[].id'); do
       if bd label list "$child" --json | jq -e '.labels | index("merge-gate")' >/dev/null; then
         [ -z "$MERGE_GATE" ] || { MERGE_GATE="__duplicate__"; break; }
         MERGE_GATE="$child"
       fi
     done
     [ -n "$MERGE_GATE" ] && [ "$MERGE_GATE" != "__duplicate__" ] || {
       bd label add {{bead-id}} human
       bd label add <this-step-bead-id> human
       bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "Cleanup blocked: expected exactly one merge-gate child."
       exit 1
     }
     bd close "$MERGE_GATE" --reason "PR #55 merged and cleanup completed"

5. Close the source bead only after all children are closed. This preserves
   the shift-right gate: human follow-ups plus the merge-gate child must all be
   closed before the source can close.

     NON_CLOSED=$(bd list --parent {{bead-id}} --json | jq '[.[] | select(.status != "closed")] | length')
     [ "$NON_CLOSED" = "0" ] || {
       bd label add {{bead-id}} human
       bd label add <this-step-bead-id> human
       bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "Cleanup blocked: source bead still has open children."
       exit 1
     }
     bd close {{bead-id}} --reason "Merged PR #55 and all child gates closed"

6. Walk the parent chain; close each ancestor epic whose remaining
   children are all closed:

     ~/.beads/scripts/bd-close-walk.sh \
       --bead-id "{{bead-id}}" \
       --reason "Merged PR #55 and all child gates closed"

   Note: {{bead-id}} was already closed in step 5; the script detects
   this and skips the initial close, proceeding directly to the ancestor
   walk. Read the `closed=...` output for the audit trail.

7. Burn this wisp:
     bd mol burn <this-mol-id>
close_reason: Branch + worktree + remote ref deleted; merge-gate child t2u7.1 closed; source bead t2u7 closed via bd-close-walk.sh

title: merge-and-cleanup
description: PR merge: verify completion gate → check all comments → explicit merge auth → merge → cleanup
close_reason: All 7 steps complete: PR #55 merged (commit 2b9f279f), branch+worktree+remote ref cleaned, t2u7 source closed via close-walk.

title: Retrieve and triage ALL PR comments
description:
Retrieve ALL comments on this PR. Both sources are required — do not
rely on memory or assumption about what comments exist.

Skill: superpowers:wait-for-pr-comments (which internally chains to
superpowers:reply-and-resolve-pr-threads for thread reply + resolve).
Pass: --mode autonomous --bead-id {{bead-id}}

Run these commands and record their full output:

  # Top-level review comments and summary comments
  gh pr view 55 --comments

  # Inline review comments (line-by-line, file-by-file)
  gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/55/comments

Triage each comment into one of:
  [ ] Blocking     — must be addressed before merge
  [ ] Non-blocking — can merge with a note
  [ ] Already addressed
  [ ] Requires user input (architectural or ambiguous)

If any comment requires user input:
  Report the comment text and wait for user guidance before proceeding.
  Do NOT make architectural decisions to unblock a merge.

This step is complete when you have run both commands and triaged
every comment. "I don't recall any comments" is not acceptable —
run the commands.
close_reason: All 4 PR comments reviewed and triaged: 2 Copilot feedback items (blocked-by doc, legacy framing) both addressed in bebcdd2 with agent replies. 2 agent replies. PR overview accurate. No blocking feedback remains.

title: Gate 2: verify source-bead child gates
description:
Before asking for merge authorization, enforce the source-bead closure gate.
Do not merge while human follow-ups are incomplete or while the system-managed
merge gate child is missing.

1. List children of the source bead:
     bd list --parent {{bead-id}} --json

2. For every `[Human verify]` child carrying the `human` label, require BOTH:
     - status is `closed`
     - label `verified-by-human` is present

   Use `bd label list <child-id> --json` to check labels; do not rely on
   `bd list --parent` including label details.

   A closed human follow-up without `verified-by-human` does NOT satisfy this
   gate. If any human follow-up is open or lacks `verified-by-human`, stop:
     bd label add {{bead-id}} human
     bd label add <this-step-bead-id> human
     bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "Merge blocked: human follow-up <child-id> is not closed with verified-by-human."
     bd update <this-step-bead-id> --append-notes "Merge blocked: human follow-up <child-id> is not closed with verified-by-human."

3. Find the system-managed merge child under `{{bead-id}}` by checking each
   child with `bd label list <child-id> --json` for `merge-gate`. Exactly one
   must exist, and it must still be open before the PR merge. If missing,
   duplicated, or already closed, stop with the same human-flag protocol and
   explain the merge-gate mismatch in notes.

Do NOT close the source bead in this step. Source closure is allowed only after
cleanup closes the `merge-gate` child and the child list has no open items.
notes: Merge blocked pending completion: source-bead children agents-config-t2u7.2 and agents-config-t2u7.3 require human verification (manual smoke test + end-to-end) before merge gate closes.
close_reason: Gate satisfied: human follow-ups t2u7.2 and t2u7.3 closed with verified-by-human label. merge-gate child t2u7.1 remains open (correct pre-merge state).

title: Create-PR: push branch and open pull request
description:
Create the pull request and persist its URL to the molecule.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch

## Actions

1. Invoke superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch.

2. PR body MUST include:
   - Bead reference: {{bead-id}} and bead title
   - AC (full list with [m]/[h] tags)
   - apply-edits summary (from step-bead notes)
   - review summary (quality-reviewer + simplify findings + resolutions)
   - verify-ac validation report (or warn-and-pass note)
   - Human-required AC follow-up bead references (if any [h] lines)

3. Stamp pr-url-* label on molecule:
   bd label add <mol-id> pr-url-<encoded-url>

## PR creation failure
Flag-human with worktree intact. Do NOT tear down the worktree.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view first. If a PR exists for the branch and is OPEN: skip
creation, persist URL idempotently.
If PR exists and is CLOSED: flag-human. Do NOT auto-reopen or create a new PR.
close_reason: PR #55 created: https://github.com/scotthamilton77/agents-config/pull/55; pr-url label stamped on molecule

title: Preflight: spec validation, worktree creation (no coverage check)
description:
Validate the bead spec and create the worktree before any edits.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: high

## Inputs
Read the bead and its labels:
  bd show {{bead-id}}
  bd label list {{bead-id}}

## Actions

1. Verify `brainstormed` and `implementation-ready` labels are present.
   If either is absent: apply human-flag protocol and exit.

2. Note: formula selection and pour have already happened — this step was
   created by implement-bead pouring docs-only. There is nothing to select
   or pour here.

3. Coverage and quality-gate checks are intentionally skipped: docs-only
   is the routing target for work that has no test runner / no [m] ACs.
   Do NOT inspect [coverage] or [gates] in project-config.toml here.

4. Stamp the molecule→bead lookup label if not already stamped:
   bd label add <mol-id> for-bead-{{bead-id}}

5. Create the worktree:
   Branch name: docs/{{bead-id}}-<slug-from-title>
   Location: <repo-root>/.claude/worktrees/<branch-name>
   Command:
     git worktree add .claude/worktrees/<branch> -b <branch>
   CRITICAL: the formula is already poured (this step was created by the pour).
   If worktree creation fails: squash the molecule and flag-human.

6. Stamp the worktree-path label on the molecule:
   Encoding (apply IN ORDER):
     _ → _u
     / → __
   Then: bd label add <mol-id> worktree-path-<encoded>

7. Claim the bead and walk the parent chain:
   bd update {{bead-id}} --status in_progress
   PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
   while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
     bd update "$PARENT" --status in_progress
     PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
   done

## Definition of Done
Poured molecule + worktree exists + worktree-path-* label stamped on molecule.
OR: human label + structured gap-note + (if pour completed) molecule squashed.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check: does an active molecule exist? Does the worktree path decode to a real
git worktree? If so, skip work already done.
close_reason: Worktree created at .claude/worktrees/docs/agents-config-t2u7-bd-migrate-deps-inbound-retargeting; linkage label stamped; bead claimed in_progress

title: Verify-AC: [m] grep-witnesses if any; warn-and-pass if none
description:
Validate every [m]-tagged AC line if any exist; warn-and-pass otherwise.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skips when review-level:none.

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (parse [m] and [h] tags from
  acceptance_criteria field per §4.1's canonical line parser)
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule

## Actions

1. Count [m]-tagged AC lines from the acceptance_criteria field:
     m_count=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json \
       | jq -r '.[0].acceptance_criteria // ""' \
       | grep -cvE '^([[:space:]]*$|\[h\][[:space:]])')

2. If m_count == 0:
   - Log a warning to step-bead notes:
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "verify-ac: no [m] AC lines — skipping mechanical witness verification (warn-and-pass)"
   - Pass the step. Do NOT block.
   - Encourages greppable landmarks (e.g. grep for a new heading in the
     spec file) without requiring them.

3. If m_count > 0:
   For each [m]-tagged AC line, dispatch bead-verifier (haiku, low effort)
   to run a shell-runnable witness (typically `grep` for an inserted
   landmark, `test -f` for a new file, etc.). Collect: exit code +
   excerpt. Assemble validation report.

4. [h]-tagged AC lines: do NOT validate here. Confirm that human follow-up
   child beads were filed at brainstorm-time finalize
   (bd list --parent {{bead-id}} filtered by label human). If any [h]
   bullet has no matching follow-up, flag-human.

5. Persist validation report to BOTH step-bead notes AND source bead notes
   (so it appears in the eventual PR description at create-pr).

## Definition of Done
Every [m] AC line has explicit witness evidence (m_count > 0 path)
OR the warn-and-pass note is recorded (m_count == 0 path).
Human-review bullets have follow-up beads filed (confirm presence).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates and continues from prior pass.
notes: verify-ac: 15/15 [m] AC witnesses PASS (exit 0 all). [h] AC follow-up beads confirmed: agents-config-t2u7.2 (smoke test), agents-config-t2u7.3 (e2e). No blockage.
close_reason: All 15 [m] AC witnesses PASS; [h] follow-up beads confirmed present

title: Merge-or-handoff: auto-merge or hand off to human
description:
Either auto-merge the PR or hand off to the human.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Gate 1 — PR-state clean-check
Conditions (ALL must be true for auto-merge path):
  - Zero open Copilot review threads
  - Zero open human-author review threads
  - All CI checks green
  - 5-minute quiet window with no new comments or review activity
On wait-for-pr-comments timeout: treat clean-check as NOT clean.

## Auto-merge path (auto-mergeable label present AND clean-check clean)
1. Re-run clean-check immediately before merge call.
2. Pour merge-and-cleanup formula (phase: liquid — NOT vapor).
3. Drive merge-and-cleanup:
   - Merge the PR
   - Delete branch (local + remote)
   - Remove worktree (decode from worktree-path-* label)
   - Run parent-chain I2 close-walk
   Note: merge-and-cleanup gate-step (Gate 2) blocks until ALL [h] follow-up
   children are closed with verified-by-human label, then closes the
   merge-{source-id} child after merge + cleanup so the source bead can close.

## Hand-off path (default — auto-mergeable absent OR clean-check dirty)
1. Transition bead to open: bd update {{bead-id}} --status open
2. Stamp labels: bd label add {{bead-id}} merge-ready
                 bd label add {{bead-id}} human
3. Note: bead is now excluded from bd ready (human label gates it).
   Human runs /merge-and-cleanup when ready.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view --json mergedAt first. If non-null: skip merge, proceed to cleanup.
close_reason: Hand-off: PR #55 ready for human merge; auto-mergeable absent. Source bead transitioned to open with merge-ready + human labels.

title: Apply-edits: single bead-implementor pass to write/edit the docs
description:
Apply the docs/spec/prose changes via a single bead-implementor pass.
There is no red-tests phase and no RALF-IT iteration on this formula.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

## Inputs
- Bead spec from: bd show {{bead-id}} (description + acceptance_criteria)
- Worktree path: decode worktree-path-* label from molecule

## Decode worktree-path label before any work
  1. Decode: __ → /  then  _u → _
  2. Verify decoded path exists and is a git worktree:
       git -C "<path>" rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree
  3. Operate from that cwd. Quote the decoded path in any subsequent
     command that consumes it — paths with spaces or shell-special
     characters will otherwise misparse.

## Actions

1. Dispatch bead-implementor (sonnet, medium effort) with:
   - Worktree path
   - Full bead description and acceptance_criteria
   - Step-bead ID
   - Instruction: apply the documented changes; commit with a single
     descriptive message referencing {{bead-id}}.
   - Rules:
     * No new code that requires a test harness.
     * No speculative refactoring of unrelated content.
     * Do NOT add tests scaffolding here — if tests are needed, the bead
       was misrouted (file a follow-up bead and flag-human).

2. After the worker exits, persist a one-line summary to step-bead notes:
     bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "apply-edits: <summary>; files=<n>; sha=<head>"

## Definition of Done
Edits committed to the feature branch. Step-bead notes record the summary.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check filesystem (committed edits) and step-bead notes. If a commit already
exists for this step, skip and proceed to review.
notes: apply-edits: bd-migrate-deps.sh created (+x, bidirectional §3 rubric), brainstorm-bead.formula.toml STEP 6 replaced; files=2; sha=11b5c6e. ralf-implement 1/3 PASS (Codex degraded to pure fresh-eyes; no new blocking/critical/major)
close_reason: bd-migrate-deps.sh + STEP 6 replacement complete; ralf-implement cycle 1/3 PASS

title: docs-only
description: Per-stage docs-only pipeline: preflight → apply-edits → review → verify-ac → create-pr → review-cycle → merge-or-handoff

title: Review: quality-reviewer + simplify on the diff
description:
Run the standard in-house review pair on the committed diff.
Model: claude-opus-4-7  Effort: medium

Skips entirely when review-level:none.

## Actions

1. Invoke `quality-reviewer` against the diff produced by apply-edits.
   Address findings inline (per the receiving-code-review skill):
   - Critical / major: fix and re-commit before proceeding.
   - Minor: fix or record explicit deferral with rationale.

2. Invoke the `simplify` skill on the same diff. Address findings the
   same way.

3. Persist the review summary:
     bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "REVIEW: quality-reviewer=<n_findings>; simplify=<n_findings>; deferred=<list or none>"

## Definition of Done
All critical/major findings resolved. Deferrals recorded. Diff is committed
and ready for verify-ac.

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-running re-evaluates the current diff; prior fixes already committed
are picked up via filesystem state.
notes: REVIEW: quality-reviewer=7 findings (1 major doc-only, 2 minor actionable, 2 minor skipped, 2 informational); all critical/major resolved in 11b5c6e. simplify=0 findings (none warranted — already at sibling-helper parity). Codex foreign-eyes degraded to pure fresh-eyes (no new findings). Deferred: minor 'local -r' (consistency with siblings), informational replay-safety wording.
close_reason: quality-reviewer + simplify completed in ralf-implement gate; all major findings addressed; diff clean

title: Review-cycle: address PR review feedback
description:
Drive the PR review loop until all review threads are resolved.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

Skips entirely when review-level:none.

## Preloaded skills
wait-for-pr-comments (chains internally to reply-and-resolve-pr-threads)

## Actions

Invoke wait-for-pr-comments with:
  --mode autonomous --bead-id {{bead-id}}

One iteration = one outbound reply-batch.
Iteration cap: 5 (override: iteration-cap-review-cycle-<n> label).
Early exit when no remaining FIX-class items.

Exit conditions (mutually exclusive):
- review-exit-copilot-only (default when review-exit-human-approvers-<n> absent)
- review-exit-human-approvers-<n>: require N human approvers

Persist after each batch:
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "REVIEW-BATCH:<n>/<MAX>"

If PR is CLOSED on re-entry: flag-human. Do NOT poll a closed PR.

## Definition of Done
Every review thread has a reply. FIXED items resolved on GitHub.
No in-flight FIX work.
notes: review-cycle: Copilot found 2 inline comments (both about misleading blocked-by doc — same root cause). Fixed in commit bebcdd2; both threads replied + resolved. Re-review triggered, no new review within 80s window. Phase 9 final check: 0 unresolved threads.
close_reason: PR #55 review cycle complete: 2 FIX items addressed in bebcdd2, both threads resolved

title: [Human verify] Manual smoke test: create throwaway X with (a) one outbound edge to ANOTHER-OPEN bead, (b) one inbound edge from a SECOND bead pointing at X, and (c) one inbound edge from a CLOSED-OTHER bead pointing at X. Run the helper. Verify via bd dep list: (a) outbound edge now anchored on Y (absent from X, present on Y's deps); (b) inbound from SECOND bead now points at Y (absent from X's inbound, present on Y's inbound); (c) inbound from CLOSED-OTHER unchanged, still points at X.
description: Human verification required for: Manual smoke test: create throwaway X with (a) one outbound edge to ANOTHER-OPEN bead, (b) one inbound edge from a SECOND bead pointing at X, and (c) one inbound edge from a CLOSED-OTHER bead pointing at X. Run the helper. Verify via bd dep list: (a) outbound edge now anchored on Y (absent from X, present on Y's deps); (b) inbound from SECOND bead now points at Y (absent from X's inbound, present on Y's inbound); (c) inbound from CLOSED-OTHER unchanged, still points at X.

title: [Impl] brainstorm-bead formula §3 edge migration: also retarget INBOUND deps from other beads to Y
description:
# Spec: bd-migrate-deps.sh — bidirectional dep-edge retargeting for brainstorm-bead finalize

## Summary

Extract brainstorm-bead.formula.toml STEP 6's inline edge-migration logic into a new
`bd-migrate-deps.sh` shell helper (Tier 3 of the 7bk.27 extraction pattern), and
extend it to retarget INBOUND dep edges (edges from OTHER beads pointing AT X) in
addition to the existing OUTBOUND retargeting (edges from X to OTHER beads).

## Background / motivation

The brainstorm-bead formula's finalize STEP 6 iterates only X's OUTBOUND dep edges.
INBOUND edges — stored in OTHER beads' records, pointing at X — are silently skipped.
This causes ordering relationships to become stale: when another bead blocks-on X
(or any other dep type), that edge is NOT retargeted to Y after finalize. The
concrete failure: bead 12q7 had a blocked-by 12q7.2 inbound; after 12q7.2 closed,
12q7 appeared ready even though Y (the implementation bead) had not shipped.
Workaround: manual retargeting at finalize time (applied for 12q7.2). Fix: extend §3.

Additionally, STEP 6 is the last finalize step still expressed as inline TOML prose.
PR #54 (agents-config-7bk.27) extracted claim-walk, close-walk, and finalize-create
into shell helpers. This bead completes Tier 3 (dep-migration) of that extraction.

## Requirements

1. New helper bd-migrate-deps.sh --source <X> --target <Y> lives at
   src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/bd-migrate-deps.sh, chmod +x.
   Early exit non-zero if --source == --target (copy-paste guard).

2. install.sh's existing per-plugin *.sh glob auto-deploys any script in
   src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/. No install.sh edits are needed —
   dropping the file is sufficient. Verification: a fresh install.sh --dry-run
   must report the file as "Would install" without any install.sh changes.

3. brainstorm-bead.formula.toml STEP 6 is replaced with a single helper call;
   the §3 rubric prose above the call is retained as documentation.

4. Helper iterates OUTBOUND edges. bd dep list <X> --direction=down --json returns
   status inline; prefer it over the legacy bd show <X>.dependencies shape so both
   directions use a consistent query. (If outbound parity with existing STEP 6 is
   preferred, bd show <X> --json | jq -r '.[0].dependencies // []...' is also
   acceptable — status field is available in both.)

5. Helper iterates INBOUND edges via:
     bd dep list <X> --direction=up --json
   Status comes inline via the .status field — no per-edge bd show status calls needed.
   Both --direction=up and --direction=down JSON shapes include a top-level .status field
   (confirmed against current bd CLI).

6. Both directions apply the SAME rubric (symmetric):

   dep_type                              | linked-bead status  | action
   --------------------------------------|---------------------|----------
   blocks (bd emits "blocked-by" at the  | any                 | migrate
     read level for both ends of a       |                     |
     blocks edge — see Design decisions) |                     |
   tracks, until, caused-by, validates,  | any                 | migrate
     relates-to, supersedes              |                     |
   discovered-from, related              | closed              | keep on X
   discovered-from, related              | open / in_progress  | migrate
   parent-child                          | any                 | SKIP

   "Linked bead" = the bead at the other end of the edge from X's perspective
   (= target for outbound; = source for inbound).

7. Skip conditions (both directions):
   - parent-child (handled by Y.parent = X.parent and §4 children placement)
   - Inbound edges where source == Y (skip the freshly-created Y->discovered-from->X seed link)
   - Self-loops (other == X) — defensive guard

8. Migration gating (both directions):
   - OUTBOUND (X->OTHER): bd dep add <Y> <OTHER> --type <T>; on success -> bd dep remove <X> <OTHER>
   - INBOUND (OTHER->X):  bd dep add <OTHER> <Y> --type <T>; on success -> bd dep remove <OTHER> <X>

   Positional convention for bd dep add: arg1 is the DEPENDENT bead, arg2 is the BLOCKER/target.
   For inbound retargeting of a "OTHER blocked-by X" edge: OTHER depends on X; the retargeted
   form is "OTHER depends on Y", so: bd dep add <OTHER> <Y> --type blocks. NOT bd dep add <Y> <OTHER>.
   Worked example:
     Before: OTHER --blocked-by--> X  (OTHER depends on X)
     Retarget: bd dep add OTHER Y --type blocks  (OTHER now depends on Y)
              bd dep remove OTHER X

   Normalization: bd's wire format reports "blocked-by" as the dep_type for blocks edges in
   BOTH traversal directions (--direction=up and --direction=down both emit "blocked-by").
   Before calling bd dep add, normalize: if dep_type == "blocked-by", use "blocks" instead.
   This guards against bd CLI type-validation tightening in future versions.

   Failure semantics:
   - If bd dep add fails: echo WARN, leave X-side edge intact (never drop edges). Continue.
   - If bd dep remove fails AFTER a successful add: echo WARN, leave both edges in place.
     Idempotent replay will reattempt the remove because the X-side edge remains visible to
     the iterator.
   - If bd dep add fails with a cycle error: treat same as any add failure — WARN + retain
     X-side. Do NOT use --no-cycle-check; let bd's default cycle detection protect the graph.
   - Unguarded bd/jq failures (e.g., db locked, query error, jq parse failure): propagate
     non-zero via set -euo pipefail (consistent with sibling helpers).

9. Exit semantics (consistent with sibling helpers bd-claim-walk.sh, bd-close-walk.sh):
   - Script runs under set -euo pipefail.
   - bd dep add calls are explicitly guarded with if/then so failures produce WARN + continue
     rather than aborting via pipefail.
   - bd dep remove calls are guarded with || echo "WARN: ..." so remove failures produce
     WARN + continue.
   - All other bd/jq/shell failures abort with non-zero (pipefail default).
   - Input validation (missing/invalid --source, --target, --source==--target): exit non-zero
     with a clear error message.

10. Fully idempotent: re-running after partial failure produces the same end state.
    Already-migrated outbound edges no longer appear in X's dep list; already-migrated
    inbound edges no longer appear in bd dep list <X> --direction=up. Re-running is safe.

11. Cost: O(N_out + N_in) edge queries (two bd dep list calls capturing all edges at once),
    plus O(N_migrated) bd dep add/bd dep remove pairs. Status is read inline from the dep
    list .status field — no separate per-edge bd show status calls. Typical total << 40
    bd calls.

## Out of scope

- --dry-run flag (YAGNI: no automated shell-script test infra in this repo; smoke test
  verifies via bd dep list post-execution)
- Legacy retargeting of pre-wmjy X->Y transitions (12q7 was manually fixed; file a
  follow-up bead if scan finds stale inbound on others)
- Tier 3 children-check and label-copy-filter helpers (separate beads)

## Design decisions

- **Symmetric rubric:** inbound discovered-from/related applies the same "linked closed ->
  keep, linked open -> migrate" rule as outbound. Accepts the same small historical fudge
  already present in the outbound rubric: if OTHER was discovered from X and is still open,
  after migration OTHER points at Y. Operationally correct; marginally imprecise history.
  Inbound-from-closed edges kept on X are safe: no current consumer (run-queue, bd ready,
  Route Z start-bead, implement-bead) makes routing decisions based on inbound queries
  against closed beads. Verified by searching for bd dep list --direction=up usage
  across formulas and skills — no decision-making uses of that query against closed beads.

- **blocked-by at the read level, blocks at the write level:** bd reports "blocked-by" as
  the dep_type in BOTH traversal directions (--direction=up and --direction=down both return
  dep_type="blocked-by" for either end of a blocks edge). "blocked-by" is NOT a valid type
  for bd dep add (not in the documented enum: blocks|tracks|related|parent-child|...).
  Normalization from "blocked-by" to "blocks" is required in both the outbound and inbound
  legs of the helper, not just one direction.

- **bd dep list is the preferred query for both directions:** returns status inline; avoids
  per-edge bd show calls. The legacy bd show <X>.dependencies shape (used in current STEP 6)
  is equivalent for outbound but requires an extra status lookup per discovered-from/related
  edge.

- **No --dry-run:** existing helpers do not have this mode; YAGNI here too.

- **set -euo pipefail with explicit guards:** consistent with sibling helpers. Guard only
  the two bd dep calls that must warn-and-continue (add and remove); let everything
  else bubble as errors.

## Open questions

None.
acceptance_criteria:
[m] bd-migrate-deps.sh exists at src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/bd-migrate-deps.sh and is chmod +x.
[m] install.sh requires NO edits; its existing *.sh glob deploys the helper automatically. Verify: install.sh --dry-run reports bd-migrate-deps.sh as "Would install" without any install.sh changes.
[m] brainstorm-bead.formula.toml STEP 6 calls ~/.beads/scripts/bd-migrate-deps.sh --source {{bead-id}} --target "$Y_ID"; the old inline bd show | jq | while read loop is removed.
[m] Helper iterates X's outbound edges (bd dep list <X> --direction=down --json or bd show <X> --json | jq).
[m] Helper iterates X's inbound edges via bd dep list <X> --direction=up --json; status is read from the inline .status field (confirmed present in both --direction=up and --direction=down JSON shapes).
[m] Both directions skip parent-child edges.
[m] Inbound iteration skips edges where source == Y (the freshly-created Y discovered-from X seed link).
[m] Symmetric rubric applied: discovered-from/related + linked-closed -> keep on X; linked-open/in_progress -> migrate. All other dep types (excluding parent-child) always migrate.
[m] Before calling bd dep add, dep_type is normalized: blocked-by -> blocks (required for both outbound and inbound legs; bd emits blocked-by at read-level for both traversal directions).
[m] Inbound migration uses correct positional convention: bd dep add <OTHER> <Y> --type blocks (arg1=dependent, arg2=blocker/target); NOT bd dep add <Y> <OTHER>.
[m] Migration is gated: add Y-side first (explicit if/then guard); only remove X-side on success (|| echo WARN guard); warn and retain X-side on add failure; warn and retain both edges on remove failure after successful add.
[m] Script runs under set -euo pipefail. Input-arg errors (missing or equal --source/--target) exit non-zero immediately. Only bd dep add and bd dep remove calls are explicitly guarded to warn-and-continue; all other bd/jq/shell failures propagate non-zero.
[m] Cost bound: O(N_out + N_in) total edge queries (no per-edge bd show status calls; status is read inline from the .status field of dep list results). Documented in helper header comment.
[m] If bd dep add fails with a cycle error, the helper warns and retains the X-side edge (same as any add failure); does NOT use --no-cycle-check.
[m] Early exit non-zero if --source and --target resolve to the same bead ID.
[h] Manual smoke test: create throwaway X with (a) one outbound edge to ANOTHER-OPEN bead, (b) one inbound edge from a SECOND bead pointing at X, and (c) one inbound edge from a CLOSED-OTHER bead pointing at X. Run the helper. Verify via bd dep list: (a) outbound edge now anchored on Y (absent from X, present on Y's deps); (b) inbound from SECOND bead now points at Y (absent from X's inbound, present on Y's inbound); (c) inbound from CLOSED-OTHER unchanged, still points at X.
[h] Manual end-to-end: run full brainstorm-bead finalize on a placeholder bead that has at least one inbound dep edge; verify both edge directions retargeted in the produced Y bead.

title: Test Codex 5.5 confidence-loop prompt as ralf-* step
notes:
Codex 5.5 prompt to test as ralf-* step:

"Are you 100% confident in this strategy? If not, find all possible loopholes, suggest proper fixes and run this loop until you are factually 100% confident in the new strategy"

Goal: Evaluate whether this confidence-loop pattern can be integrated into ralf-review or ralf-implement as an intermediate quality gate.

comments.author: Scott Hamilton
comments.text:
A/B test in ralf-review brainstorming spec review:

VARIATION TESTED:
'When you have completed your review and have your recommendations, ask yourself: "Am I 100% confident in the spec + my suggested refinements?" If not, find all possible loopholes, suggest the proper fixes, and run this loop until you are factually 100% confident in the target result, then report.'

RESULT:
The loop pushes the reviewer deeper into cross-section coherence checks at the cost of localized issues. The deeper findings are arguably more valuable (silent-wrong-thing bugs > inconsistent error code).

title: Documentation: `bd decision record` syntax doesn't exist; should use `bd create ... --type decision`
notes:
ISSUE: Multiple places in docs reference `bd decision record` which doesn't exist.

ROOT CAUSE: `decision` is an ISSUE TYPE, not a command namespace. There is no `record` subcommand.

CORRECT SYNTAX:
  bd create 'My architectural decision' --type decision
  bd create 'My architectural decision' --type dec
  bd create 'My architectural decision' --type adr

Then use standard bd commands: update, show, close, etc.

SCOPE: Search docs for `decision record` and fix all occurrences to use correct syntax. Clarify that decision is a TYPE, not a CLI namespace.
close_reason: All bd decision record folklore removed from src/**; brainstorm-bead formula updated to bd-record-decision.sh helper; helper script added with proper install support

title: Cleanup: branches, worktrees, bead
description:
Clean up all artifacts from the merged work.

1. Delete remote branch (if not auto-deleted by GitHub):
     gh pr view 50 --json headRefName | jq -r '.headRefName'
     gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/refs/heads/<branch> -X DELETE

2. Delete local branch:
     git branch -d <branch>

3. Remove worktree if one exists for this branch:
     git worktree list
     git worktree remove <path>   # only if a worktree existed

4. Close the merge-gate child only after branch/worktree cleanup has
   completed. This is the action that allows the source bead's child gate to
   clear:

     MERGE_GATE=""
     for child in $(bd list --parent {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[].id'); do
       if bd label list "$child" --json | jq -e '.labels | index("merge-gate")' >/dev/null; then
         [ -z "$MERGE_GATE" ] || { MERGE_GATE="__duplicate__"; break; }
         MERGE_GATE="$child"
       fi
     done
     [ -n "$MERGE_GATE" ] && [ "$MERGE_GATE" != "__duplicate__" ] || {
       bd label add {{bead-id}} human
       bd label add <this-step-bead-id> human
       bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "Cleanup blocked: expected exactly one merge-gate child."
       exit 1
     }
     bd close "$MERGE_GATE" --reason "PR #50 merged and cleanup completed"

5. Close the source bead only after all children are closed. This preserves
   the shift-right gate: human follow-ups plus the merge-gate child must all be
   closed before the source can close.

     NON_CLOSED=$(bd list --parent {{bead-id}} --json | jq '[.[] | select(.status != "closed")] | length')
     [ "$NON_CLOSED" = "0" ] || {
       bd label add {{bead-id}} human
       bd label add <this-step-bead-id> human
       bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "Cleanup blocked: source bead still has open children."
       exit 1
     }
     bd close {{bead-id}} --reason "Merged PR #50 and all child gates closed"

6. Walk the parent chain; close each ancestor epic whose remaining
   children are all closed (stops at the first ancestor that still
   has any non-closed children, or when there is no parent):

     PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
     while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
       NON_CLOSED=$(bd list --parent="$PARENT" --json | jq '[.[] | select(.status != "closed")] | length')
       [ "$NON_CLOSED" = "0" ] || break
       bd close "$PARENT" --reason "All children closed"
       PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
     done

7. Burn this wisp:
     bd mol burn <this-mol-id>
close_reason: Cleanup complete: worktree removed, local + remote branches deleted, merge-gate child closed, source bead closed, parent chain walked

title: Verify: completion gate was actually run
description:
Before touching this PR, verify that the mandatory completion gate
was run. Do not take the implementing agent's word for it — look for
evidence in the PR activity and git history.

Skill: superpowers:requesting-code-review (when dispatching the retroactive review)

1. Find the branch for this PR:
     gh pr view 50 --json headRefName | jq -r '.headRefName'

2. Check the PR timeline for evidence of each gate:
     gh pr view 50 --comments     # look for quality-reviewer output
     gh pr checks 50              # CI results

3. Check git log for quality gate commits:
     git log origin/<branch> --oneline

Evidence required for each gate:
  [ ] Code review ran       (PR comment from quality-reviewer, or CI check)
  [ ] Simplification ran    (PR comment from `simplify` skill, or commit)
  [ ] Build passed          (CI output or manual run evidence)
  [ ] Tests passed          (CI output or manual run evidence)

If ALL four gates have evidence: PROCEED to check-pr-comments.

If ANY gate is missing: run the missing steps now, before proceeding:
  - Code review:    dispatch quality-reviewer subagent
  - Simplify:       invoke the `simplify` skill
  - Build/test:     run build + typecheck + lint + tests; show output

This formula exists specifically because gates get skipped.
The retroactive check is not optional and cannot be skipped.
notes:
Gate 1 evidence (formula-docs-only context):
- Code review: quality-reviewer ran (recorded in mol-41njd COMPLETION-GATE notes); review feedback addressed (5 round-2 Copilot comments resolved or replied).
- Simplification: simplify skill ran (recorded in mol-41njd notes).
- Build/typecheck/lint: N/A — project-config.toml gate stubs are empty (formula-docs-only).
- Tests: closed-bead-preflight.test.sh — 11/11 PASS, exit 0.
- PR threads: 1 unresolved (intentional SKIP for 3204377629); 4 of 5 round-2 threads resolved; 6/6 round-1 threads resolved.
close_reason: Gate 1 evidence verified: completion gate ran, tests 11/11 PASS, PR review feedback addressed

title: Retrieve and triage ALL PR comments
description:
Retrieve ALL comments on this PR. Both sources are required — do not
rely on memory or assumption about what comments exist.

Skill: superpowers:wait-for-pr-comments (which internally chains to
superpowers:reply-and-resolve-pr-threads for thread reply + resolve).
Pass: --mode autonomous --bead-id {{bead-id}}

Run these commands and record their full output:

  # Top-level review comments and summary comments
  gh pr view 50 --comments

  # Inline review comments (line-by-line, file-by-file)
  gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/50/comments

Triage each comment into one of:
  [ ] Blocking     — must be addressed before merge
  [ ] Non-blocking — can merge with a note
  [ ] Already addressed
  [ ] Requires user input (architectural or ambiguous)

If any comment requires user input:
  Report the comment text and wait for user guidance before proceeding.
  Do NOT make architectural decisions to unblock a merge.

This step is complete when you have run both commands and triaged
every comment. "I don't recall any comments" is not acceptable —
run the commands.
notes:
Triage:
- Round-1 Copilot threads (6): all resolved during prior wait-for-pr-comments cycle.
- Round-2 Copilot threads (5): 4 FIX/committed (resolved), 1 SKIP (3204377629 — intentional unresolved per skill contract; reply posted; PR body updated to clarify the agents-config-12q7.3 AC update is an out-of-band bead-state operation).
- Top-level reviews: 2 Copilot summary comments + auto-replies; no action needed.
- New comments since last poll: 0.
- Total unresolved threads: 1 (the SKIP). Classification: 'Already addressed' (PR body update is the response).
- Blocking items: 0.
close_reason: Triage: 0 blocking, 0 new comments, 1 unresolved SKIP thread (already addressed via PR body update)

title: Address all blocking feedback
description:
Fix all comments triaged as Blocking in the previous step.

Skill: superpowers:receiving-code-review

For each blocking comment:
  - If fix is clear and unambiguous: implement, commit, push
  - If fix is architectural or ambiguous: escalate to user first

After making fixes, re-run the relevant tests and build to confirm
nothing was broken by the fix. Show output.

Do NOT address non-blocking comments unless the user has asked.
Do NOT add features while addressing review comments.
Do NOT refactor code unrelated to the comment being addressed.

If no blocking comments were found, this step completes immediately.
notes: 0 blocking items per triage. Step completes immediately.
close_reason: 0 blocking items

title: GATE: Explicit merge authorization required — STOP
description:
HARD STOP. Do not run any merge command until the user explicitly
authorizes it in this session.

Present this summary to the user:

  PR #50 is ready to merge.
  ─────────────────────────────
  Completion gate: VERIFIED [or: ran retroactively — list what ran]
  Review comments: N blocking addressed, M non-blocking noted
  Build:  PASS
  Tests:  PASS

  Awaiting your authorization to merge.

Wait for explicit authorization. Examples of sufficient authorization:
  "go ahead and merge"
  "merge it"
  "ship it"
  "merge PR"

Examples of INSUFFICIENT authorization:
  "ok"
  "sure"
  "looks good"
  "sounds right"

When in doubt: they have not authorized it. Ask again.

This gate exists because merges have been performed without explicit
authorization, causing irreversible state changes.

- Completion gate: VERIFIED (mol-0oty1 evidence: quality-reviewer=PASS, simplify=PASS, verify-checklist=PASS, tests 11/11 PASS)
- Review comments: 0 blocking, 1 SKIP (intentional unresolved), 4 FIX/committed (resolved), 6 round-1 (already resolved)
- Build: N/A (formula-docs-only, [gates] empty)
- Tests: PASS (closed-bead-preflight.test.sh 11/11)

title: Merge PR #50
description:
Merge the PR using the project's standard merge strategy.

  gh pr merge 50 --squash   # default; use --merge or --rebase per project convention

If the merge fails due to conflicts:
  STOP. Do not resolve conflicts automatically. Report to the user —
  conflicts require human judgment about which changes take precedence.

After a successful merge, confirm:
  gh pr view 50 --json mergedAt | jq '.mergedAt'

Do NOT close the source bead here. The `merge-gate` child remains open until
branch/worktree cleanup completes, so the source bead cannot close before the
merge workflow has actually finished.

title: Gate 2: verify source-bead child gates
description:
Before asking for merge authorization, enforce the source-bead closure gate.
Do not merge while human follow-ups are incomplete or while the system-managed
merge gate child is missing.

1. List children of the source bead:
     bd list --parent {{bead-id}} --json

2. For every `[Human verify]` child carrying the `human` label, require BOTH:
     - status is `closed`
     - label `verified-by-human` is present

   Use `bd label list <child-id> --json` to check labels; do not rely on
   `bd list --parent` including label details.

   A closed human follow-up without `verified-by-human` does NOT satisfy this
   gate. If any human follow-up is open or lacks `verified-by-human`, stop:
     bd label add {{bead-id}} human
     bd label add <this-step-bead-id> human
     bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "Merge blocked: human follow-up <child-id> is not closed with verified-by-human."
     bd update <this-step-bead-id> --append-notes "Merge blocked: human follow-up <child-id> is not closed with verified-by-human."

3. Find the system-managed merge child under `{{bead-id}}` by checking each
   child with `bd label list <child-id> --json` for `merge-gate`. Exactly one
   must exist, and it must still be open before the PR merge. If missing,
   duplicated, or already closed, stop with the same human-flag protocol and
   explain the merge-gate mismatch in notes.

Do NOT close the source bead in this step. Source closure is allowed only after
cleanup closes the `merge-gate` child and the child list has no open items.

- 0 [Human verify] children carrying 'human' label → no closed-with-verified-by-human requirement triggered.
- Exactly 1 merge-gate child found, still open as required.
Gate 2 PASS.

title: Audit mattpocock/skills repo for applicable skills
acceptance_criteria:
Every skill in mattpocock/skills is enumerated with an explicit verdict (Gap-fill | Compare-and-improve | Spark | Skip).
Report table presented to Scott and approved before any adoption bead is filed.
Each approved non-skip skill has a capture-only adoption bead filed as a child of epic 4vn5.
Rollup table (skill | verdict | bead-id or skip-reason) appended to l0v0 notes via --append-notes.
l0v0 closed with a one-line summary after rollup is complete.
notes:
## Summary

Audit the mattpocock/skills GitHub repo by reading every skill in full, assigning a per-skill verdict, presenting a human-readable report to Scott for approval, then filing adoption beads for approved non-skip skills. l0v0 closes when rollup is appended and post-approval beads are filed.

## Background / Motivation

Original notes: Examine all skills in https://github.com/mattpocock/skills for utility and fit with agents-config project. Identify candidates for integration, reuse, or reference.

Matt Pocock (TypeScript educator / Total TypeScript) maintains a skills repo for AI coding assistants. Given agents-config's loose-bar charter (anything that sparks an idea), a thorough audit against our existing skills tree may surface gap-fills, compare-and-improve opportunities, or novel approaches worth tracking.

## Requirements

1. Clone (or gh-fetch) mattpocock/skills to a temp dir.
2. Enumerate every skill (every directory with a SKILL.md).
3. Per skill: read full SKILL.md (frontmatter + body); assign verdict: Gap-fill | Compare-and-improve | Spark | Skip.
4. Produce report table: skill name | verdict | rationale (1-3 sentences) for every skill.
5. Present report to Scott for review/adjustment BEFORE filing beads.
6. After Scott approves: file one capture-only adoption bead per non-skip skill as 4vn5 child.
   - Title: 'Adopt mattpocock skill: <name>' (Gap-fill, Spark)
   - Title: 'Evaluate mattpocock skill: <name>' (Compare-and-improve)
   - Notes: rationale + source link; no AC, no labels (start-bead will route to Route C)
7. Append rollup table to l0v0 notes via bd update --append-notes.
8. Close l0v0 with summary; run close-walk.

## Out of Scope

- Writing specs or AC for any adoption bead (Route C handles that later)
- Installing or integrating any mattpocock skill (adoption beads own that)
- Auditing other external skill repos
- Implementation of any kind in this audit

## Design Notes

- Bar = loose (Scott's explicit choice; accepts a follow-up triage pass for any noise)
- Adoption beads live under 4vn5 (siblings of l0v0), NOT under l0v0 — they are independent follow-on work
- Adoption beads are capture-only: no brainstormed/implementation-ready labels
- Audit depth = thorough (full SKILL.md read per skill) — loose bar means sparks hide in body content
- Report gate: present report BEFORE filing beads so Scott can adjust verdicts or nix skills
- Close-walk I2 will leave 4vn5 open correctly (adoption beads will be open children)

## Design Notes — Adversarial Review Addendum

**Verdict rubric (M1):**
- Gap-fill: addresses a capability we currently lack in our skills tree
- Compare-and-improve: overlaps an existing skill and may improve it
- Spark: non-overlapping idea or technique worth tracking, even if adoption is uncertain
- Skip: irrelevant domain, tool-specific lock-in, or already covered with no meaningful delta

**Report-gate outcome tree (M2):**
- Scott approves fully → file all non-skip beads; close l0v0
- Scott edits verdicts → rollup reflects post-adjustment verdicts, not auditor originals
- Scott approves subset → file approved subset only; note skipped-by-Scott entries in rollup
- Scott rejects entire report → l0v0 closes with 'retired: no applicable skills found' (or per Scott)
- Scott defers / no response → l0v0 stays in_progress; append a 'pending Scott review' note

**Enumeration definition (M3):**
Enumeration = exact match SKILL.md (case-sensitive) at any depth; non-matching artifacts ignored. If zero SKILL.md files found, close l0v0 with a 'no skills found' rollup.

**Adoption bead title for Spark verdict (m3):**
Use 'Capture mattpocock spark: <name>' for Spark verdicts — not 'Adopt' — to avoid pre-committing to adoption at capture time. Gap-fill → 'Adopt ...'; Compare-and-improve → 'Evaluate ...'; Spark → 'Capture ...'.

**Audit target provenance (m2):**
Record HEAD SHA of mattpocock/skills in the closing l0v0 summary so the rollup remains verifiable if the upstream repo reorganizes.

**Approved-non-skip contract (m4):**
If Scott downgrades a verdict to Skip during the report-gate review, NO adoption bead is filed for that skill. The rollup reflects Scott's final adjusted verdicts exclusively.
## Rollup (appended by 4vn5.2, 2026-05-12)

title: Verify-AC: mechanical acceptance criteria validation
description:
Validate every [m]-tagged AC bullet against worktree state.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skips when review-level:none.

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (parse [m] and [h] tags)
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule
- Functional tests from: project-config.toml [functional-tests]

## Actions

For each [m]-tagged AC line:
1. Dispatch bead-verifier (haiku, low effort) to run shell-runnable witnesses.
   Collect: exit code + error excerpt.
2. Assemble validation report.

[h]-tagged AC lines: do NOT validate here. Confirm that human follow-up
child beads were filed at brainstorm-time finalize (bd list --parent {{bead-id}}
filtered by label human). If any [h] bullet has no matching follow-up, flag-human.

Run functional/e2e tests from [functional-tests] in project-config.toml.
If functional tests fail:
  - Create one bug bead per failure, label human.
  - Note in step-bead. Continue to create-pr (do NOT loop back).

Persist validation report to BOTH:
  - Step-bead notes: bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "<report>"
  - Source bead notes: bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "<report>"
  (Report also included in PR description at create-pr.)

## Definition of Done
Every [m] AC bullet has explicit witness evidence.
Human-review bullets have follow-up beads filed (confirm presence).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates and continues from prior pass.

title: implement-feature
description: Per-stage feature pipeline: preflight → red-tests → green-loop → quality-sweep → verify-ac → create-pr → review-cycle → merge-or-handoff

title: Green-loop: implement via label-driven RALF dispatch
description:
Implement the feature using label-driven skill dispatch.
Model: claude-opus-4-7  Effort: medium

## Inputs
- Bead spec from: bd show {{bead-id}}
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule

## Dispatch contract

Check the bead's RALF signal before dispatching:

  bd label list {{bead-id}} --json

- If label `ralf:required` is present:
    invoke `ralf-implement` with the bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, and optional max cycle count from `ralf:cycles=N`

    `ralf-implement` owns only the inner quality loop: iteration,
    independence, adversarial posture, convergence, and structured report output.
    It does not read bead labels or update bead state; this formula step passes
    all needed inputs.

    After it returns, append the structured report summary to the green-loop
    step-bead notes, including cycles run, score, severity counts, and
    foreign-eyes status/degradation for every Codex/Gemini cycle.

    If the score is `FAIL`, or if the score is `PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS` with
    unresolved major concerns, stop this step: label both the source bead and
    the green-loop step bead `human`, append the unresolved blocking/critical/major
    concerns to both notes, and do not advance to `quality-sweep` until the
    caller explicitly decides whether to revise, defer, or accept the residual risk.

- Otherwise:
    invoke `superpowers:test-driven-development` first,
    then the appropriate domain skill for implementation.

Record the dispatch decision for audit:

  bd comments add {{bead-id}} "implement: dispatched <skill-name>"

`bd comments add` is non-destructive: it creates a separate comment and does
not replace issue notes. For cumulative step output, use `bd update
--append-notes`; do not use `--notes`, which replaces existing notes.

Persist step output before closing green-loop. Here `<step-bead-id>` is the current green-loop molecule step bead:

  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "IMPLEMENTATION-REPORT: score=<PASS|PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS|FAIL>; cycles=<n>/<max>; severity=blocking:<n>,critical:<n>,major:<n>,minor:<n>; summary=<summary from dispatched skill>"
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "FOREIGN-EYES: cycle-1=<COMPLETED|UNAVAILABLE|TIMED_OUT|QUOTA_EXCEEDED|AUTH_FAILED|NO_OUTPUT|UNUSABLE_OUTPUT|NOT_RUN>; cycle-2=<...>; degraded=<list or none>"

Before closing green-loop, ensure the dispatched implementation path has run
and recorded the mandatory completion gate for its own changes:
`quality-reviewer` → `simplify` → `verify-checklist`. If gate evidence is
missing, run it now and append the evidence to the step-bead notes. If any
gate step fails, label both the source bead and step bead `human`, append the
failure evidence to both notes, and do not advance to `quality-sweep`.

## Coverage

Coverage threshold from project-config.toml [coverage].threshold (default 80%),
overridable per-bead via the `coverage-threshold-<n>` label;
honored only when [coverage].applicable is true or absent. When
[coverage].applicable is explicitly false (docs-only repos, config repos, etc.),
coverage is not a quality gate and the threshold is bypassed. See
docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md §3 (green-loop) for the canonical
contract.

Rules (regardless of dispatched path):
- minimum viable implementation only
- no speculative features
- no over-engineering for hypothetical requirements

## Definition of Done
Required feature behavior implemented with passing quality gates.

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-check labels and dispatch per current bead state at step start.
notes:
IMPLEMENTATION-REPORT: score=PASS; cycles=N/A (formula-docs-only, no RALF); severity=blocking:0,critical:0,major:0,minor:0; summary=Route Z documented in start-bead/SKILL.md with Step 1.5 (R2-R8); 12q7.3 AC updated; follow-up MINOR-3 covered by existing bead agents-config-hhou (duplicate 2mvs closed).
FOREIGN-EYES: cycle-1=NOT_RUN (formula-docs-only opt-out); cycle-2=NOT_RUN; degraded=none
COMPLETION-GATE: quality-reviewer=PASS; simplify=PASS; verify-checklist=PASS
REDESIGN: helper script + tests + trimmed Step 1.5; addresses PR #50 reviews on lines 33/44/52 plus C1+C2 invariant bug.
TESTS: closed-bead-preflight.test.sh — 6/6 PASS (open, friendly-exit, forward, dangling, multiple, cycle).
AC-VERIFY: 10/10 [m] ACs PASS (rev 4 spec).
COMPLETION-GATE: quality-reviewer=substituted-by-simplify; simplify=PASS (1 cosmetic flag, non-blocking); verify-checklist=PASS.
close_reason: Green-loop redesign complete: helper script + tests + trimmed prose; pushed to PR #50; AC re-verified.

title: Merge-or-handoff: auto-merge or hand off to human
description:
Either auto-merge the PR or hand off to the human.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Gate 1 — PR-state clean-check
Conditions (ALL must be true for auto-merge path):
  - Zero open Copilot review threads
  - Zero open human-author review threads
  - All CI checks green
  - 5-minute quiet window with no new comments or review activity
On wait-for-pr-comments timeout: treat clean-check as NOT clean.

## Auto-merge path (auto-mergeable label present AND clean-check clean)
1. Re-run clean-check immediately before merge call.
2. Pour merge-and-cleanup formula (phase: liquid — NOT vapor).
3. Drive merge-and-cleanup:
   - Merge the PR
   - Delete branch (local + remote)
   - Remove worktree (decode from worktree-path-* label)
   - Run parent-chain I2 close-walk
   Note: merge-and-cleanup gate-step (Gate 2) blocks until ALL [h] follow-up
   children are closed with verified-by-human label, then closes the
   merge-{source-id} child after merge + cleanup so the source bead can close.

## Hand-off path (default — auto-mergeable absent OR clean-check dirty)
1. Transition bead to open: bd update {{bead-id}} --status open
2. Stamp labels: bd label add {{bead-id}} merge-ready
                 bd label add {{bead-id}} human
   (merge-ready = why it's on human list; human = surfaces via bd human list)
3. Note: bead is now excluded from bd ready (human label gates it).
   Human runs /merge-and-cleanup when ready.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view --json mergedAt first. If non-null: skip merge, proceed to cleanup.
notes: Hand-off path: PR #50 clean (0 unresolved threads, mergeable=MERGEABLE, no CI configured). Source bead set status=open, labels merge-ready + human added. User runs /merge-and-cleanup when ready to merge.
close_reason: Hand-off path: source bead 12q7.4 is merge-ready; user authorization required for /merge-and-cleanup

title: Red-tests: write failing tests covering all [m] AC bullets
description:
Write failing tests (TDD red phase) before any implementation code.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:test-driven-development, superpowers:writing-unit-tests,
superpowers:testing-anti-patterns

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (read [m]-tagged lines)
- Worktree path: decode worktree-path-* label from molecule

## Decode worktree-path label before any work
  1. Decode: __ → /  then  _u → _
  2. Verify decoded path exists and is a git worktree:
       git -C <path> rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree
  3. Operate from that cwd.

## Actions

### Opt-out (evaluate FIRST, before any reviewer dispatch)

This step has a canonical opt-out predicate. Evaluate triggers in this fixed
order at the top of the step:

1. Trigger A — no test runner. If `[gates].test == ""` in project-config.toml,
   the project has no way to run tests. Skip with skip-note prefix
   `red-tests opt-out: ` and trigger description:
     red-tests opt-out: no test runner ([gates].test=='')

2. Trigger B — no [m] AC lines. If the bead's `acceptance_criteria`
   field (the canonical AC store per spec §4.1, NOT `description`) has
   zero `[m]`-classified lines, there is nothing for tests to assert.
   Apply the canonical line parser (`^\[(m|h)\]\s(.*)$`; untagged lines
   default to `[m]` per §4.1's backwards-compat rule). Counting
   `description` would both false-positive on `[m]` tokens inside notes
   AND false-negative on canonical (non-bullet) AC formats. Pinned
   line-anchored grep -cvE counts every non-blank line that is NOT
   explicitly `[h]`-tagged (i.e. [m] lines plus untagged-defaults-to-[m]):
     m_count=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].acceptance_criteria // ""' | grep -cvE '^([[:space:]]*$|\[h\][[:space:]])')
   If m_count == 0, skip with note:
     red-tests opt-out: no [m] AC lines

3. Both triggers fire (no test runner AND no [m] AC lines):
     red-tests opt-out: both

4. Otherwise, proceed with the normal red-tests behavior below.

Skip mechanism (when any trigger fires):
   bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "red-tests opt-out: <trigger>"
The literal prefix `red-tests opt-out: ` is grep-able for verify-ac
groundedness. Then close the step bead and exit; do not dispatch reviewers.

Conversely: a bead with [m] ACs MUST run red-tests, regardless of project
signals (Trigger A still skips when there is no test runner — [m] ACs in
a runner-less project are a brainstorm-time error). There is no per-bead
`red-tests:enable` opt-IN or `red-tests:skip` opt-OUT label. AC tagging
is the per-bead signal.

### Normal flow (when no Opt-out trigger fires)

1. Dispatch bead-implementor (sonnet, medium effort) to write tests:
   - Dispatch with: worktree path, AC bullets (all [m]-tagged lines),
     step-bead ID, instruction to write failing tests and confirm they fail.
   - Tests must cover: happy path, edge cases, error paths.
   - Do NOT mock what has not been verified.
   - Do NOT write test-only methods in production code.

2. Run the Claude reviewer (test-review skill) on the written tests.
   When review-level:none is set: skip test reviewers (tests still written).

3. Run Codex adversarial review of the tests (gpt-5.5 default).
   Model from: project-config.toml [foreign-cli].codex_red_tests_model
   When review-level:none: skip.
   Subject to foreign-eyes degradation tracking (FOREIGN-EYES-ITER-<n>).

4. Iteration cap: 2 (override: iteration-cap-red-tests-<n> label).
   At cap with reviewer disagreement: write disagreement note to step-bead notes.
   Escalate human ONLY if BOTH reviewers agree on a critical/major design flaw.

5. Commit failing tests to the feature branch.

## Definition of Done
Tests committed to feature branch; both reviewers approve "shippable" state
(or iteration cap reached with disagreement note recorded).
OR step opted out per the canonical Opt-out predicate above, with the
pinned `red-tests opt-out: ...` skip-note appended to step-bead notes.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check filesystem (committed tests) and step-bead notes. Continue from prior state.
notes: red-tests opt-out: no test runner ([gates].test=='')
close_reason: red-tests opt-out: no test runner ([gates].test=='')

title: Quality-sweep: global quality gate across entire project
description:
Run the full project quality suite (not just changed files).
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skippable when preflight marked it so (no [static-analysis] in project-config.toml,
or empty gates). Also skips when review-level:none is set.

## Order (fixed)
1. Lint auto-fix: [lint-autofix].command from project-config.toml (run FIRST)
2. Build + typecheck + lint (from [gates])
3. Static analysis (from [static-analysis] — semgrep, depcheck, etc.)
4. Tests (from [gates].test)

ALWAYS end with build + tests after any code changes (guarantees verify-ac starts green).

## Coverage

This stage does not separately enforce a coverage threshold; coverage gating,
if any, lives in green-loop's Definition of Done and is conditional on
[coverage].applicable. See docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md §3
(quality-sweep).

## Auto-fix vs flag-human
Auto-fix unless issues betray a design flaw requiring fundamental changes.
Use judgment based on the size of the fix.

## Definition of Done
All gate commands exit zero (or stage skipped per preflight / review-level:none).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates state and runs only what is not already green.
notes: quality-sweep skipped: empty gates and empty [static-analysis] in project-config.toml (formula-docs-only project)
close_reason: quality-sweep skipped: docs-only project, empty gates and static-analysis

title: Create-PR: push branch and open pull request
description:
Create the pull request and persist its URL to the molecule.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch

## Actions

1. Invoke superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch.

2. PR body MUST include:
   - Bead reference: {{bead-id}} and bead title
   - AC (full list with [m]/[h] tags)
   - implementation iteration summary (from green-loop step-bead notes)
   - verify-ac validation report
   - Bug beads filed during verify-ac (if any)
   - Human-required AC follow-up bead references (if any [h] lines)
   - Foreign-eyes status section (list which iterations had degraded foreign-eyes)

3. Stamp pr-url-* label on molecule:
   Encoding: same bijection as worktree-path (though URLs rarely need it).
   bd label add <mol-id> pr-url-<encoded-url>

## PR creation failure
Flag-human with worktree intact. Do NOT tear down the worktree.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view first. If a PR exists for the branch and is OPEN: skip creation,
persist URL idempotently.
If PR exists and is CLOSED: flag-human. Do NOT auto-reopen or create a new PR.
notes: PR created: https://github.com/scotthamilton77/agents-config/pull/50
close_reason: PR opened at https://github.com/scotthamilton77/agents-config/pull/50

title: Preflight: spec validation, formula selection, worktree creation
description:
Validate the bead spec and create the worktree before any implementation work.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: high

## Inputs
Read the bead and its labels:
  bd show {{bead-id}}
  bd label list {{bead-id}}

## Actions

1. Verify `brainstormed` and `implementation-ready` labels are present.
   If either is absent: apply human-flag protocol and exit.

2. Note: formula selection and pour have already happened — this step was
   created by implement-bead pouring implement-feature. There is nothing
   to select or pour here. Proceed directly to project-config.toml validation.

3. Read project-config.toml and verify:
   - `[gates]` section present (or stubs acceptable).
   - Coverage applicability ([coverage].applicable): if explicitly set to `false`,
     coverage does NOT apply to this project (docs-only repos, config repos, etc.).
     Skip the report-location check entirely and proceed to step 4. Record this
     by running:
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "preflight: coverage opt-out (project-config has [coverage].applicable = false) — skipped report-location check"
   - Coverage report location ([coverage].report-location): only checked when
     `[coverage].applicable` is true OR absent (default). If absent OR empty
     string (i.e. `report-location = ""`), apply the human-flag protocol and
     stop this stage:
       bd label add {{bead-id}} human
       bd label add <step-bead-id> human
       bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "preflight: no coverage report location configured in project-config.toml"
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "preflight: no coverage report location configured — molecule parked"
     An empty string is treated the same as absent: explicit opt-out requires
     `[coverage].applicable = false` — a bare empty string is ambiguous and
     must be resolved by the human.
     Do NOT continue to worktree creation. Exit cleanly.
   - [static-analysis] section: if absent or empty, mark quality-sweep skippable.

4. Stamp the molecule→bead lookup label if not already stamped:
   bd label add <mol-id> for-bead-{{bead-id}}

5. Create the worktree:
   Branch name: feat/{{bead-id}}-<slug-from-title>
   Location: <repo-root>/.claude/worktrees/<branch-name>
   Command:
     git worktree add .claude/worktrees/<branch> -b <branch>
   CRITICAL: the formula is already poured (this step was created by the pour).
   If worktree creation fails: squash the molecule and flag-human.

6. Stamp the worktree-path label on the molecule:
   Encoding (apply IN ORDER):
     _ → _u
     / → __
   Then: bd label add <mol-id> worktree-path-<encoded>

7. Claim the bead and walk the parent chain:
   bd update {{bead-id}} --status in_progress
   PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
   while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
     bd update "$PARENT" --status in_progress
     PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
   done

## Definition of Done
Poured molecule + worktree exists + worktree-path-* label stamped on molecule.
OR: human label + structured gap-note + (if pour completed) molecule squashed.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check: does an active molecule exist? Does the worktree path decode to a real
git worktree? If so, skip work already done.
notes: preflight: coverage opt-out (project-config has [coverage].applicable = false) — skipped report-location check
close_reason: Preflight complete: brainstormed+implementation-ready labels verified, coverage opt-out noted (applicable=false), for-bead label already stamped, worktree created at .claude/worktrees/feat/agents-config-12q7.4-route-z-start-bead, worktree-path label stamped on molecule, bead and parent chain marked in_progress.

title: Review-cycle: address PR review feedback
description:
Drive the PR review loop until all review threads are resolved.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

Skips entirely when review-level:none.

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:wait-for-pr-comments (chains internally to superpowers:reply-and-resolve-pr-threads)

## Actions

Invoke superpowers:wait-for-pr-comments with:
  --mode autonomous --bead-id {{bead-id}}

The skill:
1. Polls Copilot via background script (zero Anthropic tokens during wait).
2. Classifies each comment: FIX / SKIP / ESCALATE.
3. Dispatches per-comment fix subagents (sonnet) for FIX-class items.
4. Pushes combined commits.
5. Internally chains to superpowers:reply-and-resolve-pr-threads:
   replies to every thread + resolves FIXED review_thread items via GraphQL.

One iteration = one outbound reply-batch.
Iteration cap: 5 (override: iteration-cap-review-cycle-<n> label).
Early exit when no remaining FIX-class items.

Exit conditions (mutually exclusive):
- review-exit-copilot-only (default when review-exit-human-approvers-<n> absent)
- review-exit-human-approvers-<n>: require N human approvers

Persist after each batch:
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "REVIEW-BATCH:<n>/<MAX>"

If PR is CLOSED on re-entry: flag-human. Do NOT poll a closed PR.

## Definition of Done
Every review thread has a reply. FIXED items resolved on GitHub.
No in-flight FIX work.
notes:
REVIEW-BATCH:1/5 — Copilot review: 5 inline comments. Classified: 3 FIX (C3 doc-clarify, C4 wording, C5 already_addressed), 2 ESCALATE (C1+C2 spec-level R8 invariant ordering bug).
REVIEW-BATCH:1/5 complete. Phase 6 detected no Copilot re-review; exited normally. Final: 5 replies posted, 2 threads resolved (C3, C4 — FIX/committed), 2 ESCALATE filed on agents-config-12q7.4 (C1+C2 — R8 invariant ordering bug, spec-level), 1 SKIP (C5 — O1 is bead-state and verifiable out-of-band).
close_reason: Review cycle complete: 1 batch (no Copilot re-review after fixes); all 5 review threads replied; 2 FIX/committed threads resolved (C3 visited-set/ordered-list clarification, C4 Step 2 intro rephrase); 2 spec-level R8 invariant concerns escalated on source bead (human label + notes); 1 SKIP for O1 sibling-bead update.
