title: Green-loop: implement via label-driven RALF dispatch
description:
Implement the feature using label-driven skill dispatch.
Model: claude-opus-4-7  Effort: medium

## Inputs
- Bead spec from: bd show {{bead-id}}
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule

## Dispatch contract

Check the bead's RALF signal before dispatching:

  bd label list {{bead-id}} --json

- If label `ralf:required` is present:
    invoke `ralf-implement` with the bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, and optional max cycle count from `ralf:cycles=N`

    `ralf-implement` owns only the inner quality loop: iteration,
    independence, adversarial posture, convergence, and structured report output.
    It does not read bead labels or update bead state; this formula step passes
    all needed inputs.

    After it returns, append the structured report summary to the green-loop
    step-bead notes, including cycles run, score, severity counts, and
    foreign-eyes status/degradation for every Codex/Gemini cycle.

    If the score is `FAIL`, or if the score is `PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS` with
    unresolved major concerns, stop this step: label both the source bead and
    the green-loop step bead `human`, append the unresolved blocking/critical/major
    concerns to both notes, and do not advance to `quality-sweep` until the
    caller explicitly decides whether to revise, defer, or accept the residual risk.

- Otherwise:
    invoke `superpowers:test-driven-development` first,
    then the appropriate domain skill for implementation.

Record the dispatch decision for audit:

  bd comments add {{bead-id}} "implement: dispatched <skill-name>"

`bd comments add` is non-destructive: it creates a separate comment and does
not replace issue notes. For cumulative step output, use `bd update
--append-notes`; do not use `--notes`, which replaces existing notes.

Persist step output before closing green-loop. Here `<step-bead-id>` is the current green-loop molecule step bead:

  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "IMPLEMENTATION-REPORT: score=<PASS|PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS|FAIL>; cycles=<n>/<max>; severity=blocking:<n>,critical:<n>,major:<n>,minor:<n>; summary=<summary from dispatched skill>"
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "FOREIGN-EYES: cycle-1=<COMPLETED|UNAVAILABLE|TIMED_OUT|QUOTA_EXCEEDED|AUTH_FAILED|NO_OUTPUT|UNUSABLE_OUTPUT|NOT_RUN>; cycle-2=<...>; degraded=<list or none>"

Before closing green-loop, ensure the dispatched implementation path has run
and recorded the mandatory completion gate for its own changes:
`quality-reviewer` → `simplify` → `verify-checklist`. If gate evidence is
missing, run it now and append the evidence to the step-bead notes. If any
gate step fails, label both the source bead and step bead `human`, append the
failure evidence to both notes, and do not advance to `quality-sweep`.

## Coverage

Coverage threshold from project-config.toml [coverage].threshold (default 80%),
overridable per-bead via the `coverage-threshold-<n>` label;
honored only when [coverage].applicable is true or absent. When
[coverage].applicable is explicitly false (docs-only repos, config repos, etc.),
coverage is not a quality gate and the threshold is bypassed. See
docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md §3 (green-loop) for the canonical
contract.

Rules (regardless of dispatched path):
- minimum viable implementation only
- no speculative features
- no over-engineering for hypothetical requirements

## Definition of Done
Required feature behavior implemented with passing quality gates.

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-check labels and dispatch per current bead state at step start.

title: Preflight: spec validation, formula selection, worktree creation
description:
Validate the bead spec and create the worktree before any implementation work.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: high

## Inputs
Read the bead and its labels:
  bd show {{bead-id}}
  bd label list {{bead-id}}

## Actions

1. Verify `brainstormed` and `implementation-ready` labels are present.
   If either is absent: apply human-flag protocol and exit.

2. Note: formula selection and pour have already happened — this step was
   created by implement-bead pouring implement-feature. There is nothing
   to select or pour here. Proceed directly to project-config.toml validation.

3. Read project-config.toml and verify:
   - `[gates]` section present (or stubs acceptable).
   - Coverage applicability ([coverage].applicable): if explicitly set to `false`,
     coverage does NOT apply to this project (docs-only repos, config repos, etc.).
     Skip the report-location check entirely and proceed to step 4. Record this
     by running:
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "preflight: coverage opt-out (project-config has [coverage].applicable = false) — skipped report-location check"
   - Coverage report location ([coverage].report-location): only checked when
     `[coverage].applicable` is true OR absent (default). If absent OR empty
     string (i.e. `report-location = ""`), apply the human-flag protocol and
     stop this stage:
       bd label add {{bead-id}} human
       bd label add <step-bead-id> human
       bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "preflight: no coverage report location configured in project-config.toml"
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "preflight: no coverage report location configured — molecule parked"
     An empty string is treated the same as absent: explicit opt-out requires
     `[coverage].applicable = false` — a bare empty string is ambiguous and
     must be resolved by the human.
     Do NOT continue to worktree creation. Exit cleanly.
   - [static-analysis] section: if absent or empty, mark quality-sweep skippable.

4. Stamp the molecule→bead lookup label if not already stamped:
   bd label add <mol-id> for-bead-{{bead-id}}

5. Create the worktree:
   Branch name: feat/{{bead-id}}-<slug-from-title>
   Location: <repo-root>/.claude/worktrees/<branch-name>
   Command:
     git worktree add .claude/worktrees/<branch> -b <branch>
   CRITICAL: the formula is already poured (this step was created by the pour).
   If worktree creation fails: squash the molecule and flag-human.

6. Stamp the worktree-path label on the molecule:
   Encoding (apply IN ORDER):
     _ → _u
     / → __
   Then: bd label add <mol-id> worktree-path-<encoded>

7. Claim the bead and walk the parent chain:
   bd update {{bead-id}} --status in_progress
   PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
   while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
     bd update "$PARENT" --status in_progress
     PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
   done

## Definition of Done
Poured molecule + worktree exists + worktree-path-* label stamped on molecule.
OR: human label + structured gap-note + (if pour completed) molecule squashed.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check: does an active molecule exist? Does the worktree path decode to a real
git worktree? If so, skip work already done.

title: Merge-or-handoff: auto-merge or hand off to human
description:
Either auto-merge the PR or hand off to the human.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Gate 1 — PR-state clean-check
Conditions (ALL must be true for auto-merge path):
  - Zero open Copilot review threads
  - Zero open human-author review threads
  - All CI checks green
  - 5-minute quiet window with no new comments or review activity
On wait-for-pr-comments timeout: treat clean-check as NOT clean.

## Auto-merge path (auto-mergeable label present AND clean-check clean)
1. Re-run clean-check immediately before merge call.
2. Pour merge-and-cleanup formula (phase: liquid — NOT vapor).
3. Drive merge-and-cleanup:
   - Merge the PR
   - Delete branch (local + remote)
   - Remove worktree (decode from worktree-path-* label)
   - Run parent-chain I2 close-walk
   Note: merge-and-cleanup gate-step (Gate 2) blocks until ALL [h] follow-up
   children are closed with verified-by-human label, then closes the
   merge-{source-id} child after merge + cleanup so the source bead can close.

## Hand-off path (default — auto-mergeable absent OR clean-check dirty)
1. Transition bead to open: bd update {{bead-id}} --status open
2. Stamp labels: bd label add {{bead-id}} merge-ready
                 bd label add {{bead-id}} human
   (merge-ready = why it's on human list; human = surfaces via bd human list)
3. Note: bead is now excluded from bd ready (human label gates it).
   Human runs /merge-and-cleanup when ready.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view --json mergedAt first. If non-null: skip merge, proceed to cleanup.

title: Red-tests: write failing tests covering all [m] AC bullets
description:
Write failing tests (TDD red phase) before any implementation code.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:test-driven-development, superpowers:writing-unit-tests,
superpowers:testing-anti-patterns

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (read [m]-tagged lines)
- Worktree path: decode worktree-path-* label from molecule

## Decode worktree-path label before any work
  1. Decode: __ → /  then  _u → _
  2. Verify decoded path exists and is a git worktree:
       git -C <path> rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree
  3. Operate from that cwd.

## Actions

### Opt-out (evaluate FIRST, before any reviewer dispatch)

This step has a canonical opt-out predicate. Evaluate triggers in this fixed
order at the top of the step:

1. Trigger A — no test runner. If `[gates].test == ""` in project-config.toml,
   the project has no way to run tests. Skip with skip-note prefix
   `red-tests opt-out: ` and trigger description:
     red-tests opt-out: no test runner ([gates].test=='')

2. Trigger B — no [m] AC lines. If the bead's `acceptance_criteria`
   field (the canonical AC store per spec §4.1, NOT `description`) has
   zero `[m]`-classified lines, there is nothing for tests to assert.
   Apply the canonical line parser (`^\[(m|h)\]\s(.*)$`; untagged lines
   default to `[m]` per §4.1's backwards-compat rule). Counting
   `description` would both false-positive on `[m]` tokens inside notes
   AND false-negative on canonical (non-bullet) AC formats. Pinned
   line-anchored grep -cvE counts every non-blank line that is NOT
   explicitly `[h]`-tagged (i.e. [m] lines plus untagged-defaults-to-[m]):
     m_count=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].acceptance_criteria // ""' | grep -cvE '^([[:space:]]*$|\[h\][[:space:]])')
   If m_count == 0, skip with note:
     red-tests opt-out: no [m] AC lines

3. Both triggers fire (no test runner AND no [m] AC lines):
     red-tests opt-out: both

4. Otherwise, proceed with the normal red-tests behavior below.

Skip mechanism (when any trigger fires):
   bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "red-tests opt-out: <trigger>"
The literal prefix `red-tests opt-out: ` is grep-able for verify-ac
groundedness. Then close the step bead and exit; do not dispatch reviewers.

Conversely: a bead with [m] ACs MUST run red-tests, regardless of project
signals (Trigger A still skips when there is no test runner — [m] ACs in
a runner-less project are a brainstorm-time error). There is no per-bead
`red-tests:enable` opt-IN or `red-tests:skip` opt-OUT label. AC tagging
is the per-bead signal.

### Normal flow (when no Opt-out trigger fires)

1. Dispatch bead-implementor (sonnet, medium effort) to write tests:
   - Dispatch with: worktree path, AC bullets (all [m]-tagged lines),
     step-bead ID, instruction to write failing tests and confirm they fail.
   - Tests must cover: happy path, edge cases, error paths.
   - Do NOT mock what has not been verified.
   - Do NOT write test-only methods in production code.

2. Run the Claude reviewer (test-review skill) on the written tests.
   When review-level:none is set: skip test reviewers (tests still written).

3. Run Codex adversarial review of the tests (gpt-5.5 default).
   Model from: project-config.toml [foreign-cli].codex_red_tests_model
   When review-level:none: skip.
   Subject to foreign-eyes degradation tracking (FOREIGN-EYES-ITER-<n>).

4. Iteration cap: 2 (override: iteration-cap-red-tests-<n> label).
   At cap with reviewer disagreement: write disagreement note to step-bead notes.
   Escalate human ONLY if BOTH reviewers agree on a critical/major design flaw.

5. Commit failing tests to the feature branch.

## Definition of Done
Tests committed to feature branch; both reviewers approve "shippable" state
(or iteration cap reached with disagreement note recorded).
OR step opted out per the canonical Opt-out predicate above, with the
pinned `red-tests opt-out: ...` skip-note appended to step-bead notes.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check filesystem (committed tests) and step-bead notes. Continue from prior state.

title: Create-PR: push branch and open pull request
description:
Create the pull request and persist its URL to the molecule.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch

## Actions

1. Invoke superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch.

2. PR body MUST include:
   - Bead reference: {{bead-id}} and bead title
   - AC (full list with [m]/[h] tags)
   - implementation iteration summary (from green-loop step-bead notes)
   - verify-ac validation report
   - Bug beads filed during verify-ac (if any)
   - Human-required AC follow-up bead references (if any [h] lines)
   - Foreign-eyes status section (list which iterations had degraded foreign-eyes)

3. Stamp pr-url-* label on molecule:
   Encoding: same bijection as worktree-path (though URLs rarely need it).
   bd label add <mol-id> pr-url-<encoded-url>

## PR creation failure
Flag-human with worktree intact. Do NOT tear down the worktree.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view first. If a PR exists for the branch and is OPEN: skip creation,
persist URL idempotently.
If PR exists and is CLOSED: flag-human. Do NOT auto-reopen or create a new PR.

title: Verify-AC: mechanical acceptance criteria validation
description:
Validate every [m]-tagged AC bullet against worktree state.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skips when review-level:none.

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (parse [m] and [h] tags)
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule
- Functional tests from: project-config.toml [functional-tests]

## Actions

For each [m]-tagged AC line:
1. Dispatch bead-verifier (haiku, low effort) to run shell-runnable witnesses.
   Collect: exit code + error excerpt.
2. Assemble validation report.

[h]-tagged AC lines: do NOT validate here. Confirm that human follow-up
child beads were filed at brainstorm-time finalize (bd list --parent {{bead-id}}
filtered by label human). If any [h] bullet has no matching follow-up, flag-human.

Run functional/e2e tests from [functional-tests] in project-config.toml.
If functional tests fail:
  - Create one bug bead per failure, label human.
  - Note in step-bead. Continue to create-pr (do NOT loop back).

Persist validation report to BOTH:
  - Step-bead notes: bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "<report>"
  - Source bead notes: bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "<report>"
  (Report also included in PR description at create-pr.)

## Definition of Done
Every [m] AC bullet has explicit witness evidence.
Human-review bullets have follow-up beads filed (confirm presence).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates and continues from prior pass.

title: implement-feature
description: Per-stage feature pipeline: preflight → red-tests → green-loop → quality-sweep → verify-ac → create-pr → review-cycle → merge-or-handoff

title: Quality-sweep: global quality gate across entire project
description:
Run the full project quality suite (not just changed files).
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skippable when preflight marked it so (no [static-analysis] in project-config.toml,
or empty gates). Also skips when review-level:none is set.

## Order (fixed)
1. Lint auto-fix: [lint-autofix].command from project-config.toml (run FIRST)
2. Build + typecheck + lint (from [gates])
3. Static analysis (from [static-analysis] — semgrep, depcheck, etc.)
4. Tests (from [gates].test)

ALWAYS end with build + tests after any code changes (guarantees verify-ac starts green).

## Coverage

This stage does not separately enforce a coverage threshold; coverage gating,
if any, lives in green-loop's Definition of Done and is conditional on
[coverage].applicable. See docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md §3
(quality-sweep).

## Auto-fix vs flag-human
Auto-fix unless issues betray a design flaw requiring fundamental changes.
Use judgment based on the size of the fix.

## Definition of Done
All gate commands exit zero (or stage skipped per preflight / review-level:none).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates state and runs only what is not already green.

title: brainstorm-bead formula §3 edge migration: also retarget INBOUND deps from other beads to Y
description:
The brainstorm-bead formula's step 6 (edge migration) iterates only X's OUTBOUND dep edges via 'bd show <X-id> --json | jq .[0].dependencies'. This misses INBOUND edges — deps stored on OTHER beads' records pointing TO X.

Concrete failure: when bead 12q7 had an outbound 'blocked-by 12q7.2' edge (a merge prerequisite), 12q7's edge survives X.close unchanged. After 12q7.2 closes, the dep is satisfied, 12q7 becomes ready — but Y (the new implementation bead) hasn't shipped yet. The intended ordering is lost.

Fix: §3 should also enumerate inbound edges via 'bd list' search or by maintaining a reverse-edge query, then retarget each (remove from-OTHER-bead-to-X, add from-OTHER-bead-to-Y) per the same rubric.

Discovered during finalize of agents-config-12q7.2 (Route Z brainstorm); manually retargeted at finalize time as a workaround.
acceptance_criteria:
[m] bd-migrate-deps.sh exists at src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/bd-migrate-deps.sh and is chmod +x.
[m] install.sh requires NO edits; its existing *.sh glob deploys the helper automatically. Verify: install.sh --dry-run reports bd-migrate-deps.sh as "Would install" without any install.sh changes.
[m] brainstorm-bead.formula.toml STEP 6 calls ~/.beads/scripts/bd-migrate-deps.sh --source {{bead-id}} --target "$Y_ID"; the old inline bd show | jq | while read loop is removed.
[m] Helper iterates X's outbound edges (bd dep list <X> --direction=down --json or bd show <X> --json | jq).
[m] Helper iterates X's inbound edges via bd dep list <X> --direction=up --json; status is read from the inline .status field (confirmed present in both --direction=up and --direction=down JSON shapes).
[m] Both directions skip parent-child edges.
[m] Inbound iteration skips edges where source == Y (the freshly-created Y discovered-from X seed link).
[m] Symmetric rubric applied: discovered-from/related + linked-closed -> keep on X; linked-open/in_progress -> migrate. All other dep types (excluding parent-child) always migrate.
[m] Before calling bd dep add, dep_type is normalized: blocked-by -> blocks (required for both outbound and inbound legs; bd emits blocked-by at read-level for both traversal directions).
[m] Inbound migration uses correct positional convention: bd dep add <OTHER> <Y> --type blocks (arg1=dependent, arg2=blocker/target); NOT bd dep add <Y> <OTHER>.
[m] Migration is gated: add Y-side first (explicit if/then guard); only remove X-side on success (|| echo WARN guard); warn and retain X-side on add failure; warn and retain both edges on remove failure after successful add.
[m] Script runs under set -euo pipefail. Input-arg errors (missing or equal --source/--target) exit non-zero immediately. Only bd dep add and bd dep remove calls are explicitly guarded to warn-and-continue; all other bd/jq/shell failures propagate non-zero.
[m] Cost bound: O(N_out + N_in) total edge queries (no per-edge bd show status calls; status is read inline from the .status field of dep list results). Documented in helper header comment.
[m] If bd dep add fails with a cycle error, the helper warns and retains the X-side edge (same as any add failure); does NOT use --no-cycle-check.
[m] Early exit non-zero if --source and --target resolve to the same bead ID.
[h] Manual smoke test: create throwaway X with (a) one outbound edge to ANOTHER-OPEN bead, (b) one inbound edge from a SECOND bead pointing at X, and (c) one inbound edge from a CLOSED-OTHER bead pointing at X. Run the helper. Verify via bd dep list: (a) outbound edge now anchored on Y (absent from X, present on Y's deps); (b) inbound from SECOND bead now points at Y (absent from X's inbound, present on Y's inbound); (c) inbound from CLOSED-OTHER unchanged, still points at X.
[h] Manual end-to-end: run full brainstorm-bead finalize on a placeholder bead that has at least one inbound dep edge; verify both edge directions retargeted in the produced Y bead.
notes:
# Spec: bd-migrate-deps.sh — bidirectional dep-edge retargeting for brainstorm-bead finalize

## Summary

Extract brainstorm-bead.formula.toml STEP 6's inline edge-migration logic into a new
`bd-migrate-deps.sh` shell helper (Tier 3 of the 7bk.27 extraction pattern), and
extend it to retarget INBOUND dep edges (edges from OTHER beads pointing AT X) in
addition to the existing OUTBOUND retargeting (edges from X to OTHER beads).

## Background / motivation

The brainstorm-bead formula's finalize STEP 6 iterates only X's OUTBOUND dep edges.
INBOUND edges — stored in OTHER beads' records, pointing at X — are silently skipped.
This causes ordering relationships to become stale: when another bead blocks-on X
(or any other dep type), that edge is NOT retargeted to Y after finalize. The
concrete failure: bead 12q7 had a blocked-by 12q7.2 inbound; after 12q7.2 closed,
12q7 appeared ready even though Y (the implementation bead) had not shipped.
Workaround: manual retargeting at finalize time (applied for 12q7.2). Fix: extend §3.

Additionally, STEP 6 is the last finalize step still expressed as inline TOML prose.
PR #54 (agents-config-7bk.27) extracted claim-walk, close-walk, and finalize-create
into shell helpers. This bead completes Tier 3 (dep-migration) of that extraction.

## Requirements

1. New helper bd-migrate-deps.sh --source <X> --target <Y> lives at
   src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/bd-migrate-deps.sh, chmod +x.
   Early exit non-zero if --source == --target (copy-paste guard).

2. install.sh's existing per-plugin *.sh glob auto-deploys any script in
   src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/. No install.sh edits are needed —
   dropping the file is sufficient. Verification: a fresh install.sh --dry-run
   must report the file as "Would install" without any install.sh changes.

3. brainstorm-bead.formula.toml STEP 6 is replaced with a single helper call;
   the §3 rubric prose above the call is retained as documentation.

4. Helper iterates OUTBOUND edges. bd dep list <X> --direction=down --json returns
   status inline; prefer it over the legacy bd show <X>.dependencies shape so both
   directions use a consistent query. (If outbound parity with existing STEP 6 is
   preferred, bd show <X> --json | jq -r '.[0].dependencies // []...' is also
   acceptable — status field is available in both.)

5. Helper iterates INBOUND edges via:
     bd dep list <X> --direction=up --json
   Status comes inline via the .status field — no per-edge bd show status calls needed.
   Both --direction=up and --direction=down JSON shapes include a top-level .status field
   (confirmed against current bd CLI).

6. Both directions apply the SAME rubric (symmetric):

   dep_type                              | linked-bead status  | action
   --------------------------------------|---------------------|----------
   blocks (bd emits "blocked-by" at the  | any                 | migrate
     read level for both ends of a       |                     |
     blocks edge — see Design decisions) |                     |
   tracks, until, caused-by, validates,  | any                 | migrate
     relates-to, supersedes              |                     |
   discovered-from, related              | closed              | keep on X
   discovered-from, related              | open / in_progress  | migrate
   parent-child                          | any                 | SKIP

   "Linked bead" = the bead at the other end of the edge from X's perspective
   (= target for outbound; = source for inbound).

7. Skip conditions (both directions):
   - parent-child (handled by Y.parent = X.parent and §4 children placement)
   - Inbound edges where source == Y (skip the freshly-created Y->discovered-from->X seed link)
   - Self-loops (other == X) — defensive guard

8. Migration gating (both directions):
   - OUTBOUND (X->OTHER): bd dep add <Y> <OTHER> --type <T>; on success -> bd dep remove <X> <OTHER>
   - INBOUND (OTHER->X):  bd dep add <OTHER> <Y> --type <T>; on success -> bd dep remove <OTHER> <X>

   Positional convention for bd dep add: arg1 is the DEPENDENT bead, arg2 is the BLOCKER/target.
   For inbound retargeting of a "OTHER blocked-by X" edge: OTHER depends on X; the retargeted
   form is "OTHER depends on Y", so: bd dep add <OTHER> <Y> --type blocks. NOT bd dep add <Y> <OTHER>.
   Worked example:
     Before: OTHER --blocked-by--> X  (OTHER depends on X)
     Retarget: bd dep add OTHER Y --type blocks  (OTHER now depends on Y)
              bd dep remove OTHER X

   Normalization: bd's wire format reports "blocked-by" as the dep_type for blocks edges in
   BOTH traversal directions (--direction=up and --direction=down both emit "blocked-by").
   Before calling bd dep add, normalize: if dep_type == "blocked-by", use "blocks" instead.
   This guards against bd CLI type-validation tightening in future versions.

   Failure semantics:
   - If bd dep add fails: echo WARN, leave X-side edge intact (never drop edges). Continue.
   - If bd dep remove fails AFTER a successful add: echo WARN, leave both edges in place.
     Idempotent replay will reattempt the remove because the X-side edge remains visible to
     the iterator.
   - If bd dep add fails with a cycle error: treat same as any add failure — WARN + retain
     X-side. Do NOT use --no-cycle-check; let bd's default cycle detection protect the graph.
   - Unguarded bd/jq failures (e.g., db locked, query error, jq parse failure): propagate
     non-zero via set -euo pipefail (consistent with sibling helpers).

9. Exit semantics (consistent with sibling helpers bd-claim-walk.sh, bd-close-walk.sh):
   - Script runs under set -euo pipefail.
   - bd dep add calls are explicitly guarded with if/then so failures produce WARN + continue
     rather than aborting via pipefail.
   - bd dep remove calls are guarded with || echo "WARN: ..." so remove failures produce
     WARN + continue.
   - All other bd/jq/shell failures abort with non-zero (pipefail default).
   - Input validation (missing/invalid --source, --target, --source==--target): exit non-zero
     with a clear error message.

10. Fully idempotent: re-running after partial failure produces the same end state.
    Already-migrated outbound edges no longer appear in X's dep list; already-migrated
    inbound edges no longer appear in bd dep list <X> --direction=up. Re-running is safe.

11. Cost: O(N_out + N_in) edge queries (two bd dep list calls capturing all edges at once),
    plus O(N_migrated) bd dep add/bd dep remove pairs. Status is read inline from the dep
    list .status field — no separate per-edge bd show status calls. Typical total << 40
    bd calls.

## Out of scope

- --dry-run flag (YAGNI: no automated shell-script test infra in this repo; smoke test
  verifies via bd dep list post-execution)
- Legacy retargeting of pre-wmjy X->Y transitions (12q7 was manually fixed; file a
  follow-up bead if scan finds stale inbound on others)
- Tier 3 children-check and label-copy-filter helpers (separate beads)

## Design decisions

- **Symmetric rubric:** inbound discovered-from/related applies the same "linked closed ->
  keep, linked open -> migrate" rule as outbound. Accepts the same small historical fudge
  already present in the outbound rubric: if OTHER was discovered from X and is still open,
  after migration OTHER points at Y. Operationally correct; marginally imprecise history.
  Inbound-from-closed edges kept on X are safe: no current consumer (run-queue, bd ready,
  Route Z start-bead, implement-bead) makes routing decisions based on inbound queries
  against closed beads. Verified by searching for bd dep list --direction=up usage
  across formulas and skills — no decision-making uses of that query against closed beads.

- **blocked-by at the read level, blocks at the write level:** bd reports "blocked-by" as
  the dep_type in BOTH traversal directions (--direction=up and --direction=down both return
  dep_type="blocked-by" for either end of a blocks edge). "blocked-by" is NOT a valid type
  for bd dep add (not in the documented enum: blocks|tracks|related|parent-child|...).
  Normalization from "blocked-by" to "blocks" is required in both the outbound and inbound
  legs of the helper, not just one direction.

- **bd dep list is the preferred query for both directions:** returns status inline; avoids
  per-edge bd show calls. The legacy bd show <X>.dependencies shape (used in current STEP 6)
  is equivalent for outbound but requires an extra status lookup per discovered-from/related
  edge.

- **No --dry-run:** existing helpers do not have this mode; YAGNI here too.

- **set -euo pipefail with explicit guards:** consistent with sibling helpers. Guard only
  the two bd dep calls that must warn-and-continue (add and remove); let everything
  else bubble as errors.

## Open questions

None.

title: [Impl] Route Z: start-bead handles closed source-bead with produced-bead-<Y-id> label
description:
# Route Z: start-bead handles closed source-bead with produced-bead-<Y-id> label

**Source bead:** agents-config-12q7.2
**Status:** Implementation (post PR #50 inline-review redesign)
**Date:** 2026-05-07
**Revision:** 4 (replaces R-numbered inline-shell prose with a POSIX helper script + tests; strips project-specific noise from skill body)
**Parent:** agents-config-12q7

## Summary

Add a closed-bead preflight to the `start-bead` skill that runs before the molecule existence probe (Step 2). When the target bead is closed and carries exactly one `produced-bead-<Y-id>` label, forward routing to Y by re-entering Step 1 with Y as the new target. When closed without a `produced-bead-*` label, friendly-exit. Halt with an audit comment on dangling label, multiple labels, or cycle.

The preflight **logic** lives in a POSIX-shell helper script alongside the skill; the SKILL.md prose just describes when to run the helper and how to interpret its decision output. This keeps the skill body self-explanatory (no project-specific history, no spec-internal label conventions) and makes the preflight unit-testable.

## Conventions

- `<target-id>` / `<Y-id>` are fully-qualified bead IDs.
- "Original target" = the bead the user originally invoked `start-bead` against; preserved across forward re-entries.
- Helper script: `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/closed-bead-preflight.sh`
- Helper test:   `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/closed-bead-preflight.test.sh`

## Helper script — `closed-bead-preflight.sh`

- **F1.** POSIX shell (`#!/bin/sh`). No bash-only constructs. External deps limited to `bd`, `jq`, `grep`, `sed`, `awk`, `tr`, `printf`.
- **F2.** Invocation:
  ```
  closed-bead-preflight.sh <target-id> [--original=<id>] [--chain=<csv>]
  ```
  - `<target-id>` (positional): bead currently being evaluated.
  - `--original=<id>`: original-target bead-id; defaults to `<target-id>` (initial entry).
  - `--chain=<csv>`: comma-separated list of bead-ids visited so far, oldest first; defaults to empty (initial entry).
- **F3.** Branches on the target bead's status and `produced-bead-*` count:
  - `status != "closed"` → `decision=proceed`
  - `status == "closed"` AND `produced-bead-*` count == 0 → `decision=friendly-exit current=<target-id>`
  - `status == "closed"` AND count == 1 AND `<Y-id>` already in `<chain> ∪ {<target-id>}` → `decision=halt reason=cycle original=<id> chain=<csv>`
  - `status == "closed"` AND count == 1 AND Y does not exist (probe fails) → `decision=halt reason=dangling original=<id> intermediate=<target-id> y=<Y-id>`
  - `status == "closed"` AND count == 1 AND Y exists → `decision=forward target=<Y-id> chain=<new-csv>` where `<new-csv>` = chain extended with `<target-id>`
  - `status == "closed"` AND count >= 2 → `decision=halt reason=multiple original=<id> intermediate=<target-id> labels=<csv-of-Y-ids>`
- **F4.** All `bd` invocations use `--json` and `jq` (documented contract). No parsing of human-formatted text output.
- **F5.** Script is pure-read: no `bd update`, no `bd comments`, no state mutation. The agent driving the skill performs audit-comment writes after the script returns.
- **F6.** On unexpected error (bd failure, missing target bead, jq parse failure), print `decision=halt reason=error message=<terse>` and exit non-zero. Otherwise exit 0.

## Test script — `closed-bead-preflight.test.sh`

- **T1.** POSIX shell. Self-contained: stubs `bd` via a function or PATH-shadowed mock script that returns canned JSON for known fixture bead IDs.
- **T2.** Test cases — one per F3 branch:
  1. Open bead → `decision=proceed`.
  2. Closed, no `produced-bead-*` → `decision=friendly-exit current=<id>`.
  3. Closed, single `produced-bead-Y`, Y exists → `decision=forward target=Y chain=<csv>`.
  4. Closed, single `produced-bead-Y`, Y missing → `decision=halt reason=dangling ...`.
  5. Closed, two `produced-bead-*` labels → `decision=halt reason=multiple ...`.
  6. Closed, single `produced-bead-Y`, Y already in `<chain>` → `decision=halt reason=cycle ...`.
- **T3.** Each test prints `PASS:` or `FAIL:` with a one-line evidence excerpt. Script exits non-zero if any case fails.
- **T4.** Fixture bead IDs are generic (`target`, `Y`, `Z`); no project-specific IDs in the test.

## SKILL.md changes

- **S1.** Insert `### Step 1.5: Closed-bead preflight` between Step 1 and Step 2.
- **S2.** Section content (terse — target ~30 lines):
  1. One-paragraph rationale (closed beads may carry forwarding pointers to derivative beads; preflight short-circuits Step 2 so closed beads don't fall through to molecule resumption or Routes A/B/C).
  2. Invocation snippet showing how to run the helper and capture stdout.
  3. Decision table mapping each `decision=...` line to an agent action:
     - `proceed` → fall through to Step 2 unchanged.
     - `forward target=<Y> chain=<csv>` → re-enter Step 1 with `<target> = <Y>`; pass `--chain=<csv>` to the helper on the next invocation; original target stays fixed across forwards.
     - `friendly-exit current=<bead-id>` → emit `Bead <bead-id> is closed. Did you mean a different bead?` and stop.
     - `halt reason=dangling intermediate=<X> y=<Y>` → emit reply + audit comment on the **original target**.
     - `halt reason=multiple intermediate=<X> labels=<csv>` → same.
     - `halt reason=cycle chain=<csv>` → same.
- **S3.** Audit-comment templates appear in the section once each (dangling, multiple, cycle). They target the **original target**, not the intermediate. This is the only place the prose documents specific message text; everything else is the helper's responsibility.
- **S4.** Canonical friendly-exit suffix `is closed. Did you mean a different bead?` is documented as the helper's exact output for the friendly-exit branch.
- **S5.** **No project-specific lore.** No references to specific bead IDs, "redesigns", or epic history. No `R#` numbering convention.

## Routing decision table

- **D1.** 5-row table (excluding header) with columns `Status | Has produced-bead-* | Has implementation-ready | Trivial | Route`:

  | Status | Has `produced-bead-*` | Has `implementation-ready` | Trivial | Route |
  |--------|-----------------------|----------------------------|---------|-------|
  | closed | yes (exactly 1)       | —                          | —       | Z (forward to Y) |
  | closed | no                    | —                          | —       | exit (friendly message) |
  | open   | —                     | yes                        | —       | A |
  | open   | —                     | no                         | yes     | B |
  | open   | —                     | no                         | no      | C |

  Footnote (immediately after table): *"A closed bead with ≥2 `produced-bead-*` labels does NOT route — it halts."*

## Red flags table

- **RF1.** New row:
  - Thought: `The bead is closed, just give up`
  - Reality: `Run Step 1.5. If the helper says forward, route to the new target. Otherwise, friendly exit. Never silently fall through.`

## Sibling-bead update

- **O1.** `agents-config-12q7.3`'s `acceptance_criteria` includes the canonical line `[h] After the brainstorm-bead redesign closes X, start-bead <X-id> redirects to Y by detecting produced-bead-<Y-id> and re-entering Step 1 with Y as the new target.` Idempotent procedure preserved from prior implementation.

## Out of scope

- Updates to `rules/beads.md` lifecycle table.
- Updates to `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md`.
- Behavioral changes to Routes A/B/C.
- Promoting Route Z's failure modes to HEP escalations.
- Soft-warning UX for the open-bead-with-produced-bead-* anomaly (filed as `agents-config-hhou`, discovered-from `agents-config-12q7.2`).

## Acceptance criteria

[m] `start-bead/SKILL.md` contains a section titled `### Step 1.5: Closed-bead preflight` inserted between `### Step 1:` and `### Step 2:` sections. Verifiable: line numbers from `grep -nE '^### Step (1|1\.5|2):' src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md` must appear in numeric order (1 < 1.5 < 2).

[m] `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/closed-bead-preflight.sh` exists, is executable, and starts with `#!/bin/sh` (verifiable: `head -1` matches `#!/bin/sh`, `test -x` succeeds).

[m] `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/closed-bead-preflight.test.sh` exists, is executable, and exits 0 when run from the worktree with all test cases reporting PASS.

[m] `closed-bead-preflight.sh` implements the six decision branches of F3, each exercised by at least one test case in `.test.sh`. Verifiable: `grep -E 'PASS:' closed-bead-preflight.test.sh output` shows ≥6 PASS lines covering open/friendly-exit/forward/dangling/multiple/cycle.

[m] `start-bead/SKILL.md` Step 1.5 section documents: (a) the preflight invocation, (b) a decision table mapping each `decision=...` output to an agent action, (c) the canonical friendly-exit suffix `is closed. Did you mean a different bead?`, (d) audit-comment templates for dangling / multiple / cycle halts targeting the **original target**, (e) the Step-1 re-entry contract for the forward path.

[m] `start-bead/SKILL.md` Step 1.5 section contains NO references to specific bead IDs and NO `R#` numbered subsection headers. Verifiable: zero matches for `agents-config-` and zero matches for `^#### R[0-9]` within the Step 1.5 section.

[m] `start-bead/SKILL.md` decision table contains exactly 5 data rows (excluding header) with the columns specified in D1, including a `closed | yes (exactly 1)` row routing to Z and a `closed | no` row routing to friendly exit, plus the footnote noting that ≥2 `produced-bead-*` labels halts.

[m] `start-bead/SKILL.md` red-flags table contains a row whose Thought column is exactly `The bead is closed, just give up` and whose Reality column reads `Run Step 1.5. If the helper says forward, route to the new target. Otherwise, friendly exit. Never silently fall through.` (modulo standard markdown-table escaping).

[m] `agents-config-12q7.3`'s `acceptance_criteria` field contains text matching regex `start-bead.*redirects to Y`. The O1 procedure is byte-equal idempotent across consecutive runs after the first (state after run N byte-equals state after run N+1, for all N ≥ 1).

[m] A follow-up bead exists for the open-bead-with-produced-bead-* soft-warning UX, with `discovered-from agents-config-12q7.2`. (Already filed as `agents-config-hhou`.)
acceptance_criteria:
[m] start-bead/SKILL.md contains a section titled "### Step 1.5: Closed-bead preflight" inserted between "### Step 1:" and "### Step 2:" sections. Verifiable via: section line numbers from grep -nE '^### Step (1|1\.5|2):' src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md must appear in numeric order (1 < 1.5 < 2).
[m] The Step 1.5 section documents: (a) the bd show ... --json | jq produced-bead-extraction probe (NOT bd label list text-format), (b) the bd show ... | jq -e --arg yid Y-existence probe, (c) the canonical friendly-exit message suffix "is closed. Did you mean a different bead?", (d) explicit Step-1 re-entry contract for the forward path, (e) the visited-set cycle detection mechanism with its three loop invariants (initial seeding, top-of-R3 check, pre-re-entry add), (f) audit-comment templates for dangling-label/multiple-labels/cycle halts, (g) the original-target convention (audit comments target the original, not the intermediate chain bead).
[m] start-bead/SKILL.md decision table contains exactly 5 data rows (excluding the header row), with columns "Status | Has produced-bead-* | Has implementation-ready | Trivial | Route". The 5 rows include a "closed | yes (exactly 1) | — | — | Z" row and a "closed | no | — | — | exit" row. A footnote follows the table noting that closed with ≥2 produced-bead-* labels halts (R7).
[m] start-bead/SKILL.md red-flags table contains a row whose Thought column is exactly "The bead is closed, just give up" and whose Reality column matches verbatim (modulo standard markdown-table escaping) D3's pinned text: "Run Step 1.5. If produced-bead-<Y> exists, Route Z forwards to Y. Otherwise, friendly exit referencing the current target. Never silently fall through."
[m] start-bead/SKILL.md Step 1.5 section documents the closed-bead-without-produced-bead-* friendly-exit path including the verbatim message suffix "is closed. Did you mean a different bead?" AND specifies that the prefix is templated with the current target's bead id at runtime (not the original target).
[m] start-bead/SKILL.md Step 1.5 section names the visited-set cycle-detection mechanism explicitly (not a depth/hop counter), enumerates its three loop invariants, and states that detection emits a halt naming the cycle path.
[m] agents-config-12q7.3's acceptance_criteria field contains text matching regex "start-bead.*redirects to Y" AND the spec's O1 procedure is byte-equal idempotent across consecutive runs after the first (verifiable: the state of acceptance_criteria after run N must byte-equal its state after run N+1, for all N ≥ 1).
[m] A follow-up bead has been filed for the open-bead-with-produced-bead-* soft-warning UX (per cycle-2 review MINOR-3), with discovered-from agents-config-12q7.2.
notes:
verify-ac validation report (12q7.4):
  AC1: PASS — Step sections in order: 1 (line 24) < 1.5 (line 33) < 2 (line 188)
  AC2(a-h): PASS — counts: a=15, b=1, c=1, d=2, e=1, f=9, g=3, h=4

title: Wire implement-bead skill escalation path to HEP procedure
description:
# Wire `implement-bead` skill escalation path to HEP procedure

## Problem

The `implement-bead` skill at `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md` (currently lines ~71–75 — verify at implementation time) implements the OLD human-flag escalation pattern that PR #47's HEP pivot deprecated. The skill currently labels both the **source bead** and the **step bead** with `human` when escalating — which violates the HEP single-bead invariant established in `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §5.6.

This is structurally identical to the formula-side problem tracked by `agents-config-7bk.13.2`, but in the skill markdown (orchestrator behavior) rather than in formula TOML files (per-stage step body). It is the **other** upstream producer of HEP escalations: when a worker subagent returns `needs_human`, or when the orchestrator detects formula collisions, bad worktree paths, or other mid-flight conditions requiring human judgment, this is where the escalation gets created.

Because `implement-bead` is the central stage runner for every poured molecule, this bug affects **every** runtime escalation path that isn't merge-or-handoff. Codex adversarial review on PR #47 flagged this as `[high]` — common blockers (worker `needs_human`, formula collisions, worktree failures) escape the only readiness gate the new design trusts (`bd ready`'s blocker-aware filtering), so paused work can be re-claimed by run-queue while the human pause is unresolved.

## Required behavior (target state)

Replace the implement-bead escalation path with the HEP procedure from arch doc §5.6:

1. `bd create` a hand-off escalation bead:
   - Title: `[Stuck] <source-title>` or similar discriminator (final wording TBD during brainstorming; must NOT collide with `[Merge gate]` from `merge-or-handoff` or `[Human verify]` from `[h]` follow-ups)
   - Labels: `human` + a class-specific scenario hint (`scenario-hint:tooling-credentials`, `scenario-hint:scope-expanded`, etc., per arch §5.6 escalation procedure)
   - No parent (HEP escalation beads are orphan)
2. Append context to the escalation bead's notes: source-bead id, step-bead id, molecule id, worktree path (if present), worker output, scenario hint reasoning.
3. `bd dep add <source-id> <handoff-id>` — source becomes blocked-by the escalation bead, excluding it from `bd ready`.
4. `bd update <source-id> --status open` — source reverts from `in_progress` to `open` per HEP semantics. Ancestor epics are NOT reverted (I1 claim-walk is upward-only).
5. The source bead does **NOT** receive `human`. The step bead does **NOT** receive `human`. The molecule retains its current step state per §5.5 (step-bead status remains `in_progress`).
6. The orchestrator exits cleanly (zero exit code; the stage is paused, not failed).

## Files affected

- `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md` — escalation path block (lines ~71–75 per codex adversarial review on PR #47; verify exact lines at implementation time).
- Possibly `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md` if it shares a similar escalation-from-routing path (verify during brainstorming).
- Possibly `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/run-queue/SKILL.md` if its scan logic creates escalations directly (verify during brainstorming).

## Out of scope

- The downstream `resolve-human-bead` skill that consumes these escalations (owned by `agents-config-7bk.13`).
- The `merge-or-handoff` formula step's hand-off path (owned by `agents-config-7bk.13.2`; structurally similar but in a different file class).
- Any changes to `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml`.
- Documentation updates to the architecture doc — already done in PR #47.

## Acceptance criteria

- The implement-bead skill's escalation path implements the HEP escalation procedure (escalation bead + dep blocker + source status revert; source and step beads do NOT receive `human`).
- After escalation: source bead carries `open` status + open dep blocker; does not carry `human` or any merge-gate discriminator. Step bead is unchanged. Escalation bead carries `human` + scenario-hint discriminator; surfaces in `bd human list`.
- start-bead and run-queue skills audited for the same anti-pattern; if either creates escalations directly, fix in lockstep.
- Lockstep maintained with `agents-config-7bk.13.2` — both upstream producers (formula + skill) emit byte-for-byte equivalent escalation-bead shapes (same label set, same title-prefix discipline, same notes-attachment shape modulo class hint).
- Round-trip integration with `agents-config-7bk.13`'s resolve-human-bead skill: produced escalation bead is correctly classified as the appropriate HEP scenario (A–E based on the scenario-hint discriminator).
- Tests/build/lint pass.

- **Soft consumer**: `agents-config-7bk.13` (resolve-human-bead skill) for round-trip integration testing. No hard dep — this bead can implement and ship its own changes; integration testing happens after the resolver lands.

title: Wire merge-or-handoff formula step to HEP escalation procedure
description:
# Wire `merge-or-handoff` formula step to HEP escalation procedure

## Problem

The `merge-or-handoff` step in three formulas — `implement-feature.formula.toml`, `fix-bug.formula.toml`, `docs-only.formula.toml` — currently implements the OLD hand-off pattern that PR #47's HEP pivot deprecated. The hand-off path stamps labels directly on the source bead:

```
bd update {{bead-id}} --status open
bd label add {{bead-id}} merge-ready
bd label add {{bead-id}} human   # ← VIOLATES HEP single-bead invariant
```

This violates the single-bead `human` invariant established in `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §5.6 and `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md`. Under HEP, only the **escalation bead** carries `human`; the source bead pauses via a blocking dep.

## Required behavior (target state)

Replace the hand-off path in all three formulas with the HEP escalation procedure from arch doc §3 + §5.6:

1. `bd create` a hand-off escalation bead:
   - Title: `[Merge gate] <source-title>`
   - Labels: `human` + `merge-ready` (the `merge-ready` label discriminates merge-gate hand-off from other HEP escalation classes; resolve-human-bead Scenario G uses it to route to `/merge-and-cleanup` rather than `bd human respond/dismiss`)
   - No parent (HEP escalation beads are orphan)
2. `bd dep add <source-id> <handoff-id>` — source becomes blocked-by the escalation bead, excluding it from `bd ready`.
3. `bd update <source-id> --status open` — source reverts from `in_progress` to `open` per HEP semantics. Ancestor epics are NOT reverted (I1 claim-walk is upward-only).
4. The source bead does **NOT** receive `human` (HEP single-bead invariant).
5. The user-facing note "Human runs /merge-and-cleanup when ready" remains valid — the resolution flow is unchanged from the user's perspective; only the bead-state shape is corrected.

## Files affected
- `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml` (`merge-or-handoff` step, hand-off path block)
- `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml` (`merge-or-handoff` step, hand-off path block)
- `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/docs-only.formula.toml` (`merge-or-handoff` step, hand-off path block)

## Out of scope

- The auto-merge path in the same step (already correct).
- The downstream `resolve-human-bead` skill that consumes these escalations (owned by 7bk.13).
- Any changes to `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml` itself.
- Documentation updates — already done in PR #47.

## Acceptance criteria

- All three formulas' `merge-or-handoff` step hand-off paths implement the HEP escalation procedure (escalation bead + dep blocker + source status revert; source does NOT receive `human`).
- Source bead carries `open` status + open dep blocker after hand-off; does not carry `human` or `merge-ready`.
- Hand-off escalation bead carries `human` + `merge-ready`; surfaces in `bd human list` with the discriminator visible.
- The three formulas remain consistent with each other for the merge-or-handoff step body (lockstep maintained — diff between the three formulas' hand-off blocks should be empty modulo formula-specific variable names).
- Round-trip integration with 7bk.13's resolve-human-bead skill: produced escalation bead is correctly classified as Scenario G.
- Tests/build/lint pass.

## Dependencies

- **Soft**: 7bk.13 (resolve-human-bead skill) for round-trip integration testing. No hard dep — this bead can implement and ship its own changes; integration testing happens after both land.

title: Green-loop: implement via label-driven RALF dispatch
description:
Implement the feature using label-driven skill dispatch.
Model: claude-opus-4-7  Effort: medium

## Inputs
- Bead spec from: bd show {{bead-id}}
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule

## Dispatch contract

Check the bead's RALF signal before dispatching:

  bd label list {{bead-id}} --json

- If label `ralf:required` is present:
    invoke `ralf-implement` with the bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, and optional max cycle count from `ralf:cycles=N`

    `ralf-implement` owns only the inner quality loop: iteration,
    independence, adversarial posture, convergence, and structured report output.
    It does not read bead labels or update bead state; this formula step passes
    all needed inputs.

    After it returns, append the structured report summary to the green-loop
    step-bead notes, including cycles run, score, severity counts, and
    foreign-eyes status/degradation for every Codex/Gemini cycle.

    If the score is `FAIL`, or if the score is `PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS` with
    unresolved major concerns, stop this step: label both the source bead and
    the green-loop step bead `human`, append the unresolved blocking/critical/major
    concerns to both notes, and do not advance to `quality-sweep` until the
    caller explicitly decides whether to revise, defer, or accept the residual risk.

- Otherwise:
    invoke `superpowers:test-driven-development` first,
    then the appropriate domain skill for implementation.

Record the dispatch decision for audit:

  bd comments add {{bead-id}} "implement: dispatched <skill-name>"

`bd comments add` is non-destructive: it creates a separate comment and does
not replace issue notes. For cumulative step output, use `bd update
--append-notes`; do not use `--notes`, which replaces existing notes.

Persist step output before closing green-loop. Here `<step-bead-id>` is the current green-loop molecule step bead:

  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "IMPLEMENTATION-REPORT: score=<PASS|PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS|FAIL>; cycles=<n>/<max>; severity=blocking:<n>,critical:<n>,major:<n>,minor:<n>; summary=<summary from dispatched skill>"
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "FOREIGN-EYES: cycle-1=<COMPLETED|UNAVAILABLE|TIMED_OUT|QUOTA_EXCEEDED|AUTH_FAILED|NO_OUTPUT|UNUSABLE_OUTPUT|NOT_RUN>; cycle-2=<...>; degraded=<list or none>"

Before closing green-loop, ensure the dispatched implementation path has run
and recorded the mandatory completion gate for its own changes:
`quality-reviewer` → `simplify` → `verify-checklist`. If gate evidence is
missing, run it now and append the evidence to the step-bead notes. If any
gate step fails, label both the source bead and step bead `human`, append the
failure evidence to both notes, and do not advance to `quality-sweep`.

## Coverage

Coverage threshold from project-config.toml [coverage].threshold (default 80%),
overridable per-bead via the `coverage-threshold-<n>` label;
honored only when [coverage].applicable is true or absent. When
[coverage].applicable is explicitly false (docs-only repos, config repos, etc.),
coverage is not a quality gate and the threshold is bypassed. See
docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md §3 (green-loop) for the canonical
contract.

Rules (regardless of dispatched path):
- minimum viable implementation only
- no speculative features
- no over-engineering for hypothetical requirements

## Definition of Done
Required feature behavior implemented with passing quality gates.

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-check labels and dispatch per current bead state at step start.
notes:
IMPLEMENTATION-REPORT: score=PASS; cycles=1/1; severity=blocking:0,critical:0,major:0,minor:0; summary=Replaced Phase 5 finalize step in src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/brainstorm-bead.formula.toml with the X→Y redesign per agents-config-12q7 spec. All 13 [m] AC items addressed. TOML parse validated via Python tomllib (6 steps, all keys recognized). Discovered and fixed two bd CLI quirks during validation: (1) bd create --json returns single object not array (use .id, not .id // .[0].id); (2) bd dep edges use .type/.depends_on_id field names and parent edge type is 'parent-child' not 'parent'; bd dep remove is positional-only with no --type flag. Commit: 2139191 on feat/agents-config-12q7-brainstorm-formula-redesign.
FOREIGN-EYES: cycle-1=NOT_RUN; cycle-2=NOT_RUN; degraded=cycle-1,cycle-2 (worker fork constraint — fork-boilerplate forbids spawning sub-agents, so ralf-implement's foreign-eyes adversarial passes could not be dispatched. Self-review performed by fork against full bead spec; bd CLI quirks verified live against current bd binary).
COMPLETION-GATE: build=skip ([gates].build=''), typecheck=skip ([gates].typecheck=''), lint=skip ([gates].lint=''), test=skip ([gates].test=''), coverage=skip ([coverage].applicable=false). TOML parse PASS via Python 3.13 tomllib. quality-reviewer/simplify/verify-checklist agents not dispatched (fork constraint); orchestrator should run completion gate at green-loop close-out.
COMPLETION-GATE-AUDIT (orchestrator-run after green-loop close): quality-reviewer=PASS_AFTER_FIX (1 BLOCKING B1, 4 HIGH H1/H3/H4/H6, all fixed in b1aade8); simplify=APPLIED (X_DESC removed in f884bc0); verify-checklist=PASS (bd formula list exit 0; bd formula show brainstorm-bead exit 0; 3 commits 2139191/b1aade8/f884bc0; 1 file +427/-147).

title: implement-feature
description: Per-stage feature pipeline: preflight → red-tests → green-loop → quality-sweep → verify-ac → create-pr → review-cycle → merge-or-handoff

title: Review-cycle: address PR review feedback
description:
Drive the PR review loop until all review threads are resolved.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

Skips entirely when review-level:none.

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:wait-for-pr-comments (chains internally to superpowers:reply-and-resolve-pr-threads)

## Actions

Invoke superpowers:wait-for-pr-comments with:
  --mode autonomous --bead-id {{bead-id}}

The skill:
1. Polls Copilot via background script (zero Anthropic tokens during wait).
2. Classifies each comment: FIX / SKIP / ESCALATE.
3. Dispatches per-comment fix subagents (sonnet) for FIX-class items.
4. Pushes combined commits.
5. Internally chains to superpowers:reply-and-resolve-pr-threads:
   replies to every thread + resolves FIXED review_thread items via GraphQL.

One iteration = one outbound reply-batch.
Iteration cap: 5 (override: iteration-cap-review-cycle-<n> label).
Early exit when no remaining FIX-class items.

Exit conditions (mutually exclusive):
- review-exit-copilot-only (default when review-exit-human-approvers-<n> absent)
- review-exit-human-approvers-<n>: require N human approvers

Persist after each batch:
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "REVIEW-BATCH:<n>/<MAX>"

If PR is CLOSED on re-entry: flag-human. Do NOT poll a closed PR.

## Definition of Done
Every review thread has a reply. FIXED items resolved on GitHub.
No in-flight FIX work.
notes: REVIEW-CYCLE: round=1; comments=3; FIX=2; SKIP=1 (review_summary); ESCALATE=0; commits-pushed=2 (7b549c9 mktemp, e377b5a dep-gate); threads-resolved=2/2; final-state=clean. Worker-fork constraint: per-comment subagents could not be dispatched; FIX work executed inline by review-cycle subagent (lite-gate for mktemp, full-gate for dep-gate via cumulative tomllib parse + push verification). Reply matrix used verbatim. No bug beads filed during this cycle.
close_reason: review-cycle: 1 round, 2 FIX commits (7b549c9, e377b5a), 2/2 threads resolved, no escalations

title: Merge-or-handoff: auto-merge or hand off to human
description:
Either auto-merge the PR or hand off to the human.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Gate 1 — PR-state clean-check
Conditions (ALL must be true for auto-merge path):
  - Zero open Copilot review threads
  - Zero open human-author review threads
  - All CI checks green
  - 5-minute quiet window with no new comments or review activity
On wait-for-pr-comments timeout: treat clean-check as NOT clean.

## Auto-merge path (auto-mergeable label present AND clean-check clean)
1. Re-run clean-check immediately before merge call.
2. Pour merge-and-cleanup formula (phase: liquid — NOT vapor).
3. Drive merge-and-cleanup:
   - Merge the PR
   - Delete branch (local + remote)
   - Remove worktree (decode from worktree-path-* label)
   - Run parent-chain I2 close-walk
   Note: merge-and-cleanup gate-step (Gate 2) blocks until ALL [h] follow-up
   children are closed with verified-by-human label, then closes the
   merge-{source-id} child after merge + cleanup so the source bead can close.

## Hand-off path (default — auto-mergeable absent OR clean-check dirty)
1. Transition bead to open: bd update {{bead-id}} --status open
2. Stamp labels: bd label add {{bead-id}} merge-ready
                 bd label add {{bead-id}} human
   (merge-ready = why it's on human list; human = surfaces via bd human list)
3. Note: bead is now excluded from bd ready (human label gates it).
   Human runs /merge-and-cleanup when ready.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view --json mergedAt first. If non-null: skip merge, proceed to cleanup.
close_reason: merge-or-handoff: hand-off path (no auto-mergeable label; merge prerequisite gates direct merge)

title: Create-PR: push branch and open pull request
description:
Create the pull request and persist its URL to the molecule.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch

## Actions

1. Invoke superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch.

2. PR body MUST include:
   - Bead reference: {{bead-id}} and bead title
   - AC (full list with [m]/[h] tags)
   - implementation iteration summary (from green-loop step-bead notes)
   - verify-ac validation report
   - Bug beads filed during verify-ac (if any)
   - Human-required AC follow-up bead references (if any [h] lines)
   - Foreign-eyes status section (list which iterations had degraded foreign-eyes)

3. Stamp pr-url-* label on molecule:
   Encoding: same bijection as worktree-path (though URLs rarely need it).
   bd label add <mol-id> pr-url-<encoded-url>

## PR creation failure
Flag-human with worktree intact. Do NOT tear down the worktree.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view first. If a PR exists for the branch and is OPEN: skip creation,
persist URL idempotently.
If PR exists and is CLOSED: flag-human. Do NOT auto-reopen or create a new PR.

title: Verify-AC: mechanical acceptance criteria validation
description:
Validate every [m]-tagged AC bullet against worktree state.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skips when review-level:none.

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (parse [m] and [h] tags)
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule
- Functional tests from: project-config.toml [functional-tests]

## Actions

For each [m]-tagged AC line:
1. Dispatch bead-verifier (haiku, low effort) to run shell-runnable witnesses.
   Collect: exit code + error excerpt.
2. Assemble validation report.

[h]-tagged AC lines: do NOT validate here. Confirm that human follow-up
child beads were filed at brainstorm-time finalize (bd list --parent {{bead-id}}
filtered by label human). If any [h] bullet has no matching follow-up, flag-human.

Run functional/e2e tests from [functional-tests] in project-config.toml.
If functional tests fail:
  - Create one bug bead per failure, label human.
  - Note in step-bead. Continue to create-pr (do NOT loop back).

Persist validation report to BOTH:
  - Step-bead notes: bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "<report>"
  - Source bead notes: bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "<report>"
  (Report also included in PR description at create-pr.)

## Definition of Done
Every [m] AC bullet has explicit witness evidence.
Human-review bullets have follow-up beads filed (confirm presence).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates and continues from prior pass.

title: Preflight: spec validation, formula selection, worktree creation
description:
Validate the bead spec and create the worktree before any implementation work.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: high

## Inputs
Read the bead and its labels:
  bd show {{bead-id}}
  bd label list {{bead-id}}

## Actions

1. Verify `brainstormed` and `implementation-ready` labels are present.
   If either is absent: apply human-flag protocol and exit.

2. Note: formula selection and pour have already happened — this step was
   created by implement-bead pouring implement-feature. There is nothing
   to select or pour here. Proceed directly to project-config.toml validation.

3. Read project-config.toml and verify:
   - `[gates]` section present (or stubs acceptable).
   - Coverage applicability ([coverage].applicable): if explicitly set to `false`,
     coverage does NOT apply to this project (docs-only repos, config repos, etc.).
     Skip the report-location check entirely and proceed to step 4. Record this
     by running:
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "preflight: coverage opt-out (project-config has [coverage].applicable = false) — skipped report-location check"
   - Coverage report location ([coverage].report-location): only checked when
     `[coverage].applicable` is true OR absent (default). If absent OR empty
     string (i.e. `report-location = ""`), apply the human-flag protocol and
     stop this stage:
       bd label add {{bead-id}} human
       bd label add <step-bead-id> human
       bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "preflight: no coverage report location configured in project-config.toml"
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "preflight: no coverage report location configured — molecule parked"
     An empty string is treated the same as absent: explicit opt-out requires
     `[coverage].applicable = false` — a bare empty string is ambiguous and
     must be resolved by the human.
     Do NOT continue to worktree creation. Exit cleanly.
   - [static-analysis] section: if absent or empty, mark quality-sweep skippable.

4. Stamp the molecule→bead lookup label if not already stamped:
   bd label add <mol-id> for-bead-{{bead-id}}

5. Create the worktree:
   Branch name: feat/{{bead-id}}-<slug-from-title>
   Location: <repo-root>/.claude/worktrees/<branch-name>
   Command:
     git worktree add .claude/worktrees/<branch> -b <branch>
   CRITICAL: the formula is already poured (this step was created by the pour).
   If worktree creation fails: squash the molecule and flag-human.

6. Stamp the worktree-path label on the molecule:
   Encoding (apply IN ORDER):
     _ → _u
     / → __
   Then: bd label add <mol-id> worktree-path-<encoded>

7. Claim the bead and walk the parent chain:
   bd update {{bead-id}} --status in_progress
   PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
   while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
     bd update "$PARENT" --status in_progress
     PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
   done

## Definition of Done
Poured molecule + worktree exists + worktree-path-* label stamped on molecule.
OR: human label + structured gap-note + (if pour completed) molecule squashed.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check: does an active molecule exist? Does the worktree path decode to a real
git worktree? If so, skip work already done.
notes: preflight: coverage opt-out (project-config has [coverage].applicable = false) — skipped report-location check
close_reason: Labels verified, coverage opt-out recorded, worktree created at .claude/worktrees/feat-agents-config-12q7-brainstorm-formula-redesign, path label stamped

title: Red-tests: write failing tests covering all [m] AC bullets
description:
Write failing tests (TDD red phase) before any implementation code.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:test-driven-development, superpowers:writing-unit-tests,
superpowers:testing-anti-patterns

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (read [m]-tagged lines)
- Worktree path: decode worktree-path-* label from molecule

## Decode worktree-path label before any work
  1. Decode: __ → /  then  _u → _
  2. Verify decoded path exists and is a git worktree:
       git -C <path> rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree
  3. Operate from that cwd.

## Actions

### Opt-out (evaluate FIRST, before any reviewer dispatch)

This step has a canonical opt-out predicate. Evaluate triggers in this fixed
order at the top of the step:

1. Trigger A — no test runner. If `[gates].test == ""` in project-config.toml,
   the project has no way to run tests. Skip with skip-note prefix
   `red-tests opt-out: ` and trigger description:
     red-tests opt-out: no test runner ([gates].test=='')

2. Trigger B — no [m] AC lines. If the bead's `acceptance_criteria`
   field (the canonical AC store per spec §4.1, NOT `description`) has
   zero `[m]`-classified lines, there is nothing for tests to assert.
   Apply the canonical line parser (`^\[(m|h)\]\s(.*)$`; untagged lines
   default to `[m]` per §4.1's backwards-compat rule). Counting
   `description` would both false-positive on `[m]` tokens inside notes
   AND false-negative on canonical (non-bullet) AC formats. Pinned
   line-anchored grep -cvE counts every non-blank line that is NOT
   explicitly `[h]`-tagged (i.e. [m] lines plus untagged-defaults-to-[m]):
     m_count=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].acceptance_criteria // ""' | grep -cvE '^([[:space:]]*$|\[h\][[:space:]])')
   If m_count == 0, skip with note:
     red-tests opt-out: no [m] AC lines

3. Both triggers fire (no test runner AND no [m] AC lines):
     red-tests opt-out: both

4. Otherwise, proceed with the normal red-tests behavior below.

Skip mechanism (when any trigger fires):
   bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "red-tests opt-out: <trigger>"
The literal prefix `red-tests opt-out: ` is grep-able for verify-ac
groundedness. Then close the step bead and exit; do not dispatch reviewers.

Conversely: a bead with [m] ACs MUST run red-tests, regardless of project
signals (Trigger A still skips when there is no test runner — [m] ACs in
a runner-less project are a brainstorm-time error). There is no per-bead
`red-tests:enable` opt-IN or `red-tests:skip` opt-OUT label. AC tagging
is the per-bead signal.

### Normal flow (when no Opt-out trigger fires)

1. Dispatch bead-implementor (sonnet, medium effort) to write tests:
   - Dispatch with: worktree path, AC bullets (all [m]-tagged lines),
     step-bead ID, instruction to write failing tests and confirm they fail.
   - Tests must cover: happy path, edge cases, error paths.
   - Do NOT mock what has not been verified.
   - Do NOT write test-only methods in production code.

2. Run the Claude reviewer (test-review skill) on the written tests.
   When review-level:none is set: skip test reviewers (tests still written).

3. Run Codex adversarial review of the tests (gpt-5.5 default).
   Model from: project-config.toml [foreign-cli].codex_red_tests_model
   When review-level:none: skip.
   Subject to foreign-eyes degradation tracking (FOREIGN-EYES-ITER-<n>).

4. Iteration cap: 2 (override: iteration-cap-red-tests-<n> label).
   At cap with reviewer disagreement: write disagreement note to step-bead notes.
   Escalate human ONLY if BOTH reviewers agree on a critical/major design flaw.

5. Commit failing tests to the feature branch.

## Definition of Done
Tests committed to feature branch; both reviewers approve "shippable" state
(or iteration cap reached with disagreement note recorded).
OR step opted out per the canonical Opt-out predicate above, with the
pinned `red-tests opt-out: ...` skip-note appended to step-bead notes.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check filesystem (committed tests) and step-bead notes. Continue from prior state.
notes: red-tests opt-out: no test runner ([gates].test=='')
close_reason: red-tests opt-out: no test runner

title: Quality-sweep: global quality gate across entire project
description:
Run the full project quality suite (not just changed files).
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skippable when preflight marked it so (no [static-analysis] in project-config.toml,
or empty gates). Also skips when review-level:none is set.

## Order (fixed)
1. Lint auto-fix: [lint-autofix].command from project-config.toml (run FIRST)
2. Build + typecheck + lint (from [gates])
3. Static analysis (from [static-analysis] — semgrep, depcheck, etc.)
4. Tests (from [gates].test)

ALWAYS end with build + tests after any code changes (guarantees verify-ac starts green).

## Coverage

This stage does not separately enforce a coverage threshold; coverage gating,
if any, lives in green-loop's Definition of Done and is conditional on
[coverage].applicable. See docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md §3
(quality-sweep).

## Auto-fix vs flag-human
Auto-fix unless issues betray a design flaw requiring fundamental changes.
Use judgment based on the size of the fix.

## Definition of Done
All gate commands exit zero (or stage skipped per preflight / review-level:none).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates state and runs only what is not already green.

title: Route Z: start-bead handles closed source-bead with produced-bead-<Y-id> label
description:
Add Route Z to the start-bead skill. Detects when the target bead is CLOSED and carries a produced-bead-<Y-id> label (stamped by the redesigned brainstorm-bead formula in agents-config-12q7). Action: forward the call to start-bead <Y-id> so the user's intent — work on this thing — routes to the active implementation bead Y instead of dead-ending on the closed seed bead X.

Without Route Z, every closed source-bead carrying produced-bead-* becomes a trap for start-bead: Route C will try to wisp brainstorm-bead on a closed bead. This is a MERGE PREREQUISITE for agents-config-12q7's PR (#48) — the redesign cannot ship until Route Z lands first.

Likely touches: src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md (route definition + decision table updates). Probably docs-only.

Spec to be written via brainstorm-bead.

Did you mean a different bead?", (d) explicit Step-1 re-entry contract for the forward path, (e) the visited-set cycle detection mechanism with its three loop invariants (initial seeding, top-of-R3 check, pre-re-entry add), (f) audit-comment templates for dangling-label/multiple-labels/cycle halts, (g) the original-target convention (audit comments target the original, not the intermediate chain bead).

The 5 rows include a "closed | yes (exactly 1) | — | — | Z" row and a "closed | no | — | — | exit" row. A footnote follows the table noting that closed with ≥2 produced-bead-* labels halts (R7).
