title: Quality-sweep: global quality gate across entire project
description:
Run the full project quality suite (not just changed files).
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skippable when preflight marked it so (no [static-analysis] in project-config.toml,
or empty gates). Also skips when review-level:none is set.

## Order (fixed)
1. Lint auto-fix: [lint-autofix].command from project-config.toml (run FIRST)
2. Build + typecheck + lint (from [gates])
3. Static analysis (from [static-analysis] — semgrep, depcheck, etc.)
4. Tests (from [gates].test)

ALWAYS end with build + tests after any code changes (guarantees verify-ac starts green).

## Auto-fix vs flag-human
Auto-fix unless issues betray a design flaw requiring fundamental changes.
Use judgment based on the size of the fix.

## Definition of Done
All gate commands exit zero (or stage skipped per preflight / review-level:none).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates state and runs only what is not already green.
close_reason: Quality-sweep: gates skipped (docs-only, all gates empty, no static-analysis). Completion gate retrofit: quality-reviewer pass found 1 missed edit (stale caveat at spec line 436 contradicting canonical wording at lines 143/153/173); auto-fixed by amending commit dab08a2 → cb5b11c. Verbatim Coverage prose identity confirmed between implement-feature and fix-bug formulas (both green-loop and quality-sweep). All 17 [m] grep-witness ACs satisfied. Simplify pass: no findings — prose is concise and serves the AC requirements directly.

title: Merge-or-handoff: auto-merge or hand off to human
description:
Either auto-merge the PR or hand off to the human.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Gate 1 — PR-state clean-check
Conditions (ALL must be true for auto-merge path):
  - Zero open Copilot review threads
  - Zero open human-author review threads
  - All CI checks green
  - 5-minute quiet window with no new comments or review activity
On wait-for-pr-comments timeout: treat clean-check as NOT clean.

## Auto-merge path (auto-mergeable label present AND clean-check clean)
1. Re-run clean-check immediately before merge call.
2. Pour merge-and-cleanup formula (phase: liquid — NOT vapor).
3. Drive merge-and-cleanup:
   - Merge the PR
   - Delete branch (local + remote)
   - Remove worktree (decode from worktree-path-* label)
   - Run parent-chain I2 close-walk
   Note: merge-and-cleanup gate-step (Gate 2) blocks until ALL [h] follow-up
   children are closed with verified-by-human label AND the merge-{source-id}
   child is closed.

## Hand-off path (default — auto-mergeable absent OR clean-check dirty)
1. Transition bead to open: bd update {{bead-id}} --status open
2. Stamp labels: bd label add {{bead-id}} merge-ready
                 bd label add {{bead-id}} human
   (merge-ready = why it's on human list; human = surfaces via bd human list)
3. Note: bead is now excluded from bd ready (human label gates it).
   Human runs /merge-and-cleanup when ready.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view --json mergedAt first. If non-null: skip merge, proceed to cleanup.

5. Internally chains to superpowers:reply-and-resolve-pr-threads:
   replies to every thread + resolves FIXED review_thread items via GraphQL.

One iteration = one outbound reply-batch.
Iteration cap: 5 (override: iteration-cap-review-cycle-<n> label).
Early exit when no remaining FIX-class items.

Exit conditions (mutually exclusive):
- review-exit-copilot-only (default when review-exit-human-approvers-<n> absent)
- review-exit-human-approvers-<n>: require N human approvers

Persist after each batch:
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "REVIEW-BATCH:<n>/<MAX>"

If PR is CLOSED on re-entry: flag-human. Do NOT poll a closed PR.

## Definition of Done
Every review thread has a reply. FIXED items resolved on GitHub.
No in-flight FIX work.

title: Green-loop: RALF-IT implementation until tests pass
description:
Implement the feature (TDD green phase) via RALF-IT iterative refinement.
Model: claude-opus-4-7  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
RALF-IT methodology (embedded in controller)

## Inputs
- Committed failing tests from red-tests stage
- Bead spec from: bd show {{bead-id}}
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule
- Prior RALF-IT state: RALF-IT-ITER:<n>/<MAX> in step-bead notes (default 0)

## RALF-IT loop

MAX_ITERATIONS = 5 (override: iteration-cap-green-loop-<n> label — SOLE override path)

For each iteration:

1. Dispatch bead-implementor to write minimum code to make tests pass:
   - Effort: high on iter 1, medium on iters 2+
   - Pass: worktree path, bead spec, iteration number, prior state

2. Run quality gate (build/typecheck/lint/test) — from project-config.toml [gates].
   Gate must pass before review.

3. Run foreign-eyes review:
   - Iter 1: Codex (gpt-5.5, or codex_green_loop_iter1_model from project-config.toml)
   - Iter 2: Gemini (gemini-2.5-pro, or gemini_green_loop_iter2_model)
   - Iter 3+: pure-Claude fresh-eyes subagent
   Record degradation: FOREIGN-EYES-ITER-<n>: codex=<status>, gemini=<status>

4. When review-level:deep: run /codex:adversarial-review --model gpt-5.4 after gate.
   When review-level:none: skip per-iteration code-review and simplify.

5. Persist iteration marker AFTER iteration completes:
   bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "RALF-IT-ITER:<n>/{{MAX}}"

6. Converge or loop: if reviewers approve and tests green, exit loop.
   Otherwise continue to next iteration.

After loop:
- Run simplify skill once on the full diff (not per-iteration).
- Run quality-reviewer (claude-opus-4-7[1m]) on the full bead diff.
- If foreign-eyes degraded in 2+ of N iterations: flag-human.
  Stamp: foreign-eyes-degraded:<n>/<N> on source bead.

Coverage threshold: from project-config.toml [coverage].threshold (default 80%)
or coverage-threshold-<n> label on bead.

## Definition of Done
RALF-IT reports complete; tests green; coverage meets target; build/typecheck/lint pass.

## Idempotent re-entry
Read RALF-IT-ITER: marker from step-bead notes. Resume at n+1.
Crash mid-iteration: marker at prior value, retry failed iteration.
notes: RALF-IT-ITER:1/5 (gates stubbed, prose-only; quality-reviewer + simplify run inline on diff; all 17 [m] ACs grep-verified; identical Coverage prose between implement-feature and fix-bug confirmed via diff)

title: Verify-AC: mechanical acceptance criteria validation
description:
Validate every [m]-tagged AC bullet against worktree state.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skips when review-level:none.

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (parse [m] and [h] tags)
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule
- Functional tests from: project-config.toml [functional-tests]

## Actions

For each [m]-tagged AC line:
1. Dispatch bead-verifier (haiku, low effort) to run shell-runnable witnesses.
   Collect: exit code + error excerpt.
2. Assemble validation report.

[h]-tagged AC lines: do NOT validate here. Confirm that human follow-up
child beads were filed at brainstorm-time finalize (bd list --parent {{bead-id}}
filtered by label human). If any [h] bullet has no matching follow-up, flag-human.

Run functional/e2e tests from [functional-tests] in project-config.toml.
If functional tests fail:
  - Create one bug bead per failure, label human.
  - Note in step-bead. Continue to create-pr (do NOT loop back).

Persist validation report to BOTH:
  - Step-bead notes: bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "<report>"
  - Source bead notes: bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "<report>"
  (Report also included in PR description at create-pr.)

## Definition of Done
Every [m] AC bullet has explicit witness evidence.
Human-review bullets have follow-up beads filed (confirm presence).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates and continues from prior pass.

title: Add red-tests opt-out for docs-only beads / [m]-tagless ACs
description:
## Purpose

The implement-feature.formula.toml red-tests step assumes a code-bearing project: it dispatches a bead-implementor (sonnet) to write failing tests covering `[m]`-tagged AC bullets, runs reviewers, and commits failing tests. For docs-only beads (no `[m]` AC tags) and for projects with no test runner (`[gates].test = ""`), the step has no meaningful work and currently has no formal opt-out.

This bead adds the analogous opt-out that g73 / PR #32 added for the preflight coverage check, but for red-tests. Sibling bead **3y6** is the canonical wording source for the bundle's prose conventions; this bead mirrors them.

## Design

Decided during brainstorm: **docs-only behavior is DERIVED from existing signals, not from a new schema field.**

### Trigger predicate (canonical, evaluation order matters)

Evaluate in this fixed order at the top of the red-tests step body:

1. **Trigger A — no test runner.** If `[gates].test == ""` in `project-config.toml`, the project has no way to run tests. Skip with note `red-tests opt-out: no test runner ([gates].test=='')`.
2. **Trigger B — no `[m]` AC bullets** (implement-feature ONLY; see asymmetry below). If the bead's description has zero `[m]`-tagged AC bullets, there is nothing for tests to assert. Skip with note `red-tests opt-out: no [m] AC bullets`.
3. **Otherwise:** proceed with existing red-tests behavior (dispatch bead-implementor, write failing tests, etc.).

### Detection mechanism for Trigger B (pinned)

Naive grep over `bd show {{bead-id}}` output will false-positive on `[m]` tokens inside notes, comments, or quoted prose. Pin detection to the **description** field with a line-anchored bullet pattern:

```bash
m_count=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json \
  | jq -r '.[0].description' \
  | grep -cE '^[[:space:]]*[-*][[:space:]]*\[m\]')
```

`m_count == 0` triggers the skip. The exact command is part of the formula step body so that future readers (and Codex review of the formula) see the precise predicate.

### Conversely: `[m]` ACs are the always-trigger override

A bead with `[m]` ACs MUST run red-tests, regardless of project signals. There is **no** `red-tests:enable` opt-IN label and **no** `red-tests:skip` opt-OUT label. AC tagging is the per-bead signal. (Trigger A still skips when there is no test runner at all — `[m]` ACs in a runner-less project are a brainstorm-time error and out of scope here.)

### Bug-class asymmetry (fix-bug differs)

fix-bug encodes a regression-test invariant: the failing regression test is "proof that the bug cannot silently return" (fix-bug.formula.toml red-tests step). Allowing Trigger B (no `[m]` ACs) to skip on the bug path would silently waive that invariant for `[h]`-only bug ACs.

Therefore in **`fix-bug.formula.toml`**:

- **Trigger A applies** (no test runner — the project genuinely cannot run tests).
- **Trigger B does NOT apply** — a bug bead with zero `[m]` ACs and a non-empty `[gates].test` is a brainstorm gap, not an opt-out condition. The formula should NOT silently skip; it should proceed with red-tests as today (and the dispatched bead-implementor will fail loudly with no `[m]` lines to cover, surfacing the gap).

This asymmetry is documented in both formula bodies and in the spec doc.

### Skip-note format (pinned for verify-ac groundedness)

When red-tests opts out under either trigger, the formula appends to the step-bead notes via:

```bash
bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "red-tests opt-out: <trigger>"
```

where `<trigger>` is one of: `no test runner ([gates].test=='')`, `no [m] AC bullets`, or `both`. The exact `red-tests opt-out: ` prefix is grep-able so that verify-ac (or any auditor) can mechanically confirm a clean opt-out fired.

### Rejected alternatives

A new `[project].docs-only` master flag; a new `[tests].applicable` per-stage flag; per-bead opt-out / opt-in labels. Inference from existing signals keeps the project-config surface minimal and aligns with sibling bead **3y6**.

## Scope

1. **`src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml`** — red-tests step:
   - Add an "Opt-out" sub-section near the top of `## Actions` containing the canonical predicate (Triggers A & B), the pinned `m_count` detection command, and the pinned skip-note format.
   - Update Definition of Done to read approximately: `"Tests committed to feature branch; both reviewers approve 'shippable' state — OR step opted out per the canonical predicate, with the pinned skip-note appended to step-bead notes."`

2. **`src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml`** — red-tests step:
   - Add an "Opt-out" sub-section. **Trigger A only.** Include an explicit note explaining why Trigger B is omitted (bug-class regression-test invariant).
   - Update DoD analogously.

3. **`docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md`** §3 red-tests stage:
   - Add a brief "Opt-out." paragraph documenting the canonical predicate, the implement-feature vs fix-bug asymmetry, and the skip-note convention.
   - DoD line conditioned accordingly.

4. **`verify-ac` confirmation**: the existing `verify-ac` step iterates `[m]` lines, so a bead with zero `[m]` ACs is a no-op iteration — no edits expected. Implementer should confirm by reading the verify-ac step body. **If confirmation fails,** file a NEW follow-up bead (does not pass the sibling test for this bead) and du9 still closes on its own merits. Do NOT expand scope inline.

## Out of Scope

- A `[project].docs-only` master flag — explicitly rejected during brainstorm.
- A `[tests].applicable` per-stage flag — explicitly rejected.
- A per-bead `red-tests:skip` opt-OUT or `red-tests:enable` opt-IN label — rejected; AC tagging is the per-bead signal.
- Wider `[gates]`-aware redesign of green-loop / quality-sweep — tracked in sibling bead **3y6**.
- Adding `[m]` tag enforcement at brainstorm-time — separate concern.
- Editing the `verify-ac` step (confirmation only; new bead if broken).

## Bundle coordination

Sibling bead **3y6** is the canonical wording source. This bead's prose conventions for describing conditional stage behavior must match 3y6's. Unlike 3y6's green-loop edits (which condition a DoD clause without skipping), du9 introduces a real step-skip with a pinned step-bead note format — that is the deliberate asymmetry between the two beads.

This bead serves as a regression case once it merges: bead **agents-config-7bk.19.2** (whose closure spawned du9) was a docs-only bead with no `[m]` tags, the original failure mode that motivated this work.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [m] `implement-feature.formula.toml` red-tests step body contains the canonical "Opt-out" sub-section with both Trigger A (`[gates].test==""`) and Trigger B (no `[m]` ACs) (grep confirms).
- [m] `implement-feature.formula.toml` red-tests step body contains the pinned `m_count` detection command (grep confirms `bd show .* --json` and `grep -cE '\^\[\[\:space\:\]\]\*\[\-\*\]\[\[\:space\:\]\]\*\\\[m\\\]'` or close-equivalent appears verbatim).
- [m] `implement-feature.formula.toml` red-tests step body specifies the pinned skip-note format starting with the literal prefix `red-tests opt-out:` (grep confirms).
- [m] `implement-feature.formula.toml` red-tests DoD line reflects the opt-out branch (grep confirms wording mentions opt-out and the pinned note).
- [m] `fix-bug.formula.toml` red-tests step body has Trigger A only AND contains an explicit note explaining Trigger B is omitted due to the regression-test invariant (grep confirms BOTH the absence of an AC-count check and the presence of the "regression-test invariant" / equivalent rationale text).
- [m] `fix-bug.formula.toml` red-tests DoD line reflects the Trigger-A-only opt-out (grep confirms).
- [m] `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §3 red-tests stage has an "Opt-out." paragraph documenting the canonical predicate, asymmetry, and skip-note convention (grep confirms `"Opt-out"` near red-tests heading and mentions of both `[gates].test` and `[m]`).
- [m] No new schema field is introduced in `project-config.toml` (diff shows no additions to `[project]` / `[tests]` / `[coverage]` sections).
- [h] A docs-only bead (no `[m]` AC tags, project `[gates].test==""`) routed through implement-feature reaches green-loop without parking at red-tests, with the step-bead note `red-tests opt-out: ...` recorded.
- [h] A code-bearing bead with `[m]` tags continues to run red-tests normally, regardless of project-level signals.
- [h] A bug bead with zero `[m]` ACs and non-empty `[gates].test` does NOT silently skip — red-tests proceeds (and surfaces the brainstorm gap loudly).
acceptance_criteria:

## PR #35 Copilot review — ESCALATED items (autonomous mode, 2026-05-05)

The following Copilot comments raise architectural questions about the green-loop downstream handoff after a red-tests opt-out, and the bug-fix regression-test invariant when Trigger A fires. They are out of scope for the bundle's stated edits and require human design judgment before being addressed:

ESCALATE: 3190979506 (@Copilot, file src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml around lines 183-184): "Red-tests can now close without writing any failing tests, but the downstream green-loop handoff is still documented around committed red-phase tests / a prior red-team commit. Without an explicit green-loop branch for this opt-out path, runner-less or docs-only beads advance with no defined artifact to implement against." — rationale: legitimate architectural gap; the bundle adds the red-tests opt-out but does not specify how green-loop behaves in the opt-out branch. Could be addressed by: (a) adding an explicit green-loop opt-out paragraph that delegates implementation against ACs directly when no committed failing tests exist, or (b) restricting Trigger B to also gate green-loop's iteration logic. Decision deferred to a follow-up bead.

ESCALATE: 3190979526 (@Copilot, file src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml around line 230): same concern as 3190979506 mirrored for the fix-bug formula. — rationale: same architectural gap on the bug path; resolution should mirror the implement-feature decision once made.

ESCALATE: 3190979583 (@Copilot, file src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml around lines 216-230): "This opt-out makes runner-less bug beads skip the regression-test stage entirely, but the downstream green-loop Definition of Done is still 'Root cause fixed with regression protection'. Advancing the pipeline here creates a branch where the formula can no longer satisfy its own bug-fix invariant." — rationale: the bundle deliberately allows Trigger A on the bug path (project genuinely cannot run tests), but the green-loop DoD ("regression protection") becomes unsatisfiable in this branch. Possible resolutions: (a) explicitly document the trade-off — "when Trigger A fires on the bug path, regression protection delegates to manual verification, accepted as a project-level constraint"; (b) restrict Trigger A from fix-bug entirely and require a per-bead override label; (c) add a flag-human in green-loop on the bug path when Trigger A fired upstream. All require human design decision.

These three concerns share a common root: the bundle adds opt-outs at red-tests but does not propagate the implications to green-loop's DoD or fix-bug's regression-test invariant. A follow-up bead should be filed once the architectural direction is decided.

The remaining six Copilot comments (3190979396, 3190979436, 3190979466, 3190979486, 3190979541, 3190979561) are FIXED in this push — see commit log for the fix commit attribution.
close_reason: Merged via PR #35 (commit 9e06b8d) as bundle sibling of agents-config-3y6. 8/8 [m] ACs validated; [h] ACs deferred to post-merge smoke tests; architectural follow-up tracked as agents-config-ooun.

title: Preflight: spec validation, formula selection, worktree creation
description:
Validate the bead spec and create the worktree before any implementation work.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: high

## Inputs
Read the bead and its labels:
  bd show {{bead-id}}
  bd label list {{bead-id}}

## Actions

1. Verify `brainstormed` and `implementation-ready` labels are present.
   If either is absent: apply human-flag protocol and exit.

2. Note: formula selection and pour have already happened — this step was
   created by implement-bead pouring implement-feature. There is nothing
   to select or pour here. Proceed directly to project-config.toml validation.

3. Read project-config.toml and verify:
   - `[gates]` section present (or stubs acceptable).
   - Coverage applicability ([coverage].applicable): if explicitly set to `false`,
     coverage does NOT apply to this project (docs-only repos, config repos, etc.).
     Skip the report-location check entirely and proceed to step 4. Record this
     by running:
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "preflight: coverage opt-out (project-config has [coverage].applicable = false) — skipped report-location check"
   - Coverage report location ([coverage].report-location): only checked when
     `[coverage].applicable` is true OR absent (default). If absent OR empty
     string (i.e. `report-location = ""`), apply the human-flag protocol and
     stop this stage:
       bd label add {{bead-id}} human
       bd label add <step-bead-id> human
       bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "preflight: no coverage report location configured in project-config.toml"
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "preflight: no coverage report location configured — molecule parked"
     An empty string is treated the same as absent: explicit opt-out requires
     `[coverage].applicable = false` — a bare empty string is ambiguous and
     must be resolved by the human.
     Do NOT continue to worktree creation. Exit cleanly.
   - [static-analysis] section: if absent or empty, mark quality-sweep skippable.

4. Stamp the molecule→bead lookup label if not already stamped:
   bd label add <mol-id> for-bead-{{bead-id}}

5. Create the worktree:
   Branch name: feat/{{bead-id}}-<slug-from-title>
   Location: <repo-root>/.claude/worktrees/<branch-name>
   Command:
     git worktree add .claude/worktrees/<branch> -b <branch>
   CRITICAL: the formula is already poured (this step was created by the pour).
   If worktree creation fails: squash the molecule and flag-human.

6. Stamp the worktree-path label on the molecule:
   Encoding (apply IN ORDER):
     _ → _u
     / → __
   Then: bd label add <mol-id> worktree-path-<encoded>

7. Claim the bead and walk the parent chain:
   bd update {{bead-id}} --status in_progress
   PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
   while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
     bd update "$PARENT" --status in_progress
     PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
   done

## Definition of Done
Poured molecule + worktree exists + worktree-path-* label stamped on molecule.
OR: human label + structured gap-note + (if pour completed) molecule squashed.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check: does an active molecule exist? Does the worktree path decode to a real
git worktree? If so, skip work already done.
notes: preflight: coverage opt-out (project-config has [coverage].applicable = false) — skipped report-location check
close_reason: Preflight complete: applicable=false honored (no human-flag); worktree created at .claude/worktrees/feat+7bk.19.2-three-worker-agents; worktree-path label stamped on mol-pc4mk; bead + parent chain in_progress

title: Verify-AC: mechanical acceptance criteria validation
description:
Validate every [m]-tagged AC bullet against worktree state.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skips when review-level:none.

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (parse [m] and [h] tags)
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule
- Functional tests from: project-config.toml [functional-tests]

## Actions

For each [m]-tagged AC line:
1. Dispatch bead-verifier (haiku, low effort) to run shell-runnable witnesses.
   Collect: exit code + error excerpt.
2. Assemble validation report.

[h]-tagged AC lines: do NOT validate here. Confirm that human follow-up
child beads were filed at brainstorm-time finalize (bd list --parent {{bead-id}}
filtered by label human). If any [h] bullet has no matching follow-up, flag-human.

Run functional/e2e tests from [functional-tests] in project-config.toml.
If functional tests fail:
  - Create one bug bead per failure, label human.
  - Note in step-bead. Continue to create-pr (do NOT loop back).

Persist validation report to BOTH:
  - Step-bead notes: bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "<report>"
  - Source bead notes: bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "<report>"
  (Report also included in PR description at create-pr.)

## Definition of Done
Every [m] AC bullet has explicit witness evidence.
Human-review bullets have follow-up beads filed (confirm presence).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates and continues from prior pass.

title: Quality-sweep: global quality gate across entire project
description:
Run the full project quality suite (not just changed files).
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skippable when preflight marked it so (no [static-analysis] in project-config.toml,
or empty gates). Also skips when review-level:none is set.

## Order (fixed)
1. Lint auto-fix: [lint-autofix].command from project-config.toml (run FIRST)
2. Build + typecheck + lint (from [gates])
3. Static analysis (from [static-analysis] — semgrep, depcheck, etc.)
4. Tests (from [gates].test)

ALWAYS end with build + tests after any code changes (guarantees verify-ac starts green).

## Auto-fix vs flag-human
Auto-fix unless issues betray a design flaw requiring fundamental changes.
Use judgment based on the size of the fix.

## Definition of Done
All gate commands exit zero (or stage skipped per preflight / review-level:none).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates state and runs only what is not already green.

No quality gates to run on a docs-only project.

title: Merge-or-handoff: auto-merge or hand off to human
description:
Either auto-merge the PR or hand off to the human.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Gate 1 — PR-state clean-check
Conditions (ALL must be true for auto-merge path):
  - Zero open Copilot review threads
  - Zero open human-author review threads
  - All CI checks green
  - 5-minute quiet window with no new comments or review activity
On wait-for-pr-comments timeout: treat clean-check as NOT clean.

## Auto-merge path (auto-mergeable label present AND clean-check clean)
1. Re-run clean-check immediately before merge call.
2. Pour merge-and-cleanup formula (phase: liquid — NOT vapor).
3. Drive merge-and-cleanup:
   - Merge the PR
   - Delete branch (local + remote)
   - Remove worktree (decode from worktree-path-* label)
   - Run parent-chain I2 close-walk
   Note: merge-and-cleanup gate-step (Gate 2) blocks until ALL [h] follow-up
   children are closed with verified-by-human label AND the merge-{source-id}
   child is closed.

## Hand-off path (default — auto-mergeable absent OR clean-check dirty)
1. Transition bead to open: bd update {{bead-id}} --status open
2. Stamp labels: bd label add {{bead-id}} merge-ready
                 bd label add {{bead-id}} human
   (merge-ready = why it's on human list; human = surfaces via bd human list)
3. Note: bead is now excluded from bd ready (human label gates it).
   Human runs /merge-and-cleanup when ready.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view --json mergedAt first. If non-null: skip merge, proceed to cleanup.
close_reason: Hand-off: auto-mergeable label absent (default path); bead transitioned to open + stamped merge-ready + human labels; awaiting explicit user authorization to invoke merge-and-cleanup

title: Review-cycle: address PR review feedback
description:
Drive the PR review loop until all review threads are resolved.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

Skips entirely when review-level:none.

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:wait-for-pr-comments (chains internally to superpowers:reply-and-resolve-pr-threads)

## Actions

Invoke superpowers:wait-for-pr-comments with:
  --mode autonomous --bead-id {{bead-id}}

The skill:
1. Polls Copilot via background script (zero Anthropic tokens during wait).
2. Classifies each comment: FIX / SKIP / ESCALATE.
3. Dispatches per-comment fix subagents (sonnet) for FIX-class items.
4. Pushes combined commits.
5. Internally chains to superpowers:reply-and-resolve-pr-threads:
   replies to every thread + resolves FIXED review_thread items via GraphQL.

One iteration = one outbound reply-batch.
Iteration cap: 5 (override: iteration-cap-review-cycle-<n> label).
Early exit when no remaining FIX-class items.

Exit conditions (mutually exclusive):
- review-exit-copilot-only (default when review-exit-human-approvers-<n> absent)
- review-exit-human-approvers-<n>: require N human approvers

Persist after each batch:
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "REVIEW-BATCH:<n>/<MAX>"

If PR is CLOSED on re-entry: flag-human. Do NOT poll a closed PR.

## Definition of Done
Every review thread has a reply. FIXED items resolved on GitHub.
No in-flight FIX work.
close_reason: Review-cycle complete: 7 inline FIX comments addressed in 3 commits (957c863 + 1e1ecb3 + 2be5f6f); all 7 threads replied + resolved; review_summary acknowledged. PR #33 has clean review state.

title: Green-loop: RALF-IT implementation until tests pass
description:
Implement the feature (TDD green phase) via RALF-IT iterative refinement.
Model: claude-opus-4-7  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
RALF-IT methodology (embedded in controller)

## Inputs
- Committed failing tests from red-tests stage
- Bead spec from: bd show {{bead-id}}
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule
- Prior RALF-IT state: RALF-IT-ITER:<n>/<MAX> in step-bead notes (default 0)

## RALF-IT loop

MAX_ITERATIONS = 5 (override: iteration-cap-green-loop-<n> label — SOLE override path)

For each iteration:

1. Dispatch bead-implementor to write minimum code to make tests pass:
   - Effort: high on iter 1, medium on iters 2+
   - Pass: worktree path, bead spec, iteration number, prior state

2. Run quality gate (build/typecheck/lint/test) — from project-config.toml [gates].
   Gate must pass before review.

3. Run foreign-eyes review:
   - Iter 1: Codex (gpt-5.5, or codex_green_loop_iter1_model from project-config.toml)
   - Iter 2: Gemini (gemini-2.5-pro, or gemini_green_loop_iter2_model)
   - Iter 3+: pure-Claude fresh-eyes subagent
   Record degradation: FOREIGN-EYES-ITER-<n>: codex=<status>, gemini=<status>

4. When review-level:deep: run /codex:adversarial-review --model gpt-5.4 after gate.
   When review-level:none: skip per-iteration code-review and simplify.

5. Persist iteration marker AFTER iteration completes:
   bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "RALF-IT-ITER:<n>/{{MAX}}"

6. Converge or loop: if reviewers approve and tests green, exit loop.
   Otherwise continue to next iteration.

After loop:
- Run simplify skill once on the full diff (not per-iteration).
- Run quality-reviewer (claude-opus-4-7[1m]) on the full bead diff.
- If foreign-eyes degraded in 2+ of N iterations: flag-human.
  Stamp: foreign-eyes-degraded:<n>/<N> on source bead.

Coverage threshold: from project-config.toml [coverage].threshold (default 80%)
or coverage-threshold-<n> label on bead.

## Definition of Done
RALF-IT reports complete; tests green; coverage meets target; build/typecheck/lint pass.

## Idempotent re-entry
Read RALF-IT-ITER: marker from step-bead notes. Resume at n+1.
Crash mid-iteration: marker at prior value, retry failed iteration.
notes: Single-pass implementation (no RALF-IT iteration needed): docs-only bead with mechanical file-creation work; no test runner to satisfy ([gates] empty); coverage opt-out via [coverage].applicable = false. Foreign-eyes review skipped — bead's AC are file-existence + content-grep invariants suitable for verify-ac (next step). Follow-up bead agents-config-du9 captures the broader formula gap.
close_reason: Implementation complete: 3 agent files created (tdd-red-team, tdd-green-team, bug-diagnoser); single commit on feat+7bk.19.2-three-worker-agents; AC invariants self-verified; verify-ac step will run mechanical AC validation

title: Red-tests: write failing tests covering all [m] AC bullets
description:
Write failing tests (TDD red phase) before any implementation code.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:test-driven-development, superpowers:writing-unit-tests,
superpowers:testing-anti-patterns

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (read [m]-tagged lines)
- Worktree path: decode worktree-path-* label from molecule

## Decode worktree-path label before any work
  1. Decode: __ → /  then  _u → _
  2. Verify decoded path exists and is a git worktree:
       git -C <path> rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree
  3. Operate from that cwd.

## Actions

1. Dispatch bead-implementor (sonnet, medium effort) to write tests:
   - Dispatch with: worktree path, AC bullets (all [m]-tagged lines),
     step-bead ID, instruction to write failing tests and confirm they fail.
   - Tests must cover: happy path, edge cases, error paths.
   - Do NOT mock what has not been verified.
   - Do NOT write test-only methods in production code.

2. Run the Claude reviewer (test-review skill) on the written tests.
   When review-level:none is set: skip test reviewers (tests still written).

3. Run Codex adversarial review of the tests (gpt-5.5 default).
   Model from: project-config.toml [foreign-cli].codex_red_tests_model
   When review-level:none: skip.
   Subject to foreign-eyes degradation tracking (FOREIGN-EYES-ITER-<n>).

4. Iteration cap: 2 (override: iteration-cap-red-tests-<n> label).
   At cap with reviewer disagreement: write disagreement note to step-bead notes.
   Escalate human ONLY if BOTH reviewers agree on a critical/major design flaw.

5. Commit failing tests to the feature branch.

## Definition of Done
Tests committed to feature branch; both reviewers approve "shippable" state
(or iteration cap reached with disagreement note recorded).

## Idempotent re-entry
Check filesystem (committed tests) and step-bead notes. Continue from prior state.

title: Examine @file inclusion markers for consistency
description:
Investigate how we're currently handling @file inclusion directives across the codebase:

Current state:
- Some use tool-specific import capabilities (Claude Code native imports, etc.)
- Some rely on agent honor system ('read @this_file' directives in AGENTS.md)
- OpenCode introduced token replacement for dynamic file references

Goal: Determine if token replacement provides a path to better consistency across Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI.

Questions to resolve:
1. Current usage patterns across each tool's AGENTS.md files
2. Which @file markers are actually resolved vs. assumed-to-be-read
3. Whether token replacement can unify the different approaches
4. Impact on agent behavior if we standardize on one method
5. Breaking changes or migration path if we adopt token replacement across the board
acceptance_criteria:
Inventory documents current inclusion behavior in Claude, Codex, Gemini, and OpenCode with concrete file/path references.
Analysis distinguishes natively resolved includes from honor-system instructions and generated flattening behavior.
At least three options are evaluated (status quo, cross-tool token/flattening, and hybrid canonical startup contract) with explicit tradeoffs and risks.
Recommendation selects either a path forward or no change, with rationale tied to consistency, maintainability, and behavior reliability.
Migration and compatibility impacts are documented, including installer/template touch points and rollback posture.
Follow-on implementation beads are identified or explicitly deferred if recommendation is no-op.
notes:
## Summary
Evaluate and document how instruction-file inclusion is handled across Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and OpenCode, then recommend whether to standardize on a single strategy (including possible no-op).

## Background / Motivation
The repo currently mixes inclusion styles: `@file` directives in some tool templates, explicit startup-read instructions in Codex, and installer-time dynamic flattening for OpenCode. This creates uncertainty about what is actually machine-enforced vs. convention-driven, and whether we can reduce behavioral drift across tools.

## Requirements
- Produce a concrete inventory of current behavior per tool using source templates and installer logic.
- Separate mechanism types clearly: native include resolution, install-time flattening/token replacement, and honor-system instructions.
- Evaluate options for future standardization, including maintaining status quo.
- Provide explicit tradeoffs: reliability, portability, maintenance overhead, and migration risk.
- Recommend a path forward (or recommend no change) with rationale.
- Define migration surface and follow-on tasks if changes are recommended.

## Out of Scope
- Implementing template or installer changes in this bead.
- Updating all tool instruction files immediately.
- Altering plugin behavior beyond what is required for analysis.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Inventory documents current inclusion behavior in Claude, Codex, Gemini, and OpenCode with concrete file/path references.
- Analysis distinguishes natively resolved includes from honor-system instructions and generated flattening behavior.
- At least three options are evaluated (status quo, cross-tool token/flattening, and hybrid canonical startup contract) with explicit tradeoffs and risks.
- Recommendation selects either a path forward or no change, with rationale tied to consistency, maintainability, and behavior reliability.
- Migration and compatibility impacts are documented, including installer/template touch points and rollback posture.
- Follow-on implementation beads are identified or explicitly deferred if recommendation is no-op.

## Design Notes
- Treat OpenCode dynamic include markers as an install-time code generation mechanism, not a runtime include feature.
- Treat Codex startup-read contract as an explicit behavioral guardrail independent of `@file` semantics.
- Judge alternatives by deterministic behavior across tools first, convenience second.

## Open Questions
- None blocking for analysis. If evidence shows tool behavior ambiguity that cannot be observed from repository artifacts alone, document assumptions explicitly in the analysis output.
Verify-AC report for agents-config-jyb (mechanical AC validation)
Witness file: docs/specs/2026-05-05-file-inclusion-consistency-analysis.md

AC1: Inventory across Claude/Codex/Gemini/OpenCode with concrete refs
- Witness: headings present for all four tools
- Command: rg -n "### Claude Code|### Codex CLI|### Gemini CLI|### OpenCode" docs/specs/2026-05-05-file-inclusion-consistency-analysis.md
- Result: PASS (4 matches)

AC2: Distinguishes native include resolution vs honor-system vs flattening
- Witness: mechanism classification section and terminology matches
- Command: rg -n "natively resolved|honor-system|install-time flattening|token replacement" docs/specs/2026-05-05-file-inclusion-consistency-analysis.md
- Result: PASS (9 matches)

AC3: At least three options with tradeoffs/risks
- Witness: Option A/B/C sections
- Command: rg -n "### Option A:|### Option B:|### Option C:" docs/specs/2026-05-05-file-inclusion-consistency-analysis.md
- Result: PASS (3 matches)

AC4: Recommendation or no-op with rationale
- Witness: Recommendation section naming adopted path with rationale
- Command: rg -n "## Recommendation|Adopt Option C|no-op" docs/specs/2026-05-05-file-inclusion-consistency-analysis.md
- Result: PASS (3 matches)

AC5: Migration + compatibility + rollback posture
- Witness: Migration Plan, compatibility discussion, rollback section
- Command: rg -n "## Migration Plan|compatibility|Rollback" docs/specs/2026-05-05-file-inclusion-consistency-analysis.md
- Result: PASS (7 matches)

AC6: Follow-on beads identified or deferred for no-op
- Witness: Follow-on Beads section includes implementation bead suggestions and no-op branch
- Command: rg -n "## Follow-on Beads|no-op" docs/specs/2026-05-05-file-inclusion-consistency-analysis.md
- Result: PASS (2 matches)

Functional tests:

title: Wire green-loop and quality-sweep to honor [coverage].applicable = false
description:
## Purpose

The g73 work added `[coverage].applicable` as an explicit project-config opt-out for the preflight coverage check. Currently only preflight (and one parenthetical mention in spec §3 green-loop's "Unverifiable/unimplementable triggers" paragraph) honors the field. Green-loop's spec-doc Coverage paragraph and Definition of Done still treat coverage as unconditional; quality-sweep makes no mention of coverage at all. This bead closes the gap with prose-only edits and designates the resulting wording as canonical.

## Design

This is a documentation/wording change — no behavioral code is added. The principle (decided during brainstorm): **docs-only behavior is DERIVED from existing signals, not from a new schema field.** Specifically:

- `[coverage].applicable = false` → coverage threshold is not enforced; it does not gate green-loop's DoD, and quality-sweep does not independently impose one.
- `[gates].test = ""` → the test gate is a no-op (existing semantics; not changed by this bead).

Rejected alternatives (for future reference): a new `[project].docs-only` master flag; per-stage `[tests].applicable` / `[coverage].applicable` flag families; per-bead opt-OUT labels. Inference from existing fields keeps the project-config surface minimal.

**Note on formula vs spec-doc surfaces.** The green-loop and quality-sweep step bodies in `implement-feature.formula.toml` and `fix-bug.formula.toml` currently make NO mention of coverage — coverage handling is delegated to `ralf-implement` (which reads project-config independently) and to the documented contract in spec §3. The formula edits in this bead are therefore **ADDITIVE clarifying notes**, not amendments to existing coverage prose. The spec doc edits ARE amendments to existing prose.

## Scope

1. **`docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §3 — canonical wording lives here** (this bead establishes it; sibling bead **du9** mirrors):
   - **green-loop "Coverage." paragraph (~line 141)**: rewrite to state that the threshold is honored only when `[coverage].applicable` is true or absent; when explicitly false, coverage is not a quality gate.
   - **green-loop DoD line (~line 151)**: change `"RALF-IT reports complete; tests green; coverage meets target; build/typecheck/lint pass"` so the coverage clause reads `"coverage meets target (when [coverage].applicable is true or absent)"` or equivalent.
   - **green-loop "Unverifiable/unimplementable triggers" paragraph (~line 143)**: this paragraph already conditions the missing-report-location trigger on `[coverage].applicable`. Confirm wording remains consistent with the rewritten Coverage paragraph; no edit if already harmonious.
   - **quality-sweep section (~line 167+)**: add a brief sentence stating that quality-sweep does not separately enforce a coverage threshold; coverage gating, if any, lives in green-loop's DoD and is conditional on `[coverage].applicable`.

2. **`src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml`** — additive clarifying notes (no existing coverage prose to amend):
   - **green-loop step body**: ADD a brief "Coverage" sub-section pointing to spec §3.green-loop and noting that the threshold is bypassed when `[coverage].applicable = false`.
   - **quality-sweep step body**: ADD a brief sentence noting that this stage does not separately enforce coverage (delegates to green-loop's DoD).

3. **`src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml`** — additive clarifying notes:
   - Same additive edits applied symmetrically to green-loop and quality-sweep step bodies. Wording matches `implement-feature.formula.toml` verbatim.

4. **`project-config.toml`**:
   - Remove the now-stale "Note:" block (lines 39-41) that says green-loop and quality-sweep don't yet honor the field.
   - Verify the surrounding line comments (especially line 43's `threshold = 80 # ... ignored when applicable = false ...` and line 44's `report-location = ""` comment) remain accurate after the Note block is removed; adjust if needed.

5. **Bundle coordination**: this bead is the canonical wording source for the bundle. Sibling bead **du9** mirrors the prose convention. Unlike du9's red-tests opt-out (which fully skips the step with a step-bead note), green-loop here does NOT skip — only a DoD clause is conditioned. **No step-bead opt-out note is required for green-loop.**

## Out of Scope

- Per-bead overrides — `coverage-threshold-<n>` label semantics unchanged.
- Adding a `[project].docs-only` master flag — explicitly rejected during brainstorm.
- The `red-tests` opt-out — tracked in sibling bead **du9**.
- Any new behavioral code, new check sub-actions, or new schema fields.
- An end-to-end pipeline integration test on a real docs-only repo — see "Deferred verification" below.

## Deferred verification

The behavioral claim that a docs-only project can complete the full `implement-feature` pipeline without coverage-related blocks is `[h]`-tagged: it requires a real end-to-end molecule run, not a worktree-local check. It is captured below as an `[h]` AC and verified post-merge by human smoke test (run any small bead through the pipeline on this repo). If a follow-up regression bead is desired, file it post-merge.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [m] `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §3 green-loop "Coverage." paragraph conditions the threshold on `[coverage].applicable` (grep confirms a phrase like `"when [coverage].applicable is true or absent"` or equivalent in the paragraph).
- [m] `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §3 green-loop DoD line conditions the coverage clause on `[coverage].applicable` (grep confirms the conditional phrase appears on the DoD line).
- [m] `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §3 quality-sweep section contains a sentence stating the stage does not separately enforce a coverage threshold (grep confirms a phrase like `"does not separately enforce"` and `"coverage"` near each other in the quality-sweep section).
- [m] `implement-feature.formula.toml` green-loop step body contains an additive "Coverage" reference mentioning `[coverage].applicable` (grep confirms within the green-loop step block).
- [m] `implement-feature.formula.toml` quality-sweep step body contains the additive sentence about not separately enforcing coverage (grep confirms within the quality-sweep step block).
- [m] `fix-bug.formula.toml` green-loop step body matches the implement-feature wording for the additive Coverage reference (diff between the two formula bodies' green-loop coverage prose shows identical text).
- [m] `fix-bug.formula.toml` quality-sweep step body matches the implement-feature wording (diff shows identical additive sentence).
- [m] `project-config.toml` no longer contains the "green-loop and quality-sweep do NOT yet honor" caveat (grep confirms removal).
- [m] No new schema field is introduced in `project-config.toml` (diff shows no additions to `[project]` / `[tests]` / `[coverage]` sections beyond comment edits).
- [h] Post-merge: a docs-only project (this repo) can complete a full implement-feature pipeline run without coverage-related blocks. Verified by human smoke test, not in worktree.

Note: red-tests will opt out per du9's own predicate (no [m] code-level ACs in this docs-only repo) — that is correct behavior, self-dogfooding du9's design.
## Verify-AC report — bundle 3y6+du9 — commit cb5b11c

Validation run: 2026-05-05 in worktree feat/agents-config-3y6-coverage-opt-out-bundle.

### Bead agents-config-3y6 [m] ACs (9/9 PASS)

## PR #35 Copilot review — ESCALATED items (autonomous mode, 2026-05-05)

The following Copilot comments raise architectural questions about the green-loop downstream handoff after a red-tests opt-out, and the bug-fix regression-test invariant when Trigger A fires. They are out of scope for the bundle's stated edits and require human design judgment before being addressed:

ESCALATE: 3190979506 (@Copilot, file src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml around lines 183-184): "Red-tests can now close without writing any failing tests, but the downstream green-loop handoff is still documented around committed red-phase tests / a prior red-team commit. Without an explicit green-loop branch for this opt-out path, runner-less or docs-only beads advance with no defined artifact to implement against." — rationale: legitimate architectural gap; the bundle adds the red-tests opt-out but does not specify how green-loop behaves in the opt-out branch. Could be addressed by: (a) adding an explicit green-loop opt-out paragraph that delegates implementation against ACs directly when no committed failing tests exist, or (b) restricting Trigger B to also gate green-loop's iteration logic. Decision deferred to a follow-up bead.

ESCALATE: 3190979526 (@Copilot, file src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml around line 230): same concern as 3190979506 mirrored for the fix-bug formula. — rationale: same architectural gap on the bug path; resolution should mirror the implement-feature decision once made.

ESCALATE: 3190979583 (@Copilot, file src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml around lines 216-230): "This opt-out makes runner-less bug beads skip the regression-test stage entirely, but the downstream green-loop Definition of Done is still 'Root cause fixed with regression protection'. Advancing the pipeline here creates a branch where the formula can no longer satisfy its own bug-fix invariant." — rationale: the bundle deliberately allows Trigger A on the bug path (project genuinely cannot run tests), but the green-loop DoD ("regression protection") becomes unsatisfiable in this branch. Possible resolutions: (a) explicitly document the trade-off — "when Trigger A fires on the bug path, regression protection delegates to manual verification, accepted as a project-level constraint"; (b) restrict Trigger A from fix-bug entirely and require a per-bead override label; (c) add a flag-human in green-loop on the bug path when Trigger A fired upstream. All require human design decision.

These three concerns share a common root: the bundle adds opt-outs at red-tests but does not propagate the implications to green-loop's DoD or fix-bug's regression-test invariant. A follow-up bead should be filed once the architectural direction is decided.

The remaining six Copilot comments (3190979396, 3190979436, 3190979466, 3190979486, 3190979541, 3190979561) are FIXED in this push — see commit log for the fix commit attribution.

Let the orchestrator provide the model sequencing strategy for ralf iterations, rather than hardcoding it. This enables different platforms to optimize their model selection based on their capabilities, quota status, and cost profiles.

Experiment with either using opencode or wiring codex with a custom model provider as the secondary option. This allows ralf iterations to continue when primary orchestrators exhaust their rate limits.

title: Document bd comments vs notes distinction
description:
Document that 'bd comments add' is non-destructive (creates separate comments) and does not replace issue notes. For cumulative step output, use 'bd update --append-notes'; do NOT use '--notes' which replaces existing notes.

Also audit all skills, agents, rules, and other instructional files for redundant or conflicting documentation of this pattern. Deduplicate and establish a single source of truth in the beads-related agent instructions.
acceptance_criteria:
beads.md has new ## Notes vs Comments: bd update semantics section with 3-command table and footgun callout
implement-feature.formula.toml lines 279-281 prose replaced with one-line footgun reminder + canonical reference
fix-bug.formula.toml lines 329-331 prose replaced with one-line footgun reminder + canonical reference
wait-for-pr-comments/SKILL.md line 548 parenthetical shortened to cite canonical section
brainstorm-bead.formula.toml, create-bead/SKILL.md, and docs/specs/ files NOT modified
notes:
## Summary
Add a single canonical reference for bd's three note/comment commands to src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md, then trim duplicate rule prose in two formula files and one skill to one-line footgun reminders with citations.

## Background
bd update --notes, bd update --append-notes, and bd comments add serve distinct roles but are frequently confused. Today the distinction is explained inline in implement-feature.formula.toml, fix-bug.formula.toml, and wait-for-pr-comments/SKILL.md — three independent copies that can drift. No canonical reference exists.

Audit scope: all skills, agents, rules, and instructions were checked for duplicate rule prose (teaching the distinction). Only these 3 files contain it; others merely use the commands correctly without restating the rule and are left unchanged.

## Canonical Section to Add

Insert at the END of src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md:

---
## Notes vs Comments: bd update semantics

Three commands serve distinct roles — mixing them up causes lost context.

| Command | Semantics | When to use |
|---|---|---|
| bd update <id> --append-notes ... | Appends to existing notes | Step output, escalation context, cumulative status, run breadcrumbs |
| bd update <id> --notes ... | REPLACES existing notes entirely | Initial bead creation, or brainstorm-bead spec writes — intentional overwrites only |
| bd comments add <id> ... | Creates a separate, non-destructive comment | Lifecycle audit breadcrumbs, molecule-to-bead tracing, events that should not pollute notes |

Footgun alert: --notes is a destructive overwrite. If you mean 'add to this', use --append-notes.
---

## Files to Trim (A3 Treatment)

implement-feature.formula.toml — find this 3-line block:
  'bd comments add is non-destructive: it creates a separate comment and does
  not replace issue notes. For cumulative step output, use bd update
  --append-notes; do not use --notes, which replaces existing notes.'
Replace with:
  'bd comments add is non-destructive; do NOT use --notes (replaces notes) — use --append-notes for step output. See beads rules §Notes vs Comments.'

fix-bug.formula.toml — same text pattern, same replacement.

wait-for-pr-comments/SKILL.md — find the parenthetical:
  '(--append-notes appends; --notes would REPLACE existing notes)'
Replace with:
  '(see beads rules §Notes vs Comments)'

## Design Decisions
- Canonical home: src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md (installs to ~/.claude/rules/beads.md for all beads-aware sessions)
- Formula treatment: A3 — one-line footgun reminder + citation; formulas must remain self-contained enough to warn runtime subagents in fresh sessions where rules files may not be loaded
- Insertion point: append to end of beads.md (after existing Skill Partnership section)

## Out of Scope
- brainstorm-bead.formula.toml: uses --notes intentionally for spec replacement (correct); do NOT modify
- create-bead/SKILL.md: uses --notes at initial creation (correct); do NOT modify
- docs/specs/ files: historical design records; do NOT modify

## Acceptance Criteria
1. beads.md ends with new '## Notes vs Comments: bd update semantics' section containing the 3-command table and footgun callout
2. implement-feature.formula.toml: the 3-line bd comments add prose block replaced with the one-liner
3. fix-bug.formula.toml: same 3-line block replaced with same one-liner
4. wait-for-pr-comments/SKILL.md: the '--append-notes appends; --notes would REPLACE' parenthetical replaced with '(see beads rules §Notes vs Comments)'
5. brainstorm-bead.formula.toml, create-bead/SKILL.md, and docs/specs/ unchanged
close_reason: Canonical bd notes/comments section added to beads.md; duplicate prose trimmed from implement-feature, fix-bug formulas and wait-for-pr-comments skill. Merged in PR #42.

title: Create-PR: push branch and open pull request
description:
Create the pull request and persist its URL to the molecule.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch

## Actions

1. Invoke superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch.

2. PR body MUST include:
   - Bead reference: {{bead-id}} and bead title
   - AC (full list with [m]/[h] tags)
   - RALF-IT iteration summary (from green-loop step-bead notes)
   - verify-ac validation report
   - Bug beads filed during verify-ac (if any)
   - Human-required AC follow-up bead references (if any [h] lines)
   - Foreign-eyes status section (list which iterations had degraded foreign-eyes)

3. Stamp pr-url-* label on molecule:
   Encoding: same bijection as worktree-path (though URLs rarely need it).
   bd label add <mol-id> pr-url-<encoded-url>

## PR creation failure
Flag-human with worktree intact. Do NOT tear down the worktree.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view first. If a PR exists for the branch and is OPEN: skip creation,
persist URL idempotently.
If PR exists and is CLOSED: flag-human. Do NOT auto-reopen or create a new PR.

title: Preflight: spec validation, formula selection, worktree creation
description:
Validate the bead spec and create the worktree before any implementation work.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: high

## Inputs
Read the bead and its labels:
  bd show {{bead-id}}
  bd label list {{bead-id}}

## Actions

1. Verify `brainstormed` and `implementation-ready` labels are present.
   If either is absent: apply human-flag protocol and exit.

2. Note: formula selection and pour have already happened — this step was
   created by implement-bead pouring implement-feature. There is nothing
   to select or pour here. Proceed directly to project-config.toml validation.

3. Read project-config.toml and verify:
   - `[gates]` section present (or stubs acceptable).
   - Coverage report location ([coverage].report-location): if absent OR empty
     string (i.e. `report-location = ""`), apply the human-flag protocol and
     stop this stage:
       bd label add {{bead-id}} human
       bd label add <step-bead-id> human
       bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "preflight: no coverage report location configured in project-config.toml"
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "preflight: no coverage report location configured — molecule parked"
     An empty string is treated the same as absent: explicit opt-out requires a
     comment in project-config.toml — a bare empty string is ambiguous and must
     be resolved by the human.
     Do NOT continue to worktree creation. Exit cleanly.
   - [static-analysis] section: if absent or empty, mark quality-sweep skippable.

4. Stamp the molecule→bead lookup label if not already stamped:
   bd label add <mol-id> for-bead-{{bead-id}}

5. Create the worktree:
   Branch name: feat/{{bead-id}}-<slug-from-title>
   Location: <repo-root>/.claude/worktrees/<branch-name>
   Command:
     git worktree add .claude/worktrees/<branch> -b <branch>
   CRITICAL: the formula is already poured (this step was created by the pour).
   If worktree creation fails: squash the molecule and flag-human.

6. Stamp the worktree-path label on the molecule:
   Encoding (apply IN ORDER):
     _ → _u
     / → __
   Then: bd label add <mol-id> worktree-path-<encoded>

7. Claim the bead and walk the parent chain:
   bd update {{bead-id}} --status in_progress
   PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
   while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
     bd update "$PARENT" --status in_progress
     PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
   done

## Definition of Done
Poured molecule + worktree exists + worktree-path-* label stamped on molecule.
OR: human label + structured gap-note + (if pour completed) molecule squashed.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check: does an active molecule exist? Does the worktree path decode to a real
git worktree? If so, skip work already done.
notes: preflight: no coverage report location configured — molecule parked

title: Green-loop: RALF-IT implementation until tests pass
description:
Implement the feature (TDD green phase) via RALF-IT iterative refinement.
Model: claude-opus-4-7  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
RALF-IT methodology (embedded in controller)

## Inputs
- Committed failing tests from red-tests stage
- Bead spec from: bd show {{bead-id}}
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule
- Prior RALF-IT state: RALF-IT-ITER:<n>/<MAX> in step-bead notes (default 0)

## RALF-IT loop

MAX_ITERATIONS = 5 (override: iteration-cap-green-loop-<n> label — SOLE override path)

For each iteration:

1. Dispatch bead-implementor to write minimum code to make tests pass:
   - Effort: high on iter 1, medium on iters 2+
   - Pass: worktree path, bead spec, iteration number, prior state

2. Run quality gate (build/typecheck/lint/test) — from project-config.toml [gates].
   Gate must pass before review.

3. Run foreign-eyes review:
   - Iter 1: Codex (gpt-5.5, or codex_green_loop_iter1_model from project-config.toml)
   - Iter 2: Gemini (gemini-2.5-pro, or gemini_green_loop_iter2_model)
   - Iter 3+: pure-Claude fresh-eyes subagent
   Record degradation: FOREIGN-EYES-ITER-<n>: codex=<status>, gemini=<status>

4. When review-level:deep: run /codex:adversarial-review --model gpt-5.4 after gate.
   When review-level:none: skip per-iteration code-review and simplify.

5. Persist iteration marker AFTER iteration completes:
   bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "RALF-IT-ITER:<n>/{{MAX}}"

6. Converge or loop: if reviewers approve and tests green, exit loop.
   Otherwise continue to next iteration.

After loop:
- Run simplify skill once on the full diff (not per-iteration).
- Run quality-reviewer (claude-opus-4-7[1m]) on the full bead diff.
- If foreign-eyes degraded in 2+ of N iterations: flag-human.
  Stamp: foreign-eyes-degraded:<n>/<N> on source bead.

Coverage threshold: from project-config.toml [coverage].threshold (default 80%)
or coverage-threshold-<n> label on bead.

## Definition of Done
RALF-IT reports complete; tests green; coverage meets target; build/typecheck/lint pass.

## Idempotent re-entry
Read RALF-IT-ITER: marker from step-bead notes. Resume at n+1.
Crash mid-iteration: marker at prior value, retry failed iteration.

title: Merge-or-handoff: auto-merge or hand off to human
description:
Either auto-merge the PR or hand off to the human.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Gate 1 — PR-state clean-check
Conditions (ALL must be true for auto-merge path):
  - Zero open Copilot review threads
  - Zero open human-author review threads
  - All CI checks green
  - 5-minute quiet window with no new comments or review activity
On wait-for-pr-comments timeout: treat clean-check as NOT clean.

## Auto-merge path (auto-mergeable label present AND clean-check clean)
1. Re-run clean-check immediately before merge call.
2. Pour merge-and-cleanup formula (phase: liquid — NOT vapor).
3. Drive merge-and-cleanup:
   - Merge the PR
   - Delete branch (local + remote)
   - Remove worktree (decode from worktree-path-* label)
   - Run parent-chain I2 close-walk
   Note: merge-and-cleanup gate-step (Gate 2) blocks until ALL [h] follow-up
   children are closed with verified-by-human label AND the merge-{source-id}
   child is closed.

## Hand-off path (default — auto-mergeable absent OR clean-check dirty)
1. Transition bead to open: bd update {{bead-id}} --status open
2. Stamp labels: bd label add {{bead-id}} merge-ready
                 bd label add {{bead-id}} human
   (merge-ready = why it's on human list; human = surfaces via bd human list)
3. Note: bead is now excluded from bd ready (human label gates it).
   Human runs /merge-and-cleanup when ready.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view --json mergedAt first. If non-null: skip merge, proceed to cleanup.

title: Review-cycle: address PR review feedback
description:
Drive the PR review loop until all review threads are resolved.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

Skips entirely when review-level:none.

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:wait-for-pr-comments (chains internally to superpowers:reply-and-resolve-pr-threads)

## Actions

Invoke superpowers:wait-for-pr-comments with:
  --mode autonomous --bead-id {{bead-id}}

The skill:
1. Polls Copilot via background script (zero Anthropic tokens during wait).
2. Classifies each comment: FIX / SKIP / ESCALATE.
3. Dispatches per-comment fix subagents (sonnet) for FIX-class items.
4. Pushes combined commits.
5. Internally chains to superpowers:reply-and-resolve-pr-threads:
   replies to every thread + resolves FIXED review_thread items via GraphQL.

One iteration = one outbound reply-batch.
Iteration cap: 5 (override: iteration-cap-review-cycle-<n> label).
Early exit when no remaining FIX-class items.

Exit conditions (mutually exclusive):
- review-exit-copilot-only (default when review-exit-human-approvers-<n> absent)
- review-exit-human-approvers-<n>: require N human approvers

Persist after each batch:
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "REVIEW-BATCH:<n>/<MAX>"

If PR is CLOSED on re-entry: flag-human. Do NOT poll a closed PR.

## Definition of Done
Every review thread has a reply. FIXED items resolved on GitHub.
No in-flight FIX work.

title: implement-feature
description: Per-stage feature pipeline: preflight → red-tests → green-loop → quality-sweep → verify-ac → create-pr → review-cycle → merge-or-handoff
close_reason: Superseded by agents-config-mol-pc4mk after PR #32 (g73) shipped [coverage].applicable opt-out; the parked preflight gap is resolved in the new molecule

title: Quality-sweep: global quality gate across entire project
description:
Run the full project quality suite (not just changed files).
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skippable when preflight marked it so (no [static-analysis] in project-config.toml,
or empty gates). Also skips when review-level:none is set.

## Order (fixed)
1. Lint auto-fix: [lint-autofix].command from project-config.toml (run FIRST)
2. Build + typecheck + lint (from [gates])
3. Static analysis (from [static-analysis] — semgrep, depcheck, etc.)
4. Tests (from [gates].test)

ALWAYS end with build + tests after any code changes (guarantees verify-ac starts green).

## Auto-fix vs flag-human
Auto-fix unless issues betray a design flaw requiring fundamental changes.
Use judgment based on the size of the fix.

## Definition of Done
All gate commands exit zero (or stage skipped per preflight / review-level:none).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates state and runs only what is not already green.

title: Verify-AC: mechanical acceptance criteria validation
description:
Validate every [m]-tagged AC bullet against worktree state.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skips when review-level:none.

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (parse [m] and [h] tags)
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule
- Functional tests from: project-config.toml [functional-tests]

## Actions

For each [m]-tagged AC line:
1. Dispatch bead-verifier (haiku, low effort) to run shell-runnable witnesses.
   Collect: exit code + error excerpt.
2. Assemble validation report.

[h]-tagged AC lines: do NOT validate here. Confirm that human follow-up
child beads were filed at brainstorm-time finalize (bd list --parent {{bead-id}}
filtered by label human). If any [h] bullet has no matching follow-up, flag-human.

Run functional/e2e tests from [functional-tests] in project-config.toml.
If functional tests fail:
  - Create one bug bead per failure, label human.
  - Note in step-bead. Continue to create-pr (do NOT loop back).

Persist validation report to BOTH:
  - Step-bead notes: bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "<report>"
  - Source bead notes: bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "<report>"
  (Report also included in PR description at create-pr.)

## Definition of Done
Every [m] AC bullet has explicit witness evidence.
Human-review bullets have follow-up beads filed (confirm presence).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates and continues from prior pass.

title: Red-tests: write failing tests covering all [m] AC bullets
description:
Write failing tests (TDD red phase) before any implementation code.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:test-driven-development, superpowers:writing-unit-tests,
superpowers:testing-anti-patterns

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (read [m]-tagged lines)
- Worktree path: decode worktree-path-* label from molecule

## Decode worktree-path label before any work
  1. Decode: __ → /  then  _u → _
  2. Verify decoded path exists and is a git worktree:
       git -C <path> rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree
  3. Operate from that cwd.

## Actions

1. Dispatch bead-implementor (sonnet, medium effort) to write tests:
   - Dispatch with: worktree path, AC bullets (all [m]-tagged lines),
     step-bead ID, instruction to write failing tests and confirm they fail.
   - Tests must cover: happy path, edge cases, error paths.
   - Do NOT mock what has not been verified.
   - Do NOT write test-only methods in production code.

2. Run the Claude reviewer (test-review skill) on the written tests.
   When review-level:none is set: skip test reviewers (tests still written).

3. Run Codex adversarial review of the tests (gpt-5.5 default).
   Model from: project-config.toml [foreign-cli].codex_red_tests_model
   When review-level:none: skip.
   Subject to foreign-eyes degradation tracking (FOREIGN-EYES-ITER-<n>).

4. Iteration cap: 2 (override: iteration-cap-red-tests-<n> label).
   At cap with reviewer disagreement: write disagreement note to step-bead notes.
   Escalate human ONLY if BOTH reviewers agree on a critical/major design flaw.

5. Commit failing tests to the feature branch.

## Definition of Done
Tests committed to feature branch; both reviewers approve "shippable" state
(or iteration cap reached with disagreement note recorded).

## Idempotent re-entry
Check filesystem (committed tests) and step-bead notes. Continue from prior state.

title: implement-feature
description: TDD feature implementation: confirm → worktree → red/green/refactor → completion gate → PR → close

title: Create pull request
description:
Invoke the finishing-a-development-branch skill.

Skill: superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch

- Push the branch to origin
- Create a PR with a clear summary linked to {{bead-id}}
- PR title should be ≤70 characters
- PR body should include: what changed, why, and how to test it

Do NOT merge. Do NOT squash. Await review in the next step.

title: Implement Split ralf-it into ralf-review + ralf-implement; push outer-workflow out of both (green phase)
description:
Write the minimum code to make all failing tests pass.

Skills: superpowers:test-driven-development, then any applicable domain skill

Rules:
- Minimum viable implementation — write only what tests require
- No speculative features, no over-engineering
- No abstractions for hypothetical future requirements

Run tests after each meaningful change:
  go test ./...   (or equivalent)

This step is complete only when all tests are GREEN.
