title: Refactor for clarity (refactor phase)
description:
Clean up the implementation without changing observable behavior.
Tests must remain green throughout.

Skill: code-simplifier:code-simplifier (run as subagent)

Look for:
- Duplication (three similar lines is a smell; two is acceptable)
- Names that require a comment to understand
- Logic that could be expressed more simply
- Dead code

Re-run tests after every change. If tests break, you changed behavior.
Undo the change and try again.

This step is NOT about adding features or improving performance.
It is about making the code readable by the next person (who is you,
in six months, having forgotten everything).
close_reason: Tightened Operating Discipline preamble to remove duplication with Phase 2's parallel-vs-serial guidance; dropped redundant parenthetical and added cross-reference

title: Create pull request
description:
Invoke the finishing-a-development-branch skill.

Skill: superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch

- Push the branch to origin
- Create a PR with a clear summary linked to {{bead-id}}
- PR title should be ≤70 characters
- PR body should include: what changed, why, and how to test it

Do NOT merge. Do NOT squash. Await review in the next step.

title: Housekeeping: close, remember, file discovered work
description:
Complete the lifecycle and preserve context for future sessions.

1. Close the bead:
     bd close {{bead-id}} --reason "Implemented and PR merged"

2. Walk the parent chain; close each ancestor epic whose remaining
   children are all closed (stops at the first ancestor that still
   has any non-closed children, or when there is no parent):

     PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
     while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
       NON_CLOSED=$(bd list --parent="$PARENT" --json | jq '[.[] | select(.status != "closed")] | length')
       [ "$NON_CLOSED" = "0" ] || break
       bd close "$PARENT" --reason "All children closed"
       PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
     done

3. Record any non-obvious decisions or hard-won insights:
     bd remember "insight about this system or pattern"
     bd decision record --title="Why we chose X over Y"

4. File any work discovered during implementation. Prefer epic-sibling
   placement over orphan-with-dep:

     PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')

     # Decide placement via the sibling test (see rules/beads.md I3):
     # would this work have been on the parent epic's original plan, if
     # we'd thought of it? Yes → sibling. No → orphan + discovered-from.
     IS_SIBLING_SUBTASK=false  # set to true only when the answer is yes

     if [ -n "$PARENT" ] && [ "$IS_SIBLING_SUBTASK" = true ]; then
       # Create INSIDE the parent epic as a sibling:
       bd create "Found: ..." -t "<type>" -p "<prio>" --parent "$PARENT"
     else
       # Otherwise create as an orphan and link with discovered-from:
       NEW=$(bd create "Found: ..." -t "<type>" -p "<prio>" --json | jq -r '.id')
       bd dep add "$NEW" {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from
     fi

   Sibling test (see rules/beads.md I3): would this discovered work
   have been on the parent epic's original plan, if we'd thought of
   it? Yes → sibling (--parent). No → orphan + discovered-from.

5. Burn this wisp (if running as vapor):
     bd mol burn <this-mol-id>
close_reason: Bead b7s closed (no parent); two memories persisted (per-skill-agents-md-is-dead-file; b7s-simplify-drift-policy); no discovered work to file

title: Set up isolated git worktree
description:
Invoke the using-git-worktrees skill to create an isolated branch.

Skill: superpowers:using-git-worktrees

- Branch from main (never from another feature branch)
- Verify you are IN the worktree directory before writing any code
- Confirm no leftover state from other work

Then claim the bead and walk the parent chain:

  bd update {{bead-id}} --status in_progress

  # Walk parent chain; mark each ancestor epic in_progress
  PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
  while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
    bd update "$PARENT" --status in_progress
    PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
  done

If `implement-bead` already claimed the bead before pouring this
formula, the first `bd update` is a no-op — re-running it is safe.
close_reason: Worktree at .claude/worktrees/feat-b7s-simplify-skill on branch feat/b7s-simplify-skill; extracted SKILL.md relocated into worktree

title: Write failing tests (red phase)
description:
Write tests FIRST. No implementation code at this step.

Skills: superpowers:test-driven-development, superpowers:writing-unit-tests, superpowers:testing-anti-patterns

Rules:
- Tests must be written against the acceptance criteria on the bead (set by brainstorm-bead, restated in the confirm step)
- Cover: happy path, edge cases, error paths
- Do NOT mock what you have not verified you understand
- Do NOT write test-only methods in production code

Run the tests and confirm they FAIL. Failure is correct here.

  go test ./...           # Go projects
  pnpm test               # Node/TypeScript projects
  pytest                  # Python projects

If a test passes before any implementation, either the test is wrong
or the feature already exists. Investigate before continuing.
close_reason: Docs-only repo — no test harness (no package.json/go.mod/requirements.txt). ACs #1-#7 are mechanically inspectable content checks; AC #9 (Build/Typecheck/Tests) honored vacuously per brainstorm spec. Verification handled by 'verify with evidence' step via grep/file-existence checks.

title: Completion gate: simplify
description:
Dispatch code-simplifier subagent against changed code.

Agent: code-simplifier:code-simplifier

This step exists because the implementing agent always misses
at least one DRY violation or clarity issue. It is not optional.

Address ALL findings. Re-run tests after any changes made here.
close_reason: None warranted — file was clean after refactor. Reuse/Quality/Efficiency reviews ran serially (3-way parallel dispatch on a 130-line markdown file is overkill). All AC-pinned content (frontmatter, HTML comment, capability-shaped Phase 2 wording, explicit category enumerations) verified intact via required greps.

title: Implement Create simplify skill (port from /simplify command body) (green phase)
description:
Write the minimum code to make all failing tests pass.

Skills: superpowers:test-driven-development, then domain skill
  (e.g. typescript-developer, backend-developer, etc.)

Rules:
- Minimum viable implementation — write only what tests require
- No speculative features, no over-engineering
- No abstractions for hypothetical future requirements

Run tests after each meaningful change:
  go test ./...   (or equivalent)

This step is complete only when all tests are GREEN.

Phase 2 reviewer checklist preserved verbatim. Frontmatter name=simplify locked. Committed as e9d03ff.

description: TDD feature implementation: confirm → worktree → red/green/refactor → completion gate → PR → close

title: Completion gate: verify with evidence
description:
Run the full verification suite. Evidence is required — no claims without output.

Skill: superpowers:verification-before-completion

Required gates — show the actual command output for each:
  [ ] Tests pass        → run test suite, paste output
  [ ] Build succeeds    → run build, paste output
  [ ] Lint clean        → run linter, paste output
  [ ] Type check clean  → run type checker, paste output

Do NOT mark this step complete unless all four gates show clean output.
"I believe it passes" is not evidence. Run the commands.

HARD STOP: After this gate, do NOT commit to main and do NOT push
directly. The next step is create-pr. Go there next.

title: Delete code-simplifier agent; rewire completion-gate to invoke /simplify directly
notes:
## Update (agents-config-7bk.8 brainstorm session, 2026-05-02)

**Architecture flip — this bead's scope has narrowed.**

Original Path C ("Have completion-gate step 3 invoke /simplify directly via the orchestrator") is OBSOLETE. New plan:
- agents-config-b7s creates a portable `simplify` skill (ported from the /simplify command body)
- agents-config-7bk.8 rewrites `src/user/.claude/rules/completion-gate.md` step 3 (and ~10 other dispatch sites across the codebase) to invoke the new `simplify` skill — uniform invocation, no orchestrator-tier-only guard
- This bead (7bk.7) now ONLY deletes the broken agent file at `src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md`

**New dependency**: agents-config-7bk.7 now depends on agents-config-7bk.8 (the sweep must complete before the agent file deletion, otherwise stale dispatches pointing at a non-existent agent would proliferate).

**Original notes (preserved for context, but now partially obsolete):**

---

## Discovery (verbatim from session 5385d9fc)

1. /simplify is a slash command, NOT a skill. It lives outside this repo (installed via a plugin or sidekick injection — not present in src/). When invoked, its body is loaded into the session as a prompt.
2. The /simplify command is itself a parallel-subagent dispatcher. Its Phase 2 explicitly says: "Use the Agent tool to launch all three agents concurrently in a single message" — three reviewers (reuse, quality, efficiency), then aggregate, then apply fixes.
3. The code-simplifier agent at src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md is BROKEN. It instructs itself to "invoke the bundled /simplify skill … via the Skill tool with skill name simplify" — but no such skill exists. It points at a phantom dependency.
4. Subagents CANNOT invoke slash commands (those are user/orchestrator-initiated), so a simple "fix the reference" patch will NOT work.
5. completion-gate.md step 3 currently calls the code-simplifier agent.

## Decision (Path C) — SUPERSEDED, see Update above

Delete the code-simplifier agent layer entirely. Have completion-gate step 3 invoke /simplify directly via the orchestrator (the orchestrator CAN run slash commands).

## What must be preserved (currently encoded in the agent — needs a new home)

- Worktree scoping (operate in the supplied worktree path, not the main tree)
- Behavior-preserving discipline (apply-vs-flag classification, "no abstractions without ≥ 2 call sites")
- Project memory persistence (.claude/agent-memory/code-simplifier/ recurring-pattern capture)
- Boundary rules (no scope creep, no new dependencies, no test rewrites, no commits/pushes)

→ NEW HOME: agents-config-b7s ports these into the `simplify` skill body.

## Files involved (revised)

- src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md — DELETE (this bead)
- src/user/.claude/rules/completion-gate.md — handled by 7bk.8, not this bead
- /simplify command source — handled by b7s
- Any other rules/skills referencing code-simplifier agent — handled by 7bk.8 sweep

## Suggested execution steps (revised)

1. Verify 7bk.8 sweep is complete (`bd show 7bk.8` status=closed).
2. Confirm `grep -rn "code-simplifier" src/` returns empty (the sweep should have made it so).
3. Delete src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md.
4. Run completion gate on the change.

## Relationship to agents-config-7bk.2

Phase 2 (7bk.2) of the agent fleet epic is in flight. Original coordination still applies — 7bk.2's formula updates land independently.
close_reason: Superseded by agents-config-7bk.8 (Plan C bundled the broken-agent file deletion into the sweep PR #24)

title: Phase 2: bead-implementor agent + formula rewiring
description:
Phase 2 of agents-config-7bk. Wires the new specialized agent fleet into the bead pipeline by introducing the bead-implementor agent, augmenting implement-bead with worktree lifetime ownership, and rewiring the implement-feature and fix-bug formulas to dispatch the right agents for review/implement/verify and to invoke /simplify directly (orchestrator-side) for the simplify gate.

## Summary

Add a single named "implementor" agent (bead-implementor) that subagents are dispatched as for write-tests, implement, and diagnose steps. Move worktree creation/teardown from per-step responsibility into the implement-bead orchestrator, persisting the worktree path on the molecule via a label so every dispatched subagent receives it. Update both implementation formulas to use current agent names (post Phase 1 + post *-developer cleanup), and convert the simplify step from subagent dispatch to orchestrator-direct /simplify invocation.

## Background

Originally specced 2026-04-26 alongside the dynamic-dove plan. The world has shifted since:
- *-developer agents were deleted (commit 92fdfda; bead 1ck closed); the dynamic-dove "preload domain skills by subject area" justification is gone.
- code-debugger.md was deleted in the same commit; superpowers:systematic-debugging + root-cause-tracing replace it.
- code-reviewer was merged into quality-reviewer (commit 827ef3e).
- bead-verifier was delivered in Phase 1 (7bk.1) but is not yet wired into any formula.
- Formulas still reference stale agent paths (superpowers:code-reviewer, code-simplifier:code-simplifier).
- DISCOVERY (session 5385d9fc): /simplify is a slash COMMAND (not a skill), and the code-simplifier agent is broken (points at a phantom skill). Subagents cannot invoke slash commands, so the simplify step must be orchestrator-direct, not subagent-dispatched. The actual deletion of the code-simplifier agent is tracked separately in agents-config-7bk.7 (peer cleanup bead, non-blocking).

This bead consolidates the surviving Phase 2 work: add bead-implementor as the implementing/debugging muscle, wire bead-verifier into the verify gate, fix stale agent references, centralize worktree lifecycle in the orchestrator, and convert the simplify step to orchestrator-direct invocation.

## Requirements

1. **CREATE `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md`** — minimal "implementor" agent.
   - Frontmatter: name=bead-implementor, model=sonnet, color=blue, tools=Read+Edit+Write+Grep+Glob+Bash, memory=project.
   - skills: [superpowers:test-driven-development, writing-unit-tests, testing-anti-patterns, superpowers:using-git-worktrees, superpowers:verification-before-completion, superpowers:systematic-debugging, superpowers:root-cause-tracing]
   - Body: cd into the worktree path passed by the orchestrator (via prompt — derived from molecule label, see #2), follow preloaded TDD methodology for write-tests/implement steps, follow preloaded debugging skills for diagnose steps. Never declare done without verification evidence. NO domain-skill preloading machinery — just the fixed skills list above.

2. **AUGMENT `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md`** — orchestrator owns the worktree for the bead's full lifetime.
   - On the FIRST dispatched step that needs a worktree (typically write-tests for features, diagnose for bugs), the orchestrator invokes superpowers:using-git-worktrees, captures the path, and stamps a label on the molecule of the form `worktree-path-<sanitized>` where slashes in the path are replaced by `__` (decode on read).
   - On every subsequent subagent dispatch, the orchestrator parses the label off the molecule and injects the decoded worktree path into the subagent's prompt.
   - Worktree teardown happens in the merge-and-cleanup formula (out of scope for this bead beyond noting the contract — merge-and-cleanup already exists).
   - Document the label format and parse/encode helpers in the SKILL.md so future agents can interpret the convention.

3. **UPDATE `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml`:**
   - Replace `Agent: superpowers:code-reviewer` → `Agent: quality-reviewer` (review step).
   - Convert simplify step from `Agent: code-simplifier:code-simplifier` to ORCHESTRATOR-DIRECT: the step body instructs the orchestrator to invoke the `/simplify` slash command directly (no Agent: line), since /simplify itself dispatches three parallel subagents internally. Document this in the step text so future readers understand why simplify is structured differently from review/verify.
   - Wire `Agent: bead-implementor` into write-tests and implement steps (replacing whatever generic dispatch is currently there).
   - Wire `Agent: bead-verifier` into the verify step.

4. **UPDATE `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml`:**
   - Same review and simplify changes as #3 (review = quality-reviewer; simplify = orchestrator-direct /simplify).
   - Wire `Agent: bead-implementor` into the diagnose, write-tests, and implement steps (bead-implementor's preloaded systematic-debugging + root-cause-tracing skills cover diagnose).
   - Wire `Agent: bead-verifier` into the verify step.

5. **DROP from original spec:** no longer modify code-debugger.md (file deleted in 92fdfda; debugging skills now live as preloaded skills on bead-implementor per #1).

6. **End-to-end smoke test:** push a trivial bead through run-queue and confirm the new dispatches fire correctly — bead-implementor runs in the persisted worktree, bead-verifier reports exit codes, quality-reviewer runs in the review step, and the simplify step's orchestrator-direct /simplify invocation completes (or is skipped cleanly if 7bk.7 hasn't landed yet — confirm graceful behavior either way). Worktree label stamps on the molecule and parses correctly across step boundaries.

## Out of Scope

- Domain-skill preloading on bead-implementor (1ck closed; *-developer skills not built).
- Deletion of src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md and rewriting of completion-gate.md step 3 — tracked in agents-config-7bk.7.
- Editing the /simplify command body (also 7bk.7 territory if that bead chooses to add a Phase 0 discipline section).
- Teardown logic in merge-and-cleanup beyond confirming the existing flow can read the label-stored path.
- Changes to brainstorm-bead.formula.toml (no agent dispatch in brainstorming).
- Updating tech-lead.md or any other surviving agent (Phase 1 territory or already done).
- Replacing the existing `for-bead-<bead-id>` molecule label convention (orthogonal; worktree-path label is additive).

## Design Notes

- **Why a single bead-implementor instead of multiple specialized "implementor" variants:** the original dynamic-dove rationale was domain-skill preloading. With *-developer skills not built, there's no useful axis on which to specialize. One named role keeps formula entries traceable and gives a clear seam if domain preloading is ever revived.
- **Why label storage for worktree path:** labels are lifecycle-neutral, queryable via existing bd CLI, and don't entangle the molecule's notes/description (which formulas may overwrite). Sanitization is a known cost; the alternative (mol notes) gets clobbered by step logic that uses --notes.
- **Why bead-implementor handles diagnose:** option (a) from the brainstorm Q3 — preloading systematic-debugging + root-cause-tracing on a single agent is cheaper than building a parallel bead-debugger and gives the formula one consistent dispatch target across feature/bug pipelines.
- **Why simplify is orchestrator-direct, not subagent-dispatched:** /simplify is a slash command (not a skill) and itself dispatches three parallel reviewer subagents internally. Subagents cannot invoke slash commands, so wrapping /simplify in an "Agent: code-simplifier" dispatch is structurally impossible. The orchestrator (the agent driving the molecule) IS the only layer that can run /simplify, so the formula step body instructs it directly.
- **Worktree label encoding:** `worktree-path-<path-with-slashes-replaced-by-double-underscore>`. Decode by reversing the replacement. Example: `/Users/scott/foo/bar` → `worktree-path-__Users__scott__foo__bar`. Skills/agents that read this MUST parse from the well-known prefix.
- **Relationship with 7bk.7:** 7bk.7 (delete code-simplifier agent + rewrite completion-gate step 3) is a peer cleanup, not a blocker. 7bk.2's formula updates avoid referencing the doomed agent regardless of when 7bk.7 lands. They are independent.

## Acceptance Criteria

- src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md exists with frontmatter and body matching requirement #1.
- src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md documents and implements the worktree-label lifecycle (create-on-first-need, stamp, parse-and-inject on subsequent dispatches).
- implement-feature.formula.toml and fix-bug.formula.toml reference quality-reviewer (not superpowers:code-reviewer), do NOT reference code-simplifier:code-simplifier (simplify step is orchestrator-direct /simplify), reference bead-implementor for write-tests/implement(/diagnose), and reference bead-verifier for verify.
- A trivial test bead processed through run-queue completes successfully with the new dispatches firing in the expected steps.
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Dependencies

- ✓ agents-config-7bk.1 (Phase 1 — bead-verifier, quality-reviewer delivered)
- ✓ agents-config-1ck (closed: *-developer agents removed; dynamic-dove preload superseded)
- (peer, non-blocking) agents-config-7bk.7 — cleanup of the code-simplifier agent layer
acceptance_criteria: src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md exists with required skills and tools. implement-bead SKILL.md documents and implements worktree-label lifecycle. implement-feature.formula.toml and fix-bug.formula.toml reference quality-reviewer (not superpowers:code-reviewer), do NOT reference code-simplifier subagent (simplify is orchestrator-direct /simplify), reference bead-implementor for write-tests/implement(/diagnose), and reference bead-verifier for verify. Trivial test bead processed through run-queue completes with new dispatches firing. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: Specialized agent fleet (M3 worker fleet)
description:
Rescoped 2026-05-10 during Phase 5b beadification — was 'Specialized agent fleet for the bead pipeline' covering all per-stage subagent + cost-rightsizing + brainstorm-bead-rewiring + production-driver work; now M3-fleet-only after children spanning M1 hygiene, M2 brainstorm-readiness, M4 production-driver, post-MVP, and research-spike were redistributed.

Scope: redesign and ship the worker fleet that runs the bead pipeline end-to-end from feature-bead implementation through PR creation, including per-stage right-sized worker dispatch (worker-report-v1 schema, tdd-red-team / tdd-green-team / bug-diagnoser / bead-implementor-replacement) and cost-rightsizing controls (per-step Model:/Effort: directives in formula TOMLs; F-04 default table). Outcome: each *-developer agent becomes a skill (SKILL.md with appropriate frontmatter) that can be referenced in the 'skills:' frontmatter list of bead-implementor or invoked by the orchestrator at dispatch time. Blocks Phase 2 of dynamic-dove rollout.

title: Fix broken YAML frontmatter in 12 src/user agent files
description: All 12 agent files in src/user/.agents/agents/ fail YAML frontmatter parsing. Multi-line 'description:' with embedded <example> XML and 'Examples:' sibling keys cause 'while scanning a simple key' errors when Claude Code loads agents from ~/.claude/agents/. Fix: convert description to block scalar (| or |-) and indent body so Examples live inside the description string, preserving project convention that examples belong in frontmatter. Affects: api-developer, backend-developer, code-debugger, code-documenter, code-refactor, code-reviewer, data-scientist, database-designer, frontend-developer, javascript-developer, tech-lead, typescript-developer.
acceptance_criteria: All 12 agent files parse as valid YAML. Build passes. Typecheck passes (n/a, doc repo). Tests pass (n/a). Installer copies files unchanged in shape.
close_reason: Merged via PR #17 (squash commit b7cf3f1). All 12 agent files now parse as valid YAML.

title: Externalize long bead specs to docs/beads/ files; bead description stays a short summary referencing the doc
description: Specs live in bead fields (description, notes, design, acceptance_criteria) — the bead IS the plan. If a write balks at field size, externalize to docs/beads/<short-id>-<<slug>.md with a relative-path reference. Commit: b866455
notes:
Context: while brainstorming lu3.4, the final spec grew to ~300 lines. Writing the full spec into the bead notes field via 'bd update --notes' requires shell-escape gymnastics and replaces the field wholesale (see bug guard in lu3.4 spec R6). Manually archived lu3.4 spec to docs/specs/2026-04-19-ralf-it-form-reevaluation-design.md to preserve it.

Proposal: adopt a pattern where long bead specs live at docs/beads/<bead-id>-<slug>.md (or docs/specs/<date>-<slug>.md), and the bead's description/notes is a short summary + reference to the doc file path. The bead remains self-descriptive; the full spec is versioned in git alongside code.

Benefits:
- shell-safe authoring (edit the file directly, no CLI string escaping)
- PR-reviewable spec changes via normal diff review
- grep-friendly
- no duplication between bd's notes field and docs/specs
- bead description stays scannable in 'bd show <id>'

Open questions (for start-bead/brainstorm-bead to resolve, not now):
- Threshold for 'long enough' to externalize (line count? number of sections?)
- Should brainstorm-bead.formula.toml's write-spec step be updated to write long specs to file + summary to bd notes?
- Should create-bead / start-bead skills know about this pattern?
- Directory choice: reuse docs/specs/ vs new docs/beads/ subdir?
- How does the bead reference the doc — relative path? URL to repo? Commit-pinned?
- Do we need a 'seal' mechanism to prevent bd notes from drifting out of sync with the external doc?
close_reason: Done — convention documented in src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md. Commit b866455.

title: wait-for-pr-comments: poll-copilot-review.sh returns stale reviews on re-review cycles
description:
# Problem

After pushing fixes and re-requesting Copilot review, poll-copilot-review.sh (in the wait-for-pr-comments skill) short-circuits on attempt 1 and reports copilot_review_found — but returns the STALE prior review rather than the new one. The JSON it emits has a commit_id field pointing at the OLD commit, which the skill's Phase 3 triage then operates on.

# Impact

Auto-fix loop spins on the same comments it already addressed. Discovered on agents-config-uvt PR #12 during two re-review cycles; had to fall back to manual gh api polling with a submitted_at > last-commit-timestamp filter.

# Root cause

The script searches /pulls/N/reviews for ANY review authored by Copilot. It doesn't compare commit_id against the latest pushed commit, nor submitted_at against the re-request time.

# Fix direction

poll-copilot-review.sh should accept an optional --since-commit or --since-timestamp argument, and the Phase 4 re-review flow in wait-for-pr-comments/SKILL.md should pass the latest commit's timestamp so only newer reviews match.

# Discovered-from

agents-config-uvt

comments.author: Scott Hamilton
comments.text:
Second repro on scotthamilton77/claude-code-sidekick PR #183 (2026-05-02):

- 11:05Z: Copilot review #1 with 4 inline findings.
- After fixes pushed, copilot_work_started fired at 14:08:33Z.
- poll-copilot-review.sh --skip-request-check entered sub-phase C, immediately found review #1 (predates work_started), reported 'Copilot review found (attempt 1)' and exited 0.
- Skill emitted Variant 1 'All fixed, no Copilot re-review' final report.
- Actual second review landed at 14:14:38Z (6 minutes later) with 6 NEW inline findings — caught manually, not by the script.

Adds two UX dimensions worth fixing alongside the timestamp filter:

1. Risk multiplier: a false 'All clear' Variant 1 report is the exact moment a user authorizes merge ('merge it' / 'ship it'). The skill needs to express uncertainty when a re-review is in flight, not declare clear.

2. Pre-trigger hint: even before copilot_work_started fires, the existence of commits with author.date > last_review.submitted_at is a strong signal a re-review may be queued. The report should surface that timing relationship explicitly so users can wait if needed.

Originally filed as scotthamilton77/claude-code-sidekick:sidekick-aga3 (now deleted as duplicate of this bead).

title: Housekeeping: close, remember, file discovered work
description:
Complete the lifecycle and preserve context for future sessions.

1. Close the bead:
     bd close {{bead-id}} --reason "Implemented and PR merged"

2. If all siblings in a parent epic are now closed, close the parent:
     bd show {{bead-id}}    # check for parent
     bd list --parent=<parent-id>  # check siblings

3. Record any non-obvious decisions or hard-won insights:
     bd remember "insight about this system or pattern"
     bd decision record --title="Why we chose X over Y"

4. File any work discovered during implementation:
     bd create "Found: ..." -t bug -p 1
     bd dep add <new-id> {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from

5. Burn this wisp (if running as vapor):
     bd mol burn <this-mol-id>
close_reason: Memories saved: poll-script stale-review behavior, Copilot add-reviewer idempotency, session-label mechanism design. Discovered work filed: 58m (poll-script bug P2), zt1 (Copilot re-request bug P3), 2vr (Report-block voice chore P4), all linked discovered-from uvt. Bead close + parent-epic lu3 close deferred to merge-and-cleanup formula (pending user merge authorization on PR #12).

title: Completion gate: verify with evidence
description:
Run the full verification suite. Evidence is required — no claims without output.

Skill: superpowers:verification-before-completion

Required gates — show the actual command output for each:
  [ ] Tests pass        → run test suite, paste output
  [ ] Build succeeds    → run build, paste output
  [ ] Lint clean        → run linter, paste output
  [ ] Type check clean  → run type checker, paste output

Do NOT mark this step complete unless all four gates show clean output.
"I believe it passes" is not evidence. Run the commands.

HARD STOP: After this gate, do NOT commit to main and do NOT push
directly. The next step is create-pr. Go there next.
close_reason: Verification evidence (docs-only repo — build/test/lint/typecheck vacuous): (1) TOML parses cleanly, 5 steps present. (2) All 6 spec'd edits present at expected locations across 5 files. (3) Route A authorization gate confirmed (start-bead SKILL.md:58-60). (4) Acceptance criteria echo and 'bd mol burn <this-mol-id>' step 4 preserved in formula (lines 188, 203). (5) 3 commits, 5 files, +47/-4; no scope creep outside src/plugins/beads/.

title: Write failing tests (red phase)
description:
Write tests FIRST. No implementation code at this step.

Skills: superpowers:test-driven-development, superpowers:writing-unit-tests, superpowers:testing-anti-patterns

Rules:
- Tests must be written against the acceptance criteria from the brainstorm step
- Cover: happy path, edge cases, error paths
- Do NOT mock what you have not verified you understand
- Do NOT write test-only methods in production code

Run the tests and confirm they FAIL. Failure is correct here.

  go test ./...           # Go projects
  pnpm test               # Node/TypeScript projects
  pytest                  # Python projects

If a test passes before any implementation, either the test is wrong
or the feature already exists. Investigate before continuing.
close_reason: N/A: repo is pure documentation (per AGENTS.md: 'No build system, tests, or linting'). TDD red phase has no test harness to target. Verification for this bead is structural: target files contain the specified text, Red Flag rows appended, labels flow correctly. Structural verification happens in the verify-with-evidence step.

title: Brainstorm: requirements for Divergence 4: brainstorm → implementation hand-off
description:
Invoke the brainstorming skill BEFORE any design, tests, or code.

Skill: superpowers:brainstorming

Questions to resolve:
- What problem does this actually solve?
- Who uses it and how?
- What does "done" look like? (acceptance criteria)
- What are the edge cases and error paths?
- Any architectural implications or constraints?
- Is there a simpler approach that solves the same problem?

Output: written acceptance criteria and a clear implementation plan.

Write criteria back to the bead so it persists across sessions:
  bd update {{bead-id}} --acceptance="<one line per criterion>"

If anything is ambiguous or contradictory, stop and ask the user
before proceeding to the next step.
close_reason: Brainstorming already completed via brainstorm-bead formula (wisp-7jj, now burned). Bead has --acceptance set, brainstormed + implementation-ready labels applied, spec authored in description. Nothing to add.

title: Implement Divergence 4: brainstorm → implementation hand-off (green phase)
description:

Skills: superpowers:test-driven-development, then domain skill
  (e.g. typescript-developer, backend-developer, etc.)

Rules:
- Minimum viable implementation — write only what tests require
- No speculative features, no over-engineering
- No abstractions for hypothetical future requirements

Run tests after each meaningful change:
  go test ./...   (or equivalent)

This step is complete only when all tests are GREEN.

title: Completion gate: code review
description:
Dispatch code-reviewer subagent against ALL changed files.

Agent: superpowers:code-reviewer
Scope: every file changed since branch creation, reviewed against:
  - The implementation plan from the brainstorm step
  - SOLID and DRY principles
  - Security by design (OWASP top 10, input validation, no injection paths)
  - Architectural consistency with the rest of the codebase
  - The standards in INSTRUCTIONS.md

Address ALL findings before proceeding. If a finding is deferred,
document the rationale explicitly.

For high-stakes changes (security-sensitive, architecture shifts):
  /codex:adversarial-review --wait --model gpt-5.4

title: Refactor for clarity (refactor phase)
description:
Clean up the implementation without changing observable behavior.
Tests must remain green throughout.

Skill: code-simplifier:code-simplifier (run as subagent)

Look for:
- Duplication (three similar lines is a smell; two is acceptable)
- Names that require a comment to understand
- Logic that could be expressed more simply
- Dead code

Re-run tests after every change. If tests break, you changed behavior.
Undo the change and try again.

This step is NOT about adding features or improving performance.
It is about making the code readable by the next person (who is you,
in six months, having forgotten everything).

src/user/.agents/README.md exists and explains shared content purpose, install target, and audience.
src/user/.claude/README.md exists and explains Claude-specific content; the 4-line src/user/.claude/AGENTS.md stub is deleted in the same PR that adds the README.
src/user/.codex/README.md exists and explains Codex-specific content.
src/user/.gemini/README.md exists and explains Gemini-specific content.
No edited or added doc contains a reference to a file path, section heading, or skill that does not exist in the repo after the change.
notes:
# Documentation pass for public repo readiness

## Summary
Prepare the agents-config repo for public consumption. Scope: polish existing top-level docs, correct known drift, and fill gaps a first-time visitor expects (LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING, per-subfolder READMEs). No GitHub-specific scaffolding, no prose-level rewrites.

## Background / motivation
Repo is going public. Current state: root README.md is detailed but has drift from the rules/ reorganization; root AGENTS.md has duplicated session-completion blocks; tool-specific subfolders under src/user/ have no or near-empty READMEs; no LICENSE file exists despite README declaring MIT; no CONTRIBUTING.md. A stranger landing on the repo needs accurate install guidance and a clear picture of what each folder contains.

Primary reader: fork-and-install users, not contributors. Light contributor notes only.

## Requirements
- Add a root LICENSE file matching the MIT license already declared in README.md. Copyright holder: "Scott Hamilton". Year: 2026.
- Add a short CONTRIBUTING.md acknowledging the repo is a personal config and asking contributors to open an issue first.
- Add a README.md to each tool-specific subfolder: src/user/.agents/, src/user/.claude/, src/user/.codex/, src/user/.gemini/. Each explains purpose, contents, install target, and intended audience.
- Correct drift in existing root README.md and root AGENTS.md: any stale references to file paths, section names, or structure that have since moved (notably content relocated from CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md to rules/).
- Verify README.md Skills table and Agents section against the actual contents of src/user/.agents/skills/ and src/user/.agents/agents/.
- Delete the 4-line stub at src/user/.claude/AGENTS.md in the same PR that adds src/user/.claude/README.md (README added first, stub removed after).

## Out of scope
- .github/ scaffolding (issue templates, PR templates, workflows)
- CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
- Restructuring or rewriting the root README for style
- Changes to docs/plans/ or docs/specs/ content
- Changes to src/plugins/AGENTS.md (already in good shape) — but root README tree SHOULD mention src/plugins/
- Any non-doc changes (no code edits, no install.sh changes)
- Any edits inside the root AGENTS.md BEADS INTEGRATION v:1 marker block — auto-generated by the beads plugin, regenerated on bd runs. Only the non-generated Session Completion and Beads Quick Reference blocks that appear outside or alongside the markers should be deduplicated.

## Design notes

### Drift detection (README.md / AGENTS.md)

Concrete checks — after edits, these greps should return nothing (or only current-truth results):

- No instruction to "copy CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md.template" without also mentioning rules/.
- No claim that `<orchestration>`, `<delegation>`, `<completion-gate>`, `<delivery>`, or `<beads>` sections live in CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md. They live in INSTRUCTIONS.md (orchestration) and rules/ (the rest).
- No reference to a skill name that doesn't correspond to a directory in src/user/.agents/skills/.
- No reference to an agent name that doesn't correspond to a file in src/user/.agents/agents/.

### Skills/Agents verification

Bidirectional by directory name:
- Every directory in src/user/.agents/skills/ has a row in the README Skills table.
- Every row in the README Skills table corresponds to a real directory.
- Agents section either lists the shipping agent names or points to the directory; do NOT leave it as vague prose.

### Beads integration markers

Root AGENTS.md contains `<!-- BEGIN BEADS INTEGRATION v:1 ... -->` and `<!-- END BEADS INTEGRATION -->` markers around a plugin-managed block. This block is regenerated by the beads plugin on install and must not be edited by hand. Any deduplication work on AGENTS.md must target content OUTSIDE these markers.

### Subfolder README style

- Short (roughly 20-50 lines each). Short enough that a first-time visitor can scan.
- Link up to the root README for install flow; do not duplicate install instructions.
- Answer three questions per folder: what lives here, how does it get installed, who is it for.

### LICENSE

Canonical MIT license text. Format:
```
MIT License

Copyright (c) 2026 Scott Hamilton

Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, ...
```

### CONTRIBUTING.md

Example draft:
> This is currently a personal configuration repository. If you find it useful and want to contribute agents, skills, or commands, please open an issue to discuss before sending a PR. See AGENTS.md for file format conventions (agent frontmatter, skill SKILL.md layout, command markdown).

### Delivery

- Single branch, single PR.
- Docs-only. No code changes, no install.sh changes, no settings changes.
- Drift fixes are surgical — edit only stale claims, not prose style.

## Open questions
- None blocking. Minor copy-edit decisions during implementation are fine to decide inline.
close_reason: Merged via PR #7 (squash commit d194c06). All ACs satisfied except AC9 which was deferred and tracked as agents-config-5e3 (plugin-managed duplication requires upstream fix).

title: implement-bead §4 step 5 stamps worker-audit-* unconditionally; conflicts with audit-label scope policy
description:
## Symptom

implement-bead/SKILL.md §4 step 5 reads: 'Stamp `worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]` on the step-bead (forensic, append-only).' — no conditional on agent name.

The 'Audit-label scope' section (later in same file) declares the policy: '`worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]` labels apply ONLY to dispatches of `tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, and `bug-diagnoser` (worker-only). Review, fresh-eyes, and adversarial subagents dispatched by orchestration skills are out-of-band and are explicitly excluded from the `worker-audit-` namespace.'

These contradict. §4's per-dispatch primitive will stamp `worker-audit-general-purpose-iter<N>` when ralf-implement uses §4 to dispatch general-purpose, leaking into the audit namespace.

## Discovered during

agents-config-7bk.19.7 cycle-3 adversarial review (F5). The 19.7 spec works around this by having ralf-implement own its own audit-label stamping (R1.5 conditional), but the upstream §4 contradiction remains for any future caller of §4 with a non-named-worker doer.

## Proposed fix (upstream)

§4 step 5 should condition on agent name: `if agent_name in {tdd-red-team, tdd-green-team, bug-diagnoser}: stamp worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]; else: skip step 5`. The conditional belongs in the primitive, not in policy prose 50 lines below.

## Out of 19.7 scope

This is an implement-bead §4 issue, not a ralf-* issue. 19.7 documents the workaround; this bead tracks the upstream fix.
acceptance_criteria: implement-bead/SKILL.md §4 step 5 explicitly conditions worker-audit stamping on agent name (tdd-red-team | tdd-green-team | bug-diagnoser). Step 5 prose names the conditional; not delegated to tail policy section.
notes: Severity: MAJOR (latent contract leak — currently masked because no caller dispatches general-purpose via §4 yet). Will become live once 19.7 ships ralf-implement → §4 → general-purpose flow.

title: implement-bead/SKILL.md §6.3 references bug-diagnoser-report-v1 reproduction_steps but schema has no such field
description:
Surface: implement-bead/SKILL.md line 123 says 'extract root_cause_note (and reproduction_steps for red-tests)' from the bug-diagnoser worker-report. Reality: bug-diagnoser-report-v1.md schema (docs/specs/bug-diagnoser-report-v1.md) defines ONLY root_cause_note as the additive required field. There is no structured reproduction_steps field in the report schema.

The §5 stage-agent map at implement-bead/SKILL.md line 112 also says: '(red-tests, fix-bug) → tdd-red-team (single-regression mode; root_cause_note and reproduction_steps from upstream diagnose report)'. Same mismatch.

Triggered the question while reviewing the spec for agents-config-7bk.19.7. Surfaced by cycle-2 fresh-eyes review.

Options for resolution:
(a) Add reproduction_steps as an optional output field to bug-diagnoser-report-v1; coordinate with bug-diagnoser agent to emit it.
(b) Drop reproduction_steps from implement-bead §6.3 / §5 (the agent receives repro steps as INPUT from bead context, not produces them; downstream agents already get the bead context).
(c) Keep §6.3 wording but document the field as 'optional, may be absent' and fall back to bead context when absent.

Recommend (b) as cleanest: tdd-red-team in single-regression mode already has access to the original bead's failure evidence via bead context; no need to round-trip through the worker-report. Updates required in implement-bead/SKILL.md §5 and §6.3.

title: bd-record-decision.sh: jq error 'Cannot index object with number' after creating decision bead
description:
When invoked with --bead-id <id> --title ... --notes ... --implemented, the helper script creates the decision bead but then exits with: `jq: error (at <stdin>:12): Cannot index object with number` (exit code 5). The script does NOT set the description, does NOT add the discovered-from dep edge, and does NOT close the bead. Manual recovery: bd update --description, bd dep add --type discovered-from, bd close --reason.

Repro on 2026-05-12 during agents-config-7bk.19.7 write-spec step. Both --implemented decisions encountered the same failure; second one was filed manually without the helper. Likely cause: bd CLI JSON output shape changed; the script's jq path indexes into an object that is now a different shape (probably .[0] vs root array). Location: ~/.beads/scripts/bd-record-decision.sh line ~12 of its jq invocation.

title: Refactor: extract hep_escalate shell helper to dedupe HEP block across 5 skill files
description:
The HEP escalation procedure (`bd create` → `bd label add human` → `bd update --append-notes` → `bd dep add` → `bd update --status open`) is duplicated across 5 files with semantic drift detected:

Locations:
- src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md HEP section (canonical)
- src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md §0
- src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md (Step 2 length-0)
- src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md (Step 2 length-2+)
- src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/run-queue/SKILL.md (collapsed inline)

Drift observed:
- beads.md description="<context: what was being done, what is blocked, what is needed>"
- implement-bead description="<context: what the stage was doing, what is blocked, what is needed>"
- start-bead omits `bd update --status open` (harmless in pre-claim context but textually drifted)

Proposed: shell function `hep_escalate <source-id> <step-bead-id|N/A> <mol-id|N/A> <worktree|N/A> <scenario-hint> <title> <description>` declared once (likely in beads.md or implement-bead/lib), referenced by every skill. Eliminates ~50 lines of duplicated bash + drift hazard.

Surfaced by simplify Axis-1 review during agents-config-7bk.13 implementation.

title: Extract worker base contract into shared section of worker-report-v1.md
description: All five worker agents (tdd-red-team, tdd-green-team, bug-diagnoser, docs-edits-team, pr-comment-fixer-team) repeat near-verbatim prose for: (a) Write-tool rationale, (b) base constraints (no tracker, 40-char SHAs, no placement directives, no destructive git), (c) Operating Contract inputs boilerplate. Extracting to a 'Worker base contract' section in worker-report-v1.md and having each agent file reference it would shrink each agent by ~20 lines and eliminate the drift risk. Identified by simplify pass on acmh.13.

title: Convention: red-phase test scripts should compose absence-probe tokens at runtime
description: The red-phase test script verify-acmh13-artifacts.sh embedded literal probe tokens it was simultaneously asserting must be absent from scripts/ (X.2 cross-cutting check). This created an unsatisfiable constraint: the test file itself caused the assertion to fail. Green-phase fix used string concatenation (LEGACY_AGENT='bead-im''plementor') to avoid the literal. Convention to document: red-phase tests asserting the absence of a probe token should compose that token at runtime via concatenation or variable fragmentation, not embed it as a literal string in the test body.

title: Fix jq expression in bd-record-decision.sh (script breaks on bd create --json object output)
description: Two helper scripts in ~/.beads/scripts/ use the jq expression '(.[0].id // .id) // empty' to parse 'bd create --json' output. As of the current bd CLI, 'bd create --json' returns a single OBJECT (not an array), so '.[0]' raises 'Cannot index object with number' and the // fallback never fires. Affected: (1) bd-record-decision.sh and (2) bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh. Both scripts exit 5 due to the jq parse error even though bd create succeeds (bead IS created). Reproducers hit during agents-config-acmh.1 brainstorm session (2026-05-12). Suggested fix: replace '(.[0].id // .id) // empty' with 'if type == "array" then .[0].id else .id end // empty' in both scripts.
notes: Suggested fix: replace '(.[0].id // .id) // empty' with 'if type == "array" then .[0].id else .id end // empty' to handle both shapes. Workaround used in acmh.1: manual bd create + bd dep add + bd close instead of the script.

title: Update AGENTS.md 'Current state' — remove stale persona-vs-orchestration bullet
description: AGENTS.md 'Current state' section still lists '**Persona vs orchestration** guidance on decide-vs-escalate is not yet reconciled' as a gap. That gap was closed by PR #63 (abn9.5). Remove or rewrite the bullet to reflect current state.

title: Phase 5b PR #62 round-4 doc-quality fixes (cap-deferred)
description:
Five doc-quality findings from PR #62 Copilot round-4 review, deferred under the wait-for-pr-comments strict re-review cap. None are blockers — the PR's mechanical assimilation work is correct; these are wording, parity, and small correctness improvements for the planning artifacts.

Findings:
1. (Comment 3215072969, roadmap.md L58) M1 DoD bullet for agents-config-f298 lists --append-acceptance / --append-description but f298's title also includes --append-design. Update bullet for parity.
2. (Comment 3215072983, plan.md L9) Tech-stack note cites memory 'bd-show-json-returns-array' as the reason Python is needed for newline-bearing fields; the actual concern is literal newlines in notes/description/acceptance_criteria fields breaking jq (per src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md). Replace memory ref with that concrete rule citation.
3. (Comment 3215072989, plan.md L261) Task 3 Step 7 wires deps with bd dep add <dependent> <blocker> (M2 depends on M1, etc.), but the echo uses an arrow direction implying flow ('M1 → M2 → M3 → M4 → Post-MVP'). Reword to 'M2 depends on M1; M3 depends on M2; ...'. Also rename verification snippet's 'deps_in' to 'deps_out' (it prints outgoing deps).
4. (Comment 3215072997, plan.md L940) Audit invariant Python script builds milestone_ids from milestones.get(...) which yields None if a key is missing in /tmp/phase5b-id-map.txt; bead with parent=None then incorrectly counts as 'under a milestone'. Filter None or fail-fast on missing required IDs.
5. (Comment 3215073011, plan.md L52) Wording: 'should be slightly higher if il69 spawned more closures' should be 'spawned more beads' (new follow-ups, not closures).

Reference: PR #62 Copilot review round 4 (timestamp 2026-05-10T15:25:30Z). Strict cap was applied per round-cap protocol after rounds 1-3 burned through the natural cycle.
acceptance_criteria: All 5 findings addressed in a follow-up PR. Each thread resolved on PR #62 (or the new PR cross-references back).
close_reason: Superseded — Scott overrode the cap rule on PR #62 round 4 (2026-05-10). All 5 round-4 findings to be fixed inline on the active PR rather than deferred to follow-up.

title: Research spikes (retire-or-rescope)
description: Bucket for research-spike beads whose outcome is retire (won't-do) OR rescope (produce concrete adoption beads). Runs opportunistically — no milestone dep. Includes 7bk.24 (karpathy-guidelines), l0v0 (mattpocock skills), zetc (Codex 5.5 confidence-loop), cx6 (fork superpowers), 717 (residual lu3 risk-closer review), feedback-loop spike (new), acmh.2 (skillize tech-lead), acmh.5 (formulas-as-skills review). Reference: docs/plans/2026-05-09-roadmap.md Section 6.

title: Tier 2 (split): Skillize tech-lead (evaluate vs superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents)
description: Tier 2 follow-up from agents-config-il69 audit, split out per user gate-NOTE on REMEDIATION_PLAN.md. Question: should tech-lead be a skill rather than an agent? If yes, evaluate (a) whether it duplicates superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents and should be removed, or (b) whether the two should be used together (different scopes). Outcome: either a skillization bead with the converted skill, OR a doc note explaining when to invoke each. Touches user/.agents/agents/tech-lead.md and src/user/.agents/skills/.

title: Pipeline autonomous work to overlap external-system wait times (CI, Copilot review, GitHub merge gates)
description:
**Problem**: Autonomous overnight runs spend significant wall-clock time blocked on third-party pipelines — CI, Copilot review polling, GitHub thread reply/resolve cycles. `run-queue` advances sequentially per the docs, so while one bead waits on external systems, no other bead progresses. The result is silent wall-clock loss against the 'background overnight cranking' goal.

**Goal**: Allow the queue to advance multiple beads concurrently within safety constraints (worktree isolation, dependency invariants, repo-level locks).

**Approach sketch (high-level only — design TBD)**:
- Identify natural blocking points in the bead pipeline (PR review wait, CI wait, merge wait)
- When a bead reaches a blocking point, suspend its molecule and pop the next ready bead
- Resume suspended molecules on external-event triggers (PR comment, CI green, etc.)
- Respect worktree-per-bead isolation; serialize only operations that touch shared state (main branch, shared DBs)

**Out of scope (this bead)**: distributed multi-machine execution; new infra; rewriting run-queue from scratch.
acceptance_criteria:
- Design document in docs/plans/ describing the pipelining model and the concurrency-safety contract
- Identification of all blocking points and the events that unblock them
- Proof-of-concept that two beads can advance through their molecules with overlapping wall-clock under realistic external-system latency
- Worktree-per-bead isolation preserved; no shared-state corruption
- Telemetry showing wall-clock gain vs sequential baseline (ties to instrumentation bead)

title: [Impl] Phase 3: bead-spec agent + brainstorm-bead formula rewiring
description:
## Summary

Replace single-context brainstorm-bead execution with role-differentiated assets: two behavioral skills (bead-assess, bead-write-spec) with mechanical model/effort enforcement for main-agent phases, and one fresh-eyes subagent (bead-reviewer) for adversarial spec review.

## Background / Motivation

The existing brainstorm-bead formula runs all phases in main-agent context with no role differentiation. Two structural defects:

1. **Self-review anti-pattern**: the same context that wrote the spec reviews the spec at ralf-spec-review — exactly the bias RALF-IT doctrine forbids.
2. **No per-phase model/effort control**: assess and write-spec have meaningfully different computational demands with no mechanism to express this.

The worker-agent decomposition in 7bk.19 applied the same logic to the implementation side. This bead applies it to the brainstorm side.

## Requirements

**R1 — Create `bead-assess` skill**
- Path: `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/bead-assess/SKILL.md`
- Frontmatter: `model: opus[1m]`, `effort: medium`
- Content: behavioral instructions for the assess phase — gap identification, [m]/[h] AC tag proposals, auto-mergeable and review-level proposals with rationale, QUESTION FILTER application
- Scope: assess phase ONLY. classify-and-policy content is NOT included — 7bk.12 owns that step and its wiring entirely.

**R2 — Create `bead-write-spec` skill**
- Path: `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/bead-write-spec/SKILL.md`
- Frontmatter: `model: opus[1m]`, `effort: high`
- Content: behavioral instructions for spec writing — structured spec format (summary, background, requirements, out-of-scope, AC, design notes, open questions), bd update commands, decision recording. The superpowers:writing-plans methodology is intentionally inlined here rather than preloaded as a skill dependency — writing-plans is on the off-limits list for bead-tracked work (beads.md), so its content is absorbed into this skill's body.

**R3 — Create `bead-reviewer` agent**
- Path: `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-reviewer.md`
- model: claude-opus-4-7 (agent.md convention; note: opus[1m] is skill frontmatter convention)
- Tools: Read, Grep, Glob (read-only — see D3 for why Bash is excluded)
- Skills: superpowers:brainstorming
- Color: blue
- Role: adversarial spec review — completeness, ambiguity, feasibility, consistency, implementability; widens scope to heuristically check [m]/[h] classification and knob choices (auto-mergeable, review-level) for plausibility
- Note: existing agent.md files in this repo do not use effort: or memory: frontmatter. Introduce these fields if/when the harness supports them; for now omit.

**R4 — Update `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml`**
- assess step: REPLACE step body with thin shim. The shim's first instruction is to invoke bead-assess skill. Canonical behavioral content lives in the skill, not the formula step. Invocation MUST be first in the turn to ensure the harness model/effort override (effort: medium) covers the productive assess work.
- write-spec step: REPLACE step body with thin shim. First instruction invokes bead-write-spec skill. Same first-in-turn requirement.
- discuss step: UNCHANGED. Main-agent interactive. No skill invocation.
- ralf-spec-review step: REPLACE inline ralf-review skill invocation. New flow: (1) main agent pre-fetches spec text via `bd show {{bead-id}}` in main-agent context, (2) dispatches bead-reviewer subagent via Agent tool with spec text, review criteria, context, and max-cycles all in the prompt body. bead-reviewer has no Bash — it cannot run bd show itself. Pre-fetch is mandatory.
- Update formula header comment to reflect new execution model.

**R5 — Update arch doc catalog**
- `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §6: Update bead-assessor and bead-specwriter catalog entries to reflect skill-form implementation (bead-assess, bead-write-spec). Add cross-reference to D1 rationale. bead-reviewer agent entry preserved unchanged.

**R6 — Extend verify-artifacts.sh**
- Add file-existence checks for: bead-assess skill, bead-write-spec skill, bead-reviewer agent.

## Out of Scope

- classify-and-policy formula step and dispatch wiring (owned entirely by 7bk.12)
- Effort calibration refinement (7bk.15)
- bead-assessor.md and bead-specwriter.md agent files (see D1)
- agent:* / skill:* dispatch label overrides in formula (7bk.22, pending, scoped to implement-bead)

## Design Notes

**D1 — Skills vs agents for assess/write-spec; lifecycle contract**
Skills are required for main-agent phases because agent.md frontmatter is dispatch-time-only — it is consumed by the harness when a subagent is created via Agent tool, not for mid-session main-agent behavior. There is no mechanism to apply agent.md constraints (tool scoping, memory policy) to a step executed inline by the main agent. Skills are the correct primitive.

The Claude Code harness mechanically enforces model: and effort: frontmatter fields for the duration of the turn in which the skill is invoked. "Duration" means the rest of that agent turn — from skill invocation forward. Consequently, the skill MUST be invoked as the first action in the formula step. If the agent does productive assess work (gap analysis, question formulation) BEFORE invoking the skill, that work runs at the session's default model/effort, defeating the purpose. The thin shim design enforces this ordering: the step body says "invoke bead-assess skill" with nothing before it.

Effort levels follow arch §6 defaults: assess=medium, write-spec=high. The high
acceptance_criteria:
bead-assess skill exists at src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/bead-assess/SKILL.md with model: opus[1m] and effort: medium frontmatter
bead-write-spec skill exists at src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/bead-write-spec/SKILL.md with model: opus[1m] and effort: high frontmatter; writing-plans methodology inlined (skill is off-limits for bead-tracked work)
bead-reviewer.md agent exists at src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-reviewer.md with model: claude-opus-4-7, tools: Read+Grep+Glob, color: blue, skills: superpowers:brainstorming
brainstorm-bead.formula.toml assess step body replaced with thin shim: invokes bead-assess skill as first action
brainstorm-bead.formula.toml write-spec step body replaced with thin shim: invokes bead-write-spec skill as first action
brainstorm-bead.formula.toml discuss step is unchanged (main-agent interactive, no skill)
brainstorm-bead.formula.toml ralf-spec-review step pre-fetches spec via bd show in main-agent context then dispatches subagent_type: bead-reviewer with spec text, review criteria, context, and max-cycles all in Agent tool prompt body
docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md §6 catalog updated: bead-assessor entry changed to skill-form (bead-assess), bead-specwriter entry changed to skill-form (bead-write-spec), bead-reviewer agent entry preserved; design rationale cross-referenced
scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh extended with file-existence checks for bead-assess skill, bead-write-spec skill, and bead-reviewer agent; script passes
No bead-assessor.md or bead-specwriter.md agent files created
Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: Review karpathy-guidelines and other external skills for formula/skill value-adds
description: Audit the karpathy-guidelines skill and any other externally-sourced skills (e.g. andrej-karpathy-skills) for patterns or principles that could strengthen our bead formulas (brainstorm-bead, implement-feature, fix-bug, etc.) or warrant updates to our own skills. Produce a short list of candidate improvements with rationale. No implementation — discovery and recommendation only.

title: [Follow-up] Soft-warning UX for open bead carrying produced-bead-* label (anomaly diagnostic)
description:
Discovered during cycle-2 ralf-review of agents-config-12q7.2 (Route Z spec). When an open bead carries a produced-bead-<Y-id> label (which is unusual — suggests stuck finalize replay or manual mis-application), Route Z's Step 1.5 short-circuits at the status check (status != 'closed' exits the preflight before label evaluation). The bead proceeds through Step 2/3 normally, losing a potentially-useful diagnostic signal. Goal: emit a soft warning to the user when this anomaly is detected, without changing routing behavior.

Out of scope for the parent (12q7.4). Filed per cycle-2 MINOR-3.
close_reason: Duplicate of agents-config-hhou (existing follow-up bead for same cycle-2 MINOR-3 anomaly)

title: start-bead: soft-warning UX when open bead carries produced-bead-* (finalize-replay anomaly)
description:
When an open bead carries a produced-bead-<Y-id> label, it indicates a finalize-replay anomaly: the brainstorm-bead formula started to close X and produce Y but didn't complete the X-close step. start-bead's Step 1.5 (Route Z) short-circuits on status != closed, so this anomaly silently falls through to Routes A/B/C — losing diagnostic-signal traceability.

Add a soft-warning to start-bead Step 1.5 (or as a separate Step 1.6 'open-bead anomaly check') that emits an agent reply note when an open bead carries produced-bead-*, then continues normal routing. Non-gating; pure visibility.

Surfaced by ralf-review cycle 2 of agents-config-12q7.2 (Route Z spec) as MINOR-3.

Out of scope for 12q7.2 by design — that bead is the Route Z preflight only.

title: Research bead gates: applicability and workflow integration
description:
Investigate whether 'bead gates' exist as a concept in the beads system and what utility they might provide for our workflows.

## Questions to answer

- Does bd have a native gate or pre-condition mechanism (e.g. required labels, status checks, or hooks before transitions)?
- If not, what patterns exist or could be introduced to enforce gates (e.g. label-based guards, molecule step preconditions, custom bd hooks)?
- What workflow pain points could gates address? (e.g. prevent close while human label is present, block implementation-ready without brainstormed, require verified-by-human before close on flagged beads)
- Are there any existing formula steps or skill checks already acting as informal gates that could be formalized?
- What is the implementation cost — configuration, scripting, skill updates?

## Deliverable

A research report with findings and a recommendation: adopt a gate pattern, defer, or close as not applicable.

title: Add 'work isolation mandatory before edits' rule to AGENTS.md
notes:
Captured from uncommitted edit found in stale worktree .claude/worktrees/feat-jyb2-dynamic-include during hft housekeeping (session 66272d4a, 2026-05-06). Worktree was scheduled for removal; this bead preserves the policy text before discard.

## Proposed addition

Add a bullet under the relevant constraints section of AGENTS.md (or the analogous constraints/workflow doc — the user's intent was the root AGENTS.md):

  **Work isolation is mandatory before edits** — before the first file edit, run `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`; if it returns `main`, create/switch to a dedicated feature branch in a worktree and only then proceed

## Why

Reinforces the existing 'Isolate work' orchestration rule mechanically — gives agents a concrete pre-flight check (`git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`) instead of just 'use a worktree'. Catches the failure mode of starting edits on main without realizing it.

## Open questions for routing

- Where exactly does this belong? Candidates:
  - Top-level project AGENTS.md (which file the uncommitted edit was made to)
  - User-scoped INSTRUCTIONS.md.template under <constraints> or <orchestration>
  - A new entry in worktrees.md
- Is this a standalone rule, or should it merge with existing 'Isolate work' guidance?
- Does it need to apply to BOTH user-level and project-level AGENTS files (i.e., goes in INSTRUCTIONS.md.template so it propagates everywhere)?

## Source

Original uncommitted edit on root AGENTS.md in worktree feat-jyb2-dynamic-include, never committed.

title: brainstorm-bead writes spec to notes field but rules/beads.md says specs go in description — resolve inconsistency
description:
# Spec: Migrate brainstorm-bead spec field from notes to description

## Summary

Migrate the brainstorm-bead formula's spec-writing steps from `--notes=` to `--description=`, and update the beads.md documentation to reflect the upstream-confirmed design intent: notes accumulate, specifications are written once to description.

## Background / motivation

The beads project confirmed that field-update semantics are intentional by design:
- `--notes` / `--append-notes`: accumulation field — use for lifecycle context, run breadcrumbs, discovered constraints
- `--description`, `--design`, `--acceptance`: replace-semantics only — write once, completely, at the step that owns them
- No `--append-{description,design,acceptance}` flags exist by design (filed as f298, closed as upstream-wontfix)

The brainstorm-bead formula currently writes the spec to `--notes=`. This is the wrong field: the spec is a single authoritative document written once (by write-spec) and revised once (by finalize). That is description territory. Meanwhile, `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md` says "specs may be written directly into the bead description" — accurate once this migration lands.

## Requirements

### R1. brainstorm-bead write-spec step — migrate to --description=

In `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/brainstorm-bead.formula.toml`, the write-spec step's spec-write line:
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --notes="<full spec document>"`
→
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --description="<full spec document>"`

The migration MUST use the inline `--description=` form (not `--body-file` or `--stdin`) for symmetry with the existing inline `--acceptance=` invocation immediately above and to keep the formula's TOML triple-quoted description block straightforward.

Update the guard preamble immediately above this line. The current text reads (paraphrased): "--notes= REPLACES the entire notes field, and --acceptance= REPLACES the entire acceptance-criteria field. ... If the bead has prior investigation context (notes from create-bead, user discussion, or earlier brainstorming), you MUST either: (a) merge ... (b) preserve via bd comments add ...".

The post-migration guard MUST:
- Reference `--description=` REPLACES "the entire description field" (verbatim phrase, for greppability)
- Drop the "--notes= REPLACES the entire notes field" reference (now obsolete here)
- Reword the prior-context source list to reflect what description actually contains pre-write-spec: e.g. "prior context (the placeholder description from `bd create`, manual `--description` updates, or notes from create-bead / user discussion that should be folded into the spec)". The point is to STOP saying "notes from create-bead" as if those are at risk of overwrite — they aren't, they live in the notes field; but they MAY contain context the spec author should incorporate.
- Keep the same (a) merge / (b) preserve via `bd comments add` escape hatches
- Keep the existing greppable sentinel structure so the AC's positive grep on "REPLACES the entire description field" matches reliably

### R2. brainstorm-bead finalize step — migrate to --description=

In the same file, the finalize step's spec-revise line:
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --notes="<revised spec>"`
→
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --description="<revised spec>"`

Also update the prose header IMMEDIATELY above this line. Currently reads:
  "1. Update bead acceptance + notes with final spec revisions:"
→
  "1. Update bead acceptance + description with final spec revisions:"

The finalize step does NOT get its own destructive-write guard preamble — by the time finalize runs, write-spec has already authored the canonical spec into description. Finalize legitimately overwrites that freshly-written content with the revised version, never "prior investigation context". This is the same logic that protected revise from receiving a guard in the original hft fix.

The `--acceptance=` lines in both write-spec and finalize remain unchanged.

### R3. brainstorm-bead ralf-spec-review step — clarify open-questions sink

In the same file, Phase 4 (ralf-spec-review) has a sentence:
  "If concerns remain after cycle cap, document them explicitly as open questions in the bead notes."

Post-migration this is intentional and correct: open questions discovered during review are accumulating breadcrumbs and belong in notes. Update this line to make the mechanism explicit:
  "If concerns remain after cycle cap, document them explicitly via `bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes \"open question: <text>\"`. (Open questions discovered during review are lifecycle accumulation, distinct from the spec body which lives in description.)"

This removes any reading where "in the bead notes" might be misinterpreted post-migration.

### R4. rules/beads.md — update Notes vs Comments table

In `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md`, the "Notes vs Comments" table row for `bd update <id> --notes "..."`:

Current text ("When to use" column): "Initial bead creation, or `brainstorm-bead` spec writes — intentional overwrites only"
→
New text: "Initial bead creation where notes need seeding; intentional full-replacement of accumulated notes (rare — prefer --append-notes)"

After the table, add a callout that mentions ALL THREE flag names individually:

  **Append asymmetry**: `--append-notes` exists; `--append-description`, `--append-design`, and `--append-acceptance` do not — these fields are replace-semantics by design. If you need to accumulate content across formula steps, use `--append-notes`. If the information belongs in description/design/acceptance, restructure so one step owns the complete value.

The exact strings `append-description`, `append-design`, and `append-acceptance` MUST each appear at least once in the file post-edit (each individually verified by AC).

## Out of scope

- Bulk-migrating pre-existing beads that have specs in notes (leave alone; `bd show` displays both fields, consumers are unaffected; manual migration recipes are NOT in scope)
- Changing `--acceptance=` semantics (stays as-is in both steps)
- Changing the brainstorm-bead revise behavior (write-spec owns the field; revise loops back to write-spec to overwrite)
- Adding a guard preamble to the finalize step (explicitly NOT in scope per R2 rationale)
- Updating implement-feature, fix-bug, implement-bead, or run-queue (they consume via `bd show` which displays all fields)
- Changing how `create-bead` populates notes (orthogonal; the create-bead skill writes lifecycle context to notes via --append-notes, which is correct per design intent)

## Design notes

- Downstream consumers (implement-feature, fix-bug, implement-bead) read the spec via `bd show {{bead-id}}` which displays both description and notes. No coordinated downstream changes required.
- Pre-existing beads with specs in notes are not affected operationally — `bd show` still displays them. Mixed-state coexistence is benign and intentional.
- The write-spec guard preamble (added by agents-config-hft) protects against destructive overwrite of prior context. Post-migration the protection applies to the description field; the prior-context inventory changes accordingly (see R1).
- The finalize step is asymmetric on purpose: by the time it runs, write-spec has already written the canonical spec, so there is no "prior investigation context" to protect.
- ralf-spec-review's "open questions to notes" instruction (R3) is now explicitly aligned with the accumulation-vs-spec design: open questions are lifecycle breadcrumbs, --append-notes territory.
- Inline `--description="..."` is valid for the spec lengths brainstorm-bead produces. `--body-file` would require additional shell ceremony inside the formula's triple-quoted TOML description block; not worth it.

## Open questions

None. Scope confirmed in session 0413407c. RALF spec-review cycles 1 (Codex) and 2 (Gemini) raised five MODERATE findings total, all addressed. No third cycle (formula cap = 2).
acceptance_criteria:

finalize step prose header updated: grep -c 'Update bead acceptance + notes with' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/brainstorm-bead.formula.toml returns 0
