rules/beads.md callout mentions all three append-* flag names: grep -c 'append-description' src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md returns >=1 AND grep -c 'append-design' returns >=1 AND grep -c 'append-acceptance' returns >=1
Pre-existing beads with specs in notes unchanged (no bulk migration)
Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass. (N/A — manual + grep-based)
notes: Discovered during hft. brainstorm-bead.formula.toml write-spec step writes the spec to {{bead-id}}'s notes via --notes=. Meanwhile rules/beads.md says 'specs may be written directly into the bead description'. Resolve by either: (a) migrate brainstorm-bead.write-spec to use --description (via --body-file or similar) and update finalize/downstream, or (b) update rules/beads.md to reflect actual notes-field convention. Cross-cutting concern: the bd CLI doc nit 'bd delete --reason' (used in hft spec but actual flag is --force) can be folded into this same housekeeping pass or filed separately if it grows.

title: bd CLI: add --append-acceptance / --append-design / --append-description for full append-parity with --append-notes
notes: Discovered during hft (bead notes-overwrite trap fix). Today --append-notes is the only append-flag; --acceptance/--design/--description always overwrite. Full append-parity would let formulas safely accumulate AC/design content the same way they accumulate notes. Upstream bd binary work, not this repo.

title: Quote unquoted <path> in git -C invocations across pre-existing formulas
description:
Source: surfaced by Copilot's PR #40 round-2 review (comment 3196388664) on agents-config-ooun. Copilot flagged the unquoted git -C <path> command in docs-only.formula.toml apply-edits step (line 119); fixed in PR #40. The same anti-pattern exists in two PRE-EXISTING locations not in scope for PR #40:

- src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml line 148
- src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml line 148

Both were last touched in commit 4a1e4455 (Scott Hamilton, 2026-05-05) — predating PR #40. Per the project's minimal-edits rule, scope-creeping the fix into PR #40 was rejected; tracking here for separate disposition.

Risk: paths with spaces or shell-special characters (e.g. /Users/Scott Hamilton/foo) misparse and either fail the rev-parse check or — worse — validate the wrong directory.

Fix: change

  git -C <path> rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree

to

  git -C "<path>" rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree

in both locations, mirroring the pattern already used by implement-bead/SKILL.md line 51 (which Scott authored correctly the first time).

Provisional ACs:
- [m] git diff at HEAD shows the two lines flipped to quoted form.
- [m] No other unquoted git -C <path> patterns remain in src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/*.toml or src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/*/SKILL.md (verify with: grep -rE 'git -C <path>' src/plugins/beads/).
- [h] None — pure mechanical fix.

Trivial enough that start-bead Route B (inline) is appropriate.

title: Extract repeated formula shell blocks into helper scripts
description:
Source: PR #40 review comment 3196066711 by @scotthamilton77 on src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md line 42. Scott noted: 'Same with other code blocks in this PR' — the pattern is broader than the one block.

Origin: PR #40 (bead agents-config-ooun) introduces several formula-internal shell blocks that are duplicated across files and call sites. Each agent has to reconstruct the logic at runtime instead of invoking a stable command.

Precedent: the wait-for-pr-comments skill already follows the helper-script convention internally with write-inventory.sh and validate-inventory.sh co-located in the skill directory — invoked via shell from within the skill's prose, not reconstructed by the agent.

Goal: design a convention for extracting reusable formula shell logic into helper scripts that:
- the skill/formula prose references via a stable command line (not by re-pasting source);
- agents invoke without parsing the body;
- have unit-testable contracts (input args, stdin/stdout, exit codes);
- live somewhere agents can discover.

Open design questions (brainstorm before implementing):
- Where should cross-formula helpers live? (Skill-internal works for skill-private logic; formula-shared logic crosses skills — possibly src/plugins/beads/scripts/ or .beads/scripts/.)
- Discovery: how does an agent find the right helper? By name convention? By manifest?
- Contract format: shell-script-with-flags? Or callable bash functions sourced into the formula step?
- Compatibility: existing in-prose blocks need a deprecation path; formulas already in liquid molecules must keep working.

Specific extraction targets (initial set, expand during brainstorm):
- formula-* label collision detector (currently inlined in implement-bead SKILL.md sections 1 and 2, plus brainstorm-bead.formula.toml finalize step)
- [gates].test extractor from project-config.toml (currently inlined in implement-feature/fix-bug/brainstorm-bead — flag-based awk parser)
- parent-chain claim walk (currently inlined in beads.md I1)
- parent-chain close walk (currently inlined in beads.md I2)
- merge-gate-child idempotent creator (currently inlined in reroute step 4 of implement-feature/fix-bug)

Provisional ACs (finalize during brainstorm):
- [m] At least one helper extracted as a working script with tests (the highest-value duplicate).
- [m] Formula prose references the script by command line, not by source paste.
- [m] Convention documented in src/plugins/beads/AGENTS.md (or equivalent location decided during brainstorm).
- [h] Convention is ergonomic enough that a reviewer reading a formula understands what the helper does without opening it (clear command name + brief in-prose summary of contract).

Dependencies: blocked by PR #40 merge (the duplicated blocks land in PR #40 and become extraction targets).

title: Architecture doc §5.3/§5.4/§5.8 cross-cutting tables don't acknowledge docs-only stages
description:
Discovered during ooun review (PR-pending). The new docs-only formula introduces stage names 'apply-edits' and 'review' that the cross-cutting tables in bead-pipeline-architecture.md don't enumerate:

- §5.3 line 509 enumerates allowed stage-role-name values for session-id derivation: preflight, diagnose, red-tests, green-loop, quality-sweep, verify-ac, create-pr, review-cycle, merge-or-handoff. The docs-only stages 'apply-edits' and 'review' are missing — the session-id convention is undefined for them.
- §5.4 line 521-525 cwd table doesn't mention 'apply-edits' or 'review' (though the formula header comments specify cwd: worktree, so the implicit convention covers it).
- §5.8 line 572-582 state-channels table omits docs-only transitions (apply-edits → review, review → verify-ac).

Risk: medium. The formula's apply-edits and review steps will work without §5.3 listing them (UUIDv5 derivation works from any string), but spec drift means future readers can't tell which stage names are sanctioned.

Recommended fix: extend §5.3's enumeration to include apply-edits, review. Add docs-only rows to §5.4 and §5.8 (or a single note that docs-only stages cwd:worktree throughout, mirroring implement-feature except for preflight at repo root).

Source: ooun final quality review M4 finding.

title: Beads 2.0 future proofing: support BD_JSON_ENVELOPE=1
notes: Address the upcoming change in bd v2.0 where the JSON output format will change. Opt-in early by setting BD_JSON_ENVELOPE=1 and ensuring all scripts/agents handle the new format. Reference: docs/JSON_SCHEMA.md.

title: Replace generic routing path references (A, B, C, etc.) with functional names in create-bead and implement-bead skills
description:
The create-bead and implement-bead skills (and related documentation like start-bead) use generic routing path nomenclature such as 'Route A', 'Route B', 'Route C', 'Route D' when describing decision trees and workflow branching logic. These references lack semantic meaning and become confusing when cross-referenced or discussed in other contexts.

Replace all generic routing path identifiers with functional names that clearly describe what each path does. Examples of functional naming conventions to consider:
  - 'Route A (brainstorm)' → 'brainstorm-path' or 'spec-needed-path'
  - 'Route B (implement-ready)' → 'implementation-ready-path' or 'ready-to-code-path'
  - 'Route C (trivial)' → 'inline-implementation-path' or 'quick-fix-path'
  - 'Route D (human-flagged)' → 'human-intervention-path' or 'resolution-needed-path'

Files in scope (initial scan):
  - src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md
  - src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md
  - src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md
  - src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/resolve-human-bead/SKILL.md (if it references routing)
  - Any AGENTS.md or documentation that cross-references these paths

This is a documentation clarity and maintainability task. The logic and behavior remain unchanged; only the nomenclature is refined for clarity.

title: Refactor install.sh for modularity, testability, and maintainability
description: install.sh has grown into a monolithic script that handles multiple concerns (tool detection, file copying, template rendering, plugin installation). Break it into modular, independently-testable functions. Improve maintainability by separating concerns: detection logic, file operations, template processing, and error handling. Add test coverage for critical paths.

title: Empirical A/B testing of per-stage model/effort allocations against quality/cost outcomes
description:
Empirical A/B testing of the per-stage model/effort defaults (per 7bk.15) to validate cost vs quality outcomes with real bead processing. The F-04 default table shipped with 7bk.15 was set by argument, not data. This bead defines a methodology to gather data so future tuning is data-driven.

## Why

Several decisions in the F-04 table are calibration-by-intuition:
- preflight at sonnet-high (vs sonnet-medium): is the "architectural gate" framing right or paranoid?
- green-loop controller at opus-medium (vs opus-high): is medium effort enough for synthesis decisions?
- four haiku stages (quality-sweep, verify-ac, create-pr, merge-or-handoff): do they break down on edge cases, or stay mechanical?
- review-cycle at sonnet-medium: does it cope with high Copilot comment volume?
- diagnose (bug-class) at sonnet-high: is the cost justified by quality lift?
- red-tests Codex at gpt-5.5 vs gpt-5.4-mini: cost-vs-quality call (per TR5 reconsideration in 7bk.15)

Without empirical data, every adjustment to the table is argument-based; we may be silently sacrificing quality/reliability for cost or vice versa.

## Methodology sketch (refine at brainstorm)

1. Pick 5-10 representative beads from history (small/medium/large scope; feature/bug/chore mix; varying AC complexity).
2. For each bead, process through the pipeline N times with different stage-defaults variants:
   - Variant A: F-04 baseline (per 7bk.15 amendment 2026-05-04)
   - Variant B: cheaper (downgrade preflight + diagnose to sonnet-medium; opus-medium → sonnet-high in green-loop)
   - Variant C: expensive (upgrade four haiku stages to sonnet-medium)
3. Capture per-run metrics:
   - Total token cost (Anthropic + foreign-CLI)
   - Wall-time per stage and total
   - # of RALF iterations to converge
   - # of Copilot comments (FIX-class)
   - # of human escapes (flag-human invocations)
   - # of reverts/regressions in subsequent commits
4. Quality scoring: human (or independent reviewer agent) reviews each variant's PR, scores 1-5 on:
   - (a) test correctness
   - (b) implementation quality
   - (c) AC coverage
   - (d) idiomatic / non-hacky
5. Output: a calibration report with per-stage recommendations to amend the F-04 table.

## Dependencies

- agents-config-7bk.9 (per-stage architecture functional)
- agents-config-7bk.14 (Model:/Effort: directives mechanism shipped — required to vary defaults per run)
- agents-config-7bk.15 (initial defaults applied as baseline)

## Out of scope

- Changing the F-04 table without supporting data from this exercise.
- Building automated A/B harness in v1 (manual comparison runs are acceptable for first pass).
- Cross-project generalization (this is project-specific calibration).

## Acceptance Criteria

- A/B test methodology documented and approved
- Test fixture beads selected (5-10 representative)
- Comparison runs executed for at least 3 variants
- Quality scoring rubric agreed and applied
- Calibration report produced with specific recommendations to amend the F-04 table (or confirm it as-is)
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: Cost rightsizing: per-stage model/effort surgery across brainstorm and green-loop
description:
Authoritative source for the per-stage default model/effort table consumed by 7bk.14's directives mechanism. Plus six cost-rightsizing recommendations to apply once the per-stage architecture (7bk.9) is functional.

Full design: `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §9 (Cost model).

## Per-stage default model/effort table

These defaults are owned by THIS bead. They are consumed by 7bk.14 (Model: and Effort: directives mechanism) and applied via formula step body directives at pour time.

| Stage              | Model               | Effort | Rationale                                                       |
|--------------------|---------------------|--------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| preflight          | claude-sonnet-4-6   | high   | Architectural gate; bad decisions corrupt whole pipeline; cheap absolute cost |
| red-tests          | claude-sonnet-4-6   | medium | TDD orchestration; moderate complexity                           |
| green-loop         | claude-opus-4-7     | medium | RALF-IT controller (already opus per skill frontmatter); synthesis is highest-leverage |
| quality-sweep      | claude-haiku-4-5    | medium | Mostly shell-out (lint/build/typecheck/test/static-analysis)     |
| verify-ac          | claude-haiku-4-5    | medium | Mechanical AC matching against worktree state                    |
| create-pr          | claude-haiku-4-5    | medium | gh pr create + structured PR body composition                    |
| review-cycle       | claude-sonnet-4-6   | medium | Per-comment classify + dispatch fix subagents + reply            |
| merge-or-handoff   | claude-haiku-4-5    | medium | Branch-and-stamp                                                 |

Bug-class addition (per architecture doc §3 diagnose stage):

| Stage              | Model               | Effort | Rationale                                                       |
|--------------------|---------------------|--------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| diagnose (bug-only) | claude-sonnet-4-6  | high   | Root-cause investigation; novel reasoning over unknown bug surface |

Subagent dispatches inside each stage have their own defaults per agent frontmatter (e.g., bead-implementor, bead-verifier, quality-reviewer).

These defaults are calibration-by-argument, not data. Empirical validation is tracked in 7bk.16.

## Tier 3 cost-rightsizing recommendations

### TR1 — bead-spec persona split (overlaps with 7bk.3)
- assess + classify-and-policy → claude-sonnet-4-6 effort:medium
- write-spec → claude-opus-4-7 effort:high
- ralf-spec-review → claude-opus-4-7 effort:medium with FRESH context (per 7bk.3's amended decomposition)

### TR2 — Drop simplify from per-iteration RALF; final pass only
RALF-IT skill: quality-reviewer runs per iteration; simplify slash command runs ONCE at final review pass.

### TR3 — bead-implementor effort:high for green-loop iter 1 only
First RALF iteration: bead-implementor runs effort:high (anchors architectural choices). Subsequent iterations: effort:medium. Knob: `iteration-effort-green-loop-1: high` (or configure as RALF-IT default).

### TR4 — quality-reviewer drop opus[1m] → opus 4.7 for per-iteration
Per-iteration reviews: claude-opus-4-7 (standard 200K context). Final review pass on full bead diff: claude-opus-4-7[1m]. Implementation via context-mode knob on the agent dispatch.

### TR5 — red-tests Codex gpt-5.5 cost-vs-quality call (DEFERRED)
Stage 2 keeps gpt-5.5 default (canonical post-2026-05-04 release). Whether to downgrade to gpt-5.4-mini for test review is a cost-vs-quality decision deferred to empirical calibration via 7bk.16. Revisit if data supports downgrade.

### TR6 — quality-reviewer skill preload hygiene
Remove preloaded skills `superpowers:requesting-code-review` and `superpowers:receiving-code-review` from quality-reviewer's frontmatter (they are for the requestor and receiver of reviews, not the reviewer itself). Either no preloads or `superpowers:verification-before-completion`.

## Aggregate impact estimate

Per-bead worst-case: ~$13-17 → ~$8-10 (35-45% reduction).
- Brainstorm: ~$3 → ~$1.50 (TR1)
- green-loop: ~$8-12 → ~$5-7 (TR2 + TR3 + TR4)
- red-tests: ~$1 → ~$0.50 (TR5 if applied per 7bk.16)
- Hygiene: trivial savings (TR6)

## Dependencies

- 7bk.14 (formula model/effort directives — the mechanism)
- 7bk.3 (TR1's bead-spec split lands there)
- 7bk.9 (per-stage architecture is the surface being tuned)

## Acceptance Criteria

- TR1-TR6 applied per spec (TR5 conditionally per 7bk.16 data)

title: Audit upstream obra/superpowers plugin description in README.md and AGENTS.md
description:

> Provides the skill/agent framework referenced throughout: brainstorming, TDD, verification-before-completion, dispatching-parallel-agents, code-reviewer, code-simplifier, finishing-a-development-branch, and more

This enumeration includes `code-reviewer` and `code-simplifier` — but in our local tree those have been merged/replaced (`quality-reviewer` replaces both). The README/AGENTS.md prose describes what the upstream plugin provides, NOT what we ship — but the user reading the README has no way to tell that distinction.

Action: verify upstream `obra/superpowers` still provides those agent names. If yes, leave the doc as-is and add a clarifying parenthetical. If no, update the enumeration to match what the upstream plugin currently provides.

## Context

title: Phase 3: bead-spec agent + brainstorm-bead formula rewiring
description:
Replace single-agent brainstorm support with role-specialized bead-spec personas, drop user-scoped memory, and restore the fresh-eyes property for spec review.

Full design: `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §4.4 (Decomposed bead-spec personas) and §6 (Agent catalog).

## Why decomposed

A single bead-spec agent driving both write-spec and ralf-spec-review is the classic self-review anti-pattern: the spec writer reviews its own prior work with its own biases pre-loaded. `memory: user` further accumulates context across all brainstorm sessions for all projects, contaminating assessments with irrelevant prior project context. RALF-IT's own doctrine forbids this pattern.

Folded scope from agents-config-8sy (closes when this bead lands).

## Changes

1. Create multiple bead-spec personas (names TBD at implementation; suggested: bead-assessor, bead-specwriter, bead-reviewer):
   - All `claude-opus-4-7`
   - Effort: per-role calibration per architecture doc §6 and 7bk.15
   - `memory: NONE` (no user-scoped memory)
   - Skills: per-role (assessor: brainstorming; specwriter: brainstorming + writing-plans; reviewer: brainstorming only)
   - Tools: read-only (Read+Grep+Glob)
   - Color: distinct per persona

2. Update `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` — dispatch the appropriate agent per step:
   - assess → bead-assessor
   - write-spec → bead-specwriter
   - classify-and-policy (from 7bk.12) → bead-assessor or bead-specwriter (decide at implementation)
   - ralf-spec-review → bead-reviewer (DIFFERENT persona from specwriter — fresh-eyes property restored)

3. End-to-end test: brainstorm session dispatches each role correctly with no shared memory contamination.

## On close of this bead

Close agents-config-8sy as folded.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Multiple bead-spec personas exist at `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/`
- `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` dispatches the appropriate persona per step
- ralf-spec-review uses a DIFFERENT persona than write-spec
- No persona uses `memory: user`
- E2E brainstorm session works correctly with each role active
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
acceptance_criteria:
bead-assess skill exists at src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/bead-assess/SKILL.md with model: opus[1m] and effort: medium frontmatter
bead-write-spec skill exists at src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/bead-write-spec/SKILL.md with model: opus[1m] and effort: high frontmatter; writing-plans methodology inlined (skill is off-limits for bead-tracked work)
bead-reviewer.md agent exists at src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-reviewer.md with model: claude-opus-4-7, tools: Read+Grep+Glob, color: blue, skills: superpowers:brainstorming
brainstorm-bead.formula.toml assess step body replaced with thin shim: invokes bead-assess skill as first action
brainstorm-bead.formula.toml write-spec step body replaced with thin shim: invokes bead-write-spec skill as first action
brainstorm-bead.formula.toml discuss step is unchanged (main-agent interactive, no skill)
brainstorm-bead.formula.toml ralf-spec-review step pre-fetches spec via bd show in main-agent context then dispatches subagent_type: bead-reviewer with spec text, review criteria, context, and max-cycles all in Agent tool prompt body
docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md §6 catalog updated: bead-assessor entry changed to skill-form (bead-assess), bead-specwriter entry changed to skill-form (bead-write-spec), bead-reviewer agent entry preserved; design rationale cross-referenced
scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh extended with file-existence checks for bead-assess skill, bead-write-spec skill, and bead-reviewer agent; script passes
No bead-assessor.md or bead-specwriter.md agent files created
Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
notes:
## Summary

Replace single-context brainstorm-bead execution with role-differentiated assets: two behavioral skills (bead-assess, bead-write-spec) with mechanical model/effort enforcement for main-agent phases, and one fresh-eyes subagent (bead-reviewer) for adversarial spec review.

## Background / Motivation

The existing brainstorm-bead formula runs all phases in main-agent context with no role differentiation. Two structural defects:

1. **Self-review anti-pattern**: the same context that wrote the spec reviews the spec at ralf-spec-review — exactly the bias RALF-IT doctrine forbids.
2. **No per-phase model/effort control**: assess and write-spec have meaningfully different computational demands with no mechanism to express this.

The worker-agent decomposition in 7bk.19 applied the same logic to the implementation side. This bead applies it to the brainstorm side.

## Requirements

**R1 — Create `bead-assess` skill**
- Path: `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/bead-assess/SKILL.md`
- Frontmatter: `model: opus[1m]`, `effort: medium`
- Content: behavioral instructions for the assess phase — gap identification, [m]/[h] AC tag proposals, auto-mergeable and review-level proposals with rationale, QUESTION FILTER application
- Scope: assess phase ONLY. classify-and-policy content is NOT included — 7bk.12 owns that step and its wiring entirely.

**R2 — Create `bead-write-spec` skill**
- Path: `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/bead-write-spec/SKILL.md`
- Frontmatter: `model: opus[1m]`, `effort: high`
- Content: behavioral instructions for spec writing — structured spec format (summary, background, requirements, out-of-scope, AC, design notes, open questions), bd update commands, decision recording. The superpowers:writing-plans methodology is intentionally inlined here rather than preloaded as a skill dependency — writing-plans is on the off-limits list for bead-tracked work (beads.md), so its content is absorbed into this skill's body.

**R3 — Create `bead-reviewer` agent**
- Path: `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-reviewer.md`
- model: claude-opus-4-7 (agent.md convention; note: opus[1m] is skill frontmatter convention)
- Tools: Read, Grep, Glob (read-only — see D3 for why Bash is excluded)
- Skills: superpowers:brainstorming
- Color: blue
- Role: adversarial spec review — completeness, ambiguity, feasibility, consistency, implementability; widens scope to heuristically check [m]/[h] classification and knob choices (auto-mergeable, review-level) for plausibility
- Note: existing agent.md files in this repo do not use effort: or memory: frontmatter. Introduce these fields if/when the harness supports them; for now omit.

**R4 — Update `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml`**
- assess step: REPLACE step body with thin shim. The shim's first instruction is to invoke bead-assess skill. Canonical behavioral content lives in the skill, not the formula step. Invocation MUST be first in the turn to ensure the harness model/effort override (effort: medium) covers the productive assess work.
- write-spec step: REPLACE step body with thin shim. First instruction invokes bead-write-spec skill. Same first-in-turn requirement.
- discuss step: UNCHANGED. Main-agent interactive. No skill invocation.
- ralf-spec-review step: REPLACE inline ralf-review skill invocation. New flow: (1) main agent pre-fetches spec text via `bd show {{bead-id}}` in main-agent context, (2) dispatches bead-reviewer subagent via Agent tool with spec text, review criteria, context, and max-cycles all in the prompt body. bead-reviewer has no Bash — it cannot run bd show itself. Pre-fetch is mandatory.
- Update formula header comment to reflect new execution model.

**R5 — Update arch doc catalog**
- `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §6: Update bead-assessor and bead-specwriter catalog entries to reflect skill-form implementation (bead-assess, bead-write-spec). Add cross-reference to D1 rationale. bead-reviewer agent entry preserved unchanged.

**R6 — Extend verify-artifacts.sh**
- Add file-existence checks for: bead-assess skill, bead-write-spec skill, bead-reviewer agent.

## Out of Scope

- classify-and-policy formula step and dispatch wiring (owned entirely by 7bk.12)
- Effort calibration refinement (7bk.15)
- bead-assessor.md and bead-specwriter.md agent files (see D1)
- agent:* / skill:* dispatch label overrides in formula (7bk.22, pending, scoped to implement-bead)

## Design Notes

**D1 — Skills vs agents for assess/write-spec; lifecycle contract**
Skills are required for main-agent phases because agent.md frontmatter is dispatch-time-only — it is consumed by the harness when a subagent is created via Agent tool, not for mid-session main-agent behavior. There is no mechanism to apply agent.md constraints (tool scoping, memory policy) to a step executed inline by the main agent. Skills are the correct primitive.

The Claude Code harness mechanically enforces model: and effort: frontmatter fields for the duration of the turn in which the skill is invoked. "Duration" means the rest of that agent turn — from skill invocation forward. Consequently, the skill MUST be invoked as the first action in the formula step. If the agent does productive assess work (gap analysis, question formulation) BEFORE invoking the skill, that work runs at the session's default model/effort, defeating the purpose. The thin shim design enforces this ordering: the step body says "invoke bead-assess skill" with nothing before it.

Effort levels follow arch §6 defaults: assess=medium, write-spec=high. The high

title: Audit and prune unused skills, agents, and commands
description:
The agents-config repo has accumulated a large inventory of skills, agents, and slash commands across src/user/.agents/, src/user/.claude/, and src/plugins/. Some of these are actively used, others have never been invoked in practice. This audit-and-prune task identifies which are carrying their weight and which are dead weight.

## Why this exists
Over time, skills/agents/commands get added speculatively or for one-off needs and then never revisited. Every extra entry in the inventory is cognitive overhead for the agent at selection time and maintenance burden for the user. Pruning unused entries keeps the configuration sharp and the agent's attention focused on the tools that actually pay rent.

## What needs to be done
- Inventory all skills (src/user/.agents/skills/, src/plugins/*/skills/), agents (src/user/.agents/agents/), and commands (src/user/.claude/commands/, src/plugins/*/commands/) currently installed
- Gather usage evidence: grep transcripts/session logs if available, ask the user which ones they recognize using, cross-reference against recent PRs and commits for skills invoked
- Categorize each entry:
  - KEEP — actively used, clearly valuable
  - PRUNE — never used, redundant, or superseded
  - UNCERTAIN — escalate to user for judgment
- For PRUNE candidates: confirm with user, then remove from the repo
- For UNCERTAIN: present a short summary (name + description + last-seen-used signal) and let the user decide
- Update any references (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, skill indexes) after removals

## Notes
- This is a capture bead — brainstorm/spec work happens later when it's picked up
- Scope excludes the plugin-provided inventories (beads plugin, superpowers plugin, codex plugin, etc.) — those are managed upstream
- Consider whether a recurring lightweight audit (quarterly?) would be useful as a follow-on

title: Discovered-work snippets use <placeholder> inside bash if-condition — invalid shell if copy/pasted
design: Small-scope follow-up: three near-identical doc edits. Fits a single worktree + PR. Same methodology as 9cz (worktree → fix → code-reviewer + simplifier → PR → Copilot → merge-and-cleanup).
notes:
# Problem

In PR #13 (now merged as db41812), Copilot's second review round flagged 3 inline snippets where the discovered-work decision uses:

    if [ -n "$PARENT" ] && <work-is-sibling-subtask-of-$PARENT>; then
      bd create "..." -t <type> -p <prio> --parent "$PARENT"
    else
      NEW=$(bd create "..." -t <type> -p <prio> --json | jq -r '.id')
      bd dep add "$NEW" {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from
    fi

The `<work-is-sibling-subtask-of-$PARENT>` token is a placeholder, but in bash boolean context `<` is parsed as input redirect — so literal copy-paste would try to open a file. Unlike `<type>`/`<prio>` (placeholders inside string args, safe), the if-condition placeholder is syntactically dangerous.

Copilot's suggestion: introduce an `IS_SIBLING_SUBTASK=true/false` variable (or split the if into two separate command sequences), so the snippet is runnable-looking even if placeholder values haven't been substituted.

# Affected files

- src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md line 77
- src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml line 319 (housekeeping step 4)
- src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml line 314 (housekeeping step 4)

# Copilot comment IDs

- 3107495666 (create-bead)
- 3107495677 (implement-feature)
- 3107495680 (fix-bug)

# Acceptance

- All three if-condition snippets replaced with shell-valid form (commented `IS_SIBLING_SUBTASK` boolean, or split into two explicit command paths).
- No more `<something-with-bash-chars>` placeholders inside boolean/expression contexts.
- Reply to each of the 3 Copilot comments pointing at the fix commit; resolve the 3 threads on the new PR.
- TOML still parses.

# Discovered-from

Discovered during wait-for-pr-comments round 2 on PR #13 (agents-config-9cz). Originating bead has no parent epic, so this is orphan + discovered-from (not an epic sibling).
close_reason: Merged PR #14

title: wait-for-pr-comments: re-requesting Copilot via add-reviewer is idempotent
description:
# Problem

gh pr edit <N> --add-reviewer @copilot is idempotent when Copilot is already on the reviewer list — no copilot_work_started event fires, and no fresh review is produced. wait-for-pr-comments Phase 4's re-review path currently relies on the user (or the agent) running add-reviewer to trigger round 2+, which silently fails after round 1.

# Impact

Observed on agents-config-uvt PR #12 round 3: pushed 77bfa98, called gh pr edit --add-reviewer @copilot, waited 5+ min, no copilot_work_started fired. Had to emit a final report without round-3 feedback.

# Fix direction

Add a sub-step in Phase 4: before (or instead of) add-reviewer, run gh pr edit <N> --remove-reviewer @copilot, then --add-reviewer @copilot. The remove+add pair reliably triggers a fresh review cycle. Alternatively, document the gotcha in the skill's Phase 4 so operators know to force-refresh manually.

# Discovered-from

agents-config-uvt

title: Beads plugin: suppress 'git add failed' auto-export warning when .beads/issues.jsonl is gitignored
description: After merging PR #9 in agents-config (which gitignored .beads/issues.jsonl for PII reasons), every bd command now prints a harmless 'Warning: auto-export: git add failed: exit status 1' because the beads post-command hook tries to git-add a file that is now ignored by .gitignore. The Dolt push path works correctly — this is purely a warning hygiene issue. Fix: the beads plugin's auto-export step should check 'git check-ignore' (or equivalent) against the export path before running 'git add', and silently skip the add when the path is ignored. Affects any project that chooses the Dolt-only sync pattern.
close_reason: Cannot reproduce. Checked repo .gitignore: issues.jsonl is NOT gitignored. Ran bd ready — no auto-export warning emitted. The auto-export hook is baked into the compiled bd binary (v1.0.3), which this repo does not maintain. Likely fixed upstream or the .gitignore change was reverted. Stale bead.

title: Beads plugin duplicates Session Completion block in AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md

This is plugin-managed (inside BEGIN/END BEADS INTEGRATION markers) so manual deduplication is impossible. Options: (a) file upstream issue against the beads plugin to support a 'pointer-only' profile for the secondary file, or (b) add a non-plugin pointer pattern in our templates and stop using the minimal profile for CLAUDE.md. Discovered during agents-config-mxe public-repo docs pass.

If the algorithm changes, both copies must stay in sync. Factor into a shared shell helper (alongside setup-worker-audit.sh) or a shared doc anchor referenced by both skills.

title: [WAITING] Auto-rename session on bead start
description:
Blocked on upstream Claude Code feature requests. Re-evaluate when one or more of the following issues ships:

- anthropics/claude-code#34243 — Programmatic session rename from skills/commands (AI tool + hook + CLI subcommand proposals)
- anthropics/claude-code#50040 — Allow assistant to programmatically rename sessions via tool call
- anthropics/claude-code#44786 — Programmatic session naming: hook-callable rename + Skill tool as hook target
- anthropics/claude-code#25870 — --session-name flag for naming sessions at startup
- anthropics/claude-code#15802 — Set terminal title from within a Claude Code session

CURRENT STATE (brainstormed, not yet specced):
The UserPromptSubmit hook is the ONLY hook that supports sessionTitle output. The cleanest viable architecture uses two hooks — UserPromptExpansion (detects slash-command invocations of start-bead/implement-bead with clean command_name/command_args, no regex) and UserPromptSubmit (emits sessionTitle via marker file written by UserPromptExpansion). A skill-body !-injection marker-file approach is a fallback for natural-language invocations. Detection of user-set vs skill-set names uses format-match regex (^[a-z0-9-]+: ).

Once a programmatic rename tool or sanctioned hook output is available, this bead is ready to spec and implement.
notes:
Automatically rename the current session using bead_id and title when starting a bead (start-bead or implement-bead), unless the user has already manually set a session name.

REQUIREMENTS:
- When invoking start-bead or implement-bead on a bead, rename session to format: <bead_id>: <title>
- Only apply rename if user has NOT manually assigned a session name
- Need a mechanism to detect whether session name was set by user vs by our skills
  - If set by skills, skills should have freedom to change the session name
  - If set by user, respect and do not override

RESEARCH NEEDED:
- Investigate whether other agents (Codex, Gemini, OpenCode) have session-naming capability
- Document cross-tool implementation strategy

IMPLEMENTATION FOCUS:
- Claude-specific (at least initially)
- Session name detection logic
- Integration with start-bead and implement-bead skills

comments.author: Scott Hamilton
comments.text:
Assess findings (Step 2 / brainstorm-bead):

PERSISTENCE MECHANISM (Claude Code):
- Session display name lives in a 'custom-title' JSONL record at the start of the session's transcript file (~/.claude/projects/<proj-slug>/<sid>.jsonl).
- Schema: {"type":"custom-title", "sessionId":"...", "name":..., "summary":...}.
- CLI flag '-n/--name <name>' writes this at session START; flag is startup-only.
- Visible surfaces: prompt box (TUI), /resume picker, terminal title.
- Of 146 local sessions, 0 have a non-null custom-title name — Scott has never used --name. The mechanism exists but is unused.

NOT YET CONFIRMED:
- Runtime rename API (slash command, hook output, or in-place .jsonl edit picked up by running harness). No /rename/ /title/ /name slash command was found.
- Whether appending or overwriting the first 'custom-title' record mid-session is honored by the running TUI.
- Terminal title (ANSI escape) can always be set from any process, but is the WEAKEST of the three surfaces (lost on terminal close, not in /resume picker).

CROSS-TOOL:
- Codex, Gemini, OpenCode session-naming capability: not investigated (deferred per bead's RESEARCH NEEDED clause). Will defer to a follow-up bead unless Scott wants in-scope.

ARCHITECTURAL DECISIONS TO ESCALATE (the discuss step):
1. Runtime-rename feasibility: confirm by experiment, or accept a degraded mode (terminal-title-only)?
2. Detection of user-set vs skill-set name: sentinel prefix, format-match, or sidecar state file?
3. Scope on cross-tool: ship Claude-only and file follow-ups, or design portable helper now?
4. Integration point: skill body, hook, or helper script invoked from skill?

QUESTIONS I AM DECIDING MYSELF:
- Re-rename on switching beads: YES (bead says 'skills should have freedom to change').
- Format: '<bead-id>: <title>' as stated in bead requirements.

title: stage:<role> label stamping for robust upstream step lookup in implement-bead
description:
## What

Stamp a stage:<role> label on each step-bead at formula pour time. Update implement-bead's upstream report retrieval to use bd list --label stage:diagnose instead of the current title-prefix match.

## Why

The current lookup in implement-bead SKILL.md §6 finds the diagnose step-bead by title prefix (select(.title | startswith("Diagnose:"))). This breaks on any title rename. A stage:diagnose label stamped at pour time makes the lookup robust and intent-explicit.

## Scope (to be specced in brainstorming)

- Update fix-bug.formula.toml to stamp stage:diagnose, stage:red-tests, stage:green-loop on respective step-beads at pour time
- Update implement-feature.formula.toml similarly for its stages
- Update implement-bead/SKILL.md §6 upstream lookup to use --label stage:diagnose
- Document the stage:<role> convention for formula authors

## Dependencies

Depends on 7bk.19.4 (which shipped the title-prefix lookup this replaces).

title: Explicit agent:* / skill:* dispatch label overrides for implement-bead
description:
## What

Add explicit agent:<name> and skill:<name> label overrides to implement-bead's dispatch resolution. Allows formula authors to name specific workers or execution skills on a per-step or per-bead basis, overriding the default stage→agent map.

## Why

The current (stage, mode) → agent map is hardcoded in implement-bead/SKILL.md. Custom agents or non-standard methodologies have no way to declare their worker without editing the central map.

## Scope (to be specced in brainstorming)

- Define label namespaces: agent:<subagent_type>, skill:<skill-name>
- Precedence rule: explicit label beats default stage→agent map
- Update implement-bead/SKILL.md dispatch step 3 to check these labels before consulting the map
- Document in formula authoring conventions how and when to use overrides

## Dependencies

Depends on 7bk.19.4 (which finalizes the stage→agent map being overridden).

title: inline-execution directive for implement-bead dispatch
description:
## What

Add an inline-execution directive to implement-bead's dispatch logic. When a step-bead carries the inline signal, the orchestrator executes the step in the current session instead of dispatching a worker subagent.

## Why

Not every formula step justifies full subagent overhead. Trivial steps (version bumps, config changes, simple shell tasks) currently pay the same dispatch cost as a full TDD green-loop.

## Scope (to be specced in brainstorming)

- Define trigger mechanism (inline label on step-bead? inline field in description?)
- Update implement-bead/SKILL.md dispatch logic (step 3) to branch on inline signal before worker-dispatch path
- Define worker-report-v1 semantics for inline steps (synthetic report emitted by orchestrator itself? or bypass contract for truly trivial steps?)
- Update formula authoring conventions to document when inline is appropriate

## Out of Scope

- Changes to worker-report-v1 schema
- Changes to any formula files (implement-bead skill change only)

## Dependencies

Depends on 7bk.19.4 (which finalizes the dispatch logic this directive would bypass).

title: Tighten brainstorm-bead.formula.toml finalize Report-block voice
description:
# Context

The brainstorm-bead formula's finalize step contains a 'Report to the user' block that speaks in awkwardly mixed voice: nominally what the agent reports to the user, but includes imperatives like 'Do NOT continue to implementation in this session unless you explicitly authorize it' — reading as the agent giving the USER an imperative about the AGENT's own behavior.

# Previous action

The first code-simplifier pass on agents-config-uvt PR #12 flagged this as a candidate for a future tightening pass but declined to reshape it under the 'smallest change that improves clarity' bar.

# Proposed direction

Rewrite to first-person-agent voice:
  'I will stop here. The default is: another agent (run-queue, in a dedicated session) picks up implementation. To implement in this session instead, say explicitly ("implement it now", "do <bead-id> next", etc.) — otherwise I will not continue.'

# Discovered-from

agents-config-uvt

title: Phase 1.5: evaluate risk-closers before fork escalation
description:
# Context

After `agents-config-lu3.1` (wisp materialization fix) and `agents-config-lu3.2` (beads ↔ superpowers partnership contract) ship, residual risks remain that could still cause agents to drift from the bead protocol. Analysis in session `c06c7b55` estimated the combined fixes reduce Divergence-1/2-style failures by ~60–75% in a fresh session, but leave gaps — especially around user-directed drift and skill-description magnetism.

This bead exists to **brainstorm, decide on, and (if chosen) implement a set of non-fork alternatives before escalating to `agents-config-cx6` (fork superpowers → beadpowers plugin).**

# Purpose

Decide whether any of the four risk-closers below are worth taking, and in what order, BEFORE paying the maintenance cost of a superpowers fork.

Only after this bead is resolved (options decided + implemented OR explicitly deferred) does the fork bead `agents-config-cx6` become unblocked.

# Candidate options to evaluate

## Option A: Hook-level enforcement

A PreToolUse hook that blocks the `Skill` tool from invoking `superpowers:writing-plans`, `superpowers:executing-plans`, or `superpowers:subagent-driven-development` when a bead is in progress (claimed or in_progress state detectable via bd).

- **Pro**: active enforcement, not passive rule compliance
- **Con**: requires hook development + maintenance; hook may be noisy if bead state detection is imperfect
- **Effort**: medium

## Option B: User-direction guardrail

When the user directly requests ralf-it, writing-plans, executing-plans, or subagent-driven-development on a bead, the agent surfaces `implement-bead` as the bead-workflow alternative and asks the user to confirm. Lives as a red flag in `start-bead` / `implement-bead` SKILL.md or as a rule in `beads.md`.

- **Pro**: very cheap (~3 lines); closes the single highest-impact residual risk (user-directed drift)
- **Con**: adds friction to every user-directed skill invocation on a bead; user may find it annoying
- **Effort**: low
- **Note**: this was proposed as "Fix F" in the Divergence 2 brainstorm and explicitly deferred pending this evaluation

## Option C: Session-audit cadence

Schedule a recurring audit (e.g. weekly, or every N sessions) that scans transcripts for partnership-contract violations: instances where an agent invoked writing-plans / executing-plans / subagent-driven-development while a bead was claimed. Use a scheduled cron trigger (see `superpowers:schedule` skill) or manual cadence.

- **Pro**: detects drift early; low-cost monitoring
- **Con**: detection ≠ prevention; process overhead
- **Effort**: low-medium (depends on how automated)

## Option D: Broaden the failure-mode block in start-bead

Current Divergence 1 fix covers only "0/0 steps after wisp creation" as the named failure mode. Broaden to cover:
- "X/Y already done" (re-entry into a completed molecule)
- "blocked with no [ready] steps" (dependency misconfiguration)
- CLI error states from bd commands
- Interactive step re-entry (how does agent know to advance after user answers in the discuss step?)

Each state gets a diagnostic + recovery action in `start-bead` SKILL.md.

- **Pro**: closes several narrow-but-real edge cases from the Divergence 1 analysis
- **Con**: more skill text to maintain; risk of over-engineering
- **Effort**: low

# Decision process

1. **Observe lu3.2 in the wild**: need at least 2 fresh session audits after the partnership contract ships to establish a baseline drift rate
2. **Brainstorm session**: weigh each option against observed drift patterns
3. **Record decisions**: use `bd decision` for each option (accepted / deferred / rejected with rationale)
4. **Implement accepted options**: each becomes a sub-bead, dependency on this one
5. **Resolve this bead**: close with outcome summary
6. **Unblock cx6 or close cx6**: either escalate to fork, or close cx6 as "no longer needed"

# Trigger conditions to start this bead

Do NOT start this bead until:
- `agents-config-lu3.1` is merged AND
- `agents-config-lu3.2` is merged AND
- At least 2 subsequent session audits have been performed to gather drift baseline data

# Acceptance criteria

- Each of the four options has a `bd decision` record: accepted / deferred / rejected with rationale
- Any accepted options are implemented (as sub-beads) OR explicitly scheduled
- Final outcome summary written to this bead
- `agents-config-cx6` (fork bead) is either unblocked (go fork) or closed (partnership + risk-closers are sufficient)
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass. (for any implemented options)

# Notes

- This bead BLOCKS `agents-config-cx6`. The fork cannot proceed until this evaluation is complete.
- Residual risks list compiled from session `c06c7b55` analysis of Divergence 1 + 2 fix confidence
- Option B (user-direction guardrail) was explicitly deferred from lu3.2 scope for evaluation here
- If all four options are rejected AND lu3.2 alone is sufficient in practice, close cx6 — no fork needed
acceptance_criteria:
- Baseline window: evaluate first 10 bead-tracked sessions after lu3.2.
- Contract-violation KPI: <=10% sessions invoke writing-plans/executing-plans/subagent-driven-development while bead is claimed or in_progress.
- Lifecycle-integrity KPI: >=90% sessions follow canonical path start-bead -> molecule steps -> implement-bead (when implementation-ready).
- Recovery KPI: when violation occurs, >=95% return to bead flow within 1 corrective turn (or <=3 tool actions).
- Escalation rule: if KPIs fail after accepted risk-closers, unblock cx6; if KPIs pass without additional controls, close cx6 as no longer needed.
notes:
ROI CLARIFICATION (added 2026-05-05)

Specific divergence patterns observed in audited sessions:

1) Molecule execution drift (Divergence 1): expected start-bead -> brainstorm molecule step execution; observed 0/0 materialization and abandonment of molecule-driven execution.
2) Skill-paradigm drift (Divergence 2): expected bead lifecycle as outer loop with superpowers only inside formula steps; observed fallback into standalone superpowers chain (writing-plans -> executing path/subagent-driven-development), bypassing implement-bead and lifecycle labels.

Intent of this bead: decide whether targeted non-fork safeguards are needed to reduce recurrence of those exact drift modes before paying fork maintenance cost.
DEPRIORITIZATION RATIONALE (added 2026-05-05)

Recent sessions have not shown noticeable divergence recurrence. This bead is intentionally parked at P4 for monitor-first handling, not immediate implementation. Re-evaluate only if KPI thresholds in this bead are missed or drift frequency increases.

title: Native skill army: purpose-built skill set via synthesis assimilation strategy
description:
# Strategy: Synthesis Assimilation

## What this is

Build a purpose-built native skill set by synthesizing the best parts of external skill sources (superpowers, mattpocock/skills, original ideas) into new skills designed specifically for the bead-based workflow. This is NOT forking and maintaining upstream copies. It is:

- Reading external skills for ideas, techniques, and deltas
- Designing new skills that incorporate the best parts while discarding workflow-incompatible assumptions
- Keeping a leaner, more coherent skill set that Claude navigates without competing instruction sets burning tokens

## Why not coexist with external skills

The tenuous alliance with superpowers is a real cost: some of its skills carry incompatible workflow assumptions (e.g. brainstorming wants to go directly into its own execution model). Claude has to reconcile competing instruction sets, burning tokens and cognitive clarity. Adding more immutable external skills with overlapping triggers compounds the non-determinism problem:
- Claude is myopic in skill selection — it picks one, the delta from a better skill is lost
- If told to blend multiple skills, Claude infers an untested uber-skill with unpredictable behavior

Synthesis into purpose-built skills eliminates both failure modes.

## What assimilation means here

Not: fork + modify + maintain against upstream changes.
Instead: for each capability need, design a new skill that incorporates the best techniques from all sources, discards the parts that conflict with the bead workflow, and is fully owned here.

## Skill categories (from mattpocock audit, 2026-05-12)

**Gap-fill**: capabilities absent from the existing skill tree — new native skills, designed from scratch, informed by source ideas but not constrained by source implementations.

**Compare-and-improve**: overlaps with existing skills — synthesize the meaningful delta into the existing skill or a purpose-built replacement; discard workflow-incompatible parts.

**Spark**: novel ideas worth tracking — capture beads, evaluate when they mature.

## Meta-skill concept

A periodic agentic re-evaluation skill: given {repo}, scan changes since last evaluation, identify new ideas/skills/techniques worth semantic integration into the native architecture. NOT automated merge — agentic idea evaluation. Similar to what bead 4vn5.2 did for the initial audit.

## Relationship to 717 and the partnership contract

The 717 evaluation (risk-closers before escalation) and the partnership contract overlay rules remain valid. The synthesis strategy does not require 717 to complete first — Gap-fill work has no conflict with existing skills and can proceed independently. Compare-and-improve synthesis benefits from knowing which superpowers skills are most problematic (717's output), so those can be sequenced after 717 resolves.

## Trigger conditions (revised)

Was: 'fork only if partnership contract fails.'
Now: Gap-fill synthesis is always worth doing. Compare-and-improve synthesis proceeds as partnership contract friction accumulates evidence. The fork escalation (cx6's original scope) is absorbed into this broader synthesis mandate.

## Out of scope

- Maintaining forks of external repos
- Automated merge/sync from upstream
- Adopting external skills wholesale without synthesis
notes:
DEPRIORITIZATION RATIONALE (added 2026-05-05)

Fork escalation is intentionally deferred at P4 because recent sessions have not shown noticeable divergence recurrence. Keep this as a contingency path only; start only if 717 evaluation/KPIs indicate partnership controls are insufficient.
